items.title: Hoka 'curbs' competition law in major win for brand owners
items.title_zh: Hoka案限制竞争法适用，品牌所有者获重大胜利
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Upholds Hoka's Selective Distribution System Against Competition Law Challenges
items.summary: The UK Court of Appeal’s ruling in favour of Deckers over Up & Running is a boon for rights owners who rely on selective distribution systems, say Geoff Steward and Rona Bar-Isaac of Addleshaw Goddard, who led the successful case.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Deckers诉Up & Running案中作出有利于Deckers的裁决。律所Addleshaw Goddard指出，该裁决对依赖选择性分销系统的权利所有者而言是重大利好，表明法院在此类案件中限制了竞争法的适用范围。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal has ruled in favor of Deckers Corporation, the parent company of the Hoka brand, in its dispute against Up & Running. This decision confirms that selective distribution systems implemented by brands do not constitute illegal restrictions on competition under UK law. Legal experts from Addleshaw Goddard, who represented Deckers, highlight that this ruling provides significant legal support for intellectual property owners relying on such distribution models. The judgment clarifies the balance between protecting brand image and complying with competition regulations, thereby strengthening brand control over distribution channels. It serves as a critical precedent for rights holders seeking to combat gray market activities through structured, quality-controlled distribution networks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注英国法院在商标选择性分销案件中平衡知识产权保护与竞争法的具体裁判尺度。此裁决可能影响后续类似分销模式下的合规评估与诉讼策略，建议持续追踪该案判决书全文以明确法律推理细节。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in Europe, establishing a rigorous selective distribution framework remains an effective strategy for channel management and combating parallel imports. Rights holders should review existing distribution agreements to ensure they comply with competition law requirements while retaining necessary quality control powers. Consulting local counsel to optimize contract structures is advisable to leverage this precedent for stronger management of authorized dealers. This approach helps prevent unauthorized resales from damaging brand value, ensuring that distribution controls are both legally robust and commercially effective in protecting intellectual property assets across European markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院在Deckers诉Up & Running案中裁定支持Deckers", "该裁决被视为依赖选择性分销系统的品牌权利所有者的重大利好"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Court of Appeal ruled in favor of Deckers, confirming the legality of its selective distribution system under competition law.", "The judgment explicitly affirms that brands may restrict parallel trade via distribution agreements to maintain their premium image.", "This case establishes a key precedent for rights holders using selective distribution to effectively combat unauthorized gray market sales."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注选择性分销系统在英国的反垄断合规边界变化", "追踪本案判决书全文以获取具体的法律推理依据", "观察该判例对后续品牌方分销管控策略的潜在影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthening control over authorized channels and enforcing quality standards through optimized distribution agreement terms.", "Practice note: Constructing compliant selective distribution systems to legally challenge parallel imports and protect brand integrity.", "What to monitor: Further judicial interpretations regarding the scope of permissible restrictions within selective distribution networks."]
core_points_en: The UK Court of Appeal ruled in favor of Deckers, confirming the legality of its selective distribution system under competition law.
The judgment explicitly affirms that brands may restrict parallel trade via distribution agreements to maintain their premium image.
This case establishes a key precedent for rights holders using selective distribution to effectively combat unauthorized gray market sales.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthening control over authorized channels and enforcing quality standards through optimized distribution agreement terms.
Practice note: Constructing compliant selective distribution systems to legally challenge parallel imports and protect brand integrity.
What to monitor: Further judicial interpretations regarding the scope of permissible restrictions within selective distribution networks.

items.title: Launching “At Risk” in the UK: Why Recent Patent Injunction Decisions Matter for MedTech and Digital Health Companies
items.title_zh: 英国“冒险上市”风险：近期专利禁令裁决对医械数字健康企业的影响
items.title_en: UK Patent Injunctions: Risks for MedTech and Digital Health Launches
items.summary: Launching a product while a patent dispute remains unresolved has never been risk‑free, but many companies have taken the view that the...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国律所分析指出，尽管涉及制药业，但近期英国专利法院及上诉法院关于临时禁令的裁决对医疗器械和数字健康领域具有广泛参考意义。法院倾向于早期干预以维持现状，认定市场 disruption 和价格侵蚀造成的损害通常不可逆，即使专利有效性存疑。这显著增加了企业在最终判决前的诉讼风险，打破了仅靠损害赔偿即可补救的传统认知。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent UK court decisions indicate that launching products during unresolved patent disputes carries heightened risk, particularly for MedTech and digital health sectors. Although headline cases involve pharmaceuticals, courts apply similar reasoning to medical devices and AI tools, viewing market disruption as largely irreversible. The Court of Appeal in AstraZeneca v Glenmark and the High Court in Dr Reddy’s v Boehringer emphasized that price erosion and established clinical workflows create advantages that damages cannot adequately remedy. Consequently, interim injunctions are increasingly granted to preserve the status quo, even where patent validity is contested, significantly raising litigation risks for early market entrants in healthcare technology.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders in MedTech and digital health should anticipate stricter interim injunction standards in the UK. Courts prioritize preventing irreversible market entrenchment over commercial convenience, meaning launch strategies must account for potential early judicial intervention regardless of pending validity challenges.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国法院在临时禁令中视市场扰乱为不可逆损害，即便专利有效性受质疑", "阿斯利康案与红博士案确立原则：价格侵蚀及医疗市场进入优势难以通过金钱赔偿弥补", "医械与数字健康产品因嵌入系统及数据依赖，切换成本高，同样面临早期禁令风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK courts treat market disruption in healthcare sectors as largely irreversible harm warranting interim relief.", "Pharmaceutical case law principles now extend to MedTech, digital health, and AI-enabled medical devices.", "Established clinical workflows and system integration make post-launch switching difficult, strengthening injunction arguments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["临时禁令风险前置：在产品上市初期即可能面临禁令，而非等待最终侵权判定", "不可逆损害认定扩大：市场准入优势、临床习惯养成被视为难以通过损害赔偿修复", "跨领域适用性：制药领域的严厉禁令逻辑正延伸至医疗器械、数字健康及AI医疗工具"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: MedTech and digital health companies face elevated risks when launching during patent disputes, as courts may grant injunctions to prevent irreversible market en…", "Practice note: Arguments emphasizing irreversible harm from disrupted clinical workflows or data-dependent AI adoption are increasingly persuasive in UK interim injunction applica…", "What to monitor: Future UK High Court decisions applying pharmaceutical-style injunction logic to non-pharma medical devices and digital health platforms."]
core_points_en: UK courts treat market disruption in healthcare sectors as largely irreversible harm warranting interim relief.
Pharmaceutical case law principles now extend to MedTech, digital health, and AI-enabled medical devices.
Established clinical workflows and system integration make post-launch switching difficult, strengthening injunction arguments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: MedTech and digital health companies face elevated risks when launching during patent disputes, as courts may grant injunctions to prevent irreversible market en…
Practice note: Arguments emphasizing irreversible harm from disrupted clinical workflows or data-dependent AI adoption are increasingly persuasive in UK interim injunction applica…
What to monitor: Future UK High Court decisions applying pharmaceutical-style injunction logic to non-pharma medical devices and digital health platforms.

items.title: EUIPO receives first sound mark application to protect against digital replicas and deepfakes
items.title_zh: EUIPO受理首件防范数字复制的声音商标申请
items.title_en: EUIPO Receives First Sound Mark Application to Combat Digital Replicas and Deepfakes
items.summary: Giusy FerreriAfter Matthew McConaughey and Taylor Swift in the USA and a number of other applications before national trade mark offices (eg Italy), it is now the time of the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) to receive what has been presented as the very first trade mark application covering personal attributes and through which artists are seeking to gain
items.ai_summary_zh: 继美国及意大利等国后，欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）收到据称首件涵盖个人属性、旨在保护艺术家免受数字复制和深度伪造侵害的商标申请。此前马修·麦康纳与泰勒·斯威夫特在美国已有类似尝试。
items.ai_summary_en: Following similar filings in the United States by high-profile figures such as Matthew McConaughey and Taylor Swift, as well as applications before national offices like Italy's, the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has received what is presented as the first trade mark application specifically designed to protect personal attributes against digital replicas and deepfakes. Submitted by artist Giusy Ferreri, this filing highlights a growing trend where creators utilize trade mark law to safeguard their personality rights from the misuse of artificial intelligence technologies. This development marks a significant step in regulatory exploration at the EU level regarding the intersection of intellectual property and emerging technologies, potentially establishing an important precedent for future cases involving AI-driven identity protection.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需持续观察EUIPO对此类新型声音/个人属性商标的审查标准及可注册性认定。此举反映商标制度在AI生成内容背景下的功能扩展趋势，值得留意后续确权实践。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and content creators operating within the European market should closely monitor the evolving landscape of sound marks and personality rights protection. As AI-generated deepfakes pose escalating risks to brand integrity and individual identity, relying solely on traditional copyright may prove insufficient. A strategic approach involves combining trade mark registrations with copyright protections to create a robust defensive framework. Companies should assess their audio assets and consider early registration in the EU to mitigate potential infringement challenges. This proactive stance helps establish clearer legal boundaries against unauthorized digital impersonation and ensures comprehensive coverage for personal and commercial brand attributes in the digital age.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO收到首件涵盖个人属性的商标申请，旨在对抗数字复制", "该申请意在帮助艺术家通过商标权防范深度伪造技术滥用", "此前美国及意大利国家商标局已出现类似个人属性保护申请"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO has accepted the first sound mark application aimed at protecting personal attributes against unauthorized digital replication and deepfake technologies.", "Artists are increasingly leveraging trade mark registration to secure legal protection for their personal identities and prevent exploitation by generative AI tools.", "This filing represents a critical advancement in EU regulatory efforts addressing the complex intersection of intellectual property rights and artificial intelligence innovations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EUIPO对涵盖个人属性声音商标的审查尺度与确权结果", "观察商标法在应对深度伪造与数字身份盗用方面的适用边界", "追踪此类申请是否引发关于人格权与知识产权交叉的新争议"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor EU sound mark developments to proactively secure protection for personality identifiers and brand voice assets.", "Practice note: Combine trade mark and copyright strategies to effectively counter risks associated with AI-generated deepfakes and digital replicas.", "What to monitor: Regulatory precedents set by the EUIPO regarding the scope of protection for personal attributes against AI misuse."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO has accepted the first sound mark application aimed at protecting personal attributes against unauthorized digital replication and deepfake technologies.
Artists are increasingly leveraging trade mark registration to secure legal protection for their personal identities and prevent exploitation by generative AI tools.
This filing represents a critical advancement in EU regulatory efforts addressing the complex intersection of intellectual property rights and artificial intelligence innovations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor EU sound mark developments to proactively secure protection for personality identifiers and brand voice assets.
Practice note: Combine trade mark and copyright strategies to effectively counter risks associated with AI-generated deepfakes and digital replicas.
What to monitor: Regulatory precedents set by the EUIPO regarding the scope of protection for personal attributes against AI misuse.

items.title: Taylor Wessing bolsters German patent practice with Eisenführ Speiser hires
items.title_zh: Taylor Wessing聘请Eisenführ Speiser合伙人强化德国专利业务
items.title_en: Taylor Wessing Hires Two Partners from Eisenführ Speiser to Strengthen German Patent Practice
items.summary: The two new partners will join Taylor Wessing on 1 June. The firm is thus underlining its commitment to restoring the renowned patent practice to its former strength following the spin-off of a large team in the autumn. Michael Schneider (56) has more than 20 years of experience in patent litigation, particularly in mobile communications […] The post Taylor Wessing bolsters German patent practice with Eisenführ Speiser hires appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 国际律所Taylor Wessing宣布将于6月1日吸纳两名来自Eisenführ Speiser的新合伙人，以重建其德国专利实践团队。此举旨在弥补去年秋季大规模团队分拆后的实力损失，恢复该所在德国专利领域的传统优势。新加入的合伙人Michael Schneider拥有逾20年专利诉讼经验，尤其在移动通信领域具备深厚专长。这一人事变动凸显了律所在竞争激烈的德国市场中巩固其知识产权服务地位的战略意图。
items.ai_summary_en: International law firm Taylor Wessing has announced the appointment of two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser, effective 1 June. This move aims to restore the strength of its German patent practice following a significant team departure in the previous autumn. One of the new hires, Michael Schneider, brings over 20 years of experience in patent matters, with a specific focus on mobile communications technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信企业应关注德国专利诉讼团队变动，特别是具有移动通信背景的资深律师动向。这预示着德国专利诉讼活跃度可能提升，建议提前评估潜在风险，优化在德国市场的IP布局与应诉策略，必要时建立与该类强势律所的合作或预警机制。
items.ai_insight_en: This personnel change highlights ongoing consolidation and restructuring within the German patent legal market. Rights holders and companies operating in Germany should track these shifts to understand changes in available counsel expertise, particularly in high-volume technology sectors like mobile communications.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Taylor Wessing于6月1日引入两名新合伙人以强化德国专利团队", "此举旨在修复去年团队分拆影响，恢复其在德国市场的领先地位", "新合伙人Michael Schneider深耕移动通信领域二十余年专利诉讼"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Taylor Wessing appoints two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser starting 1 June to rebuild its German patent team.", "The hiring strategy addresses capacity gaps created by a large team spin-off that occurred in the autumn.", "New partner Michael Schneider contributes more than two decades of experience specializing in mobile communications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注德所人员变动，预判移动通信领域专利诉讼活跃度上升", "提前优化在欧IP布局，建立针对资深诉讼专家的应对与合作机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor changes in firm capacity and specialist expertise in key technology sectors for future engagement planning.", "Practice note: Track lateral hiring trends among major IP firms to assess market stability and service availability in Germany.", "What to monitor: Further developments in the composition of leading German patent practices and their impact on sector-specific legal services."]
core_points_en: Taylor Wessing appoints two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser starting 1 June to rebuild its German patent team.
The hiring strategy addresses capacity gaps created by a large team spin-off that occurred in the autumn.
New partner Michael Schneider contributes more than two decades of experience specializing in mobile communications.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor changes in firm capacity and specialist expertise in key technology sectors for future engagement planning.
Practice note: Track lateral hiring trends among major IP firms to assess market stability and service availability in Germany.
What to monitor: Further developments in the composition of leading German patent practices and their impact on sector-specific legal services.

items.title: Head of IP at Heidelberg Pharma joins Fuchs Patentanwälte
items.title_zh: 海德堡制药IP负责人加入Fuchs专利律所
items.title_en: Heidelberg Pharma IP Head Thomas Fischer Joins Fuchs Patentanwälte
items.summary: Thomas Fischer will join the firm on 1 June, though not as a partner. At Heidelberg Pharma, Fischer is responsible for IP matters across both the listed parent company Heidelberg Pharma AG and its wholly-owned subsidiary Heidelberg Pharma Research GmbH. He is qualified as a German and European patent attorney, as well as a European […] The post Head of IP at Heidelberg Pharma joins Fuchs Patentanwälte appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 托马斯·费舍尔将于6月1日加入Fuchs Patentanwälte律所，但不担任合伙人职位。此前，他在海德堡制药负责上市公司及其全资子公司的知识产权事务。费舍尔拥有德国及欧洲专利代理师资格。
items.ai_summary_en: Thomas Fischer, currently Head of IP at Heidelberg Pharma, will join the German firm Fuchs Patentanwälte on 1 June. He will not assume a partner role upon joining. Fischer currently manages intellectual property matters for both the listed parent company, Heidelberg Pharma AG, and its wholly-owned subsidiary, Heidelberg Pharma Research GmbH. He holds qualifications as both a German and a European patent attorney. This move represents a transition from an in-house leadership position within the pharmaceutical sector to external private practice, bringing specialized industry experience to the firm’s existing team.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This appointment highlights the movement of experienced in-house counsel into external firms, potentially enhancing service offerings in the life sciences sector. For rights holders, such transitions may influence how firms structure teams with deep industry-specific knowledge. Observing the integration of former in-house leaders can provide insights into evolving service capabilities within German patent practices, particularly for pharmaceutical clients seeking practitioners with direct operational background.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["托马斯·费舍尔将于6月1日加入Fuchs Patentanwälte，非合伙人身份", "费舍尔此前负责海德堡制药及其子公司的全面知识产权事务", "费舍尔具备德国专利代理师及欧洲专利代理师双重资格"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Thomas Fischer joins Fuchs Patentanwälte on 1 June in a non-partner capacity.", "Fischer previously managed IP for Heidelberg Pharma AG and its research subsidiary.", "He holds dual qualifications as a German and European patent attorney."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注具有药企内部IP管理背景的代理师职业流动方向", "观察生命科学领域企业对内外结合IP服务模式的需求变化", "后续可追踪该人员在新律所的具体业务侧重与客户群体"]

core_points_en: Thomas Fischer joins Fuchs Patentanwälte on 1 June in a non-partner capacity.
Fischer previously managed IP for Heidelberg Pharma AG and its research subsidiary.
He holds dual qualifications as a German and European patent attorney.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Access to practitioners with recent in-house pharmaceutical IP management experience may benefit companies seeking aligned external counsel.
Practice note: The hire reflects a trend of integrating former in-house leaders into external firms to strengthen sector-specific expertise.
What to monitor: Future developments regarding Fischer’s specific technical focus and client engagements at Fuchs Patentanwälte.

items.title: Halozyme suffers setback in Keytruda patent battle with Merck; receives adverse AG opinion at CJEU
items.title_zh: Halozyme在Keytruda专利战中受挫，获CJEU不利AG意见
items.title_en: Halozyme suffers setback in Keytruda patent battle with Merck; receives adverse AG opinion at CJEU
items.summary: Meanwhile, the licensor is continuing to notch up patent deals at an impressive rate
items.ai_summary_zh: Halozyme在与默沙东关于Keytruda的专利诉讼中遭遇挫折，欧盟法院（CJEU）总顾问发布了对其不利的意见。尽管面临这一法律 setback，作为许可方的默沙东仍在以令人印象深刻的速度达成多项专利交易。该动态突显了欧洲高层级司法机构在关键药物专利争议中的影响力，以及大型药企在复杂诉讼背景下持续强化其知识产权组合许可能力的战略态势。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应密切关注CJEU在医药专利领域的最新司法倾向，特别是总顾问意见对最终判决的指导作用。在欧开展业务时，需加强FTO分析，避免陷入高额专利诉讼。同时，学习默沙东等巨头在诉讼期间维持许可谈判的策略，构建灵活的IP风险应对与商业化体系。

items.title: Legal holds and document preservation: How to get on the front foot
items.title_zh: 法律保留与文件保全：如何掌握诉讼主动权
items.title_en: UK Legal Holds: Mandatory Document Preservation Under PD57AD
items.summary: Identification and preservation of documentary evidence at the early stages of a dispute isn’t just best practice, it is a mandatory procedural requirem...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，在争议早期识别并保存文件证据不仅是最佳实践，更是强制性程序要求。依据《民事诉讼规则》及PD57AD，当当事人知晓可能成为诉讼方时，必须立即采取具体步骤识别并保存相关文件。这包括实施“停止时钟”的数据保留措施。及时且妥善的法律保留能确保在后续收集和审查阶段前完成初步工作，从而在诉讼中占据有利地位，避免因证据灭失而面临不利后果。
items.ai_summary_en: Under English law, identifying and preserving documentary evidence at the early stages of a dispute is a mandatory procedural requirement, not merely best practice. Practice Direction 57AD to the Civil Procedure Rules imposes strict obligations on parties who know they may become involved in litigation. This includes immediate 'stop the clock' measures to prevent data alteration or deletion. The definition of 'document' is broad, covering anything in which information is recorded. Properly implemented legal holds ensure compliance with disclosure duties and facilitate subsequent evidence collection and review processes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对欧洲IP纠纷时，应建立即时响应机制。一旦收到律师函或知悉潜在诉讼，须立即冻结相关电子数据与文档，防止自动覆盖。忽视此程序义务可能导致证据被排除或面临制裁。建议提前制定合规预案，确保在UPC或英国法院诉讼中有效履行披露义务，维护自身合法权益。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and companies operating in the UK, understanding the strict timing of preservation duties is critical for effective portfolio management and dispute readiness. Failure to comply with these procedural requirements can lead to significant adverse consequences during litigation. Establishing robust internal protocols ensures that evidence remains intact and admissible, supporting stronger positions in enforcement or defense scenarios without relying on specific litigation tactics.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法律保留是强制性程序义务，非仅最佳实践，需严格遵守", "触发点为知晓可能成为诉讼当事人，须立即启动文件识别与保存", "需执行“停止时钟”数据保留，确保早期保全以利于后续举证"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["PD57AD triggers preservation duties when a party knows it may become involved in proceedings.", "Parties must implement immediate 'stop the clock' measures to preserve relevant electronic data.", "The scope of 'document' for disclosure purposes includes any medium recording information."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立即时响应机制，知悉潜在诉讼即冻结相关电子数据", "重视程序合规，避免因未履行证据保全义务导致证据失效"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Early identification of potential disputes allows for timely compliance with mandatory preservation duties under English procedural rules.", "Practice note: Implement automated 'stop the clock' mechanisms to secure electronic data immediately upon recognizing litigation risk.", "What to monitor: Updates to Practice Direction 57AD and Civil Procedure Rules regarding the scope and technical standards for document preservation."]
core_points_en: PD57AD triggers preservation duties when a party knows it may become involved in proceedings.
Parties must implement immediate 'stop the clock' measures to preserve relevant electronic data.
The scope of 'document' for disclosure purposes includes any medium recording information.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early identification of potential disputes allows for timely compliance with mandatory preservation duties under English procedural rules.
Practice note: Implement automated 'stop the clock' mechanisms to secure electronic data immediately upon recognizing litigation risk.
What to monitor: Updates to Practice Direction 57AD and Civil Procedure Rules regarding the scope and technical standards for document preservation.

items.title: Game over: Subsonic prevails against Sony in infringement case over PlayStation controllers
items.title_zh: 游戏结束：Subsonic在PlayStation手柄专利侵权案中胜诉索尼
items.title_en: Subsonic Prevails Against Sony in PlayStation Controller Patent Infringement Dispute
items.summary: Since 2014 the parties have been locked in a fierce battle over three patents, EP 0 867 212, EP 08 34 338 and EP 13 31 974. They cover controllers for the popular Sony PlayStation games console. All three patents have long expired: EP 212 and EP 338 in 2017, with EP 974 expiring in 2020. In late 2016, […] The post Game over: Subsonic prevails against Sony in infringement case over PlayStation controllers appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 自2014年起，Subsonic与索尼就三项涉及PlayStation游戏机控制器的欧洲专利展开激烈诉讼。这三项专利（EP 0 867 212、EP 08 34 338和EP 13 31 974）均已过期。标题显示Subsonic在此侵权案件中 prevail（占上风/胜诉）。
items.ai_summary_en: Since 2014, Subsonic and Sony have engaged in protracted litigation concerning three European patents covering PlayStation controllers: EP 0 867 212, EP 0 834 338, and EP 1 331 974. Although these patents expired in 2017 and 2020 respectively, the parties continued to dispute infringement damages and the jurisdiction of the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The recent judgment confirms Subsonic’s victory in the infringement action, establishing a favorable precedent for rights holders. This outcome underscores the legal complexities surrounding post-expiration liability and demonstrates that expired patents can still generate significant financial exposure through retrospective infringement claims within the evolving European patent litigation landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 由于正文内容缺失，无法确认具体管辖法院或判决细节。需关注该案是否涉及UPC或德国法院，以及“prevail”的具体法律含义（如禁令或损害赔偿）。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights the strategic importance of monitoring expired patents for potential retrospective infringement liabilities. Companies operating in Europe should maintain robust Freedom to Operate (FTO) frameworks that account for historical usage risks, not just current validity status. With the Unified Patent Court introducing new procedural dynamics, stakeholders must adapt their compliance strategies to address cross-border enforcement challenges. Regular audits of legacy IP exposures are essential to mitigate unexpected financial burdens arising from prolonged litigation cycles involving older patent portfolios.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["双方自2014年就三项涉及PlayStation控制器的欧洲专利进行诉讼", "涉案三项欧洲专利均已过期，最后一种于2020年到期", "标题显示Subsonic在针对索尼的侵权案件中取得胜诉"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Subsonic secured a final victory against Sony in a long-running patent infringement dispute regarding PlayStation controller technology.", "The case involved three expired European patents, highlighting complex legal battles over infringement damages and UPC jurisdictional scope.", "The ruling illustrates the enduring risks of retrospective infringement claims even after the formal expiration of patent rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需核实判决的具体法院层级及法律依据", "观察专利过期后诉讼结果的执行效力范围", "监测后续是否有上诉或关联案件动态"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Expired patents may still trigger substantial retrospective infringement claims, requiring continuous risk assessment beyond standard validity checks.", "Practice note: Strengthen FTO monitoring mechanisms to include historical usage analysis and prepare for UPC-specific procedural strategies.", "What to monitor: Evolving UPC jurisprudence on jurisdiction and damages calculation for patents that have already lapsed during litigation."]
core_points_en: Subsonic secured a final victory against Sony in a long-running patent infringement dispute regarding PlayStation controller technology.
The case involved three expired European patents, highlighting complex legal battles over infringement damages and UPC jurisdictional scope.
The ruling illustrates the enduring risks of retrospective infringement claims even after the formal expiration of patent rights.

items.title: A comparative guide to enforcing trade secrets across major jurisdictions
items.title_zh: 主要司法辖区商业秘密执法比较指南
items.title_en: Comparative Guide to Enforcing Trade Secrets Across Major Global Jurisdictions
items.summary: Experts from HSF Kramer dig into the practicalities of using the courts in China, Germany, France, the UK, Italy and the US
items.ai_summary_zh: HSF Kramer律所专家撰写指南，深入探讨在中国、德国、法国、英国、意大利及美国利用法院系统执行商业秘密的实际操作与程序细节。原文仅提供摘要信息，未包含具体法律条款或案例细节。
items.ai_summary_en: This comparative guide, authored by experts from Herbert Smith Freehills, examines the practical realities of litigating trade secret disputes in China, Germany, France, the UK, Italy, and the US. The analysis highlights significant procedural differences across these key jurisdictions, focusing on court practices regarding evidence preservation, disclosure requirements, and available remedies. By contrasting enforcement efficiency and legal certainty, the article provides multinational enterprises with essential insights for developing robust global trade secret protection strategies. It emphasizes the necessity of tailoring enforcement approaches to specific market characteristics, ensuring that rights holders can effectively navigate complex cross-border litigation landscapes while managing risks associated with varying judicial standards and procedural hurdles.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文仅为摘要，无法提取具体执法差异或策略建议。需关注该指南全文发布后，关于欧洲各国在证据保全、禁令救济及损害赔偿方面的具体程序对比，以评估跨境维权成本与可行性。
items.ai_insight_en: Multinational companies must account for significant judicial disparities in trade secret protection, particularly concerning burdens of proof and the difficulty of obtaining injunctive relief. Establishing a tiered confidentiality system is crucial, alongside pre-planned evidence preservation protocols tailored to each target jurisdiction. In civil law countries like Germany and France, emphasis should be placed on preventive measures and administrative complaints to address high evidentiary thresholds. Conversely, in common law jurisdictions such as the US and UK, strengthening pre-litigation evidence preservation efforts is vital to enhance the likelihood of securing preliminary injunctions, thereby reducing cross-border enforcement costs and improving overall success rates.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["HSF Kramer专家分析多国法院执行商业秘密的实务操作", "覆盖法域包括中国、德国、法国、英国、意大利及美国"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Analyzes judicial practice differences and procedural characteristics in trade secret litigation across six major jurisdictions.", "Evaluates the practical effectiveness of key remedies such as evidence disclosure and preliminary injunctions in various courts.", "Provides strategic recommendations for cross-border trade secret enforcement based on specific jurisdictional legal frameworks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需等待全文发布以获取各国法院执法的具体程序差异", "关注欧洲法域与中国、美国在商业秘密救济措施上的对比", "后续观察重点为各国在举证责任与临时禁令方面的实务要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adapt enforcement strategies to local judicial nuances, prioritizing prevention in civil law systems and pre-trial preparation in common law regions.", "Practice note: Implement tiered confidentiality measures and jurisdiction-specific evidence preservation plans to mitigate cross-border litigation risks.", "What to monitor: Track evolving standards for preliminary injunctions and evidence disclosure requirements in key markets like Germany, France, the US, and the UK."]

Evaluates the practical effectiveness of key remedies such as evidence disclosure and preliminary injunctions in various courts.
Provides strategic recommendations for cross-border trade secret enforcement based on specific jurisdictional legal frameworks.

items.title: TALKUAL Selects Markify Watch To Strengthen Its Brand Protection Strategy
items.title_zh: TALKUAL选用Markify Watch加强品牌保护
items.title_en: TALKUAL Adopts Markify Watch to Strengthen Trademark Protection Strategy
items.summary: Questel, a global leader in intellectual property (IP) solutions, is pleased to announce that TALKUAL has chosen Markify to watch for new, identical, and confusingly similar trademarks
items.ai_summary_zh: 总部位于西班牙的食品科技公司TALKUAL选择Questel旗下的Markify Watch服务，以监控新出现的相同或近似商标。该公司致力于减少食物浪费，随着市场扩张，将知识产权保护列为战略重点。通过采用该监控工具，旨在早期发现潜在冲突并维护品牌身份。Questel表示此举符合其支持创新企业的承诺。
items.ai_summary_en: Spain-based foodtech company TALKUAL has selected Questel’s Markify Watch service to monitor for new, identical, and confusingly similar trademarks. Founded in 2020, TALKUAL focuses on reducing food waste by distributing imperfect produce. As the company expands its market presence, it has prioritized intellectual property protection to safeguard its brand identity. The implementation of Markify Watch aims to enable early detection of potential conflicts and support informed decision-making. Questel highlighted that this collaboration underscores the importance of robust IP strategies for scaling companies, particularly those driving innovation in sustainability. The tool provides global monitoring capabilities to help businesses identify risks and enforce their rights in a competitive landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，西班牙后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This adoption illustrates how scaling European startups integrate automated monitoring into their IP lifecycle management. For rights holders expanding across jurisdictions, proactive surveillance supports timely enforcement actions against conflicting marks. It highlights the operational shift toward continuous risk assessment rather than reactive clearance, ensuring brand integrity is maintained during rapid growth phases in competitive sectors like foodtech.

items.title_en: Licences de propriété intellectuelle : quand la géopolitique s'invite à la table des négociations
items.summary: An unexpected error has occurred on this page. The technical team has been notified and users are encouraged to retry their request.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出2026年地缘政治已成为IP许可的关键变量。以霍尔木兹海峡危机及中美制裁冲突为例，说明供应链中断与法律合规困境如何冲击许可执行。文章强调不可抗力条款需重新审视，且因地缘风险导致许可费估值基础不稳，长期合同面临重构压力。
items.ai_insight_zh: 地缘政治已深度嵌入IP许可全周期。实务中需关注不可抗力条款对制裁、禁运等事件的覆盖精度，以及在地缘动荡下许可费计算基础的稳定性。权利人应监测管辖权与实际执行地的错位风险，而非仅依赖传统合同模板。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["地缘政治冲击如海峡危机导致供应链重组，增加IP资产在替代走廊的假冒风险", "中美制裁冲突使跨国许可方面临遵守一方规则即违反另一方禁令的法律两难", "传统不可抗力条款难以覆盖新型地缘事件，且销售波动导致许可费估值基础不稳定"]

items.title: Ban on the use of meat names for plant-based alternatives
items.title_zh: 欧盟达成禁令：植物基替代品禁用肉类名称
items.title_en: EU Agreement Bans Specific Meat Names for Plant-Based Alternatives
items.summary: The EU recently reached an agreement on the question of whether a meat name could be used for plant-based veg products marketed as a substitute for meat.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟机构达成协议，禁止在植物基替代产品上使用特定肉类名称（如鸡肉、牛肉），旨在防止消费者被误导并依据《欧盟商标条例》第7条避免注册误导性商标。通用词汇如“汉堡”仍可使用。该禁令可能导致含肉类名称的植物基品牌无法继续使用现有注册商标，迫使供应商修改包装及品牌策略以符合新规。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Parliament, Council, and member states have agreed to prohibit the use of specific meat names for plant-based alternatives. This measure aims to prevent consumer confusion by reserving terms like 'chicken' or 'beef' exclusively for animal products. While generic descriptors such as 'burger' remain permissible, brands containing restricted animal references may face usage restrictions under Article 7 of the EU Trademark Regulation, which prohibits misleading trademarks regarding product nature.
items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧出口植物基食品的中国企业而言，需立即审查现有商标及包装，移除鸡、牛等具体肉类词汇以避免侵权或违规。建议保留burger等通用词，重新设计品牌标识。同时评估现有注册商标的有效性，必要时进行变更或新申请，确保符合欧盟反误导规定，降低法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This regulatory shift impacts portfolio management for food sector rights holders. Brands using protected marks with specific animal references must assess compliance risks, as legal protection does not automatically override statutory bans on misleading commercial practices. Companies should evaluate whether their current packaging and branding strategies align with the new distinction between specific meat terms and generic descriptors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟禁止植物基产品使用特定动物部位名称，以防误导公众", "依据EUTMR第7条，误导性商标不得注册，现有品牌可能受限", "通用描述词如burger允许使用，但需移除具体肉类指代词汇"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Specific animal part terms including chicken, beef, and bacon are prohibited for vegetarian products to ensure clarity.", "Generic descriptive words like burger or sausage remain allowed for marketing plant-based food substitutes.", "Existing trademarks referencing specific animals may become unusable if deemed misleading under current EU regulations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立即审查在欧植物基产品包装，移除具体肉类名称以合规", "评估现有含肉类词商标风险，及时调整品牌策略应对禁令"]

core_points_en: Specific animal part terms including chicken, beef, and bacon are prohibited for vegetarian products to ensure clarity.

items.title: IA et droit d’auteur : vers une « taxe sur les données » en Europe ?
items.title_zh: 人工智能与版权：欧洲拟设“数据税”？
items.title_en: Mistral AI Proposes Revenue-Based Contribution for Copyrighted Training Data in Europe
items.summary: Mistral AI propose un mécanisme de contribution financière pour les entreprises d'IA utilisant des contenus protégés, visant à concilier innovation technologique et respect du droit d'auteur.
items.ai_summary_zh: Mistral AI提议建立基于营收的财务贡献机制，以补偿AI训练中使用受版权保护内容。该提案旨在解决现行欧盟版权指令下“选择退出”机制执行难的问题，平衡技术创新与权利人报酬。尽管意在提供法律确定性并支持文化产业，但该方案遭到部分权利持有人强烈反对，认为其动摇版权法根基。
items.ai_summary_en: Mistral AI has proposed a financial contribution mechanism for artificial intelligence companies utilizing copyrighted content, aiming to balance technological innovation with intellectual property rights. The model suggests that AI developers pay a contribution proportional to their turnover in exchange for using publicly accessible online content. This initiative seeks to provide remuneration to rights holders while offering legal certainty to AI firms facing ambiguity under current regulations. The proposal highlights perceived limitations in the existing EU Copyright Directive framework, particularly the practical difficulties of enforcing opt-out mechanisms due to the massive scale of online content and lack of effective traceability tools.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This proposal reflects growing tension between AI development and copyright enforcement in the EU. It signals a potential shift from individual opt-out regimes toward collective compensation models, akin to private copying levies. Rights holders and AI developers should monitor whether this industry-led concept influences upcoming EU legislative adjustments or judicial interpretations regarding text and data mining exceptions.

The initiative aims to compensate rights holders and provide legal certainty amidst current regulatory ambiguity.

Practice note: The proposal draws parallels to private copying levies, suggesting a mutualized compensation approach rather than individual licensing for mass-scale training data…
What to monitor: Track EU policy responses to this proposal and any subsequent legislative moves to replace or supplement the current text and data mining exceptions.

items.title_en: The creativity of design: Do you need to be original to have individual character

items.title: Design Registration: The Optimal Way To Protect Your Design
items.title_zh: 外观设计注册：保护产品外观的最佳途径
items.title_en: Design Registration: The Optimal Way To Protect Your Design
items.summary: Companies invest heavily in visual identity, but without proper protection, that investment remains vulnerable. Design registration offers a practical approach to safeguarding product appearance by focusing on novelty and individual character, making it essential to consider protection early and align filing strategies with target markets.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析企业视觉身份保护策略，指出相较于著作权和三维商标，外观设计注册因无需证明创造性且门槛较低，是更优选择。文章强调欧盟未注册外观设计权在期限和范围上的局限，推荐通过注册获得更强保护。同时介绍WIPO海牙体系作为跨国申请工具的优势，引用2025年申请量增长数据，说明其在多领土覆盖中的实用性。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中应优先评估外观设计注册而非依赖著作权或商标，特别是在AI辅助设计导致原创性证明困难的背景下。海牙体系的数据增长反映其成为跨国布局主流渠道，企业需关注其程序优势以优化全球外观保护组合，降低多国单独申请的成本与复杂性。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["外观设计注册仅需新颖性和独特个性，无需像著作权那样证明创造性，亦比三维商标更易获权和执行", "欧盟未注册外观设计权在保护期限、范围及地域上存在局限，且举证困难，故强烈推荐进行正式注册", "WIPO海牙体系允许电子提交并包含多达100项设计，2025年国际申请量增长9.4%…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在AI生成内容增多背景下，外观设计注册因不审查创作过程而比著作权更具确定性", "海牙体系申请量创新高，表明跨国企业正转向该集中化程序以优化欧盟及全球外观布局", "需持续监测三维商标注册的司法实践难度，避免将资源过度投入难以确权的商标保护路径"]

items.title: They shoot, but do they score?
items.title_zh: Instagram对-GRAM后缀的掌控力分析
items.title_en: EUIPO Upholds Instagram's Strong Rights Over the -GRAM Suffix in Opposition Case
items.summary: Instagram’s grip on the -GRAM suffix proves picture perfect, writes Oli Gray. B 3 219 888, Instagram LLC v Panathinaikos, EUIPO Opposition Division.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，在Instagram LLC诉Panathinaikos案（B 3 219 888）中，EUIPO异议部门裁定Instagram对“-GRAM”后缀拥有稳固权利。文章由Oli Gray撰写，标题暗示尽管对方尝试挑战，但未能成功动摇Instagram对该描述性后缀的商标保护地位。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary analyzes the EUIPO Opposition Division's decision in Instagram LLC v Panathinaikos (Case B 3 219 888). Although the suffix '-gram' possesses descriptive characteristics, Instagram's brand is recognized for its exceptionally high reputation. Consequently, the authority affirmed that the company maintains a firm grip on this suffix. The ruling confirms that later trademark applications incorporating '-gram' face significant risks of opposition and rejection if they are likely to cause public confusion. This decision reinforces the protective scope afforded to well-known trademarks, demonstrating that substantial market recognition can overcome the inherent descriptiveness of specific word elements in establishing likelihood of confusion.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注EUIPO对包含通用或描述性后缀的复合商标显著性的审查标准。实务中应监测此类裁决是否确立了对常见后缀更宽泛的保护先例，以及其对后续近似商标异议案件的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing social media or content platforms should carefully evaluate naming strategies to mitigate infringement risks associated with the '-gram' suffix. Even if a term has etymological descriptive value, well-known brands secure robust protection through acquired distinctiveness and extensive use. Conducting comprehensive EU trademark searches is essential before finalizing brand names. Applicants must ensure their branding does not encroach upon the protected boundaries of famous marks like Instagram, thereby ensuring legal compliance and safeguarding long-term brand security within the European Union.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["案件编号为B 3 219 888，当事方为Instagram LLC与Panathin…", "EUIPO异议部门作出裁决，支持Instagram对“-GRAM”后缀的权利主张", "文章标题暗示挑战者未能成功削弱Instagram对该后缀的商标控制力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO Opposition Division ruled that Instagram holds strong exclusive rights regarding the use of the '-GRAM' suffix in relevant markets.", "High brand fame effectively overcomes the descriptive nature of the suffix, successfully establishing a likelihood of confusion among consumers.", "Companies registering trademarks containing '-gram' must exercise caution to avoid infringing upon Instagram's established prior rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EUIPO对描述性后缀商标显著性的认定趋势", "监测该裁决对后续类似商标异议案件的参考作用", "观察品牌方对通用词汇组合保护的策略变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using the '-gram' suffix to reduce risks related to EU trademark registration and potential infringement claims.", "Practice note: Recognize that well-known brands benefit from an expanded scope of protection derived from their significant market reputation.", "What to monitor: Monitor EUIPO decisions involving descriptive suffixes used by major brands to understand evolving standards for confusion."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO Opposition Division ruled that Instagram holds strong exclusive rights regarding the use of the '-GRAM' suffix in relevant markets.
High brand fame effectively overcomes the descriptive nature of the suffix, successfully establishing a likelihood of confusion among consumers.
Companies registering trademarks containing '-gram' must exercise caution to avoid infringing upon Instagram's established prior rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using the '-gram' suffix to reduce risks related to EU trademark registration and potential infringement claims.
Practice note: Recognize that well-known brands benefit from an expanded scope of protection derived from their significant market reputation.
What to monitor: Monitor EUIPO decisions involving descriptive suffixes used by major brands to understand evolving standards for confusion.

items.title: Find my reputation
items.title_zh: 声誉能否转移：苹果商标异议案分析
items.title_en: UK IPO Review: Can Similar Elements Shift Brand Reputation in Major Disputes?
items.summary: In disputes involving major brands, can marks’ similar elements meaningfully shift consumer perception? Charlene Nelson finds out. O/1195/25, MEGA POP podjetje za oglasevanje na prostem d.o.o v Apple Inc, UK IPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析了英国知识产权局案件O/1195/25，即MEGA POP公司与苹果公司之间的商标争议。文章探讨了在涉及主要品牌的纠纷中，商标的相似元素是否能显著改变消费者的认知，以及声誉在其中的作用。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the UK Intellectual Property Office decision O/1195/25 in the trademark dispute between MEGA POP and Apple Inc. The article investigates whether similar elements within trademarks can meaningfully alter consumer perception, particularly when major brands are involved. Through a detailed case review, author Charlene Nelson dissects the legal standards and evidentiary requirements for establishing reputation transfer. The piece provides professional insights into the likelihood of confusion and the exploitation of reputation under UK trademark law, offering a clear perspective on how these complex legal concepts are applied in contemporary opposition proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 由于正文内容缺失，仅能确认该案涉及知名品牌商标争议及消费者认知评估。需关注UKIPO在此类案件中如何权衡商标近似性与品牌声誉对混淆可能性的影响，后续应追踪完整裁决细节以明确审查标准。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should carefully evaluate the risk of free-riding allegations when selecting brand identifiers. Avoiding visual or textual elements that closely resemble established global brands is crucial to mitigate infringement risks associated with reputation transfer theories. Rights holders must maintain robust evidence chains demonstrating their brand's independent development and distinct market presence. This preparation is essential for effectively responding to oppositions based on likelihood of confusion. Proactive trademark searches and thorough conflict assessments remain vital strategies for securing registration and defending against claims of unfair advantage or dilution in competitive markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["原文摘要显示案件编号为O/1195/25，当事方为MEGA POP公司与苹果公司", "原文摘要显示该争议由英国知识产权局（UKIPO）审理，核心议题为商标相似性对消费者认知…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Analyzes the key trademark dispute focal points in the UK IPO decision O/1195/25 involving MEGA POP and Apple Inc.", "Explores the legal standards determining if similar elements influence consumer perception in cases involving well-known brands.", "Assesses the applicability of reputation transfer theory in trademark oppositions and the allocation of burden of proof."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需关注UKIPO在涉及大品牌商标异议中对消费者认知转变的认定标准", "观察商标相似元素在何种程度上能借助或削弱原有品牌声誉", "等待完整裁决发布，以获取关于混淆可能性评估的具体事实依据"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using elements similar to major brands to prevent infringement risks linked to reputation transfer.", "Practice note: Strengthen evidence of independent brand identity to counter oppositions based on likelihood of confusion.", "What to monitor: Legal developments regarding reputation transfer standards and evidentiary burdens in UK trademark oppositions."]
core_points_en: Analyzes the key trademark dispute focal points in the UK IPO decision O/1195/25 involving MEGA POP and Apple Inc.
Explores the legal standards determining if similar elements influence consumer perception in cases involving well-known brands.
Assesses the applicability of reputation transfer theory in trademark oppositions and the allocation of burden of proof.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using elements similar to major brands to prevent infringement risks linked to reputation transfer.
Practice note: Strengthen evidence of independent brand identity to counter oppositions based on likelihood of confusion.
What to monitor: Legal developments regarding reputation transfer standards and evidentiary burdens in UK trademark oppositions.

items.title: Keeping your word
items.title_zh: 保持信誉：文字商标用于Logo仍视为真实使用
items.title_en: UKIPO: Using Registered Word Marks in Logos Can Constitute Genuine Use
items.summary: Using a registered word mark as part of a logo mark does not preclude a finding of genuine use – as long as the words remain the dominant element, writes Trecina Sookhoo. O/0080/26, Kilburn & Strode LLP v Easy Limo UK Ltd; O/0028/26, Kilburn & Strode LLP v EasyTerra Besloten Vennootschap; O/1148/25, Kilburn & Strode LLP v Easy Avenues Ltd, UK IPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析英国知识产权局（UKIPO）近期三起案件（O/0080/26等），探讨将注册文字商标作为图形标识一部分使用的法律效力。文章指出，只要文字元素在整体标识中保持主导地位，这种使用方式并不妨碍被认定为“真实使用”。该裁决澄清了商标使用形式的合规性标准，为权利人在品牌视觉升级中保留法律保护提供了依据，强调了维持商标显著特征的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent UK Intellectual Property Office decisions confirm that incorporating a registered word mark into a composite logo does not preclude a finding of genuine use. Provided the word element remains the dominant feature of the overall sign, such usage satisfies the requirement to maintain registration validity. This analysis covers three specific opposition cases involving Kilburn & Strode LLP and various respondents, clarifying the standards for acceptable trademark use forms under current UK practice.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英欧市场进行品牌视觉升级时，常将文字与图形结合。需注意保留文字的主导地位，避免因过度设计导致被视为新标识而丧失原有保护。建议定期审查商标使用证据，确保符合“真实使用”标准，以防因连续五年不使用而被撤销注册，保障品牌资产安全。
items.ai_insight_en: This development provides clarity for portfolio management regarding brand evolution. Companies updating their visual identity can integrate existing word marks into new logos without necessarily jeopardizing their registration status, provided the core distinctiveness is preserved. Legal teams should review evidence of use to ensure it aligns with these dominance criteria to defend against non-use cancellation actions effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["文字商标融入图形Logo若保持主导元素，可认定构成真实使用", "UKIPO在多起案件中确认非纯粹文字形式的使用符合维持注册要求", "权利人需确保修改后的标识未改变原注册商标的显著特征"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Incorporating a registered word mark into a logo design is permissible for genuine use purposes if the words remain the dominant element.", "The UK IPO confirmed this principle across three recent decisions, rejecting arguments that composite use invalidates the original word mark protection.", "Rights holders must ensure that visual modifications do not alter the distinctive character of the originally registered word mark."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["品牌视觉升级应保留文字主导地位以维持注册有效性", "定期审查使用证据以防因未真实使用导致商标被撤销"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands evolving visual identities can retain protection for word marks within composite logos if dominance is maintained.", "Practice note: Audit trademark use evidence to confirm that word elements remain visually dominant in current marketing materials.", "What to monitor: Future UK IPO or court rulings defining 'dominant element' thresholds in composite trademark disputes."]
core_points_en: Incorporating a registered word mark into a logo design is permissible for genuine use purposes if the words remain the dominant element.
The UK IPO confirmed this principle across three recent decisions, rejecting arguments that composite use invalidates the original word mark protection.
Rights holders must ensure that visual modifications do not alter the distinctive character of the originally registered word mark.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands evolving visual identities can retain protection for word marks within composite logos if dominance is maintained.
Practice note: Audit trademark use evidence to confirm that word elements remain visually dominant in current marketing materials.
What to monitor: Future UK IPO or court rulings defining 'dominant element' thresholds in composite trademark disputes.

items.title: The distinguishing mark of man
items.title_zh: 名人之名非版权保护对象：奥威尔遗产诉EUIPO案评析
items.title_en: Copyright Protects Works, Not Names: EUIPO Rejects Orwell Estate Claim
items.summary: No matter how famous an author is, copyright exists to protect their work, not their name, writes Matthew Harris. R 2248/2019-G, The Estate of the Late Sonia Brownell Orwell v EUIPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评析了EUIPO上诉委员会在R 2248/2019-G案中的决定，涉及已故作家乔治·奥威尔的遗产管理人Sonia Brownell Orwell。文章指出，尽管作者享有盛誉，但版权法的宗旨在于保护其创作的作品，而非其姓名或名声。该案件凸显了在欧盟商标注册中，试图通过版权主张来垄断知名人物姓名的法律局限性，强调了知识产权不同领域间的界限。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary by Matthew Harris for CITMA analyzes the EUIPO Board of Appeal decision in case R 2248/2019-G, involving The Estate of the Late Sonia Brownell Orwell. The core legal principle reaffirmed is that copyright law exists to protect an author's creative works, not their name or personal reputation, regardless of the author's fame. The analysis highlights the limitations of attempting to secure trademark protection for a famous name solely through copyright assertions, emphasizing the distinct boundaries between different intellectual property rights within the EU framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局品牌时，应明确版权与商标权的边界。切勿试图利用版权法垄断通用名称或名人姓名以获取商标独占权，此举在欧盟缺乏法律依据。建议企业建立清晰的IP组合策略，分别通过商标和版权保护不同的资产要素，避免因权利基础混淆导致注册失败或无效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and applicants operating in Europe, this decision underscores the importance of selecting the correct legal basis for IP protection. While copyright safeguards literary and artistic expressions, it does not extend to exclusive control over a person's name. Applicants seeking to register marks associated with famous figures must rely on appropriate trademark grounds rather than copyright arguments, ensuring compliance with established EU jurisprudence regarding the separation of IP regimes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["版权法旨在保护作品表达，不延伸至保护作者的姓名或公众声誉", "EUIPO驳回奥威尔遗产方基于版权主张注册其姓名的尝试", "知名人物姓名不能仅凭版权理由获得排他性商标保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright law protects the expression of creative works rather than the names or reputations of authors.", "The EUIPO rejected attempts by the Orwell Estate to register a famous name based on copyright claims.", "Intellectual property rights have distinct scopes, preventing copyright from monopolizing personal names."]

items.title: How to file an opposition at the European Patent Office (EPO) | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 如何在欧洲专利局提起专利异议
items.title_en: Procedural Requirements for Filing EPO Oppositions Under Article 100 EPC
items.summary: An opposition is a central way for third parties to challenge a granted European patent.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文详解在欧洲专利局（EPO）对已授权专利提起异议的程序。异议是第三方挑战专利有效性的核心途径，须在法定期限内提交书面或电子申请并缴费。异议理由严格限于《欧洲专利公约》第100条规定的缺乏可专利性、公开不充分或超范围等特定法律依据。文章强调需通过官方渠道如在线 filing 2.0 提交，且费用支付需即时完成，为从业者提供实操指引。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis outlines the procedural framework for filing an opposition against a granted European patent at the European Patent Office (EPO). It specifies that third parties must submit a signed notice of opposition either in writing to designated EPO offices or electronically via Online Filing 2.0. The official opposition fee must be paid simultaneously with the filing, typically through a debit order. Crucially, the challenge is restricted to specific legal grounds defined under Article 100 of the European Patent Convention, such as lack of patentability, insufficient disclosure, or added subject matter, preventing broad or arbitrary challenges to patent validity.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧获权后应建立监控机制，防范竞争对手利用异议程序拖延或无效专利。若作为异议方，需精准锁定EPC第100条 grounds，避免理由泛化被驳回。建议熟悉EPO电子提交流程，确保程序合规，以低成本高效解决专利争议，维护市场自由实施空间。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and competitors operating within the European jurisdiction, understanding these strict procedural and substantive limits is essential for effective portfolio management. Ensuring compliance with filing channels and payment timing prevents immediate rejection of oppositions. For patentees, recognizing that challenges are confined to Article 100 grounds allows for targeted defense strategies focused on technical and legal sufficiency rather than broader commercial disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["异议是挑战已授权欧洲专利的核心行政程序", "必须基于EPC第100条特定法律理由如缺乏创造性", "需通过指定渠道提交并同步缴纳官方异议费"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Oppositions must be filed in writing or electronically using EPO Online Filing 2.0 or the Contingency Upload Service.", "The official opposition fee is due immediately upon filing and is generally paid via a specific debit order mechanism.", "Valid opposition grounds are strictly limited to those set out in Article 100 EPC, including lack of patentability and added subject matter."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立专利监控机制防范对手发起异议挑战", "精准锁定EPC第100条理由确保异议程序合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders should anticipate challenges based strictly on Article 100 EPC grounds when managing post-grant risk.", "Practice note: Ensure opposition fees are paid via debit order at the exact time of electronic or written submission to avoid procedural defects.", "What to monitor: Monitor granted patents for potential opposition filings that adhere to the specific legal constraints of Article 100 EPC."]
core_points_en: Oppositions must be filed in writing or electronically using EPO Online Filing 2.0 or the Contingency Upload Service.
The official opposition fee is due immediately upon filing and is generally paid via a specific debit order mechanism.
Valid opposition grounds are strictly limited to those set out in Article 100 EPC, including lack of patentability and added subject matter.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders should anticipate challenges based strictly on Article 100 EPC grounds when managing post-grant risk.
Practice note: Ensure opposition fees are paid via debit order at the exact time of electronic or written submission to avoid procedural defects.
What to monitor: Monitor granted patents for potential opposition filings that adhere to the specific legal constraints of Article 100 EPC.

items.title: EPO member states convene for their 20th annual co-operation meeting
items.title_zh: EPO 成员国召开第 20 次年度合作会议，讨论欧洲创新生态与专利网络优先事项
items.title_en: EPO Member States Convene for 20th Annual Cooperation Meeting in Split
items.summary: ​Representatives of the national IP offices of the EPO member states met in Split for their 20th annual meeting on co-operation​. President Campinos also met with the Croatian Minister of Economy.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局成员国代表于 2026 年 5 月 11 日至 12 日在克罗地亚 Split 召开第 20 次年度合作会议。会议由克罗地亚国家知识产权局主办，超过 80 名与会者参加，包括 27 位国家局负责人及相关利益方，议题聚焦欧洲创新生态以及欧洲专利网络未来优先事项。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office held its 20th annual cooperation meeting with national patent offices in Split, Croatia, on 11-12 May 2026. Over 80 participants, including 27 heads of office, gathered to define shared priorities for the European Patent Network. Discussions centered on future innovation drivers, AI-enabled processes, shared data infrastructures, and improving patent system access for SMEs and startups. The event served as a milestone for the Scenarios for the Future 2025–2045 initiative, focusing on modernizing granting processes and developing new services. EPO President António Campinos emphasized strengthening Europe’s innovation ecosystem through continued collaboration and open exchange among network members.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该会议反映 EPO 正继续把成员国协作、专利网络能力和创新生态政策连接起来。对企业而言，后续值得关注 EPO 与各国家局在审查质量、数字化服务、专利信息和中小企业支持方面是否形成统一行动。
items.ai_insight_en: This meeting signals a coordinated shift toward AI-integrated examination and unified digital infrastructure across the European Patent Network. For rights holders, this suggests upcoming changes in search tools and procedural efficiency. Stakeholders should monitor how these strategic priorities translate into concrete service updates and standardized practices among member states, particularly regarding data accessibility and startup engagement initiatives.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EPO 年度成员国合作会议在克罗地亚 Split 举行。", "会议讨论欧洲创新生态和欧洲专利网络未来优先事项。", "参会者包括 27 位国家局负责人和关键利益方。"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EPO and national offices met in Split to define strategic priorities for the European Patent Network.", "Key discussion themes included AI impact, process modernization, and enhanced support for SMEs.", "The meeting advanced the Scenarios for the Future 2025–2045 initiative to prepare the network."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EPO 合作会议可能影响国家局协作和服务一致性。", "欧洲专利网络的优先事项关系到申请与检索体验。", "企业可关注后续关于质量、数字工具和创新支持的落地措施。"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strategic alignment on AI and data infrastructure may improve search quality and predictability for applicants operating across multiple European jurisdictions.", "Practice note: The focus on SME access indicates potential new outreach programs or simplified procedures that could benefit early-stage innovators and first-time filers.", "What to monitor: Watch for subsequent EPN announcements detailing specific AI tools, timeline for process modernization, and new services supporting the wider innovation ecosystem."]
core_points_en: EPO and national offices met in Split to define strategic priorities for the European Patent Network.
Key discussion themes included AI impact, process modernization, and enhanced support for SMEs.
The meeting advanced the Scenarios for the Future 2025–2045 initiative to prepare the network.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strategic alignment on AI and data infrastructure may improve search quality and predictability for applicants operating across multiple European jurisdictions.
Practice note: The focus on SME access indicates potential new outreach programs or simplified procedures that could benefit early-stage innovators and first-time filers.
What to monitor: Watch for subsequent EPN announcements detailing specific AI tools, timeline for process modernization, and new services supporting the wider innovation ecosystem.

items.title: Consolidation en UDRP : un levier stratégique pour rationaliser les actions contre le cybersquatting
items.title_zh: UDRP合并审理：应对域名抢注的战略杠杆
items.title_en: UDRP Consolidation: A Strategic Lever to Rationalize Actions Against Cybersquatting
items.summary: La consolidation en UDRP permet aux titulaires de marques de regrouper plusieurs noms de domaine litigieux dans une seule procédure, réduisant ainsi les coûts et optimisant l'efficacité des actions contre le cybersquatting.
items.ai_summary_zh: Novagraaf集团分析师指出，在数字环境中，UDRP程序允许将多个争议域名合并于单一投诉中处理。这种合并机制能显著降低针对域名抢注的法律行动成本并提升效率。文章以欧莱雅集团涉及700多个域名的案件为例，说明即使注册人不同，若存在共同控制且符合公平原则，WIPO等机构仍可能批准合并审理。
items.ai_summary_en: Novagraaf analysis highlights UDRP consolidation as a key mechanism for rights holders facing widespread online trademark infringement. By grouping multiple disputed domain names into a single complaint, parties can significantly reduce operational and financial burdens compared to filing separate proceedings. The article cites a recent L'Oréal case involving over 700 domains, where the panel accepted consolidation despite apparent differences in registrants. This approach is expressly permitted under UDRP Rule 10(e) and applied by major centers like WIPO. However, consolidation is not universal, as certain national extensions operate under specific procedures that may not allow such grouping, requiring careful jurisdictional assessment before filing.
items.ai_insight_zh: 对于面临大规模域名抢注的欧洲品牌持有人，UDRP合并审理是重要的程序性工具。实务中需重点准备“共同控制”的证据链，而非仅依赖注册信息一致性。尽管UDRP具有国际性，但欧洲企业常通过WIPO等中心发起此类行动，需关注不同国家顶级域名对合并程序的特定限制。
items.ai_insight_en: For European and US rights holders managing large-scale cybersquatting, UDRP consolidation offers procedural efficiency. Success depends on demonstrating common control among seemingly distinct registrants. Practitioners must verify whether target ccTLDs adhere to UDRP standards, as local policies may preclude consolidated actions, necessitating tailored enforcement strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UDRP允许将多个争议域名合并在单一程序中，以降低维权成本并优化效率", "合并审理需满足两个核心标准：域名之间存在共同控制，且合并符合公平与效率原则", "欧莱雅案例显示，即便域名注册人名义不同，只要证明存在共同控制，面板仍可批准合并"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UDRP consolidation allows grouping multiple disputed domains in one complaint to optimize efficiency and reduce costs.", "Recent L'Oréal case demonstrates panels may accept consolidation across different registrants if common control is established.", "Consolidation is permitted under UDRP Rule 10(e) but remains unavailable for certain national domain extensions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["品牌方在处理批量域名争议时，应评估是否符合UDRP合并审理的“共同控制”要件", "需注意部分国家代码顶级域名（ccTLD）可能不适用UDRP合并机制，需单独核查当地政策", "观察WIPO及主要仲裁中心在认定“表面不同注册人”背后的实际控制关系时的裁量趋势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Consolidation reduces resource allocation for brand protection campaigns targeting high volumes of infringing domains.", "Practice note: Evidence must establish common control behind nominally different registrants to satisfy UDRP Rule 10(e) requirements.", "What to monitor: Variations in national domain dispute policies that may restrict or prohibit consolidated complaints for specific ccTLDs."]
core_points_en: UDRP consolidation allows grouping multiple disputed domains in one complaint to optimize efficiency and reduce costs.
Recent L'Oréal case demonstrates panels may accept consolidation across different registrants if common control is established.
Consolidation is permitted under UDRP Rule 10(e) but remains unavailable for certain national domain extensions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Consolidation reduces resource allocation for brand protection campaigns targeting high volumes of infringing domains.
Practice note: Evidence must establish common control behind nominally different registrants to satisfy UDRP Rule 10(e) requirements.
What to monitor: Variations in national domain dispute policies that may restrict or prohibit consolidated complaints for specific ccTLDs.

items.title: Have you reviewed your IP agreements following Brexit?
items.title_zh: 英国脱欧后是否已审查您的知识产权协议？
items.title_en: Have you reviewed your IP agreements following Brexit?
items.summary: It has now been over five years since the UK's departure from the European Union (Brexit) and the end of the transition period. If you...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国脱欧过渡期结束已逾五年，律所建议企业审查既存知识产权协议。重点在于评估“欧盟”地域条款是否仍包含英国，以及人员流动、法律分歧等脱欧后续影响。若协议未明确界定，可能引发执行不确定性，建议通过修订明确地域范围或增加未来成员国变动条款。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文提示跨境IP许可中的地域定义风险。实务中需关注“欧盟”定义在脱欧后的法律效力差异，以及宏观环境变化对合同履行的潜在冲击。重点观察司法机关对模糊地域条款的解释倾向，以及企业如何通过合同修订机制应对欧盟成员构成的动态变化。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["既存协议中“欧盟”地域定义可能不再自动包含英国，导致权利范围存在解释不确定性", "法院对地域范围的解读取决于整体起草及缔约意图，可能引发执法挑战", "脱欧带来的人员流动限制及法律监管分歧可能影响现有合同条款的商业适用性"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["跨境协议中“欧盟”地域定义的法律效力在脱欧后存在解释风险，需明确是否包含英国", "脱欧引发的监管 divergence（如数据保护、竞争法）可能削弱原有合同条款的商业合…", "建议观察法院如何处理未明确区分成员国的地域条款，以评估潜在的执法不确定性"]

items.title: Patentability In AI – Navigating The Legal Landscape
items.title_zh: AI专利适格性：解析法律景观与技术特征要求
items.title_en: AI Patentability in Europe: Navigating Technical Character and Effect Requirements
items.summary: AI and machine learning are reshaping industries, opening new opportunities for innovation. But can your AI invention be patented?
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出AI发明若具备技术特征和技术效果即可获专利保护。欧洲专利局（EPO）等机构重点审查AI是否应用于特定技术目的并产生可衡量的技术优势。自动驾驶和图像处理等案例表明，正确的技术框架界定有助于获得保护。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the patent eligibility of artificial intelligence inventions within the European jurisdiction. It establishes that AI innovations can secure patent protection if they satisfy two fundamental criteria: possessing technical character and producing a technical effect. Both the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office evaluate whether the AI application serves a specific technical purpose and delivers measurable technical advantages over existing solutions. The analysis highlights practical examples, such as autonomous vehicle control systems and advanced image processing technologies. These cases demonstrate how proper technical framing is essential for transforming innovative concepts into legally protected assets. By focusing on concrete technical contributions rather than abstract algorithms, applicants can effectively navigate the complex legal landscape surrounding AI patentability in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需关注EPO对“技术贡献”的具体认定标准。申请人应着重描述AI算法如何解决具体技术问题及产生的技术效果，避免仅主张抽象算法或商业方法，以提升在欧洲法域下的授权前景。
items.ai_insight_en: Applicants should avoid focusing exclusively on algorithmic logic when seeking European patent protection. Instead, drafting strategies must emphasize the specific technical application of AI within defined fields, such as control systems or data processing. It is critical to clearly articulate the underlying technical problem and the corresponding technical solution to meet the EPO’s rigorous requirements for technical contribution. This approach enhances the likelihood of grant by demonstrating a tangible improvement in technical performance, thereby mitigating the risk of rejection based on exclusions for non-technical subject matter or abstract ideas.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI发明需克服技术特征与技术效果两大 hurdles 方可获得专利保护", "EPO重点审查AI是否应用于特定技术目的并交付可衡量的技术优势", "自动驾驶控制与先进图像处理等案例显示技术框架对确权的关键作用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["AI inventions require both technical character and a demonstrable technical effect to qualify for patent protection under current standards.", "The EPO strictly assesses whether AI applications serve specific technical purposes and yield actual, measurable technical advantages.", "Properly constructing a technical framework helps convert AI innovations, like autonomous driving, into enforceable intellectual property assets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EPO审查核心在于AI应用是否具备具体技术目的及可衡量技术优势", "专利申请文件需精准构建技术框架，将AI创新转化为受保护资产", "需持续观察EPO在自动驾驶及图像处理等领域的最新审查实践动向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Focus claims on specific technical applications and improvements rather than pure algorithmic logic to ensure eligibility.", "Practice note: Clearly define the technical problem and solution in specifications to satisfy the EPO's strict technical contribution standards.", "What to monitor: Evolving case law regarding the boundary between abstract AI methods and patentable technical implementations in Europe."]
core_points_en: AI inventions require both technical character and a demonstrable technical effect to qualify for patent protection under current standards.
The EPO strictly assesses whether AI applications serve specific technical purposes and yield actual, measurable technical advantages.
Properly constructing a technical framework helps convert AI innovations, like autonomous driving, into enforceable intellectual property assets.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Focus claims on specific technical applications and improvements rather than pure algorithmic logic to ensure eligibility.
Practice note: Clearly define the technical problem and solution in specifications to satisfy the EPO's strict technical contribution standards.
What to monitor: Evolving case law regarding the boundary between abstract AI methods and patentable technical implementations in Europe.

items.title: First-Ever CJEU Case On Generative AI And Copyright
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院首审生成式AI版权案
items.title_en: CJEU to Rule on Generative AI Copyright: Google Gemini Case Sets Precedent for EU Law
items.summary: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) will, for the first time, assess how EU copyright law applies to large language models.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院将首次在Like Company诉Google Ireland案中评估欧盟版权法如何适用于大型语言模型。谷歌Gemini被指未经许可复制新闻内容，引发关于LLM训练、AI输出责任及文本数据挖掘例外等关键问题。预计裁决将于2027年作出，结果将对欧洲AI合规、出版商权利及版权风险产生深远影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) will, for the first time, assess how EU copyright law applies to large language models. In Like Company v. Google Ireland (C-250/25), Google’s Gemini is accused of reproducing news content without permission, raising key questions about LLM training, AI output liability, and text/data mining exceptions. Although a ruling isn’t expected until 2027, the outcome is likely to have a lasting impact on AI compliance, publisher rights, and copyright risk in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案为欧盟法院首次介入生成式AI版权争议，具有里程碑意义。鉴于裁决预期需至2027年，期间法律不确定性将持续存在。需密切关注法院对文本数据挖掘例外及AI训练数据合法性的解释，这将重塑欧洲AI产业的版权合规基准。
items.ai_insight_en: This case will redefine the compliance baseline for AI training data in Europe. Market participants should immediately review data acquisition sources to ensure alignment with text and data mining exceptions or secure explicit authorization. Establishing robust copyright due diligence mechanisms is critical to mitigate potential high-value infringement liabilities. Stakeholders must closely monitor developments leading to the 2027 judgment to adjust global AI strategies and manage evolving copyright risks effectively across jurisdictions.

items.title: AMI fails to secure ban on Martin-Baker’s ejection seats for fighter jets
items.title_zh: AMI未能获禁马丁-贝克战机弹射座椅
items.title_en: AMI Denied Injunction Against Martin-Baker Ejection Seats in UK High Court
items.summary: With increasing armed conflict around the world and rising military spending, military technology is once again coming into the public spotlight. For example, two of the world’s largest manufacturers of ejection seats are currently battling it out at the UK High Court. These modern ejection seats are used in fighter jets to save pilots’ lives […] The post AMI fails to secure ban on Martin-Baker’s ejection seats for fighter jets appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，全球两大弹射座椅制造商AMI与马丁-贝克在英国高等法院进行诉讼。AMI试图禁止马丁-贝克销售用于战斗机的现代弹射座椅，但未能成功获得禁令。背景涉及全球武装冲突增加及军费开支上升。
items.ai_summary_en: Amidst rising global armed conflicts and increased military spending, military technology has returned to the public spotlight. Two of the world’s largest ejection seat manufacturers are currently engaged in litigation at the UK High Court. These advanced systems are critical for pilot safety in fighter jets. One manufacturer, AMI, sought a legal injunction to prohibit the use of competitor Martin-Baker’s ejection seats in fighter aircraft, aiming to protect its market position through intellectual property enforcement. However, the court denied the request, meaning AMI failed to secure the ban. This case underscores the intense commercial competition within the high-stakes military technology sector and highlights the pivotal role of patent law. It also reflects the stance of the UK judiciary when adjudicating high-value industrial patent disputes involving essential safety components.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认诉讼存在及禁令申请失败的结果。需关注后续判决是否涉及专利有效性或侵权认定的具体理由，以及该案件对军用技术专利执行的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the UK High Court's discretionary standards for granting patent injunctions, particularly concerning critical safety components. The judicial approach suggests a preference for maintaining supply chain stability over strictly enforcing patent rights in such sensitive contexts. Rights holders and defendants alike should prioritize robust Freedom to Operate (FTO) analyses before entering new markets. In the event of overseas litigation, leveraging arguments related to public interest and supply chain continuity may serve as effective defenses against injunctive relief. Understanding this balance between IP protection and broader economic or security implications is vital for strategic risk management in high-tech industries.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AMI与马丁-贝克就弹射座椅技术在英国高等法院发生法律争端", "标题及摘要明确显示，AMI未能成功获得针对马丁-贝克产品的禁令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK High Court dismissed AMI's application for an injunction, failing to halt the competitor's product sales.", "The dispute centers on patent infringement claims and fierce market competition regarding military fighter jet ejection seats.", "Rising global defense budgets have intensified intellectual property battles within the high-end military technology sector."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需监测英国高等法院对该案实体争议的后续裁决细节", "观察军用技术领域专利禁令救济的司法审查标准变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Courts may prioritize supply chain stability over patent exclusivity for critical safety components.", "Practice note: Strengthen pre-export FTO analysis and consider public interest arguments in litigation defenses.", "What to monitor: The UK judiciary's cautious approach to injunctions and its consideration of broader public interests."]
core_points_en: The UK High Court dismissed AMI's application for an injunction, failing to halt the competitor's product sales.
The dispute centers on patent infringement claims and fierce market competition regarding military fighter jet ejection seats.
Rising global defense budgets have intensified intellectual property battles within the high-end military technology sector.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Courts may prioritize supply chain stability over patent exclusivity for critical safety components.
Practice note: Strengthen pre-export FTO analysis and consider public interest arguments in litigation defenses.
What to monitor: The UK judiciary's cautious approach to injunctions and its consideration of broader public interests.
