items.title: Battery recycling moves centre stage: what the surge in circularity innovation means for industry and IP strategy
items.title_zh: 电池回收成焦点：循环创新激增对产业与IP战略的影响
items.title_en: Battery Recycling Innovation Surges: Implications for Industry and IP Strategy
items.summary: The global battery sector is entering a new phase. As electric vehicle and energy‑storage deployment accelerates, attention is rapidly shifting from how batteries are made to what happens when they reach end-of-life. According to the latest analysis from the European Patent Office and the International Energy Agency, inventions relating to battery reuse and recycling have increased more than seven‑fold over the last decade – making battery circularity one of the most dynamic areas of battery inn
items.ai_summary_zh: 全球电池行业正转向关注电池寿命终结后的处理。据欧洲专利局与国际能源署最新分析，过去十年间电池复用与回收相关发明增长超七倍，使电池循环经济成为当前最具活力的创新领域之一。这不仅关乎可持续性，更涉及材料安全、工业竞争力及知识产权战略布局。
items.ai_summary_en: The global battery sector is shifting focus from manufacturing to end-of-life management as electric vehicle and energy-storage deployment accelerates. Recent analysis by the European Patent Office and the International Energy Agency reveals that inventions related to battery reuse and recycling have increased more than seven-fold over the last decade. This surge establishes battery circularity as a highly dynamic area of innovation. The trend extends beyond sustainability, impacting materials security, industrial competitiveness, and intellectual property strategy. Rights holders must recognize this transition as a critical phase for strategic IP positioning in the evolving battery lifecycle market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This data indicates a strategic pivot toward lifecycle management, necessitating a review of IP portfolios. Companies should assess protection gaps in recycling technologies to secure competitive advantage. Monitoring EPO filing trends in circularity will help identify emerging white spaces and competitive threats in materials recovery.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲专利局数据显示，过去十年电池复用与回收相关发明增长超七倍", "电池循环经济已成为当前电池创新中最具活力的领域之一", "该趋势不仅涉及可持续性，更紧密关联材料安全、工业竞争力及IP战略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EPO and IEA data show a seven-fold increase in battery reuse and recycling inventions over the last decade.", "Battery circularity has become one of the most dynamic areas of current battery technology innovation.", "The shift impacts materials security and industrial competitiveness, requiring updated intellectual property strategies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["电池回收领域专利活动激增，显示技术创新重心向生命周期后端转移", "IP战略需结合材料安全与工业竞争力，超越单纯的环境可持续性叙事", "需持续监测EPO关于电池循环利用技术的专利分类与申请趋势变化"]

Battery circularity has become one of the most dynamic areas of current battery technology innovation.
The shift impacts materials security and industrial competitiveness, requiring updated intellectual property strategies.

What to monitor: Track EPO classification trends and filing volumes in battery circularity to identify emerging technological leaders and potential licensing opportunities.

items.title: New Court, new approaches: How the UPC is reshaping pharmaceutical patent litigation
items.title_zh: 新法院新路径：UPC如何重塑医药专利诉讼
items.title_en: UPC Case Law Reshaping Pharmaceutical Patent Litigation Standards
items.summary: UPC
items.ai_summary_zh: 自2023年6月启动以来，统一专利法院已发布逾50项医药领域裁决，确立了临时禁令、证据保全及禁令范围的程序标准。上诉法院在Boehringer诉Zentiva案中明确了“迫在眉睫”侵权的三要素标准，指出完成卫生技术评估及定价报销程序可能构成迫在眉睫的侵权，具体需结合各国监管背景判断。
items.ai_summary_en: Since its June 2023 launch, the Unified Patent Court has issued over fifty pharmaceutical rulings, establishing procedural standards for preliminary injunctions and evidence preservation. Recent Court of Appeal decisions address substantive issues like inventive step and second medical use claims. The Court clarified that activities under the Bolar exemption and CE marking processes do not constitute infringement. These rulings define imminent infringement thresholds, shaping the framework for future European pharmaceutical litigation involving originators, generics, and biosimilar manufacturers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The UPC is rapidly establishing a distinct procedural and substantive framework for pharmaceutical disputes. Rights holders must navigate new standards for proving imminent infringement to secure preliminary relief. Understanding the boundaries of non-infringing preparatory acts is critical for effective portfolio enforcement and risk assessment across participating member states.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC已发布超50项医药裁决，确立临时禁令与证据保全等程序标准", "博林格诉赞蒂瓦案确立迫在眉睫侵权三要素：准备就绪、仅待行动、阶段已设", "完成卫生技术评估及定价报销程序，在特定监管背景下可构成迫在眉睫侵权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC issued over fifty pharmaceutical rulings setting procedural standards for injunctions.", "Bolar exemption activities and CE marking are confirmed as non-infringing acts.", "Case law defines imminent infringement thresholds for preliminary injunction grants."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC判例为欧洲医药专利诉讼建立了新的程序与实体审查框架", "临时禁令中“迫在眉睫”侵权的认定高度依赖各国监管程序进展", "需持续观察上诉法院对Bolar豁免范围及CE标志非侵权性质的后续解释"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: UPC precedents now dictate strategic timing for enforcing patents against generic market entry.", "Practice note: Courts distinguish between exempt regulatory steps and actionable imminent commercial infringement.", "What to monitor: Evolving Court of Appeal interpretations of urgency and market conditions in PI requests."]

Bolar exemption activities and CE marking are confirmed as non-infringing acts.
Case law defines imminent infringement thresholds for preliminary injunction grants.

What to monitor: Evolving Court of Appeal interpretations of urgency and market conditions in PI requests.

items.summary: Patent owners have also been asked to endorse a suspension of royalty reductions for departing licensors, IAM has learned
items.ai_summary_zh: 据IAM Media独家报道，VCL HEVC/VVC专利池的许可方被要求投票，提议将专利池管理员变更为Sisvel。同时，许可方还需批准一项暂停措施，即停止对退出许可方的特许权使用费减免。这一动态反映了视频编码标准专利池管理权的潜在更迭，可能影响现有许可架构及费率结构，是SEP许可市场的重要风向标。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注Sisvel接管后的许可政策变化，特别是费率调整及合规要求。建议提前评估现有HEVC/VVC产品线的许可风险，加强与新管理方的沟通，确保在专利池转换期间的许可连续性，避免因条款变动引发潜在的侵权诉讼或高额赔偿风险。

items.title: Of a Dame and the King (of Beer): when an indefinite trade mark licence can end | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 无限期商标许可非永久：英国法院裁定可凭合理通知终止
items.title_en: UK Courts Rule Indefinite Trademark Licences Are Terminable on Reasonable Notice
items.summary: Of a Dame and the King (of Beer) – An indefinite trade mark licence doesn’t necessarily last forever and the termination of a long-running commercial arrangement doesn’t necessarily require a long period of notice to be “reasonable”.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Zaha Hadid案中裁定，“无限期”商标许可不等于“永久”，允许被许可方在给予合理通知后终止合同。随后枢密院在Budweiser案中进一步指出，合理通知期的长短取决于商业背景而非仅看合作时长。该系列判决澄清了长期商业安排中默示终止权的存在，强调合同解释需符合商业逻辑，打破了无限期许可即不可解除的传统认知。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent decisions by the UK Court of Appeal and the Privy Council clarify that an 'indefinite' trademark licence is not synonymous with a perpetual one. In the Zaha Hadid case, the Court of Appeal held that such licences carry an implied right to terminate upon reasonable notice, even if the contract expressly grants termination rights to only one party. The Privy Council further guided that determining what constitutes 'reasonable' notice depends heavily on the specific commercial context rather than merely the duration of the relationship. These rulings establish that long-running commercial arrangements involving IP rights are not locked in indefinitely without clear contractual language to the contrary.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧签署长期或无限期商标许可时，切勿默认其不可撤销。建议在合同中明确约定终止条款、通知期限及违约责任，以规避法律不确定性。若面临纠纷，应重点举证商业背景的合理性，利用判例主张默示终止权，避免陷入被动锁定的商业困境。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and licensees, these judgments underscore the importance of precise drafting in long-term IP agreements. Relying on terms like 'indefinite' creates significant uncertainty regarding exit mechanisms. Parties should explicitly define termination rights, notice periods, and post-termination obligations to avoid disputes over implied contractual terms. This approach ensures clarity for portfolio management and reduces litigation risk when commercial relationships evolve or dissolve.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["无限期商标许可不等同于永久许可，隐含双方无意永久绑定", "被许可方可依据默示权利，通过给予合理通知终止许可协议", "合理通知期的判定核心在于具体商业背景，而非单纯的合作年限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["An indefinite trademark licence is legally distinct from a perpetual licence and implies a right to terminate on reasonable notice.", "The determination of reasonable notice periods relies on commercial context and business realities, not just the length of the partnership.", "Express termination clauses favoring one party do not necessarily preclude the other party from exercising an implied right to end the agreement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["签约时需明确终止机制，避免依赖模糊的无限期条款", "争议中应侧重论证商业背景以确立合理通知期的合法性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies relying on indefinite IP licences must recognize they can be terminated by either party with reasonable notice, impacting long-term brand strategy.", "Practice note: Drafters should replace ambiguous 'indefinite' terms with explicit perpetual language or clearly defined termination procedures to ensure certainty.", "What to monitor: Future cases applying the 'commercial context' test to determine reasonable notice periods in diverse industry sectors and licensing models."]
core_points_en: An indefinite trademark licence is legally distinct from a perpetual licence and implies a right to terminate on reasonable notice.
The determination of reasonable notice periods relies on commercial context and business realities, not just the length of the partnership.
Express termination clauses favoring one party do not necessarily preclude the other party from exercising an implied right to end the agreement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies relying on indefinite IP licences must recognize they can be terminated by either party with reasonable notice, impacting long-term brand strategy.
Practice note: Drafters should replace ambiguous 'indefinite' terms with explicit perpetual language or clearly defined termination procedures to ensure certainty.
What to monitor: Future cases applying the 'commercial context' test to determine reasonable notice periods in diverse industry sectors and licensing models.

items.title: Inside INTA London and the IP ‘carnival’
items.title_zh: INTA伦敦年会回顾：全球IP界的狂欢盛会
items.title_en: INTA Annual Meeting Returns to London: Highlights from the Global IP Community
items.summary: Against a backdrop of rooftop receptions, hot-topic debates, hectic networking, and cross-city navigation, WIPR brings you highlights and key talking points from the world’s largest IP conference.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权评论（WIPR）回顾了在国际商标协会（INTA）于伦敦举办的年度会议。这是该会议时隔35年重返英国首都，吸引了来自180个国家的逾万名代表参加。尽管面临交通拥堵等挑战，与会者仍积极参与屋顶招待会及社交活动。文章强调了此次会议作为全球最大IP盛会的地位，突出了伦敦作为举办地的吸引力以及业界在商标领域的高热度讨论和网络交流机会。
items.ai_summary_en: The International Trademark Association (INTA) held its annual meeting in London for the first time in over 35 years, drawing more than 10,000 delegates from 180 countries. The five-day event featured intense networking, rooftop receptions, and debates on current trademark issues. Despite logistical challenges including transport disruptions on the Elizabeth line and DLR, attendees engaged actively with the city's historic venues. The conference reinforced London's status as a key hub for global intellectual property professionals, combining high-level industry discourse with significant social interaction across Soho, Mayfair, and the City.

items.ai_insight_en: For European and US rights holders, this gathering serves as a critical barometer for global brand protection trends and enforcement priorities. The convergence of such a large international cohort facilitates direct exchange on cross-border compliance and emerging regulatory pressures. While specific policy outcomes are not detailed here, the scale of participation underscores the importance of maintaining active engagement with major international forums to stay aligned with evolving global IP standards and peer practices.

items.title: Droit d’auteur et IA : vers une présomption de culpabilité ?
items.title_zh: 版权与AI：法国拟确立大模型侵权推定
items.title_en: France AI Copyright Proposal: Shifting Burden of Proof to LLM Providers
items.summary: Le débat sur l'encadrement juridique de l'intelligence artificielle en France franchit une nouvelle étape avec une proposition sénatoriale visant à instaurer une présomption d'exploitation des œuvres protégées par les fournisseurs de modèles de langage.
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国参议员提议建立法律推定机制，若存在迹象表明大语言模型使用了受保护作品，即推定其构成利用。此举将举证责任倒置给AI供应商，需由其证明未使用或合规使用。法国国务委员会认为该措施原则上合宪且符合欧盟法，但需调整。文章指出该机制可能与欧盟《数字单一市场版权指令》中的数据挖掘例外产生冲突，且“可信迹象”定义模糊，存在被宪法委员会审查的风险。
items.ai_summary_en: A French senatorial proposal seeks to establish a presumption that Large Language Model providers exploit protected works, marking a potential shift in copyright regulation for AI. The Conseil d'État recently validated the legislature's competence to enact this measure, deeming it constitutionally and EU-compliant subject to specific adjustments. The core mechanism reverses the burden of proof: if indicators suggest AI usage of protected content, providers must prove they did not use such data or complied with applicable rules. This rebuttable presumption aims to address information asymmetry between rights holders and tech firms, who control training data. However, the proposal raises compatibility concerns with the EU DSM Directive’s text and data mining exceptions, creating legal uncertainty regarding enforcement and definitions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该提案旨在解决权利人与AI开发者间的信息不对称，但显著改变了传统无罪推定原则。实务中需密切关注“可信迹象”的具体界定标准及其与欧盟TDM例外条款的协调情况。若通过，AI供应商将面临更高的合规举证负担，可能影响其在法国的数据训练策略及法律风险管理架构。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying generative AI models in Europe should anticipate stricter procedural hurdles in France. The proposed reversal of the burden of proof necessitates robust internal compliance frameworks. Companies must implement comprehensive data lineage tracking systems to document training data sources and secure explicit licensing agreements where required. Reliance on broad statutory exceptions may become insufficient if local presumptions of infringement are adopted. Stakeholders should closely monitor the final legislative text and parallel developments at the EU level regarding the Digital Single Market Directive. Proactive preparation is essential to mitigate litigation risks arising from potential conflicts between national procedural innovations and harmonized European copyright standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国拟立法规定：若有迹象表明AI使用受保护作品，即推定侵权，由供应商承担反证责任", "法国国务委员会确认立法者有权采取此措施，认为其目前不违反宪法或欧盟法律，但需调整", "该推定机制可能削弱欧盟版权指令下的文本与数据挖掘例外，且因标准模糊面临合宪性风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["French senators propose a rebuttable presumption that LLM providers exploit protected works, reversing the standard burden of proof in copyright disputes.", "The Conseil d'État confirmed legislative competence, finding the measure compliant with constitutional and EU law principles, pending necessary technical adjustments.", "The initiative targets information asymmetry but risks conflicting with the EU DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions framework."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["举证责任倒置将迫使AI供应商建立更透明的数据溯源与合规记录体系以应对潜在诉讼", "需观察法国立法如何界定触发推定的“可信迹象”，以避免因定义模糊而被宪法法院否决", "关注该国内法提案与欧盟《数字单一市场版权指令》中TDM例外条款的实际兼容性与冲突解决"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish rigorous data provenance systems and retain valid licensing evidence to counter potential presumptions of unauthorized use.", "Practice note: Assess current training datasets against emerging French procedural standards to ensure defensibility under reversed burden of proof regimes.", "What to monitor: Track the interplay between French national proposals and EU-level interpretations of the DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions."]
core_points_en: French senators propose a rebuttable presumption that LLM providers exploit protected works, reversing the standard burden of proof in copyright disputes.
The Conseil d'État confirmed legislative competence, finding the measure compliant with constitutional and EU law principles, pending necessary technical adjustments.
The initiative targets information asymmetry but risks conflicting with the EU DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions framework.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish rigorous data provenance systems and retain valid licensing evidence to counter potential presumptions of unauthorized use.
Practice note: Assess current training datasets against emerging French procedural standards to ensure defensibility under reversed burden of proof regimes.
What to monitor: Track the interplay between French national proposals and EU-level interpretations of the DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions.

items.title: G 1/25 “Hydroponics”: Enlarged Board hears challenge to EPO description amendment practice
items.title_zh: G 1/25案：扩大上诉委员会审理挑战欧专局说明书修改实践
items.title_en: G 1/25 Hydroponics: Enlarged Board Hears Challenge to EPO Description Amendment Practice
items.summary: Users of the European patent system will be familiar with the EPO’s long-standing practice of requiring the description to be brought into line with amended claims, both before grant and in post-grant proceedings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局扩大上诉委员会于5月8日就G 1/25“Hydroponics”案举行口头审理。该案核心争议在于欧专局长期要求将说明书与修改后的权利要求保持一致的做法是否具有法律依据，特别是2021年新政要求删除或显著标记未覆盖实施例引发的法律与实务争议。技术上诉委员会因判例分歧将此问题提交扩大上诉委员会，旨在澄清欧洲专利公约下说明书适应性的法律基础及具体要求。
items.ai_summary_en: On May 8, the EPO’s Enlarged Board of Appeal held oral proceedings in case G 1/25. The central issue is whether the European Patent Convention mandates that applicants adapt the description to align with amended claims. This referral stems from diverging case law among Technical Boards of Appeal and the EPO’s 2021 policy strengthening requirements to delete or prominently mark embodiments no longer covered by claims. In the underlying case T 697/22, the patentee amended claims during opposition appeal. Although these claims met requirements for added matter, sufficiency, novelty, and inventive step, the Board found inconsistencies with the description. Since further procedural amendment was impossible, the Board questioned the legal basis under Article 84 EPC. This referral aims to clarify the long-standing controversy regarding the legal foundation and operational uncertainty of this administrative practice.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案结果将决定欧专局说明书修改实践的合法性，直接影响专利申请与维护成本及策略。若新政被认定缺乏法律依据，申请人可避免繁琐的说明书清理工作；反之，则需严格合规。实务中应密切关注扩大上诉委员会的最终决定，以调整说明书撰写及答复策略，规避因不一致导致的程序风险或新增事项问题。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders should closely monitor the outcome of G 1/25, as it will define the compliance burden for European patent applications and opposition proceedings. If the current strict practice is upheld, rights holders must allocate more resources to precisely amend descriptions, ensuring non-covered embodiments are deleted or prominently marked to avoid procedural risks. It is advisable to optimize internal drafting standards and strategically plan embodiment layouts early in the prosecution process. These measures can help reduce future compliance costs and minimize uncertainties related to patent validation and enforcement across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["扩大上诉委员会审理G 1/25案，质疑欧专局要求说明书与修改后权利要求一致的法律依据", "年欧专局新政要求删除或显著标记未被权利要求覆盖的实施例，引发广泛争议", "技术上诉委员会因判例分歧提交三个问题，寻求澄清说明书适应性是否阻碍专利维持"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Enlarged Board examines if the EPC strictly requires adapting the description after claim amendments are made.", "Controversy arises from the 2021 policy shift and conflicting legal foundations cited by different Technical Boards of Appeal.", "The focus is on determining if specific EPC provisions provide a clear legal basis for this established administrative practice."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["扩大上诉委员会的决定将明确说明书修改义务的法律边界，影响全欧专利实务操作标准", "若维持2021年新政，申请人需投入更多资源处理说明书一致性，增加申请与维护负担", "后续需关注决定对判例统一性的影响，以及是否消除不同审查部门间的实践差异"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess the potential impact on description amendment costs in European patent applications and opposition proceedings.", "Practice note: Optimize internal drafting standards and plan embodiment layouts early to lower compliance costs and validation risks.", "What to monitor: Watch for the final decision in G 1/25 to adjust prosecution strategies regarding description consistency requirements."]
core_points_en: The Enlarged Board examines if the EPC strictly requires adapting the description after claim amendments are made.
Controversy arises from the 2021 policy shift and conflicting legal foundations cited by different Technical Boards of Appeal.
The focus is on determining if specific EPC provisions provide a clear legal basis for this established administrative practice.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess the potential impact on description amendment costs in European patent applications and opposition proceedings.
Practice note: Optimize internal drafting standards and plan embodiment layouts early to lower compliance costs and validation risks.
What to monitor: Watch for the final decision in G 1/25 to adjust prosecution strategies regarding description consistency requirements.

items.title_zh: EPO中AI聊天机器人输出作为“证据”：判例法新兴趋势
items.title_en: AI chatbot output as "evidence" at the EPO: emerging trends in case law - GJE
items.summary: This article looks at emerging themes from recent decisions of the Boards of Appeal to answer this question.
items.ai_summary_zh: 随着大型语言模型普及，当事人开始在EPO程序中引用ChatGPT等聊天机器人的回答以支持其论点。本文旨在通过分析EPO上诉委员会近期的决定，探讨AI生成内容在EPO程序中作为“证据”的使用范围及 emerging themes。
items.ai_insight_zh: 需持续监测EPO上诉委员会对AI生成内容证据效力的认定标准。目前仅知当事人开始尝试引用，具体采信规则需依据后续完整判例分析，暂无明确结论。

items.title: G1/25 hearing: turning point or status quo?
items.title_zh: G1/25听证：转折点还是维持现状？
items.title_en: EPO G1/25 Hearing: Parties Oppose Description Adaptation Requirement
items.summary: G 1/25 addresses a particularly controversial feature of EPO practice: the requirement to adapt the description. The long-anticipated hearing on G 1/25 took place on 8 May 2026, and unfolded in a manner rarely seen before the Enlarged Board of Appeal – the patent proprietor and the opponent presenting somewhat aligned positions, both opposing the preliminary opinion.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局扩大上诉委员会于2026年5月8日举行G1/25听证，聚焦异议程序中权利要求修改后是否必须相应修改说明书以消除不一致。专利权人与异议人罕见地联合反对初步意见，主张EPC无此强制规定。尽管双方提出法律解释及国际协调论据，但扩委会倾向于维持要求修改说明书的现行实践。本案将决定审查与异议阶段说明书适应性的法律义务边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Enlarged Board of Appeal held a hearing on 8 May 2026 regarding G 1/25, addressing the controversial EPO practice requiring description adaptation. Unusually, both the patent proprietor and opponent aligned against the preliminary opinion, arguing that the European Patent Convention does not mandate such adaptation. The proprietor cited the lack of explicit EPC provisions, while the opponent highlighted global harmonization concerns and Article 84 EPC implications. Despite these arguments, the Enlarged Board’s provisional view suggests it may uphold the current requirement to adapt descriptions in both prosecution and opposition proceedings to resolve inconsistencies with amended claims.
items.ai_insight_zh: 若EPO维持强制修改说明书的实践，中国申请人在欧维权成本将增加。建议企业在答复审查意见或应对异议时，提前规划说明书修改策略，确保权利要求与描述严格一致，避免因形式缺陷导致权利不稳定或额外费用。同时需关注最终裁决对全球专利实务的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral clarifies whether description adaptation is legally mandatory under the EPC or merely administrative practice. A confirmation of the status quo would solidify the current procedural burden on applicants and proprietors to ensure strict consistency between claims and description, impacting portfolio management strategies across examination and opposition stages.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["G1/25听证探讨异议中权利要求修改后是否需强制适配说明书", "当事人联合反对初步意见，主张EPC未规定此类强制性适应义务", "扩委会倾向维持现行实践，要求审查及异议中消除描述不一致"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Both parties opposed the preliminary opinion, arguing no EPC provision mandates adapting the description during proceedings.", "The opponent emphasized global harmonization and Article 84 EPC, noting the EPO's unique stance internationally.", "The Enlarged Board appears inclined to maintain the existing practice of requiring description adaptation for consistency."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需强化说明书与权利要求的一致性管理以规避合规风险", "应预判程序成本上升并优化在欧专利申请与答辩策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Confirms if strict description-claim consistency remains a formal compliance hurdle in EPO proceedings.", "Practice note: Monitor final decision to adjust drafting and amendment strategies for potential continued adaptation requirements.", "What to monitor: The Enlarged Board's final ruling on which specific EPC articles necessitate description adaptation."]
core_points_en: Both parties opposed the preliminary opinion, arguing no EPC provision mandates adapting the description during proceedings.
The opponent emphasized global harmonization and Article 84 EPC, noting the EPO's unique stance internationally.
The Enlarged Board appears inclined to maintain the existing practice of requiring description adaptation for consistency.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Confirms if strict description-claim consistency remains a formal compliance hurdle in EPO proceedings.
Practice note: Monitor final decision to adjust drafting and amendment strategies for potential continued adaptation requirements.
What to monitor: The Enlarged Board's final ruling on which specific EPC articles necessitate description adaptation.

items.title: Colour as a trademark: Lessons from Mondelēz
items.title_zh: 颜色作为商标：来自亿滋国际的经验教训
items.title_en: Colour Trademarks: Strategic Lessons from Mondelēz on Protection and Enforcement
items.summary: At INTA’s Annual Meeting in London, counsel from the parent company of Cadbury and Milka shared why colour marks remain among the most commercially valuable, and legally complex, forms of IP protection.
items.ai_summary_zh: 在伦敦举行的INTA年会上，吉百利和妙卡的母公司亿滋国际的法律顾问分享了观点。他们指出，颜色标记仍然是商业价值最高且法律上最复杂的知识产权保护形式之一。
items.ai_summary_en: At the International Trademark Association’s Annual Meeting in London, legal counsel from Mondelēz International, the parent company of Cadbury and Milka, provided critical insights into the strategic management of colour trademarks. While these marks offer immense commercial value by serving as powerful brand identifiers, they present significant legal complexities regarding registration and maintenance. The discussion highlighted that securing and defending colour rights within the European Union and United Kingdom requires rigorous evidentiary support. Rights holders must demonstrate consistent market use to establish acquired distinctiveness. Without such proof, registrations remain vulnerable to invalidation actions based on a lack of inherent distinctiveness or failure to function as a trademark.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文仅为会议导语，缺乏具体案例或法律论证细节。需关注后续是否发布关于颜色商标显著性认定或侵权判定的具体实务指引。
items.ai_insight_en: For brands relying on single colours as primary identifiers, establishing robust mechanisms to document market usage is essential in the EU and UK markets. Given the susceptibility of colour marks to cancellation due to alleged lack of distinctiveness, practitioners should advise clients to integrate colour elements within broader combination marks featuring text or graphics. This layered approach mitigates the risk of total rights loss if the standalone colour registration is challenged. Continuous monitoring of enforcement trends and judicial standards for acquired distinctiveness remains critical for safeguarding brand assets effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["亿滋法律顾问在伦敦INTA年会发言，讨论颜色商标的商业与法律属性", "原文指出颜色标记兼具高商业价值与高法律复杂性，是IP保护的重要形式"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Colour trademarks provide high commercial value but face complex legal hurdles for initial registration and ongoing maintenance.", "Maintaining validity in EU and UK jurisdictions demands strict evidence of continuous use to counter potential invalidation risks.", "Companies must implement long-term strategies ensuring complete evidentiary chains to preserve exclusive rights over colour marks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["颜色商标在欧洲司法辖区的注册门槛与维权难度仍具高度复杂性", "需观察亿滋国际后续是否披露更多关于颜色商标确权的具体策略或案例", "目前信息不足以支撑对具体费率、诉讼策略或市场格局的深度分析"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Implement strict evidence retention protocols to address the high burden of proving acquired distinctiveness for colour marks.", "Practice note: Construct combined protection systems using graphic or word elements to reduce the legal risk of cancelling standalone colour registrations.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial standards in the EU and UK regarding the threshold for distinctiveness required to maintain colour trademark validity."]
core_points_en: Colour trademarks provide high commercial value but face complex legal hurdles for initial registration and ongoing maintenance.
Maintaining validity in EU and UK jurisdictions demands strict evidence of continuous use to counter potential invalidation risks.
Companies must implement long-term strategies ensuring complete evidentiary chains to preserve exclusive rights over colour marks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Implement strict evidence retention protocols to address the high burden of proving acquired distinctiveness for colour marks.
Practice note: Construct combined protection systems using graphic or word elements to reduce the legal risk of cancelling standalone colour registrations.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial standards in the EU and UK regarding the threshold for distinctiveness required to maintain colour trademark validity.

items.title: DIAMS Infinity Redefines How Intellectual Property Is Managed In Practice
items.title_zh: DIAMS Infinity重塑知识产权管理实践
items.title_en: Dennemeyer Launches DIAMS Infinity: AI-Driven Automation Reshapes IP Portfolio Management
items.summary: The Dennemeyer Group introduces DIAMS Infinity, a next-generation IP management software that leverages artificial intelligence to automate repetitive tasks and streamline portfolio management. How does this cloud-based solution address the operational complexity and manual coordination challenges that have long plagued IP professionals managing expanding portfolios?
items.ai_summary_zh: Dennemeyer集团推出新一代IP管理软件DIAMS Infinity，旨在解决组合扩张带来的运营复杂性。该云端方案利用人工智能自动化处理重复性任务，如记录更新和期限追踪，减少人工协调风险。通过内置智能助手 contextualize 信息，提升数据质量与决策效率，帮助IP团队从行政工作中解放，专注于高价值战略分析。
items.ai_summary_en: The Dennemeyer Group has introduced DIAMS Infinity, a next-generation intellectual property management software designed to address the growing complexity of expanding IP portfolios. By leveraging artificial intelligence, the platform automates repetitive administrative tasks such as record updates and deadline tracking, significantly reducing manual coordination efforts and minimizing human error. The system utilizes built-in intelligent assistants to contextualize data in real time, ensuring information consistency across fragmented databases. This shift from manual oversight to automated execution allows IP professionals to reclaim time previously spent on routine administration. Consequently, teams can focus on high-value strategic analysis and decision-making. The solution aims to enhance operational efficiency and compliance without adding structural complexity, offering organizations greater control and speed in managing their global intellectual property assets effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此动态反映欧洲IP服务市场正加速向AI驱动的自动化管理转型。实务中需关注此类工具如何具体整合欧盟多国期限规则及UPC程序要求。对于大型组合持有人，采用集成AI的系统可能成为降低合规成本、提升数据准确性的关键基础设施，建议持续评估此类技术对传统工作流程的重塑效应。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants managing global intellectual property portfolios should evaluate AI-driven management tools to enhance operational efficiency and accuracy. Integrating automation helps mitigate compliance risks stemming from manual errors, particularly regarding critical deadlines and filings in multiple jurisdictions. By reducing administrative burdens, organizations can optimize costs and redirect resources toward strategic business support. Companies operating in Europe should assess their current IP management systems for potential AI integration to handle increasing maintenance pressures. Adopting such technologies ensures consistent compliance and strengthens competitive positioning by allowing legal teams to focus on value-added activities rather than routine coordination tasks.

items.title: IPEC insights: Prosecco PDO Consortium v Prosecco International -…
items.title_zh: IPEC驳回Prosecco国际公司驳回起诉申请
items.title_en: IPEC Dismisses Strike-Out Bid in Prosecco PDO Infringement Case Against UK Spirits Firm
items.summary: The IPEC has dismissed a strike-out bid by a British spirits company accused of misusing the protected Prosecco name, allowing the case to proceed to…
items.ai_summary_zh: 意大利Prosecco PDO保护协会在英国IPEC起诉Prosecco International Ltd滥用受保护的“Prosecco”名称。被告申请驳回起诉被拒，案件将进入审判阶段。原告指控被告在不符合PDO规范的饮料、投资合作及公司名称中使用该标识，侵犯欧盟原产地名称保护法规（已纳入英国法）及英国证明商标。此案涉及管辖权、平行诉讼及PDO保护范围等关键法律问题。
items.ai_summary_en: The Intellectual Property Enterprise Court (IPEC) has dismissed a strike-out application by Prosecco International Ltd, allowing the Consorzio Di Tutela Della Denominazione Di Origine Controllata Prosecco to proceed with its infringement claim. The Consorzio alleges that the defendant misused the protected 'Prosecco' name on websites and in corporate branding for products and services not complying with the Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) specification. The case involves claims under assimilated EU regulations and the UK Trade Marks Act 1994 regarding certification mark infringement. This procedural decision addresses jurisdictional issues and parallel proceedings, confirming that the matter will advance to trial.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国酒企出口英国需严格审查品牌命名，避免使用受保护地理标志。即使产品未直接销售，若网站提供投资或合伙机会并关联GI名称，亦可能构成侵权。建议提前进行FTO检索，确保营销材料符合UK assimilated law要求，规避高额诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights the continued robust protection of geographical indications in the UK post-Brexit, as EU PDO regulations have been assimilated into domestic law. Rights holders can enforce these protections alongside certification mark rights against entities using GI terms for unrelated commercial activities or non-compliant products. For market participants, this underscores the importance of ensuring that brand names and digital marketing strategies do not infringe upon established PDOs, even when offering services rather than physical goods.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IPEC驳回被告驳回起诉申请，案件正式进入审判程序", "原告主张被告违反EU PDO法规及英国1994年商标法证明商标条款", "争议焦点包括域名使用、非合规产品标注及公司名称侵权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The IPEC rejected the defendant's bid to strike out the claim, permitting the litigation concerning alleged misuse of the Prosecco PDO to proceed to trial.", "Claims rely on assimilated EU regulations protecting designations of origin and sections 10(2) and 10(3) of the UK Trade Marks Act 1994 regarding certification marks.", "Alleged infringements include using PROSECCO on non-compliant drinks, investment opportunities, company names, and specific domain names like prosecco.com."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口英国需警惕受保护地理标志，避免品牌命名侵权", "网络营销关联GI名称即便无实体销售也可能构成侵权"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies must ensure brand naming and digital content avoid unauthorized use of protected geographical indications to prevent infringement liability.", "Practice note: Enforcement actions may combine assimilated EU PDO regulations with national trade mark laws, including certification mark provisions under the Trade Marks Act 1994.", "What to monitor: The upcoming trial outcome will clarify the scope of PDO protection regarding online activities, investment offers, and corporate naming conventions in the UK."]
core_points_en: The IPEC rejected the defendant's bid to strike out the claim, permitting the litigation concerning alleged misuse of the Prosecco PDO to proceed to trial.
Claims rely on assimilated EU regulations protecting designations of origin and sections 10(2) and 10(3) of the UK Trade Marks Act 1994 regarding certification marks.
Alleged infringements include using PROSECCO on non-compliant drinks, investment opportunities, company names, and specific domain names like prosecco.com.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies must ensure brand naming and digital content avoid unauthorized use of protected geographical indications to prevent infringement liability.
Practice note: Enforcement actions may combine assimilated EU PDO regulations with national trade mark laws, including certification mark provisions under the Trade Marks Act 1994.
What to monitor: The upcoming trial outcome will clarify the scope of PDO protection regarding online activities, investment offers, and corporate naming conventions in the UK.

items.title: Réseaux sociaux et diffusion contrefaisante : l’image comme support d’atteinte aux droits
items.title_zh: 社交媒体与假冒传播：图像作为侵权载体
items.title_en: Paris Court Rules Social Media Image Distribution Constitutes Copyright Infringement
items.summary: Par Maître Solène DAGUIER, DTMV Avocats (CA Paris, 13 févr. 2026, n° 24/18475) La contrefaçon d’une œuvre ne résulte pas seulement de sa reproduction matérielle. Elle peut également être caractérisée par la diffusion de son image. Dans un arrêt du 13 février dernier, la Cour d’appel de Paris en donne une illustration en retenant la […] L’article Réseaux sociaux et diffusion contrefaisante : l’image comme support d’atteinte aux droits est apparu en premier sur BLIP! .
items.ai_summary_zh: 巴黎上诉法院于2026年2月13日作出判决（案号24/18475）。法院指出，作品侵权不仅限于实体复制，传播作品图像亦可构成侵权。该判决以具体案例说明了在社交媒体环境下，图像传播如何被认定为对版权的侵害。
items.ai_summary_en: In a significant ruling dated February 13, 2026 (Case No. 24/18475), the Paris Court of Appeal clarified that copyright infringement is not limited to the physical reproduction of a work. The court established that the dissemination of an image via social media platforms can independently constitute infringement. This decision underscores that in the digital environment, the widespread distribution of visual content itself violates the rights holder’s exclusive prerogatives, without necessitating proof of traditional physical copying. By recognizing digital diffusion as a distinct basis for liability, the judgment provides new judicial grounds for combating online copyright violations and reinforces the legal responsibilities of those who share unauthorized images on social networks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注法国法院在数字环境下对版权侵权形式的扩张解释。实务中应监测社交媒体图像传播行为的合规风险，特别是非实体复制场景下的侵权责任认定标准变化。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and market participants operating in France and the EU must recognize that unauthorized use of third-party visuals, fonts, or design elements on official or marketing social media accounts carries substantial litigation risk. Liability arises from the act of publication itself, regardless of whether physical copies were made. Entities should implement rigorous compliance mechanisms to verify that all digital content possesses valid authorization prior to release. Proactive management of digital asset licensing is essential to mitigate exposure to high damages claims and protect brand reputation against allegations of intellectual property violation in cross-border operations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["巴黎上诉法院确认作品侵权不限于实体复制，传播图像也可构成侵权", "法院在2026年2月13日判决中，以实例说明图像传播对权利的侵害"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Paris Court of Appeal ruled that distributing images on social media constitutes copyright infringement.", "Infringement findings are no longer restricted to material reproduction, as diffusion acts alone suffice for conviction.", "The judgment strengthens legal regulation regarding the illegal dissemination of images within the digital environment."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注法国司法对数字图像传播侵权认定的最新尺度", "监测社交媒体平台内容分发中的版权合规边界", "观察后续类似案件中对“图像传播”定性的司法一致性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strictly prohibit the unauthorized use of third-party visual materials in social media marketing campaigns to avoid infringement liabilities.", "Practice note: Establish a comprehensive end-to-end compliance review and authorization management system for all digital content publishing.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations regarding digital diffusion as a standalone basis for copyright infringement in European jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: The Paris Court of Appeal ruled that distributing images on social media constitutes copyright infringement.
Infringement findings are no longer restricted to material reproduction, as diffusion acts alone suffice for conviction.
The judgment strengthens legal regulation regarding the illegal dissemination of images within the digital environment.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strictly prohibit the unauthorized use of third-party visual materials in social media marketing campaigns to avoid infringement liabilities.
Practice note: Establish a comprehensive end-to-end compliance review and authorization management system for all digital content publishing.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial interpretations regarding digital diffusion as a standalone basis for copyright infringement in European jurisdictions.

items.title: UPC highly popular with Chinese and US claimants
items.title_zh: 中美原告高度青睐统一专利法院
items.title_en: UPC Gains Favor Among US and Chinese Claimants Following Case Management System Overhaul
items.summary: The team behind the UPC has every right to be proud. Just three years after its launch, the UPC has overcome its biggest criticism from users — the much-maligned CMS has been completely overhauled. “The successful launch of the Court’s new Case Management System marked a major milestone, showcasing the effectiveness of close cooperation between […] The post UPC highly popular with Chinese and US claimants appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）运行三年后，已完成对案件管理系统（CMS）的全面 overhaul，解决了此前用户的主要批评。新系统的成功上线被视为重要里程碑，体现了紧密合作的有效性。标题显示该法院在中美原告中颇受欢迎。
items.ai_summary_en: Three years after its inception, the Unified Patent Court has successfully addressed its most significant operational criticism by completely overhauling the previously maligned Case Management System. The successful launch of this new platform marks a major milestone for the institution, demonstrating the effectiveness of close cooperation among stakeholders in resolving early technical hurdles. Although detailed statistical data is not provided in the excerpt, the heightened interest from claimants based in China and the United States signals growing confidence in the Court’s infrastructure. This surge in participation indicates that the UPC is increasingly viewed as an attractive venue for international patent litigation, with improved technological reliability reducing procedural friction and enhancing overall judicial efficiency for global rights holders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注CMS系统升级后对诉讼效率的实际改善情况，以及中美主体在UPC的活跃度趋势。目前正文信息有限，仅确认系统更新事实，未提供具体数据或深层影响分析，建议持续监测后续详细报告。
items.ai_insight_en: The optimization of the UPC’s procedural framework reduces legal uncertainty and lowers barriers to entry for complex patent disputes. As participation from major technology markets increases, rights holders should anticipate intensified competitive dynamics within the Court. Companies operating in Europe must proactively assess their exposure to potential infringement claims, particularly in key jurisdictions like Germany. Evaluating local defense costs, historical success rates, and strategic litigation options is essential. Establishing robust patent defense mechanisms and monitoring shifts in judicial practice will help mitigate risks associated with the rising volume of cross-border patent litigation before the Unified Patent Court.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC运行三年，已彻底重构此前备受诟病的案件管理系统（CMS）", "新CMS上线被视为展示合作有效性的重大里程碑", "标题显示中国和美国原告对UPC表现出高度青睐"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC has fully replaced its criticized Case Management System three years post-launch, resolving major user concerns regarding digital infrastructure.", "The new system's successful deployment highlights effective stakeholder collaboration and significantly improves the Court's operational efficiency.", "High engagement levels from US and Chinese plaintiffs suggest the UPC is becoming a preferred forum for cross-border patent enforcement actions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注CMS系统升级后的实际运行稳定性与用户体验反馈", "监测中美企业在UPC的诉讼参与比例变化趋势", "等待更多关于UPC受欢迎程度的具体统计数据支持"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Enhanced system reliability lowers procedural risks, making the UPC a more viable venue for high-stakes patent enforcement and defense strategies.", "Practice note: Rights holders should evaluate defense costs and success rates in core jurisdictions like Germany to prepare for increased litigation activity.", "What to monitor: Shifts in UPC litigation strategies and case management trends to anticipate competitive pressures and adjust cross-border IP defenses accordingly."]
core_points_en: The UPC has fully replaced its criticized Case Management System three years post-launch, resolving major user concerns regarding digital infrastructure.
The new system's successful deployment highlights effective stakeholder collaboration and significantly improves the Court's operational efficiency.
High engagement levels from US and Chinese plaintiffs suggest the UPC is becoming a preferred forum for cross-border patent enforcement actions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Enhanced system reliability lowers procedural risks, making the UPC a more viable venue for high-stakes patent enforcement and defense strategies.
Practice note: Rights holders should evaluate defense costs and success rates in core jurisdictions like Germany to prepare for increased litigation activity.
What to monitor: Shifts in UPC litigation strategies and case management trends to anticipate competitive pressures and adjust cross-border IP defenses accordingly.

items.title: Green light for the EU-India Trade Agreement: a new landscape of opportunities for intellectual property
items.title_zh: 欧盟-印度贸易协定获政治批准：知识产权新机遇
items.title_en: EU-India Trade Agreement Receives Political Approval, Signaling New IP Opportunities
items.summary: The political green light for the free trade agreement between the European Union and India, reached in January 2026, marks a turning point in the economic relationship between the two blocs. This is one of the most ambitious agreements signed by India to date and one of the most significant strategic agreements for the EU ... Read more La entrada Green light for the EU-India Trade Agreement: a new landscape of opportunities for intellectual property se publicó primero en PONS IP .
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，欧盟与印度之间的自由贸易协定于2026年1月获得政治批准。这标志着双方经济关系的转折点，是印度签署的最雄心勃勃的协议之一，也是欧盟最具战略意义的协议之一，为知识产权领域带来新的机会格局。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union and India reached a political agreement on their free trade deal in January 2026. This milestone marks a significant shift in the economic relationship between the two blocs. Described as one of India’s most ambitious agreements and a key strategic priority for the EU, the deal establishes a new framework for bilateral cooperation. While specific intellectual property provisions are not detailed in this initial announcement, the agreement is positioned to create fresh opportunities for industrial property rights holders operating across both jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文内容仅为摘要片段，缺乏具体条款细节。需持续观察协定正式签署后关于知识产权执法、地理标志或专利保护的具体章节文本，以评估对欧洲权利人的实际合规影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This political green light signals impending changes to the IP regulatory environment between the EU and India. Rights holders should anticipate updated frameworks for protection and enforcement as the agreement moves toward finalization. Immediate action involves monitoring the release of detailed textual chapters to assess impacts on portfolio strategy and compliance obligations in these key markets.

items.title: Where Should Cases Go? Or Where Should Patent Law Be Made?
items.title_zh: 案件去向与法律制定：统一专利法院案件分配的制度反思
items.title_en: UPC Case Distribution: Where Litigation Concentrates and Patent Law Is Formed
items.summary: "Gedanken ohne Inhalt sind leer, Anschauungen ohne Begriffe sind blind." — Immanuel Kant (Thoughts without content are empty, intuitions without concepts are blind.)
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文回应关于统一专利法院（UPC）案件分配过于抽象的批评，指出问题核心在于案件集中是否扭曲了UPC法的形成条件。尽管案件集中于特定分部反映了用户信心与可预测性，但法院并非市场，其产出是法律。若法律实践仅由少数中心通过重复程序塑造，将导致早期教义固化。作者主张需审视这种由用户偏好驱动的自强化均衡，因其可能非中性地决定未来专利法的结构与发展路径。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary addresses concerns that UPC case distribution debates remain too abstract. It argues the core issue is whether concentrating cases in specific local divisions affects how UPC law is formed. While concentration reflects user confidence and predictability, a court produces law rather than merely allocating market demand. If legal practice is shaped by repeated procedures in a limited number of divisions, early doctrines may become entrenched. The self-reinforcing equilibrium driven by user choice requires scrutiny to ensure it does not non-neutrally determine the future structure of European patent law.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应密切关注UPC主要分部的司法倾向，因早期判例将深远影响后续诉讼策略。建议避免盲目追随热门法庭，需综合评估各分部的程序效率与文化差异，以规避因‘胜者通吃’效应导致的法律解释单一化风险，优化全球专利布局与应诉策略。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and counsel, understanding the structural impact of case concentration is vital for long-term portfolio strategy. The reliance on predictable divisions offers immediate procedural advantages but carries the risk of shaping a uniform legal interpretation through limited judicial perspectives. Monitoring this dynamic helps anticipate shifts in legal doctrine formation within the new unified system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["案件集中反映用户信心，但需警惕其对UPC法形成条件的潜在扭曲", "法院产出法律而非分配需求，少数中心的重复实践将固化早期教义", "单纯的数据透明或程序调整无法解决由用户偏好驱动的制度结构性问题"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Case concentration in certain UPC divisions reflects user confidence but raises questions about how law is systematically formed.", "Courts produce legal precedents, meaning concentrated procedural environments can entrench early judicial doctrines.", "User-driven case distribution creates a self-reinforcing equilibrium that structurally influences future patent law development."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC主要分部早期判例，预判未来专利法解释趋势", "综合评估各分部差异，避免单一法庭依赖带来的战略风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Concentrated litigation venues influence the evolution of UPC jurisprudence, affecting long-term enforcement predictability.", "Practice note: Evaluate divisional differences beyond speed to understand how repeated practices may solidify specific legal interpretations.", "What to monitor: Track whether self-reinforcing case distribution leads to the entrenchment of early doctrinal positions in key divisions."]
core_points_en: Case concentration in certain UPC divisions reflects user confidence but raises questions about how law is systematically formed.
Courts produce legal precedents, meaning concentrated procedural environments can entrench early judicial doctrines.
User-driven case distribution creates a self-reinforcing equilibrium that structurally influences future patent law development.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Concentrated litigation venues influence the evolution of UPC jurisprudence, affecting long-term enforcement predictability.
Practice note: Evaluate divisional differences beyond speed to understand how repeated practices may solidify specific legal interpretations.
What to monitor: Track whether self-reinforcing case distribution leads to the entrenchment of early doctrinal positions in key divisions.

items.title: A Venetian surprise: Does the UPCA transitional period really require an opt-out to access national courts?
items.title_zh: 威尼斯惊喜：UPCA过渡期是否真需退出才能诉诸国内法院？
items.title_en: UPCA Transitional Period: Is an Opt-Out Mandatory for National Court Access?
items.summary: This Kat is looking you dead in the eyeto ask: 'Are you absolutely sureyou didn't need to file that opt-out?'The transitional period of the Unified Patent Court Agreement (UPCA) is widely understood to offer a safe harbor of choice. For a period of seven years, patentees holding "classic" European patents have rested on the comfortable assumption that they can freely choose between asserting
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章质疑对《统一专利法院协议》（UPCA）过渡期的普遍理解。通常认为该时期为专利权人提供选择权的安全港，允许其在七年内自由选择诉讼 forum。作者提出反问，暗示可能无需提交退出申请即可访问国内法院，挑战现有假设。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary challenges the widespread assumption that the Unified Patent Court Agreement's transitional period provides an unconditional safe harbor for patent holders. While it is commonly believed that proprietors of classic European patents can freely choose between national courts and the UPC during the seven-year window, this analysis suggests such freedom may not be absolute. The author argues that failing to formally file an opt-out could expose rights holders to significant jurisdictional disputes. Consequently, patentees are urged to critically re-evaluate their litigation strategies for classic European patents. Ensuring strict compliance with procedural requirements is essential to avoid legal uncertainty and potential loss of access to preferred national forums.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文仅为理论探讨，未提供确凿法律依据或判例支持。实务中应继续监测UPC规则解释及后续司法实践，以确认过渡期退出机制的具体适用要求，目前不宜仅凭此文改变既定策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should not assume automatic jurisdictional flexibility under the UPCA transitional rules. Relying on ambiguous interpretations of forum selection carries the risk of being compelled into UPC proceedings due to procedural oversights. To preserve the right to litigate in national courts, rights holders must ensure that valid opt-out applications have been correctly filed for all relevant classic European patents. Immediate verification of these filings is critical to mitigate the increased costs and legal uncertainties associated with unintended UPC jurisdiction.

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Verify the submission status of UPC opt-out applications for all classic European patents immediately.", "Practice note: Avoid relying on assumed forum selection rights to prevent falling into unfavorable UPC jurisdictional procedures.", "What to monitor: Potential jurisdictional challenges arising from missing or defective opt-out filings during the transitional period."]

Practice note: Avoid relying on assumed forum selection rights to prevent falling into unfavorable UPC jurisdictional procedures.

items.title: Generative AI and the EU Copyright Law’s Blind Spots
items.title_zh: 生成式AI与欧盟版权法的盲区
items.title_en: Generative AI and the EU Copyright Law’s Blind Spots: A Legislative Miss
items.summary: Legislatures are notoriously inefficient in legislating for future technologies. These technologies are sometimes developing at the same time as legislatures are debating new laws, resulting in the legislatures missing, by a small margin, opportunities to design the laws for the new technologies.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出立法者难以跟上技术发展步伐，以TRIPS协议忽视万维网为例，类比当前欧盟版权立法与生成式AI发展的错位。尽管DSM指令包含文本和数据挖掘（TDM）例外，但立法历史显示，制定者当时并未预见生成式AI的兴起。因此，现行TDM条款并非专为生成式AI设计，这构成了欧盟版权法的潜在盲区，可能影响未来AI训练数据的法律合规性。
items.ai_summary_en: Legislatures often struggle to regulate emerging technologies effectively, as seen with the TRIPS Agreement's failure to address the World Wide Web. Similarly, the EU's 2019 Digital Single Market Directive text and data mining exceptions were not drafted with generative AI in mind. Legislative history confirms that lawmakers did not anticipate generative AI's rapid development when adopting Articles 3 and 4. This disconnect creates potential blind spots in current EU copyright law regarding AI training data compliance.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国AI企业需警惕欧盟版权法在生成式AI领域的解释风险。由于TDM例外非为AI定制，建议企业在欧开展数据训练前，进行严格的版权合规审查，并密切关注后续司法实践或立法补充，以降低侵权诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This analysis highlights a significant interpretive risk for rights holders and AI developers operating within the EU. Since the existing text and data mining framework was not tailored for generative AI, stakeholders should anticipate potential legal uncertainties regarding training data usage. Monitoring future judicial interpretations and possible legislative updates is crucial for maintaining compliance and managing portfolio risks in this evolving landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["立法滞后导致欧盟版权法未预见生成式AI对TDM例外的需求", "DSM指令TDM条款制定时，生成式AI尚未成为立法者的考量重点", "现行法律框架存在盲区，可能引发生成式AI训练数据的版权争议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EU legislators did not consider generative AI when drafting the DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions.", "Legislative history confirms Articles 3 and 4 were not designed to address generative AI technology needs.", "The parallel development of EU copyright laws and generative AI created a regulatory gap similar to past technological misses."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟TDM例外非为AI定制的合规解释风险", "加强数据训练版权审查并关注后续立法动态"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders and AI companies face uncertainty as current TDM exceptions may not adequately cover generative AI training activities.", "Practice note: Stakeholders should review data sourcing practices against the original intent of DSM Directive Articles 3 and 4 to assess exposure.", "What to monitor: Future court decisions interpreting TDM exceptions in the context of generative AI and any subsequent EU legislative amendments."]
core_points_en: EU legislators did not consider generative AI when drafting the DSM Directive's text and data mining exceptions.
Legislative history confirms Articles 3 and 4 were not designed to address generative AI technology needs.
The parallel development of EU copyright laws and generative AI created a regulatory gap similar to past technological misses.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and AI companies face uncertainty as current TDM exceptions may not adequately cover generative AI training activities.
Practice note: Stakeholders should review data sourcing practices against the original intent of DSM Directive Articles 3 and 4 to assess exposure.
What to monitor: Future court decisions interpreting TDM exceptions in the context of generative AI and any subsequent EU legislative amendments.

items.title: Présidentielle 2027 : la guerre des noms de domaine est lancée
items.title_zh: 2027法国大选：域名争夺战已打响
items.title_en: France 2027 Presidential Election: Early Domain Name Speculation and Legal Risks
items.summary: Avant les meetings, avant les affiches, avant même les déclarations officielles : tout se joue désormais sur quelques caractères tapés dans une barre d'adresse.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出2027年法国总统大选前，针对候选人姓名的域名抢注已开始。尽管遵循“先申请先得”原则，但候选人可依据法国民法典姓名权、知识产权法典商标权及民法典商业忠诚义务进行维权。主要风险包括政治性域名抢注、易混淆的打字错误抢注及处于灰色地带的 parody 网站。
items.ai_summary_en: Ahead of the 2027 French presidential election, speculative registration of candidate-related domain names has intensified. Following the first-come, first-served principle, third parties often secure URLs before official campaigns begin. While this practice aims to obstruct communication or enable resale, French law offers recourse. Rights holders may rely on Article 9 of the Civil Code for name protection, the Intellectual Property Code for trademark rights, and Article 1240 for civil liability regarding unfair competition. The analysis highlights cybersquatting and typosquatting as primary threats, noting that legal action must be swift to counteract strategic occupation of digital assets by opportunists or political opponents.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在法国法域下，域名争议不仅涉及商标侵权，还紧密关联姓名权与反不正当竞争。实务中需区分恶意抢注与受保护的political satire。权利人应监控早期域名注册行为，并在出现混淆时迅速援引民法典第1240条等规定主张权利，而非仅依赖商标程序。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders, domain disputes in France extend beyond trademark infringement to include personality rights and tort liability. Distinguishing between malicious squatting and protected political satire is critical, as confusion regarding official sources typically triggers legal intervention. Proactive monitoring of new registrations is essential to enforce claims effectively under civil law frameworks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["域名注册遵循先申请先得原则，但受民法姓名权、商标法及商业忠诚义务限制", "政治性抢注旨在阻碍沟通或转售；打字错误抢注用于误导流量或传播虚假信息", "政治讽刺虽受言论自由保护，但若造成官方混淆则构成侵权，法律将介入干预"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Speculative domain registrations occur early, leveraging the first-come, first-served rule to secure candidate names.", "Legal remedies include Civil Code name rights, trademark laws, and unfair competition provisions under Article 1240.", "Threats include cybersquatting for resale or obstruction, and typosquatting to mislead voters via similar URLs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["法国法下域名维权需结合商标、姓名权及反不正当竞争条款综合主张", "需警惕利用打字错误或近似后缀进行的流量劫持与虚假信息传播风险", "政治讽刺与侵权的界限在于是否造成公众对官方来源的混淆，需个案判断"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Domain enforcement in France requires combining trademark actions with Civil Code claims for name protection and unfair competition.", "Practice note: Legal success often depends on proving public confusion between speculative domains and official campaign sites, rather than mere registration.", "What to monitor: Early registration trends for candidate names across .fr, .com, and niche extensions to identify cybersquatting or typosquatting attempts."]
core_points_en: Speculative domain registrations occur early, leveraging the first-come, first-served rule to secure candidate names.
Legal remedies include Civil Code name rights, trademark laws, and unfair competition provisions under Article 1240.
Threats include cybersquatting for resale or obstruction, and typosquatting to mislead voters via similar URLs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Domain enforcement in France requires combining trademark actions with Civil Code claims for name protection and unfair competition.
Practice note: Legal success often depends on proving public confusion between speculative domains and official campaign sites, rather than mere registration.
What to monitor: Early registration trends for candidate names across .fr, .com, and niche extensions to identify cybersquatting or typosquatting attempts.

items.title: Feed vs The Feed : l’antériorité européenne l’emporte sur le succès commercial
items.title_zh: Feed诉The Feed：欧洲在先权利胜过商业成功
items.title_en: Paris Court Annuls 'Feed' Marks: EU Prior Rights Prevail Over Local Commercial Success
items.summary: Par Maître Etienne Bucher, avocat au Barreau de Paris – cabinet Eris Avocat Le 18 février 2026, le Tribunal judiciaire de Paris a prononcé l’annulation de quatre marques françaises comportant le signe « Feed » et a condamné in solidum la société O.K.R (anciennement Feed SA) et son fondateur à verser 179 416,41 € à la société américaine […] L’article Feed vs The Feed : l’antériorité européenne l’emporte sur le succès commercial est apparu en premier sur BLIP! .
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年2月18日，巴黎司法法院裁定撤销O.K.R公司（原Feed SA）及其创始人持有的四个包含“Feed”标志的法国商标。法院判定被告需向一家美国公司连带支付179,416.41欧元赔偿金。该案确立了欧洲在先权利优先于后续商业成功的原则。
items.ai_summary_en: On February 18, 2026, the Paris Judicial Court ordered the cancellation of four French trademarks containing the sign 'Feed', held by O.K.R (formerly Feed SA). The court recognized the prior rights of a US company’s European Union trademark, ruling that such supranational priority outweighs any commercial success achieved by the defendant in the French market. Consequently, O.K.R and its founder were held jointly and severally liable to pay approximately €179,416.41 in damages. This decision reinforces the supremacy of EU-wide trademark rights over national registrations within member states. It establishes that mere market reputation or local commercial performance cannot serve as a valid defense against legitimate prior EU rights, emphasizing the critical importance of securing a clean legal basis for brands operating across Europe rather than relying solely on national registration strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显在商标争议中，即便被告拥有显著市场成功，若存在欧洲范围内的在先权利，仍面临商标被撤销及高额赔偿风险。企业应重视早期权利排查。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders must prioritize comprehensive searches within the EUIPO database rather than relying exclusively on single-country registration checks. Even if a brand has established substantial market reputation in a specific member state, it remains vulnerable to cancellation and high damages if it infringes upon an earlier EU trademark. A strategic approach should center on securing EU-wide protection as the primary asset, supplemented by national filings where necessary. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate due diligence before market entry is essential to mitigate the risk of brand assets being invalidated due to underlying rights defects, ensuring long-term stability and compliance with European IP frameworks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["巴黎司法法院撤销了四个包含“Feed”标志的法国商标", "被告O.K.R公司及创始人被判连带赔偿美国公司约17.9万欧元", "裁判要旨显示欧洲在先权利效力优于后起商业成功"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Paris Judicial Court annulled four French 'Feed' marks, confirming the primacy of earlier EU trademark rights over national registrations.", "The ruling clarifies that local commercial success does not constitute a valid defense against infringement of prior EU trademark rights.", "O.K.R and its founder face joint liability for nearly €180,000, highlighting significant financial risks for cross-border brand conflicts."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在先权利对商标稳定性的决定性影响高于市场知名度", "跨境品牌扩张需严格评估目标市场的在先商标障碍", "需关注法国法院对“商业成功”抗辩的采信标准变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize EUIPO database searches to uncover hidden cross-border infringement risks beyond single-state registrations.", "Practice note: Secure EU-wide trademark protection as the core strategy, using national filings only as supplementary measures.", "What to monitor: Ensure robust freedom-to-operate due diligence before market entry to prevent brand invalidation and costly litigation."]
core_points_en: Paris Judicial Court annulled four French 'Feed' marks, confirming the primacy of earlier EU trademark rights over national registrations.
The ruling clarifies that local commercial success does not constitute a valid defense against infringement of prior EU trademark rights.
O.K.R and its founder face joint liability for nearly €180,000, highlighting significant financial risks for cross-border brand conflicts.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize EUIPO database searches to uncover hidden cross-border infringement risks beyond single-state registrations.
Practice note: Secure EU-wide trademark protection as the core strategy, using national filings only as supplementary measures.
What to monitor: Ensure robust freedom-to-operate due diligence before market entry to prevent brand invalidation and costly litigation.

items.title: Patenting the use of medical devices (T 0941/24)
items.title_zh: 医疗器械用途的专利申请（T 0941/24号决定）
items.title_en: EPO Decision T 0941/24: Navigating Patentability Boundaries for Medical Device Applications
items.summary: Patenting medical devices in Europe is challenging. First, there is the problem that medical use claims are not permitted for medical devices, whilst simultaneously methods of treatment or surgery using a device are excluded from patentability. However, a recent decision from the Board of Appeal in T 0941/24 demonstrates that it is sometimes possible to navigate the fine line of
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出在欧洲为医疗器械申请专利面临挑战：既不允许针对医疗器械的医疗用途权利要求，又排除使用设备的治疗或手术方法的可专利性。EPO上诉委员会在T 0941/24号决定中展示了如何在此法律限制间寻找可行路径。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) maintains strict exclusions regarding medical device patenting, prohibiting medical use claims for devices while simultaneously excluding methods of treatment or surgery from patentability. This creates a complex landscape for applicants seeking protection. The recent Board of Appeal decision in case T 0941/24 provides critical clarification on these rules. It demonstrates that despite statutory limitations, it is possible to secure patent protection for specific applications of medical devices through careful claim construction. The ruling highlights how applicants can navigate the fine legal boundaries to protect technical innovations associated with device usage, offering valuable compliance guidance for the industry.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该判例揭示了在严格排除治疗方法的法律框架下，仍可能存在特定的权利要求撰写空间。实务中需密切关注上诉委员会如何界定“非治疗方法”与“器械用途”的边界，以评估相关技术方案的授权前景。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in the European market, understanding the distinction between excluded therapeutic methods and patentable technical device features is essential. Since direct medical use claims are not permitted, innovation protection should focus on structural improvements, non-therapeutic operational modes, or specific technical parameters. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses is recommended to mitigate infringement risks. Engaging specialized patent professionals to optimize claim language ensures alignment with EPO examination standards, thereby maximizing the scope of valid protection for medical device technologies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲专利法下，医疗器械不适用医疗用途权利要求格式", "使用医疗器械进行治疗或手术的方法被明确排除在可专利性之外", "EPO上诉委员会T 0941/24号决定提供了规避上述排除条款的判例指引"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["European law prohibits medical use claims for medical devices and excludes therapeutic methods from patentability.", "Decision T 0941/24 confirms that specific device applications can be patented via precise claim drafting strategies.", "Applicants must carefully structure claims within legal limits to protect technical features of medical device uses."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注T 0941/24决定中对医疗器械权利要求具体措辞的接受标准", "观察该判例是否确立了区分器械功能与治疗步骤的新审查基准", "后续需跟踪该决定对同类医疗器械专利申请审查实践的实际影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Focus protection efforts on device structure and non-therapeutic functional innovations rather than excluded treatment uses.", "Practice note: Engage specialized agents to draft claims that strictly adhere to EPO technical feature requirements.", "What to monitor: Monitor evolving EPO case law regarding the boundary between technical device features and excluded medical methods."]
core_points_en: European law prohibits medical use claims for medical devices and excludes therapeutic methods from patentability.
Decision T 0941/24 confirms that specific device applications can be patented via precise claim drafting strategies.
Applicants must carefully structure claims within legal limits to protect technical features of medical device uses.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Focus protection efforts on device structure and non-therapeutic functional innovations rather than excluded treatment uses.
Practice note: Engage specialized agents to draft claims that strictly adhere to EPO technical feature requirements.
What to monitor: Monitor evolving EPO case law regarding the boundary between technical device features and excluded medical methods.

items.title: Emotional Perception vs Inventive Step – Challenges Ahead?
items.title_zh: 情感感知案与创造性步骤：未来的挑战？
items.title_en: Emotional Perception: Adopting COMVIK and Navigating Inventive Step Challenges in the UK
items.summary: In Emotional Perception the UK Supreme Court delivered its verdict on how the patent eligibility of computer-implemented inventions should be assessed in the UK. The decision was a tour de force , rejecting the much-maligned Aerotel approach favoured by the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO), instead putting the European COMVIK (sometimes referred to as the “any hardware”) approach at the heart of UK patent practice. However, the UK Supreme Court decision in Emotional Perception left behind
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院在“情感感知”案中裁定，拒绝英国知识产权局偏好的Aerotel方法，转而将欧洲COMVIK方法置于英国专利实践核心。然而，该裁决遗留了一个未决问题：如何在创造性步骤评估中具体实施COMVIK方法。本文探讨英国知识产权局在此方面可能面临的实施挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court’s ruling in Emotional Perception marks a significant shift in assessing patent eligibility for computer-implemented inventions. The decision rejects the long-standing Aerotel test favored by the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO), formally adopting the European Patent Office’s COMVIK approach as the core standard for determining subject matter eligibility in UK practice. However, the judgment leaves critical implementation details unresolved, particularly regarding how the COMVIK framework should be applied during inventive step assessments. This analysis examines the resulting legal ambiguities and identifies potential operational challenges the UKIPO may face when integrating this new standard into examination practice, highlighting areas where further guidance is needed to ensure consistent application.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案标志着英国专利适格性审查标准的重大转向，但具体操作细则尚不明确。需密切关注英国知识产权局后续发布的审查指南更新，以及实务中如何界定技术贡献与创造性步骤的关系，这将直接影响软件及AI相关专利的申请策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking patent protection for software-related inventions in the UK must align their drafting strategies with the EPO’s COMVIK standards rather than relying on previous Aerotel-based approaches. Emphasizing technical contributions and concrete hardware interactions is essential to demonstrate inventiveness under the new regime. Given the current ambiguity surrounding inventive step assessments, applicants should provide detailed descriptions of how technical problems are solved through specific system architectures. Monitoring forthcoming UKIPO examination guidelines will be crucial for adapting prosecution strategies and mitigating risks associated with the transitional period in legal interpretation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院拒绝Aerotel方法，确立COMVIK方法为英国计算机实施发明专利审查核心", "裁决遗留关键问题：如何在创造性步骤评估中具体落地实施COMVIK方法", "文章分析英国知识产权局在后续实践中实施创造性步骤评估时可能面临的挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court replaced the Aerotel test with the EPO's COMVIK approach for assessing software patent eligibility.", "The judgment fails to clarify specific implementation paths for applying COMVIK during inventive step evaluations.", "The UKIPO faces operational challenges and legal uncertainty in executing the new inventive step assessment standards."]

items.title: Statutory guidance: Directions for online forms
items.title_zh: UKIPO 发布在线表格法定指引，推动官方表格通过 GOV.UK 在线完成与递交
items.title_en: UKIPO Issues Statutory Guidance on Online Form Completion via GOV.UK
items.summary: Introducing the online completion and delivery of official forms through GOV.UK.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局发布《在线表格指引》，介绍通过 GOV.UK 在线完成并递交官方表格的安排。官方页面的核心信息是，相关表格提交已被纳入统一的在线流程，重点在于推动官方表格的数字化填写与交付。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has published statutory guidance titled Directions for online forms. This document introduces the framework for the online completion and delivery of official forms through the GOV.UK platform. The guidance establishes the procedural basis for digital submission channels, marking a shift toward centralized electronic processing for official IP interactions. Rights holders and practitioners should note this update as it formalizes the digital pathway for submitting required documentation to the UKIPO, replacing or supplementing previous methods with a unified online interface.
items.ai_insight_zh: 这类更新虽偏基础流程，但对英国知识产权申请和后续程序管理仍有直接影响。申请人和代理机构应把在线表格路径、内部操作指引和留痕要求同步到日常提交流程中，避免继续依赖过时的纸质或分散入口。
items.ai_insight_en: This update formalizes the digital submission infrastructure for UK IP proceedings. For international practitioners, it underscores the necessity of aligning internal docketing and filing workflows with the GOV.UK ecosystem. While specific form lists are not detailed in this brief excerpt, the statutory nature of the guidance implies mandatory compliance for future submissions. Teams should verify that their administrative processes accommodate these digital requirements to ensure procedural validity and avoid potential rejection of filings due to incorrect submission channels.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO 发布《在线表格指引》", "该指引围绕通过 GOV.UK 在线完成和递交官方表格", "更新反映官方表格流程进一步向统一数字渠道迁移"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UKIPO publishes statutory guidance directing online completion of official forms.", "Official form delivery is now integrated into the unified GOV.UK digital platform."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UKIPO 正持续把提交动作集中到 GOV.UK 数字入口", "代理机构应复核内部表格提交流程和培训材料", "流程数字化会影响提交留痕和合规操作习惯"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensures filings meet current UKIPO statutory requirements for digital submission.", "Practice note: Update internal protocols to route official form completions through GOV.UK.", "What to monitor: Future updates specifying which forms are mandated for online-only delivery."]
core_points_en: UKIPO publishes statutory guidance directing online completion of official forms.
Official form delivery is now integrated into the unified GOV.UK digital platform.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensures filings meet current UKIPO statutory requirements for digital submission.
Practice note: Update internal protocols to route official form completions through GOV.UK.
What to monitor: Future updates specifying which forms are mandated for online-only delivery.
