items.title: Withers & Rogers bets on Birmingham for new UK office
items.title_zh: Withers & Rogers押注伯明翰设立新英国办公室
items.title_en: Withers & Rogers Opens New Birmingham Office to Strengthen UK Regional Presence
items.summary: IP firm's new base will be located inside the tallest office space in the UK's ‘second city’
items.ai_summary_zh: 知识产权律所Withers & Rogers宣布在英国“第二城市”伯明翰设立新办公室。根据原文摘要及正文，该新基地将位于英国最高的办公空间内。原文未提供关于团队规模、具体业务范围或开业时间的更多细节。
items.ai_summary_en: Prominent intellectual property firm Withers & Rogers has announced the establishment of a new office in Birmingham, widely recognized as the UK's second city. The new base will be situated within the tallest office building in the region, signaling a significant commitment to expanding its legal service capabilities across the Midlands. This strategic move highlights the growing importance that top-tier IP firms place on regional markets beyond London. By physically expanding into key regional hubs, the firm aims to enhance client proximity and broaden its service coverage. This development serves as a notable indicator of the continued vitality and dynamic nature of the United Kingdom's intellectual property services market, reflecting broader trends of geographic diversification among leading legal providers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息极度有限，仅能确认律所的物理扩张动作。需持续观察该办公室后续公布的具体人员配置、业务侧重领域以及对英国中部地区IP服务市场格局的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should note the trend of major IP firms decentralizing from London to regional hubs like Birmingham. Establishing or partnering with firms possessing broad geographic coverage can significantly reduce communication barriers and management costs for clients operating across multiple jurisdictions. Selecting legal partners with robust regional networks ensures more timely and consistent support for global IP portfolios. This approach facilitates better alignment between local legal realities and corporate strategy, ultimately enhancing overall compliance efficiency and enforcement effectiveness across diverse European and UK markets.

items.title: Patents are getting pricier – and policy is the reason
items.title_zh: 专利成本攀升：政策驱动下的价值重塑
items.title_en: Patent Value Rises as UPC Enforcement Stakes Increase and US Challenges Decline
items.summary: Long Read: As PTAB challenges dry up and the UPC raises enforcement stakes, patents are regaining clout—pushing companies into earlier, costlier deals and reshaping what IP is worth
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，随着美国专利审判和上诉委员会（PTAB）挑战减少及统一专利法院（UPC）提高执法风险，专利影响力回升。这促使企业达成更早且成本更高的交易，并正在重塑知识产权的价值评估体系。文章将这一趋势归因于政策变化。
items.ai_summary_en: The intellectual property landscape is undergoing a significant shift driven by policy changes. With the decline of Patent Trial and Appeal Board challenges in the United States, patents are regaining their strategic clout. Simultaneously, the Unified Patent Court has raised enforcement stakes across Europe, increasing the potential costs and risks associated with infringement litigation. This dual dynamic is compelling market participants to engage in earlier and more expensive licensing negotiations to mitigate legal exposure. Consequently, the perceived value of patent portfolios is being reshaped, forcing companies to reassess their IP strategies. Rights holders must now navigate a complex environment where compliance pressures and acquisition costs are rising, requiring a more proactive approach to portfolio management and dispute resolution to maintain competitive advantage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC执法力度对全球专利许可谈判节奏的具体影响。鉴于正文缺失，仅能确认政策与执法环境变化是驱动专利成本上升的核心变量，具体费率结构或行业案例需待全文发布后进一步观察。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should proactively manage the heightened litigation risks introduced by the Unified Patent Court. Integrating freedom-to-operate analyses and initiating licensing discussions at earlier stages can help mitigate potential liabilities. It is crucial to monitor evolving European IP policies that impact maintenance costs and enforcement mechanisms. Companies should dynamically adjust their global IP strategies to align with these cost trends, ensuring robust compliance and sustained competitiveness. A strategic review of patent portfolios is recommended to optimize asset value while minimizing exposure to expensive enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["摘要显示PTAB挑战减少与UPC提升执法风险共同导致专利影响力回升", "摘要指出政策因素推动企业进入更早、成本更高的专利交易阶段", "摘要表明当前动态正在重塑市场对知识产权价值的认知与评估"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC enforcement increases litigation risks, restoring greater market influence and leverage to patent holders.", "Policy shifts significantly raise the costs associated with obtaining and maintaining patent rights globally.", "Companies face pressure to secure early, high-value licensing deals to avoid costly legal disputes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC执法风险上升可能改变跨国企业的专利组合管理策略", "政策导向下的专利价值重估可能导致早期许可交易成本增加", "需持续监测PTAB与UPC动态对全球专利定价机制的长期影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize early freedom-to-operate assessments and licensing negotiations to reduce UPC litigation exposure.", "Practice note: Monitor policy-driven cost increases and adapt global IP strategies to maintain compliance and competitiveness.", "What to monitor: Evolving UPC enforcement trends and European IP policy changes affecting patent valuation and maintenance."]
core_points_en: UPC enforcement increases litigation risks, restoring greater market influence and leverage to patent holders.
Policy shifts significantly raise the costs associated with obtaining and maintaining patent rights globally.
Companies face pressure to secure early, high-value licensing deals to avoid costly legal disputes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize early freedom-to-operate assessments and licensing negotiations to reduce UPC litigation exposure.
Practice note: Monitor policy-driven cost increases and adapt global IP strategies to maintain compliance and competitiveness.
What to monitor: Evolving UPC enforcement trends and European IP policy changes affecting patent valuation and maintenance.

items.title: Everyday IP: How Intellectual Property Powers The World Of Sports
items.title_zh: 日常知识产权：知识产权如何赋能体育世界
items.title_en: Everyday IP: How Intellectual Property Powers the Global Sports Industry
items.summary: From the roar of the crowd to the precision of a last-second goal, sport captivates billions worldwide. But behind every athletic achievement lies a complex web of innovation, branding, and technology—all powered by Intellectual Property. Discover how patents, trademarks, and copyrights shape the games we love, from basketball's humble beginnings to today's data-driven training systems.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文由Dennemeyer撰写，指出体育背后依赖复杂的创新、品牌和技术网络，均由知识产权驱动。文章以篮球和足球为例，说明专利保护装备创新，商标和外观设计权保护球队身份标识。文中提到2026年冬奥会等赛事将凸显体育与IP的联系，强调IP在塑造体育全球现象中的核心作用。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the critical role of intellectual property within the sports sector, framed by World Intellectual Property Day. It highlights how technological innovations, ranging from early basketball equipment to modern data-driven training systems, rely on patent protection to enhance competitive performance. Simultaneously, team logos and visual identities are secured through trademarks and design rights to establish distinct brand presence. With major global events like the 2026 Winter Olympics and FIFA World Cup approaching, IP serves as the essential infrastructure connecting athletic competition, commercial branding, and worldwide broadcasting. The analysis underscores that robust innovation and brand protection are fundamental to the successful globalization of sports enterprises.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文属律所一般性科普分析，旨在展示IP在体育产业的广泛应用。实务中需关注体育科技专利布局及品牌商标的全球注册策略，特别是在大型赛事举办前后的知识产权保护力度。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should prioritize comprehensive patent strategies for underlying sports technologies to mitigate infringement risks in this strict regulatory environment. Strengthening international trademark registration and leveraging design rights are crucial for building a robust brand moat. Companies are advised to integrate IP compliance into their product development and marketing workflows. This holistic approach ensures alignment with European legal standards while enhancing brand premium capabilities and safeguarding commercial interests against unauthorized use or counterfeiting.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["体育创新如篮球装备标准化及足球电子感应技术，依赖专利保护以提升性能与安全", "球队标志、颜色及吉祥物等身份标识，通过商标和外观设计权进行法律保护", "年冬奥会及世界杯等大型赛事，将进一步展示知识产权在体育商业化中的价值"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Sports equipment and technical innovations, such as smart footballs, depend on patent protection to elevate competitive standards.", "Team logos, colors, and visual elements utilize trademarks and design rights to construct a unique and recognizable brand identity.", "In large-scale event commercialization, intellectual property forms the foundational infrastructure for securing broadcasting rights and preserving brand value."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["体育科技企业应重视装备创新中的专利申请，确保护城河", "体育组织需强化商标与外观设计组合保护，维护品牌商业价值", "关注大型国际赛事期间的IP执法动态及品牌赞助权益保护"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Secure patent coverage for sports tech hardware to avoid infringement liabilities in the European market.", "Practice note: Implement rigorous international trademark registration and design right protections to build a strong brand defense.", "What to monitor: Evolving European enforcement trends regarding IP compliance in sports equipment and digital broadcasting sectors."]
core_points_en: Sports equipment and technical innovations, such as smart footballs, depend on patent protection to elevate competitive standards.
Team logos, colors, and visual elements utilize trademarks and design rights to construct a unique and recognizable brand identity.
In large-scale event commercialization, intellectual property forms the foundational infrastructure for securing broadcasting rights and preserving brand value.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure patent coverage for sports tech hardware to avoid infringement liabilities in the European market.
Practice note: Implement rigorous international trademark registration and design right protections to build a strong brand defense.
What to monitor: Evolving European enforcement trends regarding IP compliance in sports equipment and digital broadcasting sectors.

items.title: Saint Gobain and Splanemann defend smart glass patent at EPO
items.title_zh: 圣戈班与Splanemann在EPO捍卫智能玻璃专利
items.title_en: Saint-Gobain and Splanemann Defend Smart Glass Patent Validity at the EPO
items.summary: The market for innovations in speciality glass for the automotive industry is fiercely competitive. There have been several recent patent disputes in this industry, such as Fuyao Glass vs Saint Gobain and ACG concerning head-up displays and Sekisui Chemicals and Kuraray concerning special films for car windscreens. The current case revolves around Saint Gobain’s EP 3 774 417, […] The post Saint Gobain and Splanemann defend smart glass patent at EPO appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 汽车行业特种玻璃创新市场竞争激烈。圣戈班（Saint Gobain）与Splanemann正在欧洲专利局（EPO）就其欧洲专利EP 3 774 417进行辩护。该专利涉及智能玻璃技术。近期该领域已有福耀玻璃诉圣戈班等纠纷。
items.ai_summary_en: The automotive specialty glass sector is experiencing intense competition, marked by recent high-profile patent disputes involving major industry players. Notable conflicts include Fuyao Glass versus Saint-Gobain and ACG regarding head-up display technologies, as well as Sekisui Chemicals against Kuraray concerning special films for windshields. The current proceedings focus on European patent EP 3 774 417, held by Saint-Gobain. In this ongoing case, Saint-Gobain has joined forces with Splanemann to defend the patent’s validity before the European Patent Office (EPO). This legal action aims to uphold the intellectual property rights protecting smart glass technology. The dispute underscores the escalating strategic importance of IP protection in critical automotive components, reflecting a broader trend where leading companies actively litigate to secure their technological advantages and market positions in this rapidly evolving field.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注EPO对该智能玻璃专利有效性的最终裁定。鉴于汽车行业玻璃技术竞争加剧，相关专利的稳定性将影响市场准入。建议持续追踪该案在EPO异议部门的程序进展及引用对比文件情况。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the automotive supply chain should closely monitor the patent strategies of dominant holders like Saint-Gobain in Europe, particularly regarding smart glass and head-up display innovations. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses prior to product launch is essential to mitigate infringement risks. Companies should also evaluate potential responses to enforcement actions, such as pursuing cross-licensing agreements or initiating invalidation proceedings. Proactive IP management ensures business continuity and protects export operations from disruptive litigation threats in key European markets.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Saint-Gobain collaborates with Splanemann to actively defend the validity of its smart glass patent during EPO proceedings.", "Competition in automotive specialty glass is fierce, with frequent cross-border infringement and invalidation disputes emerging recently.", "Patent EP 3 774 417 is central to the case, highlighting the significant commercial value of advanced smart window technologies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EPO对EP 3 774 417专利有效性的审查结果", "监测汽车智能玻璃领域的专利冲突频率与趋势", "观察圣戈班在该技术分支的专利组合防御策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess Saint-Gobain's European patent barriers in smart glass to conduct pre-export freedom-to-operate checks.", "Practice note: Monitor EPO opposition outcomes and consider timely invalidation challenges to neutralize potential infringement liabilities.", "What to monitor: Strategic shifts in smart glass and HUD patent portfolios among leading automotive component suppliers."]

Competition in automotive specialty glass is fierce, with frequent cross-border infringement and invalidation disputes emerging recently.
Patent EP 3 774 417 is central to the case, highlighting the significant commercial value of advanced smart window technologies.

Practice note: Monitor EPO opposition outcomes and consider timely invalidation challenges to neutralize potential infringement liabilities.
What to monitor: Strategic shifts in smart glass and HUD patent portfolios among leading automotive component suppliers.

items.title: Moderna sued by CureVac, continuing covid jab maker’s highly eventful start to 2026
items.title_zh: CureVac起诉Moderna，延续其2026年多事之秋
items.title_en: CureVac Sues Moderna, Adding to Nine Patent Actions Against mRNA Pioneer in 2026
items.summary: The mRNA innovator has now been sued by nine (sets of) patent owners, including four this year.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，mRNA技术 innovator Moderna 已被 CureVac 起诉。这是 Moderna 面临的第九起（组）专利所有者诉讼，其中今年已有四起。该事件标志着 Moderna 在2026年开局阶段持续面临密集的法律挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: Moderna faces intensified legal pressure as CureVac initiates a new patent infringement lawsuit, marking a turbulent start to 2026 for the mRNA technology leader. This action brings the total number of distinct patent owners suing Moderna to nine, with four cases filed within the current year alone. The surge in litigation underscores the fiercely competitive intellectual property landscape surrounding mRNA vaccines and therapeutics. As multiple rights holders target Moderna’s portfolio, the company confronts significant challenges regarding its market position and the stability of its core patent assets. This accumulation of disputes highlights the strategic importance of robust IP defense and the high stakes involved in securing freedom to operate within this rapidly evolving biotechnological sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注 CureVac 诉讼的具体管辖法院及涉案专利范围。鉴于 Moderna 年内频繁涉诉，应持续监测其在全球主要市场的专利防御态势及潜在和解动向，特别是欧洲地区的司法进展。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the mRNA and broader biotechnology sectors should closely monitor the evolving patent landscapes in Europe and the US, particularly concerning core sequence claims and delivery system technologies. Conducting thorough Freedom to Operate (FTO) analyses is critical to identify and navigate high-risk patent thickets that could impede commercialization. Establishing proactive patent watch mechanisms allows companies to anticipate potential infringement risks and prepare defensive strategies. Furthermore, maintaining flexibility through cross-licensing negotiations or preparing invalidation arguments can provide essential leverage in resolving disputes and protecting long-term market access against aggressive enforcement by established rights holders.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CureVac 已对 Moderna 提起专利诉讼", "Moderna 目前共面临九起（组）专利所有者诉讼", "在今年（2026年）内，Moderna 已新增四起此类诉讼"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CureVac launches fresh patent infringement proceedings against Moderna, significantly escalating the company's ongoing legal difficulties in early 2026.", "Nine separate sets of patent owners have now sued Moderna, with four new cases filed this year indicating a sharp increase in litigation activity.", "Intense IP competition in the mRNA sector places substantial pressure on leading firms, exposing them to multi-party enforcement actions and validity challenges."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["监测 CureVac 诉讼的具体受理法院及主张权利基础", "观察 Moderna 应对密集专利诉讼的整体策略变化", "跟踪2026年新增诉讼对 Moderna 欧洲市场运营的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen FTO analysis for core mRNA technologies to navigate complex European and US patent barriers effectively.", "Practice note: Implement robust patent warning systems and prepare flexible strategies involving invalidation proceedings or cross-licensing agreements.", "What to monitor: Track emerging litigation trends and key patent grants in mRNA sequences and lipid nanoparticle delivery systems across major jurisdictions."]
core_points_en: CureVac launches fresh patent infringement proceedings against Moderna, significantly escalating the company's ongoing legal difficulties in early 2026.
Nine separate sets of patent owners have now sued Moderna, with four new cases filed this year indicating a sharp increase in litigation activity.
Intense IP competition in the mRNA sector places substantial pressure on leading firms, exposing them to multi-party enforcement actions and validity challenges.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen FTO analysis for core mRNA technologies to navigate complex European and US patent barriers effectively.
Practice note: Implement robust patent warning systems and prepare flexible strategies involving invalidation proceedings or cross-licensing agreements.
What to monitor: Track emerging litigation trends and key patent grants in mRNA sequences and lipid nanoparticle delivery systems across major jurisdictions.

items.title: Can AI be sued?
items.title_zh: 英国司法任务组咨询：AI致损责任如何适用普通法？
items.title_en: UK Jurisdiction Taskforce Consults on Applying English Private Law to AI Harm
items.summary: The UK Jurisdiction Taskforce (UKJT) is consulting on a Legal Statement exploring how English private law might deal with harm caused by AI systems. Th...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国司法任务组（UKJT）正就法律声明进行咨询，探讨英格兰私法如何处理AI系统造成的损害。鉴于缺乏专门的AI责任制度，UKJT旨在澄清现有普通法原则如何适用于AI引发的新颖情况，以消除法律不确定性，促进技术创新。该声明不提议新法，而是解释法院如何适应性地应用现有法律，为科技界提供明确的风险指引。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Jurisdiction Taskforce (UKJT) is consulting on a Legal Statement addressing how English private law handles harm caused by AI systems. The UKJT aims to provide legal certainty to the business and technology sectors, arguing that uncertainty regarding legal risk may hinder AI adoption and innovation. With no specific AI liability regimes in the UK, the statement seeks to clarify how existing common law principles apply to autonomous technologies. The UKJT emphasizes that its goal is not to propose new legislation but to explain how courts are likely to adapt established legal frameworks to address novel situations arising from AI development.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧AI企业需关注普通法对自主系统责任的解释趋势。虽无新法，但司法适应性可能带来不可预见的判例风险。建议企业完善内部合规与风险评估机制，积极参与咨询反馈，以影响最终法律立场的形成，确保业务模式符合潜在的责任认定标准。
items.ai_insight_en: This consultation highlights the UK's strategy of leveraging existing common law flexibility rather than introducing rigid, sector-specific AI liability statutes. For rights holders and tech companies operating in or targeting the UK market, understanding how courts may incrementally adapt tort and contract principles to autonomous agents is critical. The process offers an opportunity to shape the interpretation of legal risk, ensuring that business models align with evolving judicial approaches to AI accountability.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKJT咨询旨在澄清AI致损时英格兰普通法的适用规则", "无专门AI责任法，依赖灵活且成熟的普通法原则进行增量发展", "目标是为商业界提供法律确定性，消除对AI创新的法律风险顾虑"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UKJT consults on clarifying how English private law addresses harm caused by autonomous AI systems.", "No specific AI liability regimes exist in the UK, relying instead on flexible common law principles.", "The initiative aims to reduce legal uncertainty for businesses without proposing new statutory laws."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注普通法对AI自主性致损责任的司法适应趋势", "完善合规机制并参与咨询，以降低潜在法律风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies deploying AI should assess how current common law principles might assign liability for autonomous system errors.", "Practice note: Engaging with the consultation allows stakeholders to influence the final Legal Statement's interpretation of legal risk.", "What to monitor: Watch for the final Legal Statement to understand how English courts may adapt precedents to novel AI scenarios."]
core_points_en: The UKJT consults on clarifying how English private law addresses harm caused by autonomous AI systems.
No specific AI liability regimes exist in the UK, relying instead on flexible common law principles.
The initiative aims to reduce legal uncertainty for businesses without proposing new statutory laws.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies deploying AI should assess how current common law principles might assign liability for autonomous system errors.
Practice note: Engaging with the consultation allows stakeholders to influence the final Legal Statement's interpretation of legal risk.
What to monitor: Watch for the final Legal Statement to understand how English courts may adapt precedents to novel AI scenarios.

items.title: How ZEISS Strengthens Its Online Brand Protection And Trademark Searches With Questel
items.title_zh: 蔡司如何借助Questel强化在线品牌保护与商标检索
items.title_en: ZEISS Integrates Questel Solutions to Enhance Online Brand Protection and Trademark Search Efficiency
items.summary: Holger Gensmantel, Head of Trademarks, shares how Questel supports the brand defense strategy at Carl Zeiss AG, and the benefits of integrating online brand protection with advanced trademark search tools.
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国光学巨头蔡司（ZEISS）为应对日益复杂的在线侵权挑战，与Questel合作实施集成解决方案。通过结合在线品牌保护工具、Pliano案件管理系统及Markify ProSearch检索平台，蔡司实现了侵权行为的集中监测、优先排序及高效执法，同时优化了商标 clearance 搜索流程。此举显著提升了品牌防御效率，减少了行政负担，加速了日常商标运营决策，强化了其在全球数字渠道的品牌价值维护能力。
items.ai_summary_en: Carl Zeiss AG has partnered with Questel to implement an integrated suite of trademark management tools, addressing the growing complexity of online brand protection. By combining Questel’s Online Brand Protection solution with the Pliano case management system and Markify ProSearch, ZEISS centralizes the detection of online infringements across digital channels. This integration allows for the prioritization of threats and streamlined enforcement workflows, while simultaneously optimizing trademark search processes. The initiative aims to reduce administrative burdens on internal teams and accelerate decision-making in daily trademark operations, strengthening the company's overall brand defense strategy against misuse in global markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应重视数字化品牌资产管理，借鉴头部企业经验，引入自动化监测与智能检索工具。面对欧盟严格的知识产权执法环境，建立集中的案件管理系统有助于快速响应跨境侵权，降低合规风险，提升品牌出海的安全性与运营效率。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates the operational shift among major rights holders toward consolidating brand defense and prosecution tools within unified platforms. For European and US legal teams, integrating monitoring data directly into case management systems reduces fragmentation and improves response times to online infringements. items.title: How Technology Is Reshaping Patent Translation Operations
items.title_zh: 技术如何重塑专利翻译运营

items.summary: Patent translation and international filing are becoming increasingly complex as IP teams face rising cost pressure, growing global filing volumes, and the need to maintain both quality and operational efficiency across jurisdictions.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文介绍了一场关于利用技术优化专利翻译与国际申请流程的网络研讨会。随着全球申请量增加及成本压力上升，企业正从分散的邮件协调转向集中化、基于指令的工作流。SanDisk专家分享了如何通过标准化指令和批量决策，在确保法律准确性的同时降低行政负担并提升跨辖区一致性。
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧洲多语言环境使专利翻译成为关键合规环节。实务中应关注从人工协调向技术驱动工作流的转型趋势，重点监测集中化管理对降低行政负担及提升跨辖区一致性的实际效果，以及第三方验证在质量控制中的应用。

items.title: AVERA-IP Selects Questel’s Qthena To Enhance AI-Driven Patent Drafting And Prosecution Workflows
items.title_zh: AVERA-IP选用Questel Qthena增强AI驱动专利工作流
items.title_en: AVERA-IP Adopts Questel Qthena to Enhance AI-Driven Patent Drafting and Prosecution Workflows
items.summary: Questel, a leading provider of intellectual property (IP) and innovation management solutions, today announced that AVERA-IP has selected its Qthena solution to support and optimize its patent drafting and prosecution activities.
items.ai_summary_zh: 总部位于米兰的独立知识产权咨询公司AVERA-IP宣布选用Questel的Qthena解决方案，以支持并优化其专利撰写和审查活动。该方案利用人工智能辅助起草语言、审查审查意见及建议结构化回复，旨在减少重复性工作。AVERA-IP强调，所有法律技术评估、专业判断及战略决策仍由其律师和顾问完全控制，确保在提升效率的同时维持高质量标准。
items.ai_summary_en: Questel, a prominent provider of intellectual property and innovation management solutions based in France, has announced that AVERA-IP, an independent IP consultancy headquartered in Italy, has selected its Qthena platform. This strategic adoption aims to optimize patent drafting and prosecution workflows significantly. The Qthena solution utilizes advanced artificial intelligence to assist professionals with drafting language, reviewing office actions, and suggesting structured responses. These capabilities are designed to reduce repetitive administrative tasks while ensuring high standards of consistency across documents. Crucially, AVERA-IP emphasizes that all legal assessments, technical evaluations, and strategic decision-making remain strictly under the control of its attorneys and consultants. This approach ensures effective human-machine collaboration, maintaining compliance and professional judgment. By integrating these tools, the firm seeks to accelerate timelines and enhance the overall value of clients' innovation assets through more efficient and reliable IP processes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧洲专利服务机构正加速整合AI工具以提升撰写与审查效率，但明确界定“人机协作”边界，坚持人类专家对核心法律判断和战略决策的最终控制权。此趋势反映行业在追求生产力提升的同时，高度重视服务质量一致性及职业责任合规，需关注AI辅助工具在复杂案件中的实际效能与局限性。
items.ai_insight_en: European IP service providers are increasingly integrating AI into core workflows, signaling a broader industry shift toward automated assistance for drafting and prosecution. Market participants should evaluate how such tools impact cross-border patent strategies, particularly regarding efficiency gains in foreign filings. It is critical to establish robust human-in-the-loop review mechanisms to ensure outputs meet stringent European examination standards. While AI can accelerate initial drafts, reliance on unverified automated content poses compliance risks. Firms must balance technological adoption with rigorous quality control to maintain professional liability standards and achieve consistent results in diverse jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AVERA-IP选用Questel Qthena方案，利用AI辅助专利撰写与审查流程", "AI功能涵盖起草语言准备、审查意见分析及建议回复，旨在减少重复劳动", "firm强调律师保留对法律评估、专业判断及战略决策的完全控制权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["AVERA-IP selects Questel's Qthena AI tool to streamline and optimize complex patent drafting and prosecution operational workflows.", "AI assists with drafting and response generation, but final legal assessments and strategic decisions remain exclusively with human professionals.", "The initiative targets improved efficiency and document consistency, allowing teams to focus resources on high-value strategic tasks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧洲IP服务商采用AI辅助撰写已成为提升效率的行业实践方向", "机构明确区分AI辅助任务与人类核心判断，强调专业人员对战略决策的控制", "需持续观察AI工具在应对复杂审查意见及保持文档一致性方面的实际表现"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate AI-assisted drafting tools to potentially improve the speed and quality of overseas patent applications and portfolio management.", "Practice note: Implement strict human-machine collaborative review protocols to ensure all submissions comply with rigorous European patent examination standards.", "What to monitor: Track the evolving integration of AI in European IP services and assess its impact on cross-border layout efficiency and regulatory compliance."]
core_points_en: AVERA-IP selects Questel's Qthena AI tool to streamline and optimize complex patent drafting and prosecution operational workflows.
AI assists with drafting and response generation, but final legal assessments and strategic decisions remain exclusively with human professionals.
The initiative targets improved efficiency and document consistency, allowing teams to focus resources on high-value strategic tasks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate AI-assisted drafting tools to potentially improve the speed and quality of overseas patent applications and portfolio management.
Practice note: Implement strict human-machine collaborative review protocols to ensure all submissions comply with rigorous European patent examination standards.
What to monitor: Track the evolving integration of AI in European IP services and assess its impact on cross-border layout efficiency and regulatory compliance.

items.title: Carpmaels in Conversation: Ep. 41 - IP challenges in the age of social media - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 社交媒体时代的知识产权挑战
items.title_en: Carpmaels in Conversation: Ep. 41 - IP challenges in the age of social media - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.summary: Social media has reshaped how products are discovered, promoted and sold, creating new marketing opportunities for brand owners and unprecedented access for consumers. Platforms such as TikTok and Instagram have also changed how authenticity is perceived and valued, and influencer marketing has contributed to the growth of online counterfeiting and accelerated consumer demand for “dupes” […]
items.ai_summary_zh: Carpmaels & Ransford发布播客，探讨社交媒体如何重塑产品营销及真实性认知。重点分析网红营销助推在线仿冒品增长、“平替”文化兴起带来的法律边界模糊问题，以及升级再造对品牌声誉的影响。结合案例法与平台工具，讨论品牌方在数字环境中应对不正当竞争与执法的策略。
items.ai_insight_zh: 品牌方需关注社交媒体环境下“平替”文化与升级再造引发的商标侵权及不正当竞争风险。实务中应密切监测平台执法工具的有效性，并评估网红营销中的真实性披露合规性，以应对快速变化的数字市场环境。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["社交媒体与网红营销加剧了在线仿冒品增长及消费者对“平替”产品的需求", "合法营销与不正当竞争之间的法律界限日益模糊，带来合规挑战", "升级再造（Upcycling）在为品牌带来奖励的同时，也构成了潜在的声誉风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注“平替”文化对商标显著性及混淆可能性的法律冲击", "监测社交平台针对仿冒品的执法工具更新及实际执行效果", "评估升级再造产品流通中的品牌声誉管理与知识产权边界"]

This trend reflects a strategic effort to balance the strict rigidity of legal frameworks with the need for equitable outcomes in specific disputes. For stakeholders in the European intellectual property landscape, these developments offer critical insights into the UPC's maturing judicial practice. Understanding these subtle shifts in rule application is essential for navigating the current patent litigation environment effectively, as the court continues to establish its distinct standards and operational norms.

items.title_zh: 人类定义与生物现实之辩（T 630/24）

items.summary: As the recent furore over claim interpretation and description amendments illustrates, patent attorneys love nothing more than to argue about the meaning of words. As we know, words are contextual things, and can shift their meaning depending on the words around them and the perspective of the reader. Definitions are particularly unstable in biological sciences, where terminology is notoriously
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评述EPO上诉委员会案例T 630/24，探讨生物学科学中术语定义的不稳定性。指出专利代理人常就词语含义争论，而词义随语境及读者视角变化，尤其在生物科学领域，定义往往难以固定。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines EPO Board of Appeal decision T 630/24, focusing on the stability of terminology definitions within biotechnology patents. The article highlights that scientific terms in biology often lack uniform standards, causing claim interpretation to depend heavily on context and the reader's perspective. The decision emphasizes the necessity of balancing rigorous legal definitions with the objective realities of biological science. This approach provides significant guidance for drafting patent applications and managing description amendments during subsequent examination proceedings, ensuring clarity amidst evolving scientific understanding.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注EPO在生物技术案件中对模糊术语的解释倾向。鉴于生物学术语的多义性，申请人在撰写时应注意定义的清晰度，以应对潜在的解释争议。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should proactively assess the generality of biological terms to avoid ambiguous expressions that trigger examination risks. It is advisable to clearly define key biological terms within the specification to meet strict EPO examination standards. Additionally, monitoring similar precedents allows for optimizing claim layouts, thereby reducing the risk of rejection or invalidation caused by vague definitions. This strategic approach ensures robust protection while navigating the complex intersection of legal language and biological facts.

items.title: The forum of choice? Energy at the UPC
items.title_zh: 首选论坛？能源行业的UPC诉讼趋势
items.title_en: The Forum of Choice? How the UPC Is Becoming the Preferred Venue for Energy Sector Patent Disputes
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文正文缺失，仅依据标题判断。标题显示该文章探讨统一专利法院（UPC）是否成为能源行业专利争议的首选诉讼地。来源为IAM Media，属于对UPC在特定技术领域应用情况的观察或评论。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines how the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has emerged as a preferred venue for resolving patent disputes within the energy sector. As the court’s operations mature, its ability to deliver cross-border rulings and specialized judicial expertise is attracting significant attention from major energy enterprises. The article evaluates current litigation trends, highlighting the efficiency advantages the UPC offers in handling complex cross-border infringement cases compared to traditional national proceedings. Furthermore, it discusses the potential impact of these developments on the competitive landscape of the European energy technology market. For rights holders and market participants, understanding the strategic implications of UPC litigation is increasingly critical. Companies must adapt their intellectual property strategies to navigate this evolving environment effectively, ensuring they are well-positioned to manage risks and leverage procedural efficiencies in an era of intensified IP competition across Europe.

items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the European energy sector should closely monitor UPC case law developments to assess risks associated with their existing patent portfolios. This approach helps protect market share and prevents passive positioning in cross-border disputes, ensuring robust defense and offense capabilities in the unified patent system.

items.title: Renewed reshuffle in the PV industry driven by IP enforcement
items.title_zh: 知识产权执法驱动光伏行业新一轮重组
items.title_en: European PV Sector Faces Structural Reshuffle Driven by Intensified IP Enforcement
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文正文及摘要缺失，仅依据标题判断：IAM Media报道指出，光伏（PV）行业正经历由知识产权执法驱动的新一轮重组。未提供具体涉案主体、法域或执法细节。
items.ai_summary_en: IAM Media reports that the European photovoltaic industry is undergoing a significant structural reorganization driven by heightened intellectual property enforcement activities. The increasing frequency of patent infringement litigation and the strategic use of injunctive relief are fundamentally altering the competitive landscape. This trend highlights how rights holders are leveraging robust IP execution strategies to consolidate market positions and eliminate competitors. For market participants, these developments underscore the critical importance of navigating strict intellectual property protections in technology-intensive sectors. Companies must adapt their commercial strategies to address the pressures of market integration and legal challenges, ensuring resilience against aggressive enforcement actions that are reshaping industry dynamics across Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 证据不足模式：需关注后续披露的具体执法案例、涉及的法域（如UPC或成员国法院）以及受影响的主要市场参与者。目前无法评估对许可费率或供应链的具体影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the European photovoltaic sector should prioritize comprehensive freedom-to-operate assessments to mitigate litigation risks. Establishing robust monitoring systems for patent landscapes allows companies to anticipate potential infringement claims and secure supply chain stability. Proactive legal strategies, including preparing for invalidation proceedings or negotiating cross-licenses, are essential for managing disputes effectively. By integrating these defensive measures, businesses can better navigate the complex regulatory environment, protect market access, and maintain operational continuity amidst intensifying IP enforcement trends.

items.title: When a creator loses the right to their own name: a brief reflection on the Jo Malone case
items.title_zh: 创作者丧失姓名权？Jo Malone案反思
items.title_en: Jo Malone Dispute: When Contractual Limits Restrict a Creator's Use of Their Own Name
items.summary: The recent dispute involving British perfumer Jo Malone brings to light an interesting legal question: is it possible for someone to be prevented from using their own name in commercial activities?
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国调香师Jo Malone将品牌售予雅诗兰黛后，因在新品牌“Jo Loves”与Inditex合作中使用“created by Jo Malone”字样引发诉讼。雅诗兰黛指控其违反合同限制并构成假冒。文章指出，尽管姓名属人格权，但作为商标转让后，其商业使用权受合同约束。在特定领域使用原姓名可能产生不当竞争优势，损害原品牌价值，故受限部分仅为商业用途而非民事身份识别。
items.ai_summary_en: The recent litigation between Estée Lauder and perfumer Jo Malone highlights the tension between personal name rights and commercial trademark assignments. After selling her original brand, Malone faced legal action for using 'created by Jo Malone' in promotions for a new venture with Inditex. The case underscores that while personality rights protect civil identity, contractual agreements can strictly limit the commercial use of a founder's name in specific sectors to prevent consumer confusion and protect brand equity.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在收购海外品牌时，需审慎拟定创始人姓名使用条款，明确商业与非商业使用的界限。若创始人保留同名新业务，应评估潜在侵权风险，避免通过暗示关联获取不当竞争优势，以防陷入高额诉讼及商誉受损困境。
items.ai_insight_en: This dispute illustrates the critical importance of precise drafting in acquisition agreements regarding founder names. Rights holders must clearly define the scope of permitted post-sale usage to avoid ambiguity. For practitioners, the case serves as a reminder that personal name rights do not automatically override explicit contractual waivers in commercial contexts, particularly where brand dilution or consumer deception is alleged.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["姓名作为商标转让后，其商业使用权受合同约定严格限制", "在竞争产品上使用原姓名可能构成商标侵权或市场假冒行为", "限制仅针对商业领域的商标性使用，不影响个人的民事姓名权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Assigning a personal name as a trademark transfers economic rights and imposes contractual restrictions on future commercial use.", "Using a former founder's name in competing products may constitute trademark infringement or passing off under UK law.", "Contractual limitations apply specifically to commercial activities, leaving the individual free to use their name for civil identification."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["并购时需明确界定创始人姓名的商业使用边界与违约责任", "避免利用原品牌声誉进行误导性宣传，防范不正当竞争风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Acquirers should secure comprehensive rights to founder names and define strict boundaries for any retained personal usage.", "Practice note: Drafting must distinguish between civil identity use and commercial trademark exploitation to mitigate breach of contract risks.", "What to monitor: Observe how UK courts balance personality rights against contractual obligations in high-profile creator disputes."]
core_points_en: Assigning a personal name as a trademark transfers economic rights and imposes contractual restrictions on future commercial use.
Using a former founder's name in competing products may constitute trademark infringement or passing off under UK law.
Contractual limitations apply specifically to commercial activities, leaving the individual free to use their name for civil identification.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Acquirers should secure comprehensive rights to founder names and define strict boundaries for any retained personal usage.
Practice note: Drafting must distinguish between civil identity use and commercial trademark exploitation to mitigate breach of contract risks.
What to monitor: Observe how UK courts balance personality rights against contractual obligations in high-profile creator disputes.

items.title: Start to finish: Marathon milestone highlights IP’s role in sport
items.title_zh: 始末回顾：马拉松里程碑凸显知识产权在体育中的作用
items.title_en: Marathon Milestones Highlight Intellectual Property's Critical Role in Modern Sports
items.summary: Millions watched marathon world records get smashed on the same weekend as the IP community celebrated ‘IP in sport’ for World IP Day. Marisa Woutersen asked FIFA, the UKIPO, the Copyright Alliance and sports IP lawyers to reflect on the theme.
items.ai_summary_zh: 适逢世界知识产权日，社区庆祝“体育中的知识产权”主题。同期马拉松世界纪录被打破。作者采访了国际足联、英国知识产权局、版权联盟及体育知识产权律师，请其就该主题发表看法。
items.ai_summary_en: Coinciding with the breaking of multiple marathon world records, the global intellectual property community observed World IP Day under the theme 'IP in Sport.' This commentary synthesizes perspectives from key industry stakeholders, including FIFA, the UK Intellectual Property Office, the Copyright Alliance, and specialized sports IP attorneys. The discussion focuses on the evolving landscape of protecting and valuing intellectual property within major sporting events. It underscores the pivotal function of IP rights in securing commercial interests, combating piracy, and safeguarding brand integrity. The article reflects broader industry attention on the complexities of the sports IP ecosystem, emphasizing how robust legal frameworks are essential for sustaining the economic viability and reputational security of contemporary athletic competitions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文仅为世界知识产权日的主题综述与多方观点集锦，未提供具体法律分析或案例细节。需关注英国知识产权局及行业组织后续是否发布关于体育版权保护的具体政策指引或执法重点。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants must prioritize compliance regarding event broadcasting rights and sponsorship boundaries to mitigate infringement risks. Establishing comprehensive global IP monitoring mechanisms is essential to detect and prevent unauthorized commercial exploitation. Rights holders should study established frameworks, such as those employed by international federations, to optimize copyright layouts for event content. Strengthening these legal foundations enhances brand protection levels in international markets and improves commercial monetization capabilities through secure licensing and enforcement strategies.

items.title: UPC Engages With Prosecution History In Claim Interpretation
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院在权利要求解释中参考审查历史
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Division Consults Prosecution History in Claim Interpretation
items.summary: A recent decision from the Düsseldorf Local Division in UPC_CFI_351/2024 saw the Court consulted the prosecution history to resolve a question in claim interpretation. Most European courts do not rely...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院杜塞尔多夫地方法院在案件UPC_CFI_351/2024中，为澄清权利要求术语含义而查阅了专利审查历史。尽管欧洲通常不承认禁止反悔原则，但法院指出审查期间的评论可反映本领域技术人员认知。法院据此驳回被告关于术语被重新定义的追加事项攻击，维持专利有效。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Düsseldorf Local Division examined prosecution history in case UPC_CFI_351/2024 to resolve a claim interpretation dispute. Although European courts generally do not apply prosecution history estoppel, the defendant argued that the patentee redefined “bottom side” as “bottom plane” during Rule 71(3) EPC proceedings. The Court reviewed the file wrapper and determined the patentee had actually argued against such a narrow interpretation. Consequently, the Court rejected the added matter attack and upheld the patent. This decision illustrates that while the UPC primarily relies on claims, description, and drawings, it may consult prosecution records to understand the skilled person’s perspective and verify assertions regarding term definitions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案显示UPC在特定情形下会实质性接触审查档案以辅助解释，虽未确立禁止反悔原则，但表明审查过程中的陈述可能影响法院对技术术语及专利有效性的判断，需关注此类解释路径的后续适用。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling signals that the UPC may engage with file wrapper arguments despite the general absence of prosecution history estoppel in Europe. Rights holders should ensure consistency between prosecution responses and litigation positions, as the Court may use these records to assess the skilled person’s understanding. While not establishing strict estoppel, this approach allows opponents to challenge claim scope using prior statements. Practitioners must prepare for potential scrutiny of prosecution files in both infringement and validity proceedings at the UPC.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["杜塞尔多夫地方法院在UPC_CFI_351/2024案中查阅审查历史以解决权利要求解释…", "法院认定专利权人在EPC规则71(3)通知回复中未将“底侧”重新定义为“底平面”", "基于审查历史记录，法院驳回被告的追加事项攻击并维持涉案专利有效性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Düsseldorf Local Division consulted prosecution history to interpret claim terms in UPC_CFI_351/2024.", "Court rejected defendant’s argument that patentee redefined “bottom side” during EPC Rule 71(3) proceedings.", "Patent validity upheld as Court found no added matter based on the prosecution record."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC在权利要求解释中开始有限度地参考审查历史，而非仅依赖说明书和附图", "审查期间的意见陈述可能被用于反驳对方关于术语重新定义或追加事项的指控", "需持续观察UPC后续判例是否扩大审查历史在侵权或无效程序中的证据效力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prosecution statements may influence UPC claim interpretation, requiring alignment between filing strategies and enforcement positions.", "Practice note: Courts may review file wrappers to verify assertions about term definitions, even without formal prosecution history estoppel.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions to determine if reliance on prosecution history expands beyond clarifying the skilled person’s state of mind."]
core_points_en: Düsseldorf Local Division consulted prosecution history to interpret claim terms in UPC_CFI_351/2024.
Court rejected defendant’s argument that patentee redefined “bottom side” during EPC Rule 71(3) proceedings.
Patent validity upheld as Court found no added matter based on the prosecution record.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prosecution statements may influence UPC claim interpretation, requiring alignment between filing strategies and enforcement positions.
Practice note: Courts may review file wrappers to verify assertions about term definitions, even without formal prosecution history estoppel.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions to determine if reliance on prosecution history expands beyond clarifying the skilled person’s state of mind.

items.title: Help us UK Supreme Court, you’re our only hope (for digital replicas)
items.title_zh: 求助英国最高法院：数字复制品的唯一希望
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Appeal: Unjust Enrichment and Digital Replicas Take Center Stage
items.summary: Frames from Star Wars: A New Hope and Rogue One: A Star Wars Story - LucasfilmWho would have thought that Princess Leia’s ‘holographic’ plea in Star Wars: Episode IV – A New Hope would set the scene for an epic legal argument. Very quietly, over the last three years, a storm has been brewing over the interpretation of unjust enrichment in the UK. Culminating in an application for appeal to the
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出，过去三年英国关于不当得利解释的法律争议逐渐发酵，现已 culminating（达到高潮/最终阶段）为一项向英国最高法院提出的上诉申请。该争议背景与《星球大战》中莱娅公主的全息影像情节相关联，核心聚焦于数字复制品（digital replicas）引发的法律问题。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary examines a significant legal dispute developing in the United Kingdom over the past three years, focusing on the interpretation of unjust enrichment principles. The core controversy specifically addresses the use of digital replicas and their implications for copyright law. The case has now progressed to an application for appeal before the UK Supreme Court, representing a critical opportunity to resolve complex issues regarding compensation for new forms of intellectual property infringement. Using the iconic holographic message from Star Wars as a metaphor, the article underscores the potential far-reaching impact of the court’s decision. It highlights how the final judgment will shape future copyright protection frameworks and influence the calculation of damages for digital content, urging the highest court to provide clear and definitive legal guidance for the industry.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注英国最高法院如何界定数字复制品语境下的不当得利原则。此上诉结果可能为肖像权、表演者权及版权交叉领域提供关键判例指引，影响数字内容创作与使用的合规边界。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating digital platforms, software services, or AI-driven content generation should closely monitor this proceeding. A stricter judicial interpretation of unjust enrichment could significantly elevate exposure to infringement liabilities and increase potential compensation awards. Rights holders and licensees are advised to conduct thorough compliance reviews to assess these emerging risks. Furthermore, it is prudent to incorporate specific risk allocation mechanisms into licensing negotiations to mitigate potential financial impacts. Proactive assessment of liability exposure and robust contractual safeguards will be essential for navigating the evolving landscape of digital IP enforcement and avoiding substantial punitive consequences.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注最高法院对数字复制品不当得利认定的裁判逻辑", "观察该判例对影视、游戏行业数字形象使用的潜在影响", "追踪后续判决是否确立新的数字版权侵权赔偿计算标准"]

The case has reached the UK Supreme Court via appeal, aiming to establish new standards for calculating damages in digital copyright infringement disputes.
The anticipated ruling will profoundly influence copyright enforcement strategies and available remedies for the European digital content industry.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the UK Supreme Court's evolving jurisprudence on unjust enrichment concerning digital replicas to assess infringement liability exposure.
Practice note: Strengthen internal compliance protocols and evaluate potential damages risks for digital business operations within the UK jurisdiction.
What to monitor: Develop flexible licensing strategies that account for heightened compensation risks and potential shifts in copyright remedy calculations.

items.title: Five reasons why courts should not set rates for patent pools
items.title_zh: 评论：法院不应设定专利池费率的五大理由
items.title_en: Opinion: Five Reasons Why Courts Should Not Set Rates for Patent Pools
items.summary: Saturday opinion: A court-set pool rate might sound fair in theory, but in practice it could destabilise patent pools and slow the spread of standardised technology
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文是一篇观点评论，主张法院在理论上设定的专利池费率虽看似公平，但在实践中可能破坏专利池的稳定性。文章指出，司法干预定价机制会阻碍标准化技术的广泛传播与采用。作者列举了五个主要理由，强调应尊重市场机制和权利人与被许可人之间的商业谈判，而非依赖司法裁决来确定许可费率，以维护创新生态的健康发展。
items.ai_summary_en: This opinion piece argues that while court-determined patent pool rates may appear equitable in theory, they risk destabilizing the structural integrity of pools and impeding the widespread adoption of standardized technology. The author outlines five key reasons against judicial intervention in rate-setting, emphasizing that market mechanisms are better suited to determine reasonable licensing terms. This commentary highlights growing concerns within the European intellectual property community regarding excessive judicial involvement in commercial licensing negotiations, particularly for standard-essential patents (SEPs). It underscores the critical need to balance the interests of rights holders with the broader goal of facilitating efficient technology dissemination through voluntary, market-driven agreements rather than imposed legal mandates.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业参与欧洲标准制定时，应警惕司法定价风险。建议优先通过商业谈判达成许可协议，避免陷入诉讼僵局。同时，密切关注欧盟关于FRAND许可的最新政策动向，建立灵活的知识产权合规与应对策略，以降低海外运营的法律成本与市场准入障碍。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies engaged in SEP licensing within Europe, reliance on commercial negotiation remains preferable to seeking or resisting judicial rate-setting. Market participants should actively leverage market dynamics to secure favorable licensing terms, avoiding passive acceptance of court-imposed outcomes. Strengthening internal patent portfolio management is essential to enhance bargaining power during discussions. Additionally, stakeholders must remain vigilant regarding evolving EU policies and judicial trends that could expand court authority over patent pool fees, as such shifts may introduce significant compliance risks and alter the competitive landscape for technology licensing across the region.

items.title: Comment sécuriser ou céder ses droits sur une œuvre créée à l’aide d’une intelligence artificielle ?
items.title_zh: 如何保障或转让AI生成作品的权利？
items.title_en: Comment sécuriser ou céder ses droits sur une œuvre créée à l’aide d’une intelligence artificielle ?
items.summary: Introduction L’intégration de l’intelligence artificielle dans les processus créatifs redéfinit les contours classiques du droit d’auteur. Dès lors qu’une œuvre est générée, en tout ou partie, par un outil algorithmique, une interrogation essentielle s’impose : comment sécuriser juridiquement cette création et en organiser la cession des droits ? En droit

items.title: Crystal Bar: Contempt application against litigant and its lawyers struck out
items.title_zh: Crystal Bar案：针对当事人及律师的藐视法庭申请被驳回
items.title_en: UK Court Strikes Out Contempt Application Against Litigant and Lawyers in Crystal Bar Trademark Dispute
items.summary: This Kat has covered the dispute between Shenzhen SKE Technology Ltd (SKE) and Bargain Busting Ltd (BBL) previously (IPKat here). It is a case that perhaps ought to be relatively simple, in that it is a battle over who has the earliest rights to the words CRYSTAL BAR in relation to vapes. However, it has become something of an attritional war between the two parties, and more
items.ai_summary_zh: 深圳SKE科技公司与Bargain Busting公司就电子烟产品“CRYSTAL BAR”字样最早权利归属发生争议。该案已演变为消耗战。最新进展显示，针对一方当事人及其律师提出的藐视法庭申请已被法院驳回。
items.ai_summary_en: This case involves a dispute between Shenzhen SKE Technology Ltd and Bargain Busting Ltd regarding the earliest rights to the trademark CRYSTAL BAR for vaping products. Although the core issue appears straightforward, the litigation has evolved into an attritional conflict characterized by intense procedural maneuvering. The latest development sees the court formally striking out an application for contempt of court brought against one of the parties and its legal representatives. This outcome highlights the complexities of high-stakes intellectual property litigation in the United Kingdom, where aggressive tactical moves can lead to significant procedural setbacks. The report analyzes how such disputes often extend beyond substantive trademark rights to encompass broader strategic battles over compliance and conduct within the judicial process.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了商标优先权争议在诉讼中可能异化为程序性对抗的风险。藐视法庭申请被驳回表明法院对程序滥用的审查趋严。需关注后续实体审理中关于“最早使用”证据的认定标准及诉讼成本分担情况。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in UK intellectual property disputes should exercise caution regarding procedural conduct to avoid allegations of contempt or other sanctions. Aggressive litigation tactics may backfire, leading to increased costs and potential adverse rulings on procedural grounds. It is advisable to engage experienced local counsel who can ensure strict adherence to civil procedure rules while focusing resources on substantiating core trademark ownership claims. Maintaining professional standards throughout the proceedings helps mitigate compliance risks and prevents unnecessary diversion of efforts into protracted procedural conflicts that do not directly advance the substantive merits of the case.

items.title: Examen DHS : comprendre les exigences techniques du CPVO et de l’INOV ?
items.title_zh: DHS审查：理解CPVO与INOV的技术要求
items.title_en: Understanding CPVO and INOV Technical Requirements for DUS Examination in Plant Variety Rights
items.summary: Introduction L’obtention d’un certificat d’obtention végétale (COV) qu’il soit national ou communautaire repose intégralement sur la démonstration de trois critères biologiques fondamentaux : la distinction, l’homogénéité et la stabilité. Ces critères, désignés par l’acronyme DHS en français (ou DUS en anglais : Distinctness, Uniformity, Stability), constituent le socle technique sur
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，获取国家或共同体植物新品种证书（COV）完全依赖于证明三个基本生物标准：特异性、一致性和稳定性（法语缩写DHS，英语缩写DUS）。这些标准构成了CPVO和INOV技术审查的基础。
items.ai_summary_en: Securing a plant variety right, whether at the national or Community level, fundamentally depends on demonstrating three biological criteria: Distinctness, Uniformity, and Stability (DUS). These standards form the technical foundation for legal protection. This analysis examines the specific technical requirements and procedural details applied by the Community Plant Variety Office (CPVO) and the French National Institute for Seeds and Seedlings (INOV) during examination. It clarifies how applicants can navigate biological testing to meet statutory authorization conditions, ensuring smoother approval processes for variety rights applications across relevant jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文仅提供了DHS/DUS标准的定义性介绍，未展开具体审查流程或案例。需关注后续是否发布关于CPVO与法国INOV在具体作物测试方法上的差异分析，以及审查实践的最新动态。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants seeking protection in Europe must prioritize strict adherence to CPVO DUS technical standards. Conducting preliminary tests against official guidelines helps verify that variety traits are stable and sufficiently distinct from existing varieties. Maintaining comprehensive breeding records is critical to substantiate claims during examination and mitigate rejection risks due to insufficient technical data. Proactive compliance ensures robust intellectual property positioning and reduces the likelihood of costly delays or refusals in the authorization process.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["植物新品种证书（COV）的获取依赖于证明特异性、一致性和稳定性三个生物标准", "上述标准在法语中称为DHS，在英语中称为DUS，是技术审查的核心基础"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Plant variety right grants strictly rely on meeting the three fundamental biological standards of distinctness, uniformity, and stability.", "The CPVO and INOV enforce clear technical specifications and rigorous examination procedures for DUS testing compliance.", "Accurately understanding and satisfying these technical thresholds is essential for obtaining valid and enforceable variety rights protection."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需关注CPVO与成员国机构在DHS测试执行层面的具体协调机制", "观察不同法域下对特异性、一致性、稳定性证据要求的细微差别", "监测植物新品种审查指南的更新及其对申请策略的潜在影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Exporters and breeders targeting the EU market must strictly follow CPVO DUS technical examination standards to secure rights.", "Practice note: Conduct pre-testing against official guidelines and maintain detailed breeding records to mitigate authorization risks.", "What to monitor: Evolving technical guidance from CPVO and INOV regarding DUS testing protocols and evidence requirements."]
core_points_en: Plant variety right grants strictly rely on meeting the three fundamental biological standards of distinctness, uniformity, and stability.
The CPVO and INOV enforce clear technical specifications and rigorous examination procedures for DUS testing compliance.
Accurately understanding and satisfying these technical thresholds is essential for obtaining valid and enforceable variety rights protection.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Exporters and breeders targeting the EU market must strictly follow CPVO DUS technical examination standards to secure rights.
Practice note: Conduct pre-testing against official guidelines and maintain detailed breeding records to mitigate authorization risks.
What to monitor: Evolving technical guidance from CPVO and INOV regarding DUS testing protocols and evidence requirements.

items.title: Article 3(d) of the SPC regulation: CJEU referral questions meaning…
items.title_zh: 德联邦专利法院就SPC法规第3(d)条向CJEU提请预裁
items.title_en: German Court Refers Question on Veterinary SPCs and 'First Authorisation' to CJEU
items.summary: The German Federal Patent Court has referred to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) a question on the interpretation of Article 3(d) of the…
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国联邦专利法院就SPC法规第3(d)条中“首次上市许可”的定义向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请预裁。案件源于勃林格殷格翰因活性成分环索奈德已有人类用药许可而被DPMA驳回兽药SPC申请。争议焦点在于：若同一活性成分已获人类药物授权，其兽药营销授权是否仍构成SPC意义上的“首次授权”。此问题直接关联Santen案判例对Neurim案的背离，将决定跨物种或新适应症药物的专利延长期限资格。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Federal Patent Court has referred a question to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) regarding Article 3(d) of the Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) Regulation. The referral concerns whether a marketing authorisation for a veterinary medicinal product constitutes the 'first authorisation' if the active ingredient was previously authorised for human use. This follows the German Patent and Trade Mark Office's rejection of Boehringer Ingelheim's SPC application for ciclesonide, citing the CJEU's Santen decision. The case seeks clarification on whether the Santen ruling precludes SPCs for veterinary applications of known human drugs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕欧洲SPC审查趋严，特别是针对已知活性成分的新适应症或新剂型开发。若核心成分已在欧获批，后续衍生产品的SPC申请可能因非“首次授权”被拒。建议企业在研发早期进行FTO分析，评估SPC获取可能性，并考虑通过布局新晶型、新制剂等外围专利构建替代保护策略，以弥补SPC缺失带来的市场独占期损失。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral highlights ongoing uncertainty in interpreting 'first authorisation' for products with cross-species or multi-indication histories. While Santen narrowed the scope for new therapeutic uses, the specific interplay between human and veterinary authorisations remains contested. Rights holders should track this development closely, as the outcome will define the eligibility criteria for SPCs in sectors where active ingredients are repurposed across different regulatory domains.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德联邦专利法院提请CJEU解释SPC法规第3(d)条关于“首次上市许可”的定义", "争议焦点为：已有人类药物授权的活性成分，其兽药授权能否视为SPC首次授权", "此案结果将澄清Santen案后兽药与新适应症SPC申请的审查标准与法律边界"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The German Federal Patent Court asks if a veterinary marketing authorisation is the 'first authorisation' under Article 3(d) when the active ingredient was previously authorised f…", "Boehringer Ingelheim sought an SPC for its veterinary respiratory product Aservo EquiHaler, but the DPMA refused based on the earlier human authorisation for Alvesco.", "The DPMA applied the CJEU's Santen ruling, which established that a new therapeutic application does not constitute a first authorisation if the active ingredient was already auth…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["已知活性成分新应用面临SPC授予障碍，需提前评估合规风险", "建议布局外围专利以弥补SPC缺失，确保持续市场竞争力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies developing veterinary medicines from known human active ingredients face potential SPC barriers if prior human authorisations exist.", "Practice note: Applicants must carefully assess the chronological order of all marketing authorisations for an active ingredient when planning SPC strategies in Europe.", "What to monitor: The CJEU's eventual ruling will clarify whether the Santen logic strictly applies to distinct regulatory pathways such as human versus veterinary markets."]
core_points_en: The German Federal Patent Court asks if a veterinary marketing authorisation is the 'first authorisation' under Article 3(d) when the active ingredient was previously authorised f…
Boehringer Ingelheim sought an SPC for its veterinary respiratory product Aservo EquiHaler, but the DPMA refused based on the earlier human authorisation for Alvesco.
The DPMA applied the CJEU's Santen ruling, which established that a new therapeutic application does not constitute a first authorisation if the active ingredient was already auth…
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies developing veterinary medicines from known human active ingredients face potential SPC barriers if prior human authorisations exist.
Practice note: Applicants must carefully assess the chronological order of all marketing authorisations for an active ingredient when planning SPC strategies in Europe.
What to monitor: The CJEU's eventual ruling will clarify whether the Santen logic strictly applies to distinct regulatory pathways such as human versus veterinary markets.

items.title_en: Unified Patent Court: indirect infringement and preliminary…
items.summary: Two recent cases from the Munich Local Division of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) relate to indirect infringement, leading to the grant of a preliminary…
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院慕尼黑地方法院在两起近期案件中裁定授予临时禁令，重点涉及间接侵权认定。在Hand Held Products诉Scandit案中，法院认为软件开发包（SDK）虽可能用于制造侵权产品，但缺乏直接侵权所需的确定性，故不构成直接侵权，但需结合具体事实评估间接侵权责任及禁令范围。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC对软件工具类间接侵权的审查门槛，强调直接侵权需具备高度确定性。实务中需关注推广材料等证据对“应知”状态的证明力，以及法院在平衡禁令范围时的裁量逻辑，这对涉及组件或开发工具的专利维权具有参考意义。

items.title: Advocate General in C 456/24 Halozyme: The MA has the final say on what constitutes an “active ingredient” - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 总佐审官意见：上市许可决定SPC活性成分认定
items.title_en: Advocate General in C 456/24 Halozyme: The MA has the final say on what constitutes an “active ingredient” - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.summary: Following widespread litigation over Halozyme’s two SPC families for Herceptin Hylecta® and MabThera®, inconsistent decisions across Europe led to a much-needed referral in 2024 to Europe’s highest court, the CJEU. The key issue for the CJEU to grapple with is whether recombinant human hyaluronidase can be considered an “active ingredient” within the meaning of Article […]
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对Halozyme公司SPC申请争议，捷克最高行政法院向欧盟法院（CJEU）提请裁决。核心争议在于重组人透明质酸酶在上市许可（MA）中被列为辅料时，是否仍属SPC条例定义的“活性成分”。总佐审官Emiliou发表法律意见，主张应以MA中的分类为准，即明确列为辅料的物质不能视为活性成分。若采纳该意见，因缺乏首个上市许可，相关SPC申请将依据条例第3(d)条被驳回。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了以监管文件而非药理作用实质来界定SPC保护客体的倾向。药企需重新审视组合药物中非主要活性成分的SPC布局策略，注意MA申报分类对后续知识产权保护的锁定效应。需等待CJEU最终判决以确认该解释是否具有终局约束力，目前仅为总佐审官建议。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["总佐审官建议以上市许可（MA）文件中的物质分类作为判定SPC“活性成分”的最终依据", "若物质在MA中明确指定为辅料，则不能被认定为SPC条例意义上的活性成分", "捷克最高行政法院因无法解决法律适用分歧，就Halozyme案向CJEU提请初步裁决"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["上市许可（MA）中的物质分类对SPC申请具有决定性影响，企业需在注册阶段统筹IP策略", "总佐审官意见倾向于形式审查标准，可能限制通过外部证据主张辅料为活性成分的空间", "需密切关注CJEU最终判决是否采纳该意见，这将统一欧洲各国对SPC活性成分认定的司法实践"]

items.title: Le partage d’informations par le client avec une intelligence artificielle : l’absence de responsabilité de l’avocat et du conseil en propriété industrielle.
items.title_zh: 客户向AI分享信息：律师与IP顾问免责
items.title_en: Client Disclosure to AI: No Liability for Lawyers or IP Attorneys
items.summary: Introduction La circulation des données sensibles s’est accélérée, tout comme l’usage des outils d’IA générative. Une question se pose alors : les informations partagées avec une IA, puis avec un avocat ou un conseil, restent-elles vraiment confidentielles ? Si les échanges avec un professionnel du droit sont protégés, le recours
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅包含标题与引言片段。指出敏感数据流通与生成式AI使用加速，引发关于客户先与AI交互再与法律专业人士沟通时保密性的疑问。标题明确主张在此情形下律师及工业产权顾问不承担责任。
items.ai_summary_en: As the circulation of sensitive data accelerates alongside the widespread adoption of generative AI tools, a critical question arises regarding confidentiality. If information is first shared with an AI system and subsequently transmitted to a lawyer or industrial property consultant, does it remain truly confidential? While communications with legal professionals are protected by professional secrecy, this analysis highlights that such protection does not extend to information previously exposed via public AI platforms. The core argument posits that if a client voluntarily inputs data into a public AI tool, resulting in disclosure, the subsequent involvement of legal counsel does not restore confidentiality. Consequently, professionals bear no liability for negligence or breach of secrecy concerning information that was already made public by the client’s prior actions with AI systems.
items.ai_insight_zh: 证据不足模式：原文未提供法律依据或具体场景分析。需关注法国法律界如何界定AI介入对职业保密特权（secret professionnel）的影响，以及该免责结论的适用边界。
items.ai_insight_en: Companies operating in Europe must exercise extreme caution when utilizing public generative AI models for intellectual property matters. Inputting core technical details or sensitive business information into these tools before consulting qualified legal counsel risks rendering such data publicly available. This premature disclosure can fatally undermine patent novelty requirements or invalidate trade secret protections under EU law. Furthermore, rights holders cannot hold their legal advisors liable for the consequences of such self-inflicted disclosures. It is imperative to establish strict internal protocols prohibiting the use of public AI for sensitive data prior to obtaining professional legal advice to preserve IP rights and maintain enforceable confidentiality standards.

items.title: UK High Court sees an increase in new cases as tensions with UPC intensify
items.title_zh: 随着与UPC紧张加剧，英高等法院新案增加
items.title_en: UK High Court Patent Filings Rise as Jurisdictional Tensions with UPC Intensify
items.summary: In recent years, the UK High Court — like other national courts — had seen a decrease in newly filed patent cases, reaching an all-time low in 2022. However, last year, according to the UK civil justice statistics, the UK High Court recorded significantly more cases than in 2024. The rise from 44 to 58 […] The post UK High Court sees an increase in new cases as tensions with UPC intensify appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文显示，英国高等法院专利新案在2022年创历史新低后出现反弹。根据英国民司法统计数据，去年案件数从44件增至58件。标题指出这一增长背景是与统一专利法院（UPC）之间的紧张关系加剧。正文片段仅重复了上述数据对比，未提供详细原因分析。
items.ai_summary_en: After reaching an all-time low in 2022, the number of new patent cases filed in the UK High Court rebounded significantly last year, rising to 58 filings. This increase marks a reversal of previous downward trends and occurs against the backdrop of the Unified Patent Court's (UPC) operational expansion. The data suggests that litigants are actively recalibrating their forum selection strategies amidst growing jurisdictional competition between national courts and the UPC. As the UPC's influence grows, the UK judicial system appears to be leveraging its procedural efficiency and specific legal advantages to maintain its position as a preferred venue for international patent disputes. This dynamic highlights intensifying competition for high-value intellectual property litigation within Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注英国法院与UPC之间管辖权竞争或策略选择对案件流向的影响。目前材料仅呈现数据变化与宏观背景关联，缺乏具体驱动因素分析，建议持续监测后续案件类型分布及当事人选择论坛的具体理由。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should reassess their litigation strategies given the shifting dynamics between the UK High Court and the UPC. The resurgence of cases in London indicates that rights holders value the court's established expertise and procedural predictability despite the UPC's broader geographic reach. Companies must carefully evaluate whether to pursue standalone UK actions or utilize the unitary patent system based on enforcement needs and cost considerations. Understanding these jurisdictional nuances is critical for effective IP portfolio management and risk mitigation in cross-border disputes.

items.title: Nokia unveils 10% Q1 patent revenue jump, but predicts “largely flat” licensing net sales for 2026
items.title_zh: 诺基亚Q1专利营收增10%但预计2026年许可销售持平
items.title_en: Nokia Reports 10% Q1 Patent Revenue Rise, Forecasts Flat Licensing Sales for 2026
items.summary: Finnish company continues deal-making run in Q1 2026
items.ai_summary_zh: 芬兰公司诺基亚在2026年第一季度继续推进交易，其专利许可营收实现10%的增长。然而，该公司预测2026年的许可净销售额将“基本持平”。原文正文极短，仅包含标题与摘要信息，未提供具体交易对象或财务细节。
items.ai_summary_en: Nokia continued its active licensing momentum in the first quarter of 2026, achieving a 10% year-on-year increase in patent revenue. Despite this positive short-term performance, the company projects that licensing net sales will remain largely flat throughout 2026. This trajectory reflects a maturing and stabilizing Standard Essential Patent (SEP) licensing market in Europe. The trend indicates that negotiations between major rights holders and implementers are normalizing, without significant market volatility or new waves of major litigation. These developments provide clear financial benchmarks for the industry, suggesting a shift towards predictable, stable licensing environments rather than aggressive expansion or disruptive legal conflicts.
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should note that Nokia’s flat revenue forecast suggests licensing rates may stabilize rather than surge, offering a window for cost management. Implementers can leverage this period to optimize internal patent portfolios and pursue comprehensive licensing agreements to lock in costs, avoiding prolonged disputes. Additionally, stakeholders must closely track the implementation of EU SEP regulations, as these rules will significantly influence negotiation strategies. Aligning compliance frameworks with emerging regulatory requirements is essential for maintaining favorable terms and mitigating legal risks in future licensing discussions.

items.title: [Guest post] The well-known status of a non-European trade mark in the EU: The Frisby case
items.title_zh: 非欧洲商标在欧盟的驰名地位：Frisby案
items.title_en: Guest Post: Establishing Well-Known Status for Non-EU Trademarks in the EU via the Frisby Case
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest contribution by Katfriend Salvatore Marcenaro (Wolf Méndez Abogados Asociados) providing updates on the Frisby case. Here’s what Salvatore writes:  The well-known status of a non-European trade mark in the EU: The Frisby caseby Salvatore MarcenaroRecently, the highly interesting (and delicious) case of the Colombian fried chicken
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系IPKat刊登的客座文章，由Wolf Méndez Abogados Asociados律师Salvatore Marcenaro撰写。文章更新了关于哥伦比亚炸鸡品牌“Frisby”的案件进展，重点探讨非欧洲商标在欧盟境内获得驰名商标地位的法律问题。
items.ai_summary_en: This guest contribution by Salvatore Marcenaro analyzes the Frisby case, focusing on a Colombian fried chicken brand seeking well-known trademark status within the European Union. The article examines how non-European rights holders can demonstrate reputation across member states under EU law. It highlights the critical role of such recognition in combating bad-faith registrations and infringement. The analysis provides practical guidance for multinational brands navigating EU trademark protection, illustrating how established fame can secure rights even without comprehensive local registration, thereby offering strategic insights for cross-border brand enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注欧盟法院或成员国法院如何依据巴黎公约及欧盟商标条例，认定仅在域外知名的商标在欧盟境内的驰名状态。此类判例将影响跨国品牌在欧维权策略，特别是针对未注册但具知名度的标识保护范围。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should prioritize accumulating robust evidence of use and promotion within the EU market to support claims of well-known status. Even without full trademark registration, demonstrating significant reputation can facilitate cross-class protection against infringers. Proactively organizing marketing data and consumer recognition metrics is essential to defend against potential bad-faith filings. This approach strengthens legal positions in opposition and cancellation actions, ensuring that brand equity built abroad translates into enforceable rights within the European jurisdiction.
