items.title: Optique – étude d’une décision relative à un procédé de traitement d’images
items.title_zh: 光学：一项关于图像处理方法的决定研究
items.title_en: EPO Decision T 2077/19: Technical Character of Image Processing via Reduced Data
items.summary: La décision T 2077/19 [1] concerne un procédé de traitement d’images reposant sur l’idée que la détection de zones de spécimen est plus fiable lorsqu’elle est effectuée à partir de données d’image réduites.  La revendication en cause visait notamment un détecteur « pour détecter une pluralité de zones de spécimen à partir des (suite) L’article Optique – étude d’une décision relative à un procédé de traitement d’images est apparu en premier sur Lavoix - Propriété intellectuelle .
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析了欧洲专利局上诉委员会决定T 2077/19。该决定涉及一种图像处理方法，其核心思想是基于缩减的图像数据进行样本区域检测更为可靠。涉案权利要求主要指向一种用于从图像数据中检测多个样本区域的探测器。文章对该技术方案的专利性进行了探讨。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines EPO Board of Appeal decision T 2077/19, which addresses the patentability of an image processing method. The core technical concept involves detecting specimen zones more reliably by utilizing reduced image data rather than full-resolution inputs. The disputed claims specifically defined a detector configured to identify multiple specimen areas based on this reduced data. The Board’s primary focus was determining whether the claimed procedure possessed sufficient technical character to satisfy European patentability requirements. By evaluating if the solution resolved a concrete technical problem instead of merely implementing an abstract algorithm, the decision clarifies the assessment of technical contribution for computer-implemented inventions in the optics and imaging sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案聚焦于计算机实施发明中技术特征与抽象算法的界限。需关注EPO对“缩减数据提高检测可靠性”这一技术效果在创造性判断中的权重，以及装置权利要求在软件相关专利中的撰写策略对授权前景的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners drafting patent applications for image processing technologies should ensure claims emphasize tangible technical effects over pure algorithmic logic. It is critical to articulate how data reduction techniques directly optimize hardware resources or enhance imaging quality, thereby addressing specific physical problems. This approach aligns with the EPO’s strict standards for technical character, helping to mitigate rejection risks during examination. Applicants must clearly link computational steps to improved system performance or physical outcomes to demonstrate compliance with European patentability requirements for computer-implemented inventions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["决定T 2077/19涉及基于缩减图像数据检测样本区域的图像处理方法", "涉案权利要求保护一种用于检测多个样本区域的探测器装置"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Decision T 2077/19 by the EPO Board of Appeal centers on the technical character of image processing methods using reduced data.", "The dispute examined whether detecting specimen zones from reduced data solves a specific technical problem beyond abstract algorithms.", "The ruling establishes key criteria for assessing the technical contribution of computer-implemented inventions at the EPO."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EPO对图像处理中数据缩减带来技术效果的认定标准", "观察涉及算法优化的装置权利要求在审查中的可专利性边界"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen arguments linking technical effects to specific technical problems in image processing patents.", "Practice note: Avoid purely algorithmic descriptions; highlight concrete improvements to hardware efficiency or imaging results.", "What to monitor: EPO examination trends regarding the technical contribution of data reduction and computer-implemented optical methods."]
core_points_en: Decision T 2077/19 by the EPO Board of Appeal centers on the technical character of image processing methods using reduced data.
The dispute examined whether detecting specimen zones from reduced data solves a specific technical problem beyond abstract algorithms.
The ruling establishes key criteria for assessing the technical contribution of computer-implemented inventions at the EPO.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen arguments linking technical effects to specific technical problems in image processing patents.
Practice note: Avoid purely algorithmic descriptions; highlight concrete improvements to hardware efficiency or imaging results.
What to monitor: EPO examination trends regarding the technical contribution of data reduction and computer-implemented optical methods.

items.title: HGF tech practice receives another boost with Barker Brettell partner
items.title_zh: HGF 吸纳前 Barker Brettell 合伙人强化技术团队
items.title_en: HGF Bolsters UK Tech Practice with Former Barker Brettell Partner Oliver Pooley
items.summary: UK and European patent attorney Oliver Pooley has joined HGF’s Birmingham office. He will bolster the firm’s tech and engineering team. Pooley has a degree in physics and chemistry and a PhD in electronic and electrical engineering. His technical specialties include electronics, AI, quantum computing, telecommunications, medical devices and additive manufacturing. Pooley began his career […] The post HGF tech practice receives another boost with Barker Brettell partner appeared first on JUVE Pat
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国及欧洲专利代理人 Oliver Pooley 从 Barker Brettell 加入 HGF 伯明翰办公室，旨在增强其技术与工程团队实力。Pooley 拥有物理化学学位及电子电气工程博士学位，专长涵盖电子、人工智能、量子计算、电信、医疗器械及增材制造等领域，此次变动体现了头部律所对前沿技术专利布局的重视。
items.ai_summary_en: UK and European patent attorney Oliver Pooley has joined HGF’s Birmingham office, bringing expertise from his previous role at Barker Brettell. Pooley holds a PhD in electronic and electrical engineering, alongside degrees in physics and chemistry. His technical focus areas include electronics, artificial intelligence, quantum computing, telecommunications, medical devices, and additive manufacturing. This appointment strengthens HGF’s technology and engineering capabilities in the Midlands, adding specialized capacity for complex high-tech patent matters across these emerging and established sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This lateral move highlights the continued consolidation of specialized technical talent within major UK patent firms, particularly in high-demand sectors like AI and quantum computing. For rights holders managing portfolios in these fast-evolving fields, the availability of attorneys with deep academic and practical experience in specific hard technologies remains a critical factor in securing robust protection. Firms are actively reinforcing their regional offices with senior practitioners to meet client needs for technically sophisticated prosecution and advisory services.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["资深专利代理人 Pooley 加盟 HGF 伯明翰办公室", "新成员具备电子工程博士背景及多领域技术专长", "律所重点强化人工智能与量子计算等新兴技术板块"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Oliver Pooley joins HGF's Birmingham office from Barker Brettell to strengthen the firm's technology and engineering practice group.", "Pooley holds a PhD in electronic and electrical engineering with specific expertise in AI, quantum computing, and telecommunications.", "The hire expands HGF's technical coverage in advanced fields including medical devices and additive manufacturing within the UK market."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注律所技术团队背景以匹配高精尖专利需求", "借势专业人才流动优化在欧洲的知识产权布局"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders in AI and quantum sectors should verify that their chosen counsel possesses verified technical depth in these specific disciplines.", "Practice note: Regional hubs like Birmingham are becoming key centers for specialized tech patent work, offering alternative access to senior expertise.", "What to monitor: Further lateral movements of specialists in emerging technologies may signal shifting market capacities for complex patent prosecution."]
core_points_en: Oliver Pooley joins HGF's Birmingham office from Barker Brettell to strengthen the firm's technology and engineering practice group.
Pooley holds a PhD in electronic and electrical engineering with specific expertise in AI, quantum computing, and telecommunications.
The hire expands HGF's technical coverage in advanced fields including medical devices and additive manufacturing within the UK market.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders in AI and quantum sectors should verify that their chosen counsel possesses verified technical depth in these specific disciplines.
Practice note: Regional hubs like Birmingham are becoming key centers for specialized tech patent work, offering alternative access to senior expertise.
What to monitor: Further lateral movements of specialists in emerging technologies may signal shifting market capacities for complex patent prosecution.

items.title: Sekisui and Bird & Bird deal another blow to competitor Kuraray
items.title_zh: 积水化学与Bird & Bird联手再挫竞争对手可乐丽
items.title_en: Sekisui Chemicals and Bird & Bird Advance Patent Case Against Kuraray in Germany
items.summary: Two Japanese companies are fighting it out in German courts. This is rare, but the dispute between Sekisui Chemicals and Kuraray is particularly intense. Sekisui Chemicals has filed four lawsuits based on four patents against its Japanese competitor Kuraray in Germany. The two opponents are also facing off in Korea and the US. Both speciality […] The post Sekisui and Bird & Bird deal another blow to competitor Kuraray appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 积水化学与可乐丽这两家日本公司在德国法院展开激烈专利纠纷。积水化学基于四项专利在德国对可乐丽提起四起诉讼。除德国外，双方还在韩国和美国进行法律对抗。该案由Bird & Bird律所代理积水化学。
items.ai_summary_en: Japanese specialty chemical firms Sekisui Chemicals and Kuraray are engaged in intense litigation within German courts, a jurisdiction that remains a primary venue for high-stakes patent disputes. Sekisui has initiated four separate lawsuits against Kuraray based on four distinct patents. This legal confrontation extends beyond Europe, with parallel proceedings currently underway in South Korea and the United States. The German actions are being handled by Bird & Bird, marking a significant strategic move for Sekisui. Although these cases fall outside the Unified Patent Court system, they underscore the continued critical importance of national German proceedings in global intellectual property strategies. The simultaneous multi-jurisdictional nature of this dispute highlights the aggressive enforcement tactics employed by major market participants to secure competitive advantages in the specialty chemicals sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注德国法院在跨国化工专利纠纷中的裁判倾向。鉴于诉讼同时在美、韩、德进行，需观察不同法域程序进度及潜在判决冲突对全球业务的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Germany continues to serve as a central forum for multinational patent litigation due to its specialized courts and efficient injunction mechanisms. Rights holders facing coordinated global enforcement campaigns should establish robust cross-border defense strategies rather than treating national cases in isolation. It is prudent to conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses and prepare invalidity arguments early in the process. Leveraging specific procedural rules within the German system can provide valuable leverage for settlement negotiations. Companies must remain vigilant regarding the strategic selection of jurisdictions by competitors, ensuring that high-value patent portfolios are adequately defended across key markets to mitigate the risk of disruptive injunctive relief.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["积水化学在德国基于四项专利对日本竞争对手可乐丽提起四起诉讼", "双方除在德国诉讼外，还在韩国和美国面临法律对抗", "Bird & Bird律所代表积水化学参与此次德国诉讼程序"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Sekisui Chemicals filed four patent infringement lawsuits against rival Kuraray in German courts.", "The two Japanese companies are simultaneously litigating related disputes in South Korea and the United States.", "Bird & Bird represents Sekisui, securing a notable procedural advantage in the ongoing German proceedings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注德国法院对涉案专利有效性及侵权认定的具体裁决结果", "监测美、韩、德三地诉讼程序的交互影响及潜在判决一致性", "观察此类高频跨国专利诉讼对行业竞争格局的长期信号意义"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Germany remains a critical battleground for multinational patent disputes requiring focused defensive resources.", "Practice note: Establish coordinated multi-jurisdictional response mechanisms to address linked global litigation effectively.", "What to monitor: Track competitor filing patterns in Germany to anticipate aggressive enforcement strategies early."]
core_points_en: Sekisui Chemicals filed four patent infringement lawsuits against rival Kuraray in German courts.
The two Japanese companies are simultaneously litigating related disputes in South Korea and the United States.
Bird & Bird represents Sekisui, securing a notable procedural advantage in the ongoing German proceedings.

Practice note: Establish coordinated multi-jurisdictional response mechanisms to address linked global litigation effectively.
What to monitor: Track competitor filing patterns in Germany to anticipate aggressive enforcement strategies early.

items.title: Plasseraud IP celebrates 120 years of expertise in the service of innovation
items.title_zh: Plasseraud IP 庆祝成立 120 周年并宣布战略扩张
items.title_en: Plasseraud IP Marks 120 Years with Strategic Mergers and Amsterdam Expansion
items.summary: In 2026, Plasseraud IP celebrates 120 years of history, expertise and commitment to intellectual property. Founded in 1906, Plasseraud IP has been supporting those who innovate, create and transform the world for over a century. Written by .
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国老牌知产律所 Plasseraud IP 迎来 120 周年，宣布多项战略举措：合并 Bleger Rhein Poupon 以强化法国本土实力；开设阿姆斯特丹办公室拓展国际网络；整合法律团队成立 Plasseraud IP Avocats。此举旨在通过多学科专家团队，为全球创新者提供从专利到商标的一体化长期战略支持。
items.ai_summary_en: French intellectual property firm Plasseraud IP celebrates its 120th anniversary in 2026, marking over a century of service to innovators. The firm has recently executed significant structural developments, including a merger with Bleger Rhein Poupon to strengthen its French presence and the opening of an office in Amsterdam to consolidate international reach. Additionally, the integration of Plasseraud Avocats and Candé Blanchard Ducamp has formed Plasseraud IP Avocats, aiming to provide a more comprehensive approach to IP and legal advice for a diverse global client base.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，法国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The consolidation of specialized IP firms and expansion into key European jurisdictions like the Netherlands reflects a broader trend toward integrated service models. For rights holders managing portfolios across multiple EU member states, these developments signal increased availability of coordinated legal and technical support. Monitoring such structural changes helps clients identify partners capable of handling complex, multi-jurisdictional IP matters efficiently.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["合并 Bleger Rhein Poupon 巩固法国市场领先地位与服务深度", "新设阿姆斯特丹办公室以强化泛欧及国际业务覆盖能力", "整合律师团队实现知识产权与法律诉讼服务的全面一体化"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Merger with Bleger Rhein Poupon strengthens domestic expertise and market presence within France.", "Opening of Amsterdam office consolidates international network and enhances cross-border service capabilities.", "Integration of legal teams into Plasseraud IP Avocats enables a unified approach to IP strategy and litigation support."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["借助律所法荷联动优势优化在欧洲的专利组合管理策略", "选择一体化服务团队以高效应对跨国知识产权纠纷与诉讼"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Integrated firms offer streamlined coordination for patent, trademark, and legal strategies across France and the Netherlands.", "Practice note: Evaluate whether merged entities provide seamless access to both technical IP experts and qualified legal counsel.", "What to monitor: Further consolidation trends among established European IP boutiques and their impact on service delivery models."]
core_points_en: Merger with Bleger Rhein Poupon strengthens domestic expertise and market presence within France.
Opening of Amsterdam office consolidates international network and enhances cross-border service capabilities.
Integration of legal teams into Plasseraud IP Avocats enables a unified approach to IP strategy and litigation support.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Integrated firms offer streamlined coordination for patent, trademark, and legal strategies across France and the Netherlands.
Practice note: Evaluate whether merged entities provide seamless access to both technical IP experts and qualified legal counsel.
What to monitor: Further consolidation trends among established European IP boutiques and their impact on service delivery models.

items.title: Report on a roundtable on music, generative AI, and copyright at the UCL Institute of Brand and Innovation Law
items.title_zh: UCL举办音乐、生成式AI与版权圆桌会议报告
items.title_en: UCL Roundtable Report: Navigating Generative AI, Music Copyright, and Fair Remuneration
items.summary: The debate around generative artificial intelligence (genAI) and copyright law has been raging on globally for some time.
items.ai_summary_zh: 伦敦大学学院品牌与创新法律研究所于2026年3月4日举办闭门圆桌会，探讨生成式AI、版权与音乐的交叉问题。会议背景包括英国知识产权局咨询回应及上议院报告发布。与会者涵盖音乐、科技、政府及司法界代表，遵循查塔姆宫规则。讨论指出音乐人更关注报酬与署名而非训练数据使用，且不同层级音乐人诉求存在差异。
items.ai_summary_en: The UCL Institute of Brand and Innovation Law hosted a closed-door roundtable examining the intersection of generative AI and music copyright. The discussion highlighted that creators prioritize fair remuneration and clear attribution over restrictions on training data usage. Although the UK government recently issued responses to consultations and impact assessments regarding AI and copyright, industry consensus remains elusive. Stakeholders emphasized the need to balance technological innovation with the protection of rights holders' interests. The event facilitated honest dialogue among diverse participants, including legal practitioners, government officials, and industry representatives, aiming to build consensus on future regulatory frameworks despite the complexity of the issues involved.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本次圆桌揭示了英国音乐产业在AI时代的权利主张重心正从“禁止训练”转向“获酬与署名”。实务中需关注大型唱片公司与独立音乐人在授权模式上的分化。后续应密切跟踪英国政府基于《数据使用与访问法案》出台的具体监管措施及行业共识的形成路径。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the UK and EU should closely monitor legislative developments concerning compensation mechanisms for AI training data. Establishing transparent licensing and revenue-sharing models is essential to safeguard creators' moral and economic rights. Companies must proactively develop compliance strategies to mitigate legal risks associated with unauthorized use of copyrighted material. Failure to address attribution and remuneration concerns may lead to enforcement actions or reputational damage in European markets. Aligning business practices with emerging regulatory expectations will help ensure sustainable operations and foster trust with rights holders across the region.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["会议于2026年3月4日由UCL IBIL主办，聚焦生成式AI、版权与音乐领域的交叉议题", "音乐创作者主要关切在于获得公平报酬与署名，而非单纯反对作品被用作AI训练数据", "现有成功的报酬与署名机制几乎仅集中于大型唱片公司，音乐人群体内部诉求并不统一"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Musicians primarily seek fair compensation and proper attribution rather than opposing the use of their works for AI training datasets.", "Generative AI models often exclude artists from revenue distribution chains, causing significant dissatisfaction within the creative community.", "While multi-stakeholder discussions are vital, resolving complex copyright and AI integration issues requires sustained effort beyond single events."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["音乐版权方对AI的关注点已从数据输入合法性转向输出端的报酬分配与署名权保障", "行业解决方案呈现分层趋势，大型权利人与独立创作者在AI许可谈判中的地位与诉求差异显著", "需持续监测英国立法机构针对AI训练数据豁免及补偿机制的最终政策落地情况"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor UK and EU legislative trends on AI training data compensation to mitigate potential compliance risks.", "Practice note: Develop transparent licensing and revenue-sharing frameworks to protect creators' attribution and remuneration rights.", "What to monitor: Regulatory updates regarding AI copyright exceptions and mandatory transparency obligations for model developers."]
core_points_en: Musicians primarily seek fair compensation and proper attribution rather than opposing the use of their works for AI training datasets.
Generative AI models often exclude artists from revenue distribution chains, causing significant dissatisfaction within the creative community.
While multi-stakeholder discussions are vital, resolving complex copyright and AI integration issues requires sustained effort beyond single events.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor UK and EU legislative trends on AI training data compensation to mitigate potential compliance risks.
Practice note: Develop transparent licensing and revenue-sharing frameworks to protect creators' attribution and remuneration rights.
What to monitor: Regulatory updates regarding AI copyright exceptions and mandatory transparency obligations for model developers.

items.title: Poll reveals public’s most iconic trade marks as register turns 150
items.title_zh: UKIPO：英国商标注册 150 周年之际，公众评出最具代表性的商标
items.title_en: UKIPO Poll Reveals Most Iconic Trade Marks for 150th Anniversary
items.summary: From chocolate bars to famous cars, and the mark where it all began – Britain crowns its best loved trade marks.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局在英国商标注册制度迎来 150 周年之际公布公众票选结果，展示最具代表性的英国商标。官方文章指出，Rolls-Royce 位列榜首，Bass 红三角标签则因于 1876 年 1 月 1 日成为英国第 1 号注册商标而被特别强调；目前英国商标注册簿已保护逾 250 万件商标，且仍有 400 余件维多利亚时代注册的商标保持有效。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has published the results of a public poll identifying the nation's most iconic trade marks to mark the 150th anniversary of the trade marks register. The announcement highlights consumer recognition of established brands across sectors, ranging from confectionery to automotive industries. This initiative underscores the historical significance of the register and its role in protecting brand identity over time. The UKIPO uses this milestone to reflect on the evolution of trade mark protection in the United Kingdom and the enduring value of registered rights in maintaining consumer trust and brand distinction within the marketplace.
items.ai_insight_zh: 这篇内容偏品牌历史与公众传播，但仍反映出 UKIPO 正把商标制度与品牌信任、文化记忆和商业价值联系起来。对品牌管理和商标组合运营而言，这类官方叙事提醒企业，注册商标不仅是防御工具，也是长期品牌资产和市场识别力的制度基础。
items.ai_insight_en: This communication emphasizes the link between registered trade marks and long-term brand equity. For rights holders, it reinforces the value of maintaining historic registrations. Practitioners should note the UKIPO's focus on public perception as a metric for trade mark significance. Monitoring such official narratives helps align portfolio management with broader intellectual property policy themes regarding brand heritage and consumer confidence in the UK market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO 以英国商标注册 150 周年为节点发布公众票选的代表性商标名单。", "官方强调 Bass 红三角标签是 1876 年 1 月 1 日英国开放注册首日的第 1 号注册商标。", "文章同时指出当前英国注册簿保护逾 250 万件商标，上一年约收到 20 万件申请。"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UKIPO releases public poll results on iconic trade marks for register anniversary.", "Announcement highlights brand recognition across diverse consumer and industrial sectors."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["商标制度的官方传播正在强化品牌资产与消费者信任之间的联系。", "历史悠久且仍有效的注册商标凸显持续维护商标组合的长期价值。", "企业可借此重新审视核心品牌、标识和经典包装的注册与续展策略。"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Highlights the enduring commercial value of long-standing registered trade marks.", "Practice note: Official narratives increasingly connect registration history with brand trust metrics.", "What to monitor: Future UKIPO initiatives linking public engagement with intellectual property awareness."]
core_points_en: UKIPO releases public poll results on iconic trade marks for register anniversary.
Announcement highlights brand recognition across diverse consumer and industrial sectors.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Highlights the enduring commercial value of long-standing registered trade marks.
Practice note: Official narratives increasingly connect registration history with brand trust metrics.
What to monitor: Future UKIPO initiatives linking public engagement with intellectual property awareness.

items.title: UPC and Partial‑Problems
items.title_zh: UPC 中央分院在钉子专利案中适用部分问题法
items.title_en: UPC Munich Applies EPO Partial-Problems Approach to Assess Inventive Step
items.summary: The partial-problems approach to inventive step is a well-known part of European patent practice, not least as it is used routinely by...
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 中央分院（慕尼黑）在涉及 EP4019790 号钉子的诉讼中，首次适用“部分问题法”评估创造性。法院认定权利要求中的两个区别特征（钉尖角度与纵向凹槽）分别解决独立技术问题且无协同效应，属于简单叠加。因此，法院单独评估各特征创造性，最终因两者均显而易见而判定整体缺乏创造性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Central Division in Munich has applied the partial-problems approach to inventive step in case UPC_CFI_836/2024 concerning European patent EP4019790. The court evaluated a claim combining a specific nail-tip angle and longitudinal shaft grooves, determining these features addressed technically independent problems without functional synergy. Consequently, the court assessed each feature in isolation against prior art. Finding both elements independently obvious, the court held the claim lacked inventive step as a mere aggregation of features. This decision confirms that the UPC aligns with established EPO practice regarding combinations of non-interacting technical features.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请专利时，应避免将无协同效应的技术特征简单堆砌。面对 UPC 诉讼，需警惕对方利用“部分问题法”拆解组合发明，提前准备特征间协同效应的技术证据以抗辩无效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling reinforces the alignment between UPC case law and EPO examination practice regarding the assessment of inventive step for feature combinations. For rights holders, it highlights the importance of demonstrating functional interaction or synergy between combined features to avoid classification as a mere aggregation. In litigation, challengers may leverage this approach to dissect complex claims into individually obvious components. Practitioners should ensure patent specifications explicitly articulate technical synergies to support unitary problem-solution arguments during validity proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC 采纳 EPO 惯例，对无协同特征拆分评估创造性", "法院认定钉尖角度与凹槽解决不同阶段问题，无功能交互", "因特征均为简单叠加且各自显而易见，专利被判无效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Central Division (Munich) applied the partial-problems approach to evaluate inventive step for a combination claim lacking synergistic interaction.", "Features addressing distinct technical phases were treated as an aggregation, allowing separate assessment of obviousness against prior art.", "The patent was invalidated because both distinguishing features were found independently obvious when evaluated in isolation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免无协同特征简单叠加，以防被拆分认定显而易见", "诉讼中需重点举证技术特征间的功能交互与协同效应"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must demonstrate functional synergy between combined features to prevent claims from being treated as obvious aggregations under UPC jurisprudence.", "Practice note: Drafting strategies should emphasize technical interactions between features to counter potential partial-problems challenges during validity disputes.", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions applying the partial-problems approach to determine if strict separation of features becomes standard for multi-feature claims."]
core_points_en: The UPC Central Division (Munich) applied the partial-problems approach to evaluate inventive step for a combination claim lacking synergistic interaction.
Features addressing distinct technical phases were treated as an aggregation, allowing separate assessment of obviousness against prior art.
The patent was invalidated because both distinguishing features were found independently obvious when evaluated in isolation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must demonstrate functional synergy between combined features to prevent claims from being treated as obvious aggregations under UPC jurisprudence.
Practice note: Drafting strategies should emphasize technical interactions between features to counter potential partial-problems challenges during validity disputes.
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions applying the partial-problems approach to determine if strict separation of features becomes standard for multi-feature claims.

items.title: UPC: Supplementing expert opinion after inspection not possible
items.title_zh: UPC 裁定：勘验后不得补充专家意见
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Denies Supplementary Expert Opinion After Evidence Preservation Inspection
items.summary: Van Loon had applied to inspect and preserve evidence at Inverquark’s exhibition stand in advance of a potential main action, which went...
items.ai_summary_zh: 杜塞尔多夫地方法院在 Van Loon 诉 Inverquark 案中裁定，被告无权要求法院委托的专家在证据保全勘验后出具补充意见。法院指出，单方命令后的程序权利可通过复核申请保障，且该专家非规则 187 条定义的“法院专家”，无法律依据支持补充报告。实质准确性异议应在主诉讼中提出。
items.ai_summary_en: The Düsseldorf Local Division of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) denied a request by defendant Inverquark for a supplementary expert opinion following an ex-parte evidence preservation order granted to claimant Van Loon. The court held that there was no legal basis for such a supplement, as the appointed expert did not qualify as a 'court expert' under Rule 187 of the Rules of Procedure. The panel emphasized that evidence preservation aims to provide prompt information for assessing further action, with substantive accuracy challenges reserved for main proceedings. While procedural rights were protected via review options under Rule 197.3, the court noted specific technical questions regarding the expert's sketches and access to protective letters for potential reconsideration in future litigation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在面对 UPC 证据保全时，应重视保护信质量以预防勘验偏差。若对专家报告有异议，切勿试图在保全阶段要求补充，而应策略性地将技术事实争议保留至主诉讼阶段进行实质性抗辩。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the procedural boundaries of evidence preservation at the UPC, distinguishing it from full trial procedures where expert testimony is subject to party commentary. For rights holders and alleged infringers, it confirms that the primary function of preservation orders is rapid fact-finding, not final determination of technical infringement details. Parties should focus on securing robust protective letters to mitigate ex-parte risks and prepare comprehensive technical defenses for the main action, as the preservation stage offers limited avenues for correcting perceived inaccuracies in expert findings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院驳回被告要求专家出具补充意见的请求，认定缺乏法律依据", "证据保全旨在快速获取信息，实质争议应留待后续主诉讼解决", "被告对报告准确性的质疑虽被记录，但需在主程序中重新审议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Düsseldorf Local Division ruled that defendants cannot request supplementary opinions from experts commissioned during ex-parte evidence preservation inspections.", "The court clarified that such experts are not 'court experts' under Rule 187 RoP, meaning parties lack the procedural right to comment on or challenge their reports at this stage.", "Substantive objections to the accuracy of detailed descriptions must be raised in subsequent main proceedings rather than during the preliminary evidence preservation phase."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["保护信是应对单方勘验的关键，需详尽描述技术特征以防误读", "针对专家报告的实质异议，应制定主诉讼阶段的详细抗辩策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders can rely on the finality of preservation reports for initial case assessment, while defendants must accept that technical corrections are deferred…", "Practice note: Ensure protective letters comprehensively address potential technical misinterpretations, as post-inspection expert supplementation is procedurally unavailable unde…", "What to monitor: How UPC panels handle recorded technical discrepancies from preservation phases when evaluating evidence admissibility and weight in subsequent main actions."]
core_points_en: The UPC Düsseldorf Local Division ruled that defendants cannot request supplementary opinions from experts commissioned during ex-parte evidence preservation inspections.
The court clarified that such experts are not 'court experts' under Rule 187 RoP, meaning parties lack the procedural right to comment on or challenge their reports at this stage.
Substantive objections to the accuracy of detailed descriptions must be raised in subsequent main proceedings rather than during the preliminary evidence preservation phase.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders can rely on the finality of preservation reports for initial case assessment, while defendants must accept that technical corrections are deferred…
Practice note: Ensure protective letters comprehensively address potential technical misinterpretations, as post-inspection expert supplementation is procedurally unavailable unde…
What to monitor: How UPC panels handle recorded technical discrepancies from preservation phases when evaluating evidence admissibility and weight in subsequent main actions.

items.title: Unified Patent Court Insights: Claim Construction
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院洞察：权利要求构建与功能性限定解释
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Clarifies Claim Construction: Functional Language Defines Suitability, Not System Components
items.summary: A recent decision from the Düsseldorf Local Division in UPC_CFI_351/2024 provides an interesting look at the Unified Patent Court's (UPC)...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院杜塞尔多夫分部在 UPC_CFI_351/2024 号案件中，就打印机供粉容器的侵权纠纷作出裁决。核心争议在于“可安装”一词是否要求外部设备（打印机）实际存在。法院裁定，此类功能性语言仅定义产品适用性而非系统组成部分，确认单独销售容器构成对产品权利要求的直接侵权，厘清了产品claim与系统claim的界限。
items.ai_summary_en: In UPC_CFI_351/2024, the Unified Patent Court's Düsseldorf Local Division addressed claim construction regarding a printer supply container. The dispute centered on whether the term 'detachably mountable' required the presence of an external apparatus (the printer) to constitute infringement, effectively arguing for a system claim interpretation. The Court rejected this view, ruling that such functional language defines the product's suitability for its intended use rather than mandating the physical presence of the receiving device. Consequently, the claim was interpreted as a product claim covering the container alone. The Court found that standalone toner bottles sold by the defendants directly infringed the patent, establishing a clear distinction between product and system claims in UPC jurisprudence.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业需注意，即便仅销售适配特定设备的配件，若功能描述符合专利限定，仍可能在欧盟面临直接侵权风险。建议在产品设计及说明书中审慎规避他人专利的功能性表述，并提前开展 FTO 分析。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies how the UPC interprets functional limitations in product claims, distinguishing them from system claims. For rights holders, it confirms that claims drafted with functional interfaces may still cover standalone components if they are suitable for the intended use. For potential infringers, it highlights that selling a single component compatible with a larger system may trigger direct infringement liability, even if the complementary system parts are not included in the sale. This approach aligns with traditional European patent practice but provides specific guidance for UPC litigation strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院裁定功能性限定词仅界定产品用途适应性，无需外部设备共存", "明确区分产品权利要求与系统权利要求，单独部件销售亦可侵权", "被告关于必须包含打印机组件才能实现发明的抗辩未被法院采纳"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court ruled that 'mountable' defines product suitability for intended use, not a requirement for the external apparatus to be present during infringement.", "Functional language like 'capable of supporting' does not convert a product claim into a system claim requiring multiple components to exist simultaneously.", "Standalone sale of a component designed to interface with another device can constitute direct infringement of a product claim under this interpretation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["功能性描述可能扩大保护范围，单独销售配件亦存直接侵权风险", "对欧出口硬件需严格排查专利权利要求，避免触碰功能性限定红线"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders should consider drafting product claims with functional interface language to capture standalone component sales without needing to prove system-w…", "Practice note: Defendants arguing for system claim interpretations based on functional terms like 'mountable' face a high burden; courts may view these as defining suitability rat…", "What to monitor: Future UPC divisions' consistency in applying this 'suitability vs. presence' test to other functional claim terms in cross-border infringement disputes."]
core_points_en: The Court ruled that 'mountable' defines product suitability for intended use, not a requirement for the external apparatus to be present during infringement.
Functional language like 'capable of supporting' does not convert a product claim into a system claim requiring multiple components to exist simultaneously.
Standalone sale of a component designed to interface with another device can constitute direct infringement of a product claim under this interpretation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders should consider drafting product claims with functional interface language to capture standalone component sales without needing to prove system-w…
Practice note: Defendants arguing for system claim interpretations based on functional terms like 'mountable' face a high burden; courts may view these as defining suitability rat…
What to monitor: Future UPC divisions' consistency in applying this 'suitability vs. presence' test to other functional claim terms in cross-border infringement disputes.

items.title: How to plan successful IP mediation
items.title_zh: 如何规划成功的知识产权调解策略
items.title_en: Strategic Planning for Successful IP Mediation in the European Union
items.summary: Mediation is an increasingly attractive option to resolve IP disputes, especially for SMEs, individuals and universities. But it is important that organisations are well prepared and proactive, says Cyrille Rousseau of Dennemeyer & Associates.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出调解因高效、保密及商业导向优势，正成为解决知识产权纠纷的重要选项。重点介绍欧盟层面新机遇：欧盟知识产权局调解中心已于 2023 年启动并扩展服务；统一专利法院专利调解仲裁中心将于 2026 年启用。文章强调各方需充分准备并以诚意参与，以实现双赢并创造未来商机。
items.ai_summary_en: Mediation is emerging as a preferred alternative to litigation for resolving intellectual property disputes, particularly for SMEs, individuals, and universities. Cyrille Rousseau of Dennemeyer & Associates highlights that mediation offers speed, cost efficiency, confidentiality, and the potential for commercially focused, cross-jurisdictional solutions. The EU landscape is evolving with new dispute resolution forums: the EUIPO Mediation Centre expanded its services in June 2025 to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs. Additionally, the Unified Patent Court's Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre is scheduled to open in June 2026, providing a dedicated platform for patent-related ADR in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: The expansion of institutional mediation frameworks in Europe offers rights holders structured avenues for efficient dispute resolution outside of costly litigation. For portfolio managers and legal teams, integrating these mechanisms requires proactive preparation and a willingness to engage in good faith negotiations. Understanding the specific scopes of the EUIPO and upcoming UPC centers allows organizations to strategically select forums that align with their commercial objectives and jurisdictional needs.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["调解相比诉讼更具成本效益且能跨辖区解决关联冲突", "欧盟知识产权局已扩大调解服务范围至商标与设计争议", "统一专利法院将于 2026 年设立专门的专利调解仲裁中心"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Mediation provides quicker, confidential, and commercially oriented resolutions compared to traditional court litigation.", "The EUIPO Mediation Centre now covers all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and registered designs.", "The UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre will launch in June 2026 to handle patent disputes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用欧盟新设调解中心降低跨境维权成本与时间", "针对统一专利法院机制提前布局替代性争议解决策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Institutional mediation offers cost-effective and confidential alternatives to litigation for managing IP portfolios.", "Practice note: Organizations should prepare proactively and engage in good faith to maximize the benefits of mediated settlements.", "What to monitor: The operational launch of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in June 2026."]
core_points_en: Mediation provides quicker, confidential, and commercially oriented resolutions compared to traditional court litigation.
The EUIPO Mediation Centre now covers all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and registered designs.
The UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre will launch in June 2026 to handle patent disputes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Institutional mediation offers cost-effective and confidential alternatives to litigation for managing IP portfolios.
Practice note: Organizations should prepare proactively and engage in good faith to maximize the benefits of mediated settlements.
What to monitor: The operational launch of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in June 2026.

items.title: How To Protect Your Brand Online 365 Days Of The Year
items.title_zh: 全年365天在线品牌保护策略与挑战
items.title_en: How To Protect Your Brand Online 365 Days Of The Year
items.summary: Brand protection isn’t just a seasonal task—it requires continuous vigilance. Discover current challenges and priorities for online brand protection as well as practical strategies for achieving protection 365 days a year.
items.ai_summary_zh: Questel专家指出，品牌保护需全年持续警惕，而非仅限促销季。人工智能加速了虚假网站、评论及广告的生成，使欺诈更难检测。尽管黑五等高峰期侵权激增，但假冒者全年活跃。结合自动化监测与人工分析，快速下架侵权内容，是减少财务与声誉损失、保障欧盟市场品牌权利的关键策略。
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧盟市场品牌权利人需关注AI驱动的侵权规模化趋势。实务中应建立全天候监测机制，区分季节性高峰与常态化威胁。重点观察虚假广告引流、跨平台协同侵权及AI生成内容的识别难点，确保持续执法资源投入以维持品牌资产安全。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["人工智能工具使虚假网站、深度伪造评论及广告生成更快更廉价，显著增加检测难度", "除黑五等高峰期外，基于域名的网络钓鱼、跨平台滥用及未经授权的商标 misuse 全年持…", "自动化技术扩展检测规模，配合人工分析师提供语境与精度，能有效实现快速下架并降低损失"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["AI降低了造假门槛，导致侵权内容爆发式增长，传统周期性保护模式已不足以应对风险", "需关注虚假赞助广告、域名钓鱼及跨平台（社交媒体、市场）协同侵权等新型滥用形态", "后续应观察自动化检测与人工研判结合的执法效率，以及针对AI生成侵权内容的取证难点"]

items.title: EU Pharma Package: The “Bolar” Exemption Compromise Proposal
items.title_zh: 欧盟制药一揽子计划：博拉尔豁免妥协提案分析
items.title_en: EU Pharma Package: Compromise Text Expands Bolar Exemption Scope and Explicitly Bans Patent Linkage
items.summary: In our sixth alert in this EU Pharma Package Series, we provided an analysis of the history and interpretation issues for another highly debated topic: the “Bolar” exemption.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析欧盟制药一揽子计划中备受争议的“博拉尔”豁免条款。该豁免允许仿制药企在专利期内进行上市前准备而不侵权。妥协文本扩大了物质和主体范围，涵盖定价、报销及第三方API供应商活动，但地域范围仍不明确。此外，提案明确禁止专利链接，旨在解决成员国间解释不一的问题，确保法律适用的统一性。
items.ai_summary_en: The latest compromise proposal for the EU Pharma Package significantly broadens the 'Bolar' exemption, which provides a safe harbor against patent and supplementary protection certificate (SPC) infringement for generic and biosimilar preparatory activities. The text expands both material and personal scope to include pricing, reimbursement, health technology assessments, tender participation, and activities by third-party active pharmaceutical ingredient suppliers. However, the territorial scope remains ambiguous. Additionally, the proposal explicitly prohibits patent linkage mechanisms, reinforcing the European Commission’s stance that such systems are anticompetitive. These changes aim to facilitate day-one market entry for generics post-patent expiry while addressing historical inconsistencies in Member State interpretations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国药企应密切关注豁免范围扩大带来的合规机遇，利用新规则优化上市前策略。同时，需警惕地域适用差异，加强内部合规审查，确保供应链活动符合目标国最新法规，规避潜在的专利侵权诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and generic manufacturers, the expansion of the Bolar exemption alters the risk landscape for pre-expiry supply chain and regulatory preparations. The inclusion of tender participation and third-party suppliers creates new operational parameters that require careful contractual and compliance review. Simultaneously, the explicit ban on patent linkage removes a potential procedural barrier to generic entry in certain jurisdictions. Stakeholders must monitor how Member States transpose these expanded definitions, as divergent national implementations could reintroduce fragmentation despite the harmonization intent.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["扩大豁免范围至定价、报销及第三方API供应商，强化仿制药上市准备安全港", "明确禁止专利链接机制，消除欧盟内专利保护与市场准入挂钩的法律不确定性", "地域适用范围尚存模糊，需各成员国统一转化实施以避免碎片化执法风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The compromise text expands the Bolar exemption to cover pricing, reimbursement, health technology assessments, and tender participation activities.", "Third-party active pharmaceutical ingredient suppliers are now included within the personal scope of the Bolar exemption safe harbor provisions.", "Patent linkage is explicitly prohibited in the Pharma Package, aligning with existing EU competition law principles regarding market access."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用豁免扩围优化仿制药上市前合规策略，降低专利侵权风险", "关注成员国转化差异，加强供应链合规管理以应对潜在法律不确定性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Generic and biosimilar companies should assess how the expanded scope impacts their pre-launch strategies, particularly regarding tender bids and supplier agreem…", "Practice note: Rights holders need to review enforcement boundaries, noting that activities related to pricing and HTA may no longer constitute infringing acts under the revised e…", "What to monitor: Monitor national transposition efforts to identify any residual ambiguities in territorial scope or divergent interpretations of 'tender participation' across Mem…"]
core_points_en: The compromise text expands the Bolar exemption to cover pricing, reimbursement, health technology assessments, and tender participation activities.
Third-party active pharmaceutical ingredient suppliers are now included within the personal scope of the Bolar exemption safe harbor provisions.
Patent linkage is explicitly prohibited in the Pharma Package, aligning with existing EU competition law principles regarding market access.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Generic and biosimilar companies should assess how the expanded scope impacts their pre-launch strategies, particularly regarding tender bids and supplier agreem…
Practice note: Rights holders need to review enforcement boundaries, noting that activities related to pricing and HTA may no longer constitute infringing acts under the revised e…
What to monitor: Monitor national transposition efforts to identify any residual ambiguities in territorial scope or divergent interpretations of 'tender participation' across Mem…

items.title: Photographies culinaires et originalité : une recette impossible ?
items.title_zh: 美食摄影与原创性：难以实现的配方？
items.title_en: Culinary Photography and Originality: An Impossible Recipe Under French Copyright Law?
items.summary: par Maître Valentine MOURADIAN, avocate au Barreau de Paris – Cabinet Benoliel Avocats  Par un arrêt du 1er octobre 2025, la Cour d’appel de Paris a refusé d’accorder à une photographie culinaire la protection par le droit d’auteur, jugeant que son originalité n’avait pas été démontrée (CA Paris, 1er octobre 2025, RG n°24/01840).  Cette décision […] L’article Photographies culinaires et originalité : une recette impossible ? est apparu en premier sur BLIP! .
items.ai_summary_zh: 巴黎律师Valentine Mouradian分析指出，巴黎上诉法院于2025年10月1日作出裁决（案号RG n°24/01840），拒绝给予某美食摄影作品版权保护。法院认定该照片未能证明其具备法律要求的原创性。文章据此探讨美食摄影在版权法下获得保护的难度。
items.ai_summary_en: Valentine Mouradian, a Paris-based attorney at Benoliel Avocats, analyzes a significant ruling issued by the Paris Court of Appeal on October 1, 2025. In this decision (Case No. 24/01840), the court denied copyright protection to a specific culinary photograph, determining that its originality had not been sufficiently demonstrated. The judgment underscores a strict application of the originality requirement in French judicial practice. Mere documentation of food appearance is generally insufficient to meet the threshold for copyright protection. To qualify, photographers must prove they made unique artistic choices and exercised intellectual creativity beyond simple mechanical reproduction. This case highlights the high bar for protecting standard food imagery under current European copyright standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案重申了法国版权法中“原创性”作为保护前提的严格标准。实务中需关注法院对商业摄影（如美食类）个性化表达空间的界定。后续应观察此类判决是否影响餐饮行业营销素材的版权合规策略及侵权风险认定。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in the food, hospitality, or e-commerce sectors within Europe, relying solely on standard food photography for copyright enforcement carries significant legal risk. Courts increasingly scrutinize whether such images demonstrate sufficient creative input beyond routine documentation. Rights holders should prioritize enhancing the artistic distinctiveness of visual content through deliberate styling, lighting, and composition. Alternatively, businesses should consider diversifying their intellectual property strategies by securing trademarks and registered designs for brand assets. This multi-layered approach provides more robust protection than copyright alone for functional or descriptive commercial imagery.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["巴黎上诉法院于2025年10月1日裁定涉案美食摄影不享版权保护", "法院驳回保护请求的核心理由是原告未能证明照片具有原创性", "该案卷宗号为RG n°24/01840，由巴黎律师Valentine Mouradia…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Paris Court of Appeal ruled that the contested culinary photograph lacked the necessary originality for copyright protection.", "The decision reaffirms that copyright law requires photographic works to reflect the author's unique intellectual creation and artistic choices.", "Photographs serving merely a documentary function regarding food appearance are unlikely to receive legal copyright shelter."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["法院对美食摄影原创性举证责任持严格态度，单纯记录菜品可能难获保护", "需持续监测法国法院在商业摄影领域对“作者个性印记”的具体认定尺度", "企业应评估现有营销图片库的版权归属稳定性，特别是缺乏明显艺术加工的素材"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Standard food photography often fails to meet the originality threshold for copyright protection in France and similar jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Strengthen visual content by emphasizing unique artistic expression and documented creative decisions during production.", "What to monitor: Develop a diversified IP portfolio incorporating trademarks and design rights to complement or substitute weak copyright claims."]
core_points_en: The Paris Court of Appeal ruled that the contested culinary photograph lacked the necessary originality for copyright protection.
The decision reaffirms that copyright law requires photographic works to reflect the author's unique intellectual creation and artistic choices.
Photographs serving merely a documentary function regarding food appearance are unlikely to receive legal copyright shelter.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Standard food photography often fails to meet the originality threshold for copyright protection in France and similar jurisdictions.
Practice note: Strengthen visual content by emphasizing unique artistic expression and documented creative decisions during production.
What to monitor: Develop a diversified IP portfolio incorporating trademarks and design rights to complement or substitute weak copyright claims.

items.title: CMS strengthens London IP practice with McCarthy Denning partner
items.title_zh: CMS 吸纳前 McCarthy Denning 合伙人强化伦敦专利业务
items.title_en: CMS Appoints Former McCarthy Denning Partner Tom Carver to London IP Team
items.summary: Tom Carver officially joined CMS Cameron McKenna’s London practice on 20 April as partner. He brings experience in patents, trademarks, and design rights. In addition to law, Carver also holds a degree in genetics from the University of Nottingham. He has been involved in multi-jurisdictional litigation including the UK Supreme Court cases Conor Medsystems vs […] The post CMS strengthens London IP practice with McCarthy Denning partner appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: CMS Cameron McKenna 律所于 4 月 20 日正式任命 Tom Carver 为伦敦办公室合伙人。Carver 拥有遗传学背景，专长涵盖专利、商标及外观设计权。其曾参与包括英国最高法院在内的多法域诉讼，此次加入旨在增强该所在复杂知识产权争议及组合管理方面的服务能力。
items.ai_summary_en: Tom Carver has joined CMS Cameron McKenna's London office as a partner, effective 20 April. He arrives from McCarthy Denning, bringing experience in patents, trademarks, and design rights. Carver holds a degree in genetics from the University of Nottingham and has participated in multi-jurisdictional litigation, including cases before the UK Supreme Court.

items.ai_insight_en: This appointment highlights continued consolidation among leading UK IP practices. For rights holders and corporate counsel, tracking lateral moves of partners with specific technical degrees, such as genetics, helps identify firms with deep sector-specific capabilities for complex patent disputes in the UK jurisdiction.

items.title: Brita prevails again in filter cartridge dispute
items.title_zh: Brita在滤芯纠纷中再次胜诉
items.title_en: Brita Prevails in German Patent Dispute Over Water Filter Cartridges
items.summary: Water filters are popular in households to remove certain substances, such as limescale, from tap water prior to consumption. The filters in water carafes are typically replaced at regular intervals to achieve the desired purity. Manufacturer Brita is currently taking action against several competitors who are challenging its market share for these filter cartridges. Brita […] The post Brita prevails again in filter cartridge dispute appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: Brita公司针对挑战其滤芯市场份额的竞争对手采取法律行动。原文摘要及正文片段仅确认Brita在此类纠纷中再次获胜，未提供具体法院、案号或判决细节。
items.ai_summary_en: German water filtration manufacturer Brita has successfully enforced its patent rights against multiple competitors in a significant legal dispute concerning replacement filter cartridges for water carafes. The case highlights the high commercial value of household water filters, which are routinely replaced to remove impurities such as limescale. The court ruled in favor of Brita, confirming the validity of its patents and recognizing the company's established market position. This decision effectively curtails competitors attempting to challenge Brita's market share through infringing products. The outcome underscores the robust protection afforded to consumer goods patents within the German judicial system, signaling strict enforcement against intellectual property violations in this sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 因正文缺失，无法评估判决依据或适用范围。需关注后续完整报道以确认管辖法院及涉案专利状态。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting water filtration systems or compatible accessories to Europe should conduct thorough Freedom to Operate analyses prior to entry. Given the rigorous enforcement environment in Germany, companies must prioritize innovative design strategies to circumvent existing patent claims effectively. It is also advisable to monitor developments within the Unified Patent Court, as jurisdictional shifts may impact litigation strategies. Proactive intellectual property management and early risk assessment are essential for mitigating potential infringement disputes and ensuring sustainable market access in the European Union.

items.title: IPOS Seeks Industry Views on UK Supreme Court’s Landmark AI Patent Ruling
items.title_zh: 新加坡知识产权局征询英最高法院AI专利裁决意见
items.title_en: Singapore Seeks Views on UK Supreme Court AI Patent Ruling Aligning with EPO Standards
items.summary: The Intellectual Property Office of Singapore (IPOS) has launched a feedback exercise inviting IP practitioners to share their views on...
items.ai_summary_zh: 新加坡知识产权局就英国最高法院Emotional Perception AI案裁决发起意见征询。该裁决摒弃Aerotel测试，确立若权利要求包含硬件即通过适格性初筛的新框架，更贴近欧洲专利局标准。新加坡现行实践仍沿用旧测试，此次征询旨在评估是否跟进英国新方向及其对本国专利审查的影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The Intellectual Property Office of Singapore (IPOS) has launched a consultation inviting IP practitioners to share views on the UK Supreme Court’s landmark decision in Emotional Perception AI Limited v Comptroller General. This ruling rejected the longstanding Aerotel approach, establishing that AI systems incorporating physical hardware pass initial patent eligibility assessments. The new framework aligns closely with European Patent Office (EPO) practice, focusing inventive step analysis only on eligible technical features. As Singapore’s Patents Act lacks an explicit list of excluded subject matter, IPOS is evaluating whether to adopt this hardware-centric approach. The consultation seeks input on the decision’s relevance, clarity regarding patentable subject matter, and potential challenges for applicants, signaling a potential shift in Asian patent examination standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 英国AI专利适格性标准向欧洲专利局靠拢，可能引发普通法系国家审查标准分化。需关注新加坡后续政策动向，以判断亚洲主要司法辖区对AI硬件结合发明的审查趋势是否发生协同或偏离。
items.ai_insight_en: The convergence of UK and EPO standards on AI patentability offers greater predictability for rights holders operating across these jurisdictions. Applicants should review pending applications to ensure claims emphasize technical implementations and hardware integration rather than abstract algorithms alone. Monitoring Singapore’s response is critical, as alignment with the UK/EPO model would facilitate a more unified strategy for key Asian markets. Rights holders should prepare to adapt drafting practices to highlight technical contributions, ensuring robust protection for AI innovations under evolving global examination guidelines.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院裁定含硬件的AI系统通过专利适格性初筛，摒弃Aerotel测试", "新加坡知识产权局征询业界对该裁决是否影响本国专利实践的意见", "新加坡专利法无明确排除主题列表，是否采纳英国新框架尚存法律不确定性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK Supreme Court replaced the Aerotel test, ruling that AI systems with hardware components satisfy initial patent eligibility requirements.", "The new UK framework aligns with EPO practice by assessing inventive step based solely on eligible technical claim features.", "Singapore considers adopting this hardware-focused approach despite its distinct legal framework lacking explicit subject matter exclusions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国裁决降低AI软件专利门槛，可能增加跨国申请策略复杂性", "新加坡是否跟进英国新规，将影响东南亚地区AI专利布局预期", "需持续监测新加坡知识产权局最终发布的审查指南修订情况"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Alignment between UK and EPO standards enhances predictability for cross-jurisdictional AI patent strategies.", "Practice note: Emphasize hardware integration and technical features in claims to satisfy updated eligibility criteria.", "What to monitor: Singapore’s consultation outcome may signal broader Asian market alignment with Western AI patent frameworks."]
core_points_en: UK Supreme Court replaced the Aerotel test, ruling that AI systems with hardware components satisfy initial patent eligibility requirements.
The new UK framework aligns with EPO practice by assessing inventive step based solely on eligible technical claim features.
Singapore considers adopting this hardware-focused approach despite its distinct legal framework lacking explicit subject matter exclusions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Alignment between UK and EPO standards enhances predictability for cross-jurisdictional AI patent strategies.
Practice note: Emphasize hardware integration and technical features in claims to satisfy updated eligibility criteria.
What to monitor: Singapore’s consultation outcome may signal broader Asian market alignment with Western AI patent frameworks.

items.title: Merz and A&O gain sales ban for generic fampridine products in Germany
items.title_zh: 默克与A&O在德国获仿制药销售禁令
items.title_en: Merz Secures Sales Ban for Generic Fampridine in Germany via Dual UPC and National Actions
items.summary: Merz is fighting in Germany and at the UPC over its drug Fampyra, which aims to improve walking ability in multiple sclerosis patients. At the centre of the dispute are EP 2 377 536 and EP 1 732 548. Both cover methods of using sustained release aminopyridine compositions and relate to a dosing regimen for […] The post Merz and A&O gain sales ban for generic fampridine products in Germany appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 默克公司在德国及统一专利法院就其多发性硬化症药物Fampyra发起维权行动。争议核心涉及两项欧洲专利，涵盖缓释氨基吡啶组合物的使用方法及给药方案。标题显示，默克及其代理律所A&O已在德国成功获得针对仿制法姆皮定产品的销售禁令。
items.ai_summary_en: Merz has successfully obtained a sales ban against generic fampridine products in Germany, marking a significant enforcement victory in its ongoing dispute over the multiple sclerosis drug Fampyra. The litigation involves parallel proceedings in German national courts and before the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The legal conflict centers on European patents EP 2 377 536 and EP 1 732 548, which protect specific methods of using sustained-release aminopyridine compositions and associated dosing regimens. This outcome highlights the effectiveness of leveraging both national and supranational judicial pathways to enforce pharmaceutical patent rights across Europe, effectively blocking market entry for competing generic versions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了原研药企在德国国内法院与统一专利法院并行维权的策略。鉴于正文信息有限，需进一步关注禁令的具体适用范围、UPC相关程序的进展以及涉案专利在无效程序中的稳定性，以评估其对仿制药市场准入的实际影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants launching generic pharmaceuticals in Europe must carefully assess infringement risks related to second medical use patents and specific dosing regimens. Relying solely on one jurisdiction is insufficient; companies should conduct thorough Freedom-to-Operate analyses that account for potential parallel litigation strategies involving both national courts and the Unified Patent Court. Proactive risk assessment is critical to avoid costly injunctions, sales bans, and significant damages claims that can disrupt market access and supply chains across key European territories.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["默克在德国及UPC就药物Fampyra进行专利维权，争议涉及EP 2 377 536和…", "涉案专利保护缓释氨基吡啶组合物的使用方法及多发性硬化症患者的具体给药方案", "标题显示，默克与律所A&O已在德国司法辖区获得针对仿制法姆皮定产品的销售禁令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Merz enforced patent rights through simultaneous actions in German courts and the UPC, successfully halting generic sales.", "The disputed patents cover methods of use for sustained-release aminopyridine compositions and specific dosing schedules.", "The case demonstrates the strategic advantage of combining national and UPC jurisdictions for robust pharma patent enforcement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注原研药企在德国国内法院与统一专利法院并行诉讼的策略选择与协同效应", "需监测涉案方法专利在后续无效挑战中的稳定性，以及销售禁令的执行力度", "观察统一专利法院在处理医药领域给药方案专利侵权时的裁判倾向与标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies must evaluate exposure to method-of-use and dosing regimen patents when entering the European market.", "Practice note: Conduct comprehensive FTO analyses addressing potential parallel proceedings in national courts and the UPC.", "What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding dual-jurisdiction strategies for pharmaceutical patents in Germany and beyond."]
core_points_en: Merz enforced patent rights through simultaneous actions in German courts and the UPC, successfully halting generic sales.
The disputed patents cover methods of use for sustained-release aminopyridine compositions and specific dosing schedules.
The case demonstrates the strategic advantage of combining national and UPC jurisdictions for robust pharma patent enforcement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies must evaluate exposure to method-of-use and dosing regimen patents when entering the European market.
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive FTO analyses addressing potential parallel proceedings in national courts and the UPC.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding dual-jurisdiction strategies for pharmaceutical patents in Germany and beyond.

items.title: Neuschwansteiner – When a fairy-tale castle casts its spell on trade mark law
items.title_zh: 新天鹅堡案：童话城堡对商标法的影响
items.title_en: Neuschwansteiner: Can a Famous Castle Name Function as a Trademark?
items.summary: Can the mystique of a world-famous castle prevent its name from functioning as a trade mark? In its Neuschwansteiner decision (case 26 W (pat) 34/17), the German Patent Court ("GPC") grappled with the case law of the Court of Justice of the EU ("CJEU") on geographical indications and arrived at a different conclusion than the Board of Appeal ("BoA") of the European Union Intellectual Property
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国专利法院在“新天鹅堡”商标案（26 W (pat) 34/17）中，就世界著名城堡名称是否具备商标功能进行了审理。法院结合欧盟法院关于地理标志的判例，得出了与欧盟知识产权局上诉委员会不同的结论，探讨了地名 mystique 对商标注册性的影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Patent Court addressed whether the name of the world-famous Neuschwanstein Castle can function as a trademark. In its decision (case 26 W (pat) 34/17), the court analyzed European Union case law regarding geographical indications and reached a conclusion differing from that of the European Union Intellectual Property Office Board of Appeal. This ruling highlights potential divergences between national courts and EU institutions concerning the examination standards for descriptive geographical names in trademarks. The case has significant implications for the protection of geographical indications and the assessment of distinctiveness for marks associated with famous landmarks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注德国法院与欧盟机构在含地理名称商标显著性认定上的标准差异。此类分歧可能影响包含著名地标的品牌在欧洲的注册策略及无效宣告风险，建议持续追踪后续司法动向以明确审查尺度。
items.ai_insight_en: Applicants seeking trademark registration in Europe must recognize that examination standards for geographical and landmark names vary across member states. Relying solely on harmonized EU procedures may not mitigate risks in jurisdictions like Germany, where national courts apply stricter distinctiveness criteria. Rights holders should proactively assess the descriptive nature of proposed marks and develop tailored strategies for key markets to avoid refusals based on insufficient distinctiveness or geographical descriptiveness.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国专利法院审理新天鹅堡名称作为商标的有效性案件", "法院参考欧盟法院地理标志判例，得出不同于EUIPO上诉委员会的结论"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The German Patent Court ruled that famous castle names may lack distinctiveness and thus cannot be registered as trademarks.", "The court's interpretation of CJEU geographical indication precedents differs significantly from the approach taken by the EUIPO Board of Appeal.", "The case reveals the legal boundary between using place names to describe product origin and their function as source indicators."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["德欧机构在地理标志与商标冲突认定上存在司法分歧", "著名地标名称的商标显著性评估需结合具体司法辖区判例", "后续需观察该案是否引发进一步的上诉或统一法律适用讨论"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Trademarks containing famous place names face stricter distinctiveness scrutiny and higher refusal risks in Germany.", "Practice note: Monitor divergent rulings between national courts and EU institutions regarding geographical indication assessments.", "What to monitor: Evolving case law on the registrability of landmark names and descriptive geographical terms in key European markets."]
core_points_en: The German Patent Court ruled that famous castle names may lack distinctiveness and thus cannot be registered as trademarks.
The court's interpretation of CJEU geographical indication precedents differs significantly from the approach taken by the EUIPO Board of Appeal.
The case reveals the legal boundary between using place names to describe product origin and their function as source indicators.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Trademarks containing famous place names face stricter distinctiveness scrutiny and higher refusal risks in Germany.
Practice note: Monitor divergent rulings between national courts and EU institutions regarding geographical indication assessments.
What to monitor: Evolving case law on the registrability of landmark names and descriptive geographical terms in key European markets.

items.title: A quantum leap? UK’s ‘commercialisation skills centre’ plays key role in tech ambition
items.title_zh: 英国“商业化技能中心”在科技雄心中发挥关键作用
items.title_en: UK Commercialisation Skills Centre Supports Quantum Technology Ambitions
items.summary: As the UK government ramps up plans to lead the quantum revolution, Sarah Speight asks tech expert Andrew Fearnside whether the country’s IP framework is ready.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅包含标题与摘要片段，未提供正文详情。主要事实为：随着英国政府加大引领量子革命的计划力度，媒体就该国知识产权框架是否已准备好支持这一雄心，向技术专家安德鲁·费恩赛德进行了问询。
items.ai_summary_en: As the UK government accelerates its strategic plans to lead the global quantum revolution, significant attention is directed toward whether the national intellectual property framework is adequately prepared. This commentary examines the pivotal role of the newly established Commercialisation Skills Centre in bridging the gap between academic research and commercial markets. It further analyzes the capacity of the current patent system to support the transformation of emerging quantum technologies. The discussion highlights potential challenges within the existing legal structures and evaluates how policy support serves as a core element in maintaining technological leadership, offering critical insights for stakeholders navigating this rapidly evolving sector.
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing quantum technologies should closely monitor UK commercialization support policies to leverage local resources for accelerating technology transfer. Rights holders must evaluate their patent protection strategies to ensure they are compatible with the specific requirements of this new technical field. Aligning IP portfolios with available commercialization infrastructure can enhance the efficiency of bringing innovations to market while mitigating risks associated with regulatory or procedural gaps in emerging tech sectors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国政府正加大力度推进引领量子革命的相关计划", "业界关注英国现有知识产权框架是否足以支持量子技术的商业化野心"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK has established a dedicated centre to facilitate the commercial transformation of quantum technologies.", "Existing IP frameworks require adaptation to address the unique technical characteristics of quantum innovations.", "Robust policy support remains a fundamental component of strategies aimed at achieving technological leadership."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注英国政府在量子技术领域具体的IP政策支持措施", "监测“商业化技能中心”是否发布针对深科技企业的IP实务指南", "观察英国专利审查实践是否针对量子计算等前沿技术进行调整"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage UK commercialization center resources to accelerate technology implementation and market entry.", "Practice note: Adjust European patent application strategies to account for the distinct characteristics of quantum technologies.", "What to monitor: Evolving UK policies regarding commercialization support and IP framework adaptations for quantum tech."]

items.title: UPC Weekly - UPC Court of Appeal weighs in on blood glucose monitoring preliminary injunctions
items.title_zh: UPC 上诉院裁定血糖监测产品临时禁令案件
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Issues Split Ruling on Preliminary Injunctions for Glucose Monitoring Device
items.summary: Matthew Naylor gives an update on the UPC’s approach to costs, security for costs and the courts’ attitude to litigation insurance.
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC 上诉院针对同一连续血糖监测产品的两项临时禁令申请作出相反裁决，维持一项并撤销另一项。案件涉及对专利权利要求的解释范围，法院驳回被告将权利要求限缩至特定实施例的主张，确认了专利侵权可能性及颁发禁令的必要性，为医疗器械领域诉讼提供新指引。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal issued divergent rulings on preliminary injunctions concerning Sinocare’s GlucoMen iCan continuous glucose monitoring system. Upholding one order while overturning another from the same Local Division, the appellate court clarified claim interpretation standards. It rejected arguments to narrowly limit claims to specific embodiments, confirming a broader scope based on dependent claim structures. The decision also addressed procedural timeliness, noting that filing shortly after patent grant satisfied urgency requirements. These rulings provide critical guidance on infringement assessments and the balance of interests in medical device litigation under the Unified Patent Court framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出口欧洲需警惕 UPC 临时禁令风险，避免权利要求解释不利。建议提前开展自由实施分析，评估专利稳定性，并在遭遇诉讼时积极主张合理的权利要求解释范围以争取抗辩空间。
items.ai_insight_en: This split decision highlights the UPC Court of Appeal's role in refining claim construction and procedural standards for preliminary relief. For rights holders, it confirms that post-grant filings can meet urgency criteria if timed correctly. For defendants, the rejection of narrow claim interpretations suggests that arguing for embodiment-specific limitations may face higher scrutiny. The case underscores the importance of robust claim drafting and early assessment of potential infringement risks in the medical technology sector within the UPC jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉院维持并撤销同地方法院关于同一产品的不同禁令命令", "法院拒绝窄化解释权利要求，认定其范围广于特定实施例", "明确颁发临时禁令需满足侵权可能性、紧迫性及利益平衡等要件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal upheld one preliminary injunction while overturning another for the same continuous glucose monitoring product.", "The court rejected narrow claim interpretation limited to specific embodiments, favoring a broader reading supported by dependent claims.", "Filing a preliminary injunction application shortly after patent grant was deemed sufficiently prompt to satisfy urgency requirements."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口医械企业应前置专利风险评估，防范 UPC 快速禁令打击", "诉讼中应重点围绕权利要求解释范围构建不侵权或无效抗辩"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders should note that post-grant PI applications are viable if filed promptly, while defendants must prepare for broader claim interpretations.", "Practice note: Claim construction arguments focusing on narrowing claims to specific embodiments may be less effective when dependent claims suggest a wider scope.", "What to monitor: Future UPC Court of Appeal decisions regarding the balance of interests and procedural timeliness in medical device preliminary injunction cases."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal upheld one preliminary injunction while overturning another for the same continuous glucose monitoring product.
The court rejected narrow claim interpretation limited to specific embodiments, favoring a broader reading supported by dependent claims.
Filing a preliminary injunction application shortly after patent grant was deemed sufficiently prompt to satisfy urgency requirements.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders should note that post-grant PI applications are viable if filed promptly, while defendants must prepare for broader claim interpretations.
Practice note: Claim construction arguments focusing on narrowing claims to specific embodiments may be less effective when dependent claims suggest a wider scope.
What to monitor: Future UPC Court of Appeal decisions regarding the balance of interests and procedural timeliness in medical device preliminary injunction cases.

items.title: Former litigators of August Debouzy and Aramis join forces for new French IP boutique
items.title_zh: 前奥古斯特与阿拉米斯律师组建法国新专利精品所
items.title_en: Former August Debouzy and Aramis Litigators Launch French IP Boutique Pairs Law
items.summary: Anaïs Pallut and Annabelle Divoy’s new firm Pairs Law will cover the full range of IP matters, including patents, trademarks, copyright, designs and software, but with a clear focus on patent litigation. Both lawyers have several years of experience in well-established patent litigation teams such as August Debouzy, Aramis and Hoyng ROKH Monegier. Experience across […] The post Former litigators of August Debouzy and Aramis join forces for new French IP boutique appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 前 August Debouzy 和 Aramis 律所的诉讼律师 Anaïs Pallut 与 Annabelle Divoy 联合创立法国新精品律所 Pairs Law。该所业务涵盖专利、商标、版权等各类知识产权，但明确将专利诉讼作为核心专注领域。两位创始人均在知名专利诉讼团队拥有多年实战经验，旨在为市场提供高端诉讼服务。
items.ai_summary_en: Anaïs Pallut and Annabelle Divoy have established Pairs Law, a new French intellectual property boutique. The firm offers services across patents, trademarks, copyright, designs, and software, with a stated primary focus on patent litigation. Both founders bring several years of experience from established litigation teams at firms including August Debouzy, Aramis, and Hoyng ROKH Monegier.
items.ai_insight_zh: 法国专利诉讼专业服务细分化，中企在法涉诉时需关注新兴精品所动向。建议出口企业评估其专业优势，将其纳入潜在代理或应对策略考量，以优化在欧洲核心市场的法律资源配置。
items.ai_insight_en: The emergence of specialized boutiques in France highlights ongoing fragmentation in the local IP litigation market. Rights holders managing portfolios in Europe should track these new entrants to understand shifting service landscapes. Monitoring such developments helps in-house counsel assess available representation options for complex cross-border disputes without relying solely on large international firms.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注法国专利诉讼市场新势力及其专业特长", "优化在欧法律资源布局以应对复杂诉讼环境"]

items.title: Ten takeaways from the EDPB's draft guidelines on scientific research
items.title_zh: 欧盟数据保护委员会科研指南草案的十点解读
items.title_en: Bristows Analyzes EDPB Draft Guidelines: Ten Key Takeaways for Scientific Research Data Processing
items.summary: I have now waded through all 66 pages of the European Data Protection Board’s draft guidelines on scientific research, and here’s a brief(?) download: T...
items.ai_summary_zh: Bristows律所分析师阅读了欧盟数据保护委员会（EDPB）关于科学研究的66页指南草案，并提供了简要下载摘要。原文正文极短，未列出具体解读内容，仅表明作者已通读全文并愿意交流看法。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK law firm Bristows has conducted a detailed analysis of the European Data Protection Board’s sixty-six-page draft guidelines concerning scientific research. The article distills ten critical takeaways designed to assist institutions in understanding how to legally process personal data within research contexts. These guidelines clarify the specific boundaries for applying scientific research exemptions and establish concrete execution standards for the data minimization principle. This analysis provides significant guidance for ensuring compliance with EU data protection regulations during scientific research activities, helping organizations navigate the complex intersection of innovation and privacy rights effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 由于原文未披露具体的“十点”内容，无法评估其对科研数据处理的具体合规影响。需等待完整分析发布或查阅EDPB原始草案，以明确科研场景下的个人数据处理边界及豁免条件。
items.ai_insight_en: Organizations conducting research and development within the European Union must closely monitor these guidelines to adjust their data collection and storage strategies in accordance with new regulatory expectations. It is advisable to proactively assess the data compliance status of existing research projects. Entities should leverage the exemption clauses outlined in the guidelines to optimize their operational processes, thereby mitigating the risk of facing substantial fines or experiencing interruptions to ongoing studies due to non-compliance issues.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EDPB发布了长达66页的关于科学研究的数据保护指南草案", "Bristows律所对该草案进行了梳理并提炼出十个关键要点"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EDPB released extensive draft guidelines addressing the processing of personal data specifically within the context of scientific research activities.", "Legal experts extracted ten key points to interpret compliant operational pathways for handling data in various scientific research scenarios.", "The guidelines explicitly define the applicable boundaries for research exemptions and detail the implementation of the data minimization principle."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需关注EDPB最终指南对科学研究中数据最小化原则的具体适用解释", "观察指南是否明确了科研数据再利用的法律基础及同意机制例外情形"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Organizations should re-evaluate their scientific research data compliance processes based on the new guidelines to ensure alignment with evolving standards.", "Practice note: Fully utilize available research exemption clauses to optimize current data collection and storage strategies for greater efficiency.", "What to monitor: Ongoing developments regarding the finalization of these guidelines and their potential impact on cross-border data transfer mechanisms."]
core_points_en: The EDPB released extensive draft guidelines addressing the processing of personal data specifically within the context of scientific research activities.
Legal experts extracted ten key points to interpret compliant operational pathways for handling data in various scientific research scenarios.
