items.title: UPC Weekly - Costs: reversed / agreed / guaranteed
items.title_zh: UPC周报：费用逆转、协议与担保
items.title_en: UPC Weekly: Judicial Reversal of Costs, Party Agreements, and Security for Costs Orders
items.summary: 2026 Week 15
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文正文及摘要仅包含“2026 Week 15”及标题信息，无具体案件事实或法律分析内容。依据证据不足模式，仅能确认该资讯为Mewburn Ellis发布的关于统一专利法院（UPC）费用议题的周报，涉及费用逆转、达成协议或担保等主题，具体细节缺失。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines three pivotal recent rulings by the Unified Patent Court concerning litigation costs. The discussion covers the Court’s reassessment and adjustment of cost allocations, the judicial recognition of party agreements regarding fee sharing, and instances where security for costs was mandated. These cases clarify the UPC’s judicial tendencies in managing litigation expenses, emphasizing the critical importance of procedural compliance and financial preparedness in cross-border patent disputes. The insights provide concrete reference points for parties to accurately estimate litigation risks and navigate the financial complexities inherent in UPC proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 由于缺乏正文细节，无法评估具体费用裁决趋势。需关注后续完整报告中关于UPC费用分摊原则的实际应用案例，特别是费用逆转与担保要求的司法实践动向。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in UPC litigation must prioritize the security for costs mechanism, ensuring adequate liquidity to address potential sudden demands. Establishing a detailed budget model is essential for effective financial planning. Furthermore, monitoring judicial discretion regarding cost apportionment allows for strategic negotiation to mitigate overall expenses. Strengthening awareness of procedural compliance is equally vital to avoid unnecessary financial burdens resulting from procedural defects. Proactive management of these elements helps rights holders control risk exposure and optimize resource allocation during complex cross-border patent enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题显示资讯主题涵盖UPC诉讼中的费用逆转、双方协议费用及费用担保机制", "原文摘要仅标注时间为2026年第15周，未提供具体判例或规则更新细节"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC reassessed specific case costs, issuing decisions to reverse or adjust previous cost allocation rulings.", "The Court confirmed that parties may agree on cost sharing, respecting these settlements and recording them officially.", "The Court clarified its authority to require plaintiffs to provide security for costs in specific risk-mitigation scenarios."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需观察UPC在费用逆转裁定中的具体考量因素", "需关注当事人达成费用协议对法院裁决的影响", "后续应监测费用担保在UPC诉讼中的适用频率与标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize security for costs mechanisms and plan liquidity early to handle unexpected financial demands.", "Practice note: Develop precise budget models and leverage negotiation to reduce total cross-border patent litigation costs.", "What to monitor: Track judicial discretion on cost apportionment and ensure strict procedural compliance to avoid penalties."]
core_points_en: The UPC reassessed specific case costs, issuing decisions to reverse or adjust previous cost allocation rulings.
The Court confirmed that parties may agree on cost sharing, respecting these settlements and recording them officially.
The Court clarified its authority to require plaintiffs to provide security for costs in specific risk-mitigation scenarios.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize security for costs mechanisms and plan liquidity early to handle unexpected financial demands.
Practice note: Develop precise budget models and leverage negotiation to reduce total cross-border patent litigation costs.
What to monitor: Track judicial discretion on cost apportionment and ensure strict procedural compliance to avoid penalties.

items.title: Brandsmiths acquires Sipara, moves into UK’s top 10 TM filing firms
items.title_zh: Brandsmiths收购Sipara跻身英国商标申请前十强
items.title_en: Brandsmiths Acquires Sipara, Entering UK's Top Ten Trademark Filing Firms
items.summary: While Sipara will continue operating under its existing name and leadership for now, both firms plan to present a united front at the INTA Annual Meeting in London
items.ai_summary_zh: Brandsmiths收购Sipara后，晋升为英国商标申请量排名前十的律所。Sipara目前将继续保留原有名称和管理团队独立运营。双方计划在伦敦举行的INTA年度会议上以统一形象亮相。
items.ai_summary_en: UK-based trademark agency Brandsmiths has announced the completion of its acquisition of peer firm Sipara. This strategic merger significantly expands Brandsmiths' operational scale, successfully propelling it into the top ten law firms in the United Kingdom for trademark filing volume. Although Sipara will continue to operate independently under its existing name and leadership team in the short term to ensure business continuity, both entities plan to present a unified front at the upcoming International Trademark Association Annual Meeting in London. This development marks a notable step toward further consolidation within the UK trademark service market, highlighting the growing trend of integration among leading intellectual property service providers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案反映英国商标代理服务市场的整合趋势。需关注并购后两家机构在业务协同、客户资源整合及后续品牌统一方面的具体进展，以及其对英国商标申请服务市场格局的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: The concentration of the UK trademark service market is increasing, with head effects becoming more pronounced. Rights holders should monitor changes in service processes following large-scale firm integrations and assess the global coordination capabilities of these consolidated entities. It is advisable to leverage the evolving competitive landscape to re-examine existing agency cooperation models. Ensuring that brands receive efficient strategic support requires careful evaluation of how agent changes impact maintenance strategies and overall service efficiency in this consolidating market environment.

items.ai_summary_zh: IAM Media报道，科技巨头三星与诺基亚分别与其专利许可对手达成和解。三星获得了Hannibal IP持有的5G标准必要专利许可，而诺基亚则就Nyckel UI Technologies拥有的物联网（IoT）专利达成了授权协议。这两项交易均涉及Longhorn IP旗下的实体，标志着双方在相关技术领域的知识产权争议得到解决，反映了全球通信与物联网领域专利许可市场的活跃态势及头部企业对合规运营的重视。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧应密切关注此类NPE许可动向，尤其是5G与IoT领域。建议提前进行FTO分析，建立专利预警机制，并参考行业头部企业的许可费率与条款，制定灵活的谈判策略，以规避高额诉讼成本与市场禁入风险。

items.title: European patent office fee increases: What applicants must know
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局费用上调：申请人须知
items.title_en: European Patent Fee Shifts: EPO Modest Rise Contrasts with Sharp UK Hike
items.summary: Europe’s latest patent fee increases are modest at the EPO but include a rare steep hike in the UK, forcing applicants to reassess filing routes, portfolio value, and long-term cost strategies, say James Bell and Yelena Morozova.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出，欧洲最新的专利费用调整中，欧洲专利局（EPO）的涨幅较为温和。相比之下，英国出现了罕见的大幅费用上涨。这一差异迫使申请人重新评估其专利申请路线、组合价值以及长期成本策略。文章由James Bell和Yelena Morozova撰写，旨在分析费用变化对申请人的影响。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent adjustments to European patent fees present a divergent landscape for rights holders. The European Patent Office has implemented modest increases, keeping overall costs relatively predictable and manageable for applicants. In stark contrast, the United Kingdom Intellectual Property Office has introduced a rare and significant fee hike. This substantial increase in UK charges significantly elevates the financial burden for maintaining or validating patents within that jurisdiction. Consequently, market participants are compelled to reassess their strategic filing routes, evaluate the commercial value of existing portfolios, and refine long-term cost management strategies. The disparity between the two offices necessitates a careful review of resource allocation to ensure that patent protection remains economically viable across different European territories.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注EPO与英国费用结构分化对跨国申请策略的影响。实务中应监测不同法域成本变化对投资组合估值的具体冲击，以及申请人如何据此调整长期预算规划，而非仅依赖单一司法辖区的成本模型。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should critically reassess the cost-benefit ratio of maintaining patent protection in the UK given the sharp fee increases. For markets where commercial returns do not justify the elevated costs, adjusting validation strategies or relying on centralized European Patent Office procedures may offer more efficient resource utilization. Companies should avoid retaining low-value patents in high-cost jurisdictions solely out of habit. Instead, a data-driven approach to portfolio pruning and strategic routing is essential to mitigate financial pressure while preserving key intellectual property assets across Europe.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲专利局最新专利费用涨幅 modest，整体调整幅度较小", "英国专利费用出现罕见大幅上涨，与欧洲大陆形成鲜明对比", "费用差异迫使申请人重新审视申请路径、组合价值及长期成本策略"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The European Patent Office implements modest fee increases, ensuring that overall procedural costs remain largely controlled and predictable for applicants.", "The UK Intellectual Property Office enacts a rare, steep fee hike, substantially increasing the financial burden for local patent applications and validations.", "Applicants must urgently re-evaluate filing routes and long-term portfolio strategies to address the growing cost disparity between European jurisdictions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EPO与英国费用走势分化，可能改变传统欧洲申请路径的经济性", "申请人需动态评估专利组合在不同法域下的持有成本与价值匹配度", "长期成本策略需纳入各国费用波动因素，避免静态预算导致的偏差"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Re-evaluate the cost-effectiveness of UK patent holdings to avoid unnecessary expenditure in non-core markets.", "Practice note: Leverage European Patent Office procedures to optimize cross-border application paths and reduce reliance on costly national validations.", "What to monitor: Track further regulatory changes in national IP offices that may impact the total cost of ownership for European patent portfolios."]
core_points_en: The European Patent Office implements modest fee increases, ensuring that overall procedural costs remain largely controlled and predictable for applicants.
The UK Intellectual Property Office enacts a rare, steep fee hike, substantially increasing the financial burden for local patent applications and validations.
Applicants must urgently re-evaluate filing routes and long-term portfolio strategies to address the growing cost disparity between European jurisdictions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Re-evaluate the cost-effectiveness of UK patent holdings to avoid unnecessary expenditure in non-core markets.
Practice note: Leverage European Patent Office procedures to optimize cross-border application paths and reduce reliance on costly national validations.
What to monitor: Track further regulatory changes in national IP offices that may impact the total cost of ownership for European patent portfolios.

items.title: Patents In Construction. A Technology Dispute Starts Much Earlier Than With The Filing Of A Lawsuit
items.title_zh: 建筑业专利：技术纠纷早于诉讼立案即已始
items.title_en: Patents in Construction: Technology Disputes Arise Before Litigation Filing
items.summary: In the construction industry, success is nowadays less often determined only by the lowest price or deadline. Technology has become a new field of competitive struggle, as it dictates the conditions on construction sites. Practice shows that it is the patents that increasingly often determine who can build, sell, and make money from innovations, and who will be forced to pay high compensation for violating someone else's rights
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出建筑行业竞争重心转向技术，专利成为决定市场准入与赔偿风险的关键。波兰建筑专利纠纷逐年增加且复杂化，涵盖屋顶、幕墙等领域。EPO数据显示欧盟建筑相关专利申请持续高位，绿色建材占比提升。专利保护范围扩展至智能方案、复合材料及施工设备等改进型创新。
items.ai_summary_en: Technology now drives competitive advantage in the construction industry, with patents determining market access and liability for infringement. In Poland, patent disputes are increasing in frequency and complexity, covering areas from roofing and facade structures to specialized hospital air systems. Protection extends beyond breakthrough inventions to include practical improvements that reduce costs or enhance quality, such as smart solutions, durable composites, and advanced insulation. As intellectual property becomes a key component of company valuation, rights holders must recognize that exclusive rights create market monopolies. The shift indicates that technical competition dictates site conditions, making IP strategy central to commercial success and risk management in modern construction projects across the sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，波兰后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Construction firms should view IP as a core business asset rather than a legal afterthought. With disputes escalating in Poland, early identification of protectable improvements is critical. Companies must assess their portfolios for both defensive and offensive value, recognizing that even incremental technical gains can secure market exclusivity and mitigate infringement risks in an increasingly technology-driven sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["波兰建筑行业专利纠纷数量与复杂度逐年上升，涵盖屋顶技术及医疗设备等多元领域", "EPO数据显示欧盟建筑相关专利申请量维持高位，环境技术与绿色建筑专利占比日益重要", "专利保护对象不仅限于突破性发明，更包括降低成本、缩短工期的改进型技术与智能方案"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Polish construction patent disputes are rising in number and complexity, covering diverse technical fields.", "Protection targets practical improvements like smart systems and composites, not just breakthrough inventions.", "Intellectual property assets now significantly influence company valuation and competitive positioning in construction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建筑行业专利纠纷在波兰呈上升趋势，显示该法域执法活跃度提高", "EPO数据印证绿色建筑与智能技术为研发与申请热点，具高商业价值", "需持续监控非突破性但具实效的技术改进是否纳入专利保护范围"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Patents determine market access and financial liability, making IP central to construction company valuation.", "Practice note: Secure protection for incremental improvements like smart systems and materials, not only major breakthroughs.", "What to monitor: Rising complexity of patent disputes in Poland across roofing, facades, and specialized installation technologies."]

items.title: George Orwell: Is fame an obstacle to trademark protection?
items.title_zh: 乔治·奥威尔：名声是否阻碍商标保护？
items.title_en: EUIPO Grand Board: Fame Can Render Names Descriptive in Cultural Sectors
items.summary: Discover why the George Orwell trademark was partially rejected in the EU, and the challenges of registering famous names as trademarks.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局大上诉委员会维持对“GEORGE ORWELL”商标的部分驳回。因作者知名度极高，公众将名字视为文化产品内容主题而非来源标识，故在文化相关类别上缺乏显著性且具描述性。尽管人名通常可注册，但例外情况下名声会导致其丧失商标功能。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO Grand Board of Appeal upheld the partial refusal of the GEORGE ORWELL trademark for cultural goods and services. The Board ruled that the author’s exceptional fame causes the relevant public to perceive the name as indicating the subject matter of products like books and media, rather than their commercial origin. Consequently, the sign was deemed descriptive and lacking distinctiveness for classes covering audiovisual media, printed matter, and entertainment activities. This decision highlights that while personal names are typically distinctive, extreme renown can transform them into descriptive indicators of content characteristics, thereby barring registration in sectors directly associated with the individual’s legacy.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies that high renown does not guarantee trademark protection for personal names. In cultural sectors, fame may trigger descriptiveness refusals if consumers view the name as content-related. Rights holders should anticipate higher evidentiary burdens when registering famous names for associated goods, as distinctiveness is assessed through the lens of public perception regarding the name’s informational value versus its source-identifying function.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["大上诉委员会认定乔治·奥威尔之名在文化领域具描述性，因公众视其为内容主题", "极高知名度导致该名称无法发挥识别商业来源的商标功能，因而缺乏显著性", "申请人及国际商标协会抗辩称，对名人姓名的处理存在不平等待遇问题"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Grand Board ruled George Orwell’s fame makes the name descriptive of cultural content.", "Public perceives the name as subject matter indicator, not commercial source identifier.", "Trademark refused for lack of distinctiveness in media, print, and entertainment classes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["名人姓名在强关联商品上可能因被感知为内容主题而丧失商标显著性", "需关注公众认知如何将高知名度人名转化为商品特征而非来源标识", "后续需观察此类裁决对其他人格权或名人品牌商标注册策略的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Famous names face heightened refusal risks in culturally related classes due to perceived descriptiveness.", "Practice note: Distinctiveness assessments now weigh whether fame transforms a name into a content characteristic indicator.", "What to monitor: Future EUIPO decisions applying this fame-based descriptiveness standard to other historical or celebrity figures."]
core_points_en: Grand Board ruled George Orwell’s fame makes the name descriptive of cultural content.
Public perceives the name as subject matter indicator, not commercial source identifier.
Trademark refused for lack of distinctiveness in media, print, and entertainment classes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Famous names face heightened refusal risks in culturally related classes due to perceived descriptiveness.
Practice note: Distinctiveness assessments now weigh whether fame transforms a name into a content characteristic indicator.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO decisions applying this fame-based descriptiveness standard to other historical or celebrity figures.

items.title: Win without gain: Microsoft prevails in cost dispute with Suinno
items.title_zh: 微软在Suinno专利案费用争议中胜诉
items.title_en: Microsoft Prevails in Cost Dispute with Suinno but Secures No Substantive Relief
items.summary: Finnish company Suinno is the owner of EP 2 671 173 covering a “method and means for browsing by walking”. It relates to people browsing the internet by walking to discover search results as they come into close proximity. The Helsinki-based company accuses Microsoft of infringing the patent and sued the US tech giant at […] The post Win without gain: Microsoft prevails in cost dispute with Suinno appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 芬兰公司Suinno拥有EP 2 671 173号专利，涉及“通过行走浏览”技术。该公司指控微软侵权并提起诉讼。最新进展显示，微软在与Suinno的费用争议中获胜，尽管Suinno是专利权人，但在该程序性争议中未获实质收益。
items.ai_summary_en: Suinno, a Finnish entity holding European patent EP 2 671 173 for a 'walking browsing' method, initiated infringement proceedings against Microsoft in Helsinki. The patent covers technologies enabling users to discover internet search results while walking. Although Microsoft successfully defended its position in the subsequent cost dispute regarding legal fees, it did not receive any substantive compensation or financial benefit from the ruling. This outcome underscores a critical distinction in European patent litigation: procedural victories on ancillary matters like costs do not automatically translate into commercial gains or damages. The case illustrates how rights holders and defendants may incur significant expenses without achieving meaningful economic resolution, highlighting the complex risk profile of enforcing or defending intellectual property rights in cross-border jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案处于证据不足模式，仅能确认费用争议结果。需关注后续侵权实体审理进展及芬兰法院对欧洲专利侵权判定的具体立场，目前缺乏足够信息评估对市场的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaging in European patent disputes must carefully evaluate the cost-benefit ratio of litigation strategies. A favorable ruling on procedural issues, such as legal costs, does not guarantee overall financial success if substantive infringement claims remain unproven or if no damages are awarded. Companies should anticipate scenarios where legal wins result in net losses due to high operational and legal expenditures. It is advisable to develop diversified dispute resolution plans that include settlement options alongside traditional litigation. Proactive management of procedural cost risks is essential to avoid situations where defending or enforcing rights leads to pyrrhic victories rather than sustainable business outcomes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["芬兰公司Suinno拥有EP 2 671 173号专利，指控微软侵权", "微软在与Suinno的诉讼费用争议中取得胜诉", "原文仅披露费用争议结果，未提供侵权判定细节"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Suinno holds European patent EP 2 671 173 covering walking-based browsing technology and accused Microsoft of infringement.", "Microsoft won the specific cost dispute against Suinno but failed to secure any actual monetary compensation or damages.", "The case demonstrates that procedural victories in patent litigation do not necessarily equate to tangible commercial benefits."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["仅知悉费用争议结果，侵权实体判决尚不明确", "需持续监控芬兰法院对该欧洲专利侵权的最终认定", "观察此类程序性胜诉对整体诉讼策略的实际影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigation success does not always yield financial returns; parties must weigh total costs against potential recovery.", "Practice note: Effective risk management requires assessing procedural cost exposure early in European patent enforcement actions.", "What to monitor: Track judicial trends in awarding costs versus substantive damages to refine future litigation budgeting."]
core_points_en: Suinno holds European patent EP 2 671 173 covering walking-based browsing technology and accused Microsoft of infringement.
Microsoft won the specific cost dispute against Suinno but failed to secure any actual monetary compensation or damages.
The case demonstrates that procedural victories in patent litigation do not necessarily equate to tangible commercial benefits.

Practice note: Effective risk management requires assessing procedural cost exposure early in European patent enforcement actions.
What to monitor: Track judicial trends in awarding costs versus substantive damages to refine future litigation budgeting.

items.title: Distribution issues: Italian Competition Authority fines Morellato for resale price maintenance and online marketplace restrictions
items.title_zh: 意竞争局因转售限价及平台禁令罚没莫雷拉托
items.title_en: Italian Competition Authority Fines Morellato for Resale Price Maintenance and Discriminatory Online Marketplace Bans
items.summary: On 31 March 2026, the Italian Competition Authority (ICA) announced that it had imposed a fine of just under €26 million on Morellato, a leading Italian...
items.ai_summary_zh: 意大利竞争管理局对钟表商莫雷拉托处以近二千六百万欧元罚款，认定其实施转售价格维持及歧视性禁止经销商使用电商平台。尽管欧盟法院允许奢侈品基于品牌形象限制平台销售，但本案因品牌方自身利用该平台而构成歧视，违反比例原则。
items.ai_summary_en: On 31 March 2026, the Italian Competition Authority (ICA) imposed a fine of nearly €26 million on Morellato for antitrust violations. The authority found that the watch and jewellery manufacturer engaged in resale price maintenance by preventing distributors from freely setting prices. Additionally, Morellato prohibited authorised distributors from selling on online marketplaces like Amazon and eBay while simultaneously using these platforms for its own sales. The ICA ruled this ban discriminatory and disproportionate, rejecting arguments based on brand image preservation under the CJEU’s Coty judgment. This decision underscores strict enforcement against hardcore restrictions and non-uniform application of selective distribution rules.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企应审查分销协议，避免设定最低转售价。若限制经销商入驻电商平台，须确保自身未在同一平台销售，以防被认定为歧视性限制而招致反垄断重罚。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights significant compliance risks for brands operating selective distribution systems in Europe. While the CJEU’s Coty judgment permits luxury goods suppliers to restrict third-party marketplace use to protect brand image, such restrictions must be proportionate and non-discriminatory. Rights holders must ensure their internal sales practices align with distributor obligations. Discrepancies between supplier behavior and contractual restrictions can lead to severe penalties and invalidate justifications based on brand protection or anti-counterfeiting measures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["固定或最低转售价格属核心限制，系近年欧盟及各成员国执法重点", "禁止使用电商平台需统一非歧视，品牌方自用则不得禁止经销商使用", "该案警示选择性分销体系中网络销售限制的合规边界与比例原则"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Resale price maintenance remains a hardcore competition restriction and a key enforcement priority for national authorities and the European Commission.", "Online marketplace bans must be applied uniformly; suppliers cannot restrict distributors while utilizing the same platforms for direct sales.", "The ICA rejected proportionality defenses, citing Commission Guidelines that deem such restrictions inappropriate when the supplier self-preferences the channel."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严禁在协议中约定固定或最低转售价格，规避核心限制风险", "实施平台禁令须保持中立，品牌方自营与经销商政策必须一致"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands must audit selective distribution agreements to ensure online sales restrictions do not conflict with the supplier's own commercial activities on the same…", "Practice note: Avoid fixed or minimum resale prices entirely, as these constitute hardcore restrictions with high enforcement risk across EU jurisdictions.", "What to monitor: Monitor national competition authority decisions regarding the proportionality of online marketplace bans, particularly where suppliers engage in direct-to-consum…"]
core_points_en: Resale price maintenance remains a hardcore competition restriction and a key enforcement priority for national authorities and the European Commission.
Online marketplace bans must be applied uniformly; suppliers cannot restrict distributors while utilizing the same platforms for direct sales.
The ICA rejected proportionality defenses, citing Commission Guidelines that deem such restrictions inappropriate when the supplier self-preferences the channel.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands must audit selective distribution agreements to ensure online sales restrictions do not conflict with the supplier's own commercial activities on the same…
Practice note: Avoid fixed or minimum resale prices entirely, as these constitute hardcore restrictions with high enforcement risk across EU jurisdictions.
What to monitor: Monitor national competition authority decisions regarding the proportionality of online marketplace bans, particularly where suppliers engage in direct-to-consum…

items.title: Awaiting the CJEU’s judgment in IKEA (C-298/23): trade mark rights vs freedom of expression
items.title_zh: 等待欧盟法院在宜家案中的判决：商标权与言论自由
items.title_en: CJEU Judgment Pending in IKEA Case: Trademark Rights Versus Freedom of Expression
items.summary: One of the most anticipated rulings of the CJEU in trade mark law this year is the one in the IKEA case (C-298/23), which concerns the widely discussed conflict between trade mark rights and freedom of expression. See on this point also the previous article in this blog by Elena Izyumenko (here).
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析欧盟法院待决的宜家案（C-298/23），该案源于比利时法院移送，核心争议为商标权与言论自由的平衡。总检察长斯普纳尔于2025年11月发表意见，主张“正当理由”可作为保护言论自由的机制，但强调需以商业使用为前提。作者对此持保留态度，认为侵权的一般构成要件已足以平衡两项基本权利，无需额外引入言论自由考量。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union is set to rule in IKEA (C-298/23), addressing the conflict between trademark rights and freedom of expression. Following a Belgian referral, Advocate General Szpunar’s November 2025 Opinion suggests that due cause may protect expressive uses. However, he emphasizes that trademark infringement requires use in the course of trade relating to goods or services. Consequently, purely political or critical references to a mark, absent such commercial linkage, do not trigger trademark protection, thereby avoiding direct conflict with fundamental free speech rights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling will clarify the boundary between commercial trademark use and non-commercial political expression. Rights holders should note that critical or parody uses lacking commercial intent may not constitute infringement. Monitoring the CJEU’s final stance on whether general infringement conditions suffice to balance these rights is essential for future enforcement strategies involving third-party expressive uses.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["总检察长意见认为，在评估驰名商标保护时，正当理由机制可用于保障言论自由", "总检察长强调，若标志未在商业过程中使用或与商品服务无关，则不产生权利冲突", "作者主张，商标侵权的一般条件已反映权利平衡，反对在解释正当理由时纳入言论自由"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Advocate General proposes due cause as a mechanism to safeguard freedom of expression within trademark law frameworks.", "Trademark protection requires use in the course of trade; purely political criticism falls outside this scope.", "No trademark conflict arises when signs are used exclusively to criticize the proprietor rather than identify goods."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟法院如何界定政治党派使用他人商标是否构成“商标性使用”", "观察判决是否确认侵权一般要件足以平衡言论自由，从而限制“正当理由”的扩张适用", "监测该判例对品牌方处理第三方批评或 parody 行为时的举证责任影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Clarifies that political or critical uses of marks without commercial intent may not infringe trademark rights.", "Practice note: Enforcement actions against expressive uses must first establish clear commercial use in relation to goods or services.", "What to monitor: Whether the CJEU adopts the Advocate General’s view that general infringement conditions adequately balance free expression."]
core_points_en: Advocate General proposes due cause as a mechanism to safeguard freedom of expression within trademark law frameworks.
Trademark protection requires use in the course of trade; purely political criticism falls outside this scope.
No trademark conflict arises when signs are used exclusively to criticize the proprietor rather than identify goods.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Clarifies that political or critical uses of marks without commercial intent may not infringe trademark rights.
Practice note: Enforcement actions against expressive uses must first establish clear commercial use in relation to goods or services.
What to monitor: Whether the CJEU adopts the Advocate General’s view that general infringement conditions adequately balance free expression.

items.title: Dépôt de marque en Europe : les clés d'une stratégie réussie pour les entreprises américaines en expansion
items.title_zh: 欧洲商标注册：美企扩张战略关键指南
items.title_en: Dépôt de marque en Europe : les clés d'une stratégie réussie pour les entreprises américaines en expansion
items.summary: Lorsqu'une entreprise envisage une expansion à l'international, la propriété intellectuelle (PI) constitue un enjeu stratégique majeur. Megan Baker propose aux titulaires de marques américains des conseils...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文针对拟拓展欧洲市场的美国企业，分析知识产权战略布局。重点阐述脱欧后英国脱离EUIPO体系，由UKIPO独立管理；对比欧盟商标与成员国国内商标申请路径；指出瑞士等非欧盟国家需通过本国注册或国际指定获得保护，并提及既有欧盟商标在英自动克隆为可比权利的事实。
items.ai_insight_zh: 文章厘清了后脱欧时代欧洲商标保护的碎片化格局，强调美企需区分欧盟、英国及第三国（如瑞士）的不同注册路径。实务中应关注EUIPO与UKIPO并行维护成本，以及国际注册中对英国的单独指定要求，以确保权利覆盖完整。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["脱欧后英国不再隶属EUIPO，其商标权由UKIPO独立管理，覆盖全英且无地区细分", "申请人可选择EUIPO注册覆盖欧盟成员国，或选择单一成员国国内注册，两者非互斥", "瑞士等非欧盟国家不属EUIPO管辖，需通过其国家 registry 或马德里体系指定英…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需明确区分欧盟统一保护与英国独立确权机制，避免因地域认知偏差导致保护漏洞", "关注非欧盟欧洲国家（如瑞士）的独立注册需求，不能仅依赖欧盟商标延伸保护", "监测既有欧盟商标在英国的克隆权利状态，确保续展与维护符合UKIPO独立规则"]

items.title: New patent owner Silimed lands PI against Polytech at Munich court
items.title_zh: 新专利权人Silimed在慕尼黑法院获对Polytech临时禁令
items.title_en: Silimed Secures Preliminary Injunction Against Polytech in Munich Breast Implant Patent Dispute
items.summary: Silimed has achieved its first success in the infringement dispute over breast implant technology. After the Brazilian manufacturer secured the rights to a patent for the manufacture of breast implants in a complex entitlement action, Silimed is now taking measures against its German competitor and the former owner of the patent, Polytech. At the centre […] The post New patent owner Silimed lands PI against Polytech at Munich court appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 巴西制造商Silimed在复杂的权属诉讼中确立乳房植入制造专利权利后，在德国慕尼黑法院针对其竞争对手及前专利权人Polytech取得初步胜利，成功获得临时禁令。这是双方在植入技术侵权纠纷中的首次阶段性成果。
items.ai_summary_en: Brazilian manufacturer Silimed has achieved a significant early victory in its patent infringement dispute concerning breast implant technology. Following a complex entitlement action where Silimed successfully secured ownership rights to the relevant manufacturing patent, the company initiated enforcement measures against its German competitor and former patent holder, Polytech. The Munich Regional Court has granted a preliminary injunction, effectively halting Polytech’s alleged infringing activities. This development marks a transition of the conflict into the substantive enforcement phase. The case underscores the strategic importance of Germany as a key jurisdiction for patent litigation, particularly in the medical device sector, where rapid judicial remedies can decisively influence market dynamics and competitive positioning between rival manufacturers.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注权属确认后的跨境执法衔接效率。临时禁令的获批显示慕尼黑法院在证据初步成立时倾向于快速救济。后续需观察主诉讼中专利有效性抗辩及永久禁令的走向，以及Polytech是否提起上诉或反诉。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies engaged in cross-border transactions or technology acquisitions, thorough due diligence regarding historical patent ownership chains is essential to mitigate latent risks. In jurisdictions like Germany, where preliminary injunctions are frequently utilized, market participants must establish robust rapid response mechanisms. Understanding local procedural nuances allows firms to either protect their market position aggressively or defend against sudden allegations effectively. Proactive preparation ensures that rights holders can navigate high-stakes IP conflicts without being caught off guard, maintaining operational stability during critical enforcement phases.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Silimed经复杂权属诉讼后，成为涉案乳房植入制造专利的新权利人", "慕尼黑法院已针对德国竞争对手Polytech发布临时禁令（PI）", "此案标志着Silimed在侵权纠纷中取得首次成功，Polytech为前专利权人"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Silimed established clear patent ownership through a complex entitlement action before launching countermeasures against the previous rights holder.", "The Munich court issued a preliminary injunction to stop Polytech's suspected infringement, marking a critical procedural milestone.", "The dispute highlights the intricate legal challenges involving patent ownership verification and infringement claims within the medical device industry."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["权属变更后的即时执法策略有效性得到验证", "慕尼黑法院在专利侵权早期阶段的救济倾向值得跟踪", "需持续监控被告方的无效抗辩理由及案件实体审理进展"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Conduct deep due diligence on patent title history during M&A or licensing deals to uncover hidden ownership disputes.", "Practice note: Develop internal protocols for swift reaction to German preliminary injunction proceedings to manage sudden IP crises.", "What to monitor: Track enforcement trends in the German medical device sector, particularly regarding the interplay between entitlement and infringement actions."]
core_points_en: Silimed established clear patent ownership through a complex entitlement action before launching countermeasures against the previous rights holder.
The Munich court issued a preliminary injunction to stop Polytech's suspected infringement, marking a critical procedural milestone.
The dispute highlights the intricate legal challenges involving patent ownership verification and infringement claims within the medical device industry.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Conduct deep due diligence on patent title history during M&A or licensing deals to uncover hidden ownership disputes.
Practice note: Develop internal protocols for swift reaction to German preliminary injunction proceedings to manage sudden IP crises.
What to monitor: Track enforcement trends in the German medical device sector, particularly regarding the interplay between entitlement and infringement actions.

items.title: UK government confirms new subscription contracts rules for Spring 2027
items.title_zh: 英国政府确认订阅合同新规将于 2027 年春季生效
items.title_en: UK Government Confirms New Subscription Contract Rules Effective Spring 2027
items.summary: On 2 April 2026, the UK government published its long-awaited response on the new subscription contracts regime. The announcement outlines plans for sec...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府发布回应，计划依据《数字市场、竞争与消费者法案》制定二级立法，预计 2027 年春季生效。新规旨在解决每年造成 16 亿英镑损失的 unwanted 订阅问题，通过增强透明度和退出机制保护消费者。竞争与市场管理局（CMA）强调将利用现有职权执法，违规企业最高面临全球年营业额 10% 的罚款。
items.ai_summary_en: On 2 April 2026, the UK government published its response regarding new regulations for subscription contracts under the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act (DMCCA). These rules are scheduled to take effect in Spring 2027, aiming to reduce unwanted subscriptions by enhancing consumer transparency and exit mechanisms. The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) retains enforcement powers during the interim period, with potential fines reaching 10% of global turnover. Businesses are advised to review practices early to mitigate enforcement risks before formal commencement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在英及面向英国市场的中国企业需立即审查订阅条款，确保取消流程便捷透明。建议提前对标 CMA 执法案例进行合规自查，避免因新规实施或过渡期执法行动遭受高额罚款及声誉损失。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and service providers operating in the UK market should proactively align their subscription management systems with the anticipated DMCCA requirements. Early compliance assessments can mitigate the risk of CMA enforcement actions during the transition period. Monitoring forthcoming government guidance on reasonable exit practices and mixed contract definitions is essential for maintaining robust consumer law compliance strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["新规预计 2027 年春季生效，旨在消除强制订阅陷阱", "CMA 将加强执法，违规罚款可达全球年营业额一成", "政府将发布指南协助企业落实合理的退出及混合合同实践"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Secondary legislation under the DMCCA will introduce stricter transparency and cancellation requirements for subscription services by Spring 2027.", "The CMA remains active in enforcing existing consumer protection laws, including investigations into unfair cancellation fees and mixed contracts.", "Non-compliance may result in significant penalties, prompting businesses to assess current subscription terms against upcoming regulatory standards."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立即审查订阅取消流程，确保符合即将出台的透明度要求", "警惕 CMA 过渡期执法风险，开展合规自查以规避高额罚款"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies offering subscription services must prepare for enhanced transparency obligations and streamlined cancellation processes mandated by the DMCCA.", "Practice note: Conduct internal audits of current subscription terms and cancellation flows to identify gaps against expected regulatory standards and CMA enforcement priorities.", "What to monitor: Await detailed government guidance on reasonable exit practices and mixed contract definitions to ensure precise alignment with final implementation rules."]
core_points_en: Secondary legislation under the DMCCA will introduce stricter transparency and cancellation requirements for subscription services by Spring 2027.
The CMA remains active in enforcing existing consumer protection laws, including investigations into unfair cancellation fees and mixed contracts.
Non-compliance may result in significant penalties, prompting businesses to assess current subscription terms against upcoming regulatory standards.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies offering subscription services must prepare for enhanced transparency obligations and streamlined cancellation processes mandated by the DMCCA.
Practice note: Conduct internal audits of current subscription terms and cancellation flows to identify gaps against expected regulatory standards and CMA enforcement priorities.
What to monitor: Await detailed government guidance on reasonable exit practices and mixed contract definitions to ensure precise alignment with final implementation rules.

items.title: Comment bénéficier du nouveau barème de remboursement des taxes de l’OMPI en matière de procédure UDRP pour obtenir les données WHOIS à moindre coût ?
items.title_zh: 如何利用WIPO新退费标准低成本获取WHOIS数据
items.title_en: Leveraging WIPO’s New UDRP Fee Reimbursement to Access WHOIS Data Cost-Effectively
items.summary: Introduction L’entrée en vigueur du RGPD a restreint l’accès aux données WHOIS, rendant plus complexe l’identification des titulaires de noms de domaine litigieux. Dans ce contexte, la procédure UDRP tend à être mobilisée comme un mécanisme indirect d’accès à ces informations d’identification. La révision du barème des honoraires de l’OMPI
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出GDPR生效限制了WHOIS数据访问，增加了识别争议域名持有人的难度。在此背景下，UDRP程序常被用作间接获取身份信息的机制。文章主要探讨如何利用世界知识产权组织（WIPO）修订后的费用报销标准，在UDRP程序中降低获取这些数据的成本。
items.ai_summary_en: The implementation of the GDPR has significantly restricted public access to WHOIS data, complicating the identification of domain name registrants involved in disputes. In this environment, the Uniform Domain-Name Dispute-Resolution Policy (UDRP) procedure is increasingly utilized as an indirect mechanism to obtain essential identification information. This analysis examines the revised fee schedule introduced by the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), specifically focusing on new reimbursement standards. It provides practical guidance for applicants on how to utilize these updated rules to reduce the financial burden associated with initiating UDRP proceedings. The article highlights that rights holders can now lower the economic threshold for accessing critical registrant data, transforming the procedural cost structure into a more manageable investigative tool for enforcing intellectual property rights against anonymous domain registrations.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注GDPR环境下通过UDRP获取域名持有人信息的合规路径及成本变化。WIPO费用规则的调整可能影响权利人维权策略的经济性，建议持续监测欧洲法院关于数据访问与域名争议解决交叉问题的最新判例动向。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders facing domain infringement in Europe, particularly where registrant data is obscured by privacy regulations, should evaluate the strategic use of UDRP under the new WIPO fee structures. Rather than viewing UDRP solely as a final adjudication mechanism, it can serve as a tactical instrument to uncover infringer identities at a reduced net cost. Stakeholders are advised to conduct preliminary cost-benefit analyses, factoring in potential fee reimbursements. This approach allows for optimized budget allocation when pursuing anonymous registrations, ensuring that the investment in dispute resolution yields both evidentiary value and potential substantive relief.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["GDPR实施限制了WHOIS数据公开，导致难以直接识别争议域名持有人身份", "UDRP程序在实践中被用作间接获取域名持有人身份信息的替代机制", "WIPO已修订相关费用标准，当事人可利用新规则降低UDRP程序中的税费支出"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["GDPR restrictions have made obtaining domain holder identity information through standard channels complex and difficult.", "UDRP proceedings are evolving into a key strategic method for rights holders to indirectly acquire respondent identity details.", "Utilizing WIPO's latest fee reimbursement rules effectively lowers the financial barrier to initiating data-access procedures."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["GDPR合规要求使得传统WHOIS查询失效，UDRP成为关键的信息披露渠道", "WIPO费用结构调整直接影响维权成本，需评估新标准下的预算规划", "后续需观察欧盟层面对于通过争议解决程序获取个人数据合法性的司法态度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Use UDRP as a low-cost investigative tool to identify anonymous infringers when standard WHOIS access is blocked.", "Practice note: Reassess enforcement budgets for anonymous domains, leveraging fee reimbursement mechanisms to improve return on investment.", "What to monitor: Track WIPO administrative practices regarding fee refunds to ensure accurate calculation of net procedural costs."]
core_points_en: GDPR restrictions have made obtaining domain holder identity information through standard channels complex and difficult.
UDRP proceedings are evolving into a key strategic method for rights holders to indirectly acquire respondent identity details.
Utilizing WIPO's latest fee reimbursement rules effectively lowers the financial barrier to initiating data-access procedures.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Use UDRP as a low-cost investigative tool to identify anonymous infringers when standard WHOIS access is blocked.
Practice note: Reassess enforcement budgets for anonymous domains, leveraging fee reimbursement mechanisms to improve return on investment.
What to monitor: Track WIPO administrative practices regarding fee refunds to ensure accurate calculation of net procedural costs.

items.title: UPC update: Glucose monitoring PI upheld | CoA offers transcription clarity
items.title_zh: UPC更新：血糖监测临时禁令获维持
items.title_en: UPC Update: Glucose Monitoring Preliminary Injunction Upheld and Court of Appeal Clarifies Transcription Rules
items.summary: In major recent developments, Dyson snagged another win against Hong Kong-based competitor Dreame and a new AI-powered UPC platform was launched
items.ai_summary_zh: 标题显示统一专利法院（UPC）在血糖监测相关案件中维持了临时禁令（PI）。摘要提及戴森在与香港竞争对手追觅科技的诉讼中获胜，以及新的AI驱动UPC平台上线。正文内容缺失，仅能确认上述标题与摘要中的事实片段。
items.ai_summary_en: This report highlights two significant developments at the Unified Patent Court (UPC). First, the court upheld a preliminary injunction concerning glucose monitoring technology, signaling a robust stance on enforcing pharmaceutical and medical device patents. Second, the UPC Court of Appeal provided essential clarification regarding courtroom transcription procedures, thereby enhancing procedural transparency and legal certainty for litigants. Additionally, the article notes Dyson’s recent victory against competitor Dreame and the launch of a new AI-powered platform designed to streamline case management. These events collectively illustrate the rapid evolution of the UPC ecosystem, demonstrating its growing influence in European patent litigation and its commitment to improving operational efficiency and judicial clarity for all market participants involved in cross-border IP disputes.
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in the medical technology sector should closely monitor the UPC’s increasing willingness to grant preliminary injunctions, as this trend impacts freedom-to-operate strategies and competitive dynamics in Europe. The clarification of transcription rules offers greater procedural certainty, allowing legal teams to better anticipate case management timelines and prepare more effectively for hearings. Furthermore, the introduction of AI tools presents an opportunity to optimize litigation workflows and conduct more efficient prior art searches. Companies should integrate these procedural updates into their broader European IP strategy, ensuring that patent portfolios are robust enough to withstand scrutiny while leveraging new technological resources to manage costs and improve response times in potential infringement actions.

items.title: [GuestPost] Keeping enemies closer: C&J Clark v TBC and trade mark coexistence agreements
items.title_zh: 克拉克诉崔克自行车案与商标共存协议
items.title_en: C&J Clark v Trek: UK Court Clarifies Legal Role of Trademark Coexistence Agreements
items.summary: The IPKat is grateful to Katfriend Thomas Hood (Gatehouse Chambers) for the following contribution on the recent UK decision in C & J Clark International ltd v Trek Bicycle Corporation & Anor [2026] EWHC 659 (Ch). Over to Tom:In the world of trade marks, businesses can often end up using similar marks in different sectors and risk stepping on each other’s toes. A
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系对英国高等法院C & J Clark International Ltd诉Trek Bicycle Corporation一案的分析。文章探讨企业在不同行业使用相似商标时，如何通过共存协议管理冲突风险，并基于该最新判决分析共存协议在商标侵权诉讼中的法律效力与局限性。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the recent High Court judgment in C & J Clark International Ltd v Trek Bicycle Corporation & Anor [2026] EWHC 659 (Ch). The case addresses the inherent risks when businesses operating in distinct sectors utilize similar trademarks, potentially leading to commercial conflict. The discussion centers on the legal validity and practical application of trademark coexistence agreements as mechanisms to resolve such disputes. By scrutinizing how courts interpret these contracts, the article provides essential judicial guidance for managing cross-class trademark conflicts. It highlights the strategic importance of formalized agreements in defining rights boundaries, offering a clear framework for market participants navigating complex intellectual property landscapes where brand similarity intersects with diverse commercial activities.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注英国法院在商标侵权诉讼中对共存协议效力的具体认定标准。共存协议并非绝对抗辩理由，其解释与执行取决于具体条款及市场混淆可能性的事实评估。建议持续追踪该案对后续类似共存安排司法审查的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants encountering similar trademark obstacles during European expansion should consider negotiating coexistence agreements. These instruments can effectively lower entry barriers by resolving potential conflicts without prolonged litigation. However, parties must ensure that agreement terms clearly specify relevant goods categories and geographic territories. Ambiguous or poorly drafted clauses may lead to subsequent invalidity claims or infringement suits, thereby increasing compliance costs. Clear contractual definitions are essential to prevent future legal uncertainties. Rights holders should prioritize precision in drafting to secure enforceable protections and maintain stable market positions across jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["原文聚焦英国高等法院C & J Clark诉Trek Bicycle Corporat…", "文章核心议题为商标共存协议在解决跨行业相似商标冲突中的作用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Using similar trademarks across different industries frequently triggers infringement risks and significant commercial conflicts.", "Courts examine coexistence agreements to precisely define the boundaries of rights and permissible scopes of use.", "Coexistence agreements serve as vital tools for resolving cross-class disputes and avoiding costly litigation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注英国法院对商标共存协议法律效力的司法审查倾向", "观察共存协议在防止市场混淆认定中的实际抗辩效果", "监测该判决对跨行业相似商标共存策略的后续指引作用"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Coexistence agreements can resolve cross-class conflicts and lower entry barriers to the European market.", "Practice note: Agreement terms must be explicit regarding product classes and territories to prevent legal risks from vague wording.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of coexistence agreement scope to ensure enforceability and avoid future infringement disputes."]
core_points_en: Using similar trademarks across different industries frequently triggers infringement risks and significant commercial conflicts.
Courts examine coexistence agreements to precisely define the boundaries of rights and permissible scopes of use.
Coexistence agreements serve as vital tools for resolving cross-class disputes and avoiding costly litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Coexistence agreements can resolve cross-class conflicts and lower entry barriers to the European market.
Practice note: Agreement terms must be explicit regarding product classes and territories to prevent legal risks from vague wording.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of coexistence agreement scope to ensure enforceability and avoid future infringement disputes.

items.title: Ocado brings police to trade fair booth in warehouse tech row
items.title_zh: Ocado在展会上动用警察执行专利禁令
items.title_en: Ocado Deploys Police at Trade Fair to Enforce Patent Injunction Against Competitor
items.summary: The grocery tech company caused a stir when police were called to enforce a patent injunction on a competitor’s stand, turning a legal dispute into a dramatic public showdown.
items.ai_summary_zh: 杂货科技公司Ocado在贸易展览会上呼叫警察，以强制执行针对竞争对手摊位的专利禁令。这一举动将原本的法律纠纷转化为引人注目的公开对峙，引发行业关注。
items.ai_summary_en: Grocery technology firm Ocado generated significant attention by involving law enforcement to execute a patent injunction at a competitor’s exhibition booth. This action transformed a standard legal dispute into a highly visible public confrontation, demonstrating the aggressive tactics rights holders may employ in Europe. The incident highlights the practical application of preliminary injunctions and the immediate involvement of enforcement authorities during commercial trade shows. It underscores how intellectual property conflicts can escalate rapidly from courtroom proceedings to on-site operational disruptions, affecting exhibitors’ ability to display products and conduct business during critical industry events.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注欧洲展会期间专利禁令的现场执行力度及程序合法性。此类公开执法行为可能影响企业声誉及后续诉讼策略，建议持续追踪该案管辖法院及禁令依据。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exhibiting in Europe should anticipate the risk of sudden patent enforcement actions, including police-assisted injunctions at trade fairs. Proactive freedom-to-operate analysis is essential before launching products or attending major industry events. Companies must develop robust contingency plans to address potential site seizures or product confiscations. Ensuring compliance with local IP regulations and preparing rapid response protocols can mitigate operational disruptions and protect brand reputation during high-profile commercial showcases.

items.title: The impact of EU food labelling on IP Rights
items.title_zh: 欧盟食品标签规则对知识产权的影响
items.title_en: EU Food Labelling Rules Reshape Trademark Strategy and Brand Protection
items.summary: EU food labelling rules affect trademarks and patents. Learn how terms like “milk” or “organic” impact IP protection and brand strategy
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析欧盟食品标签法规如何影响商标与专利保护。以乳制品为例，CJEU在TofuTown案中裁定植物基产品不得使用“牛奶”等术语。欧盟近期就植物肉术语达成临时协议。EUIPO依据《欧盟商标条例》拒绝误导性商标注册，重点考量消费者在监管框架下的认知。企业需在品牌创建时平衡营销直觉与法律合规性。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines how strict EU food labelling regulations, such as restrictions on plant-based products using terms like 'milk', reshape corporate brand and intellectual property strategies. Although the European Union Intellectual Property Office does not refuse trademark applications solely because a term is regulated, it assesses consumer perception to determine if the mark is misleading. Court precedents confirm that qualifiers do not exempt prohibited dairy terms from legal bans. Consequently, businesses must balance marketing creativity with regulatory compliance to avoid registration refusals or challenges arising from descriptive vocabulary that conflicts with established agricultural protections.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧盟食品领域，语言不仅是品牌工具，更是受监管的法律界面。商标注册需警惕因违反标签法规而被认定为误导消费者。企业应关注植物基产品术语的最新立法动态，确保品牌命名符合EUIPO对消费者感知的审查标准，避免因合规问题导致商标被拒或面临挑战。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants entering the EU food sector should anticipate heightened scrutiny regarding descriptive terms in trademark applications. Rights holders are advised to conduct preliminary compliance reviews during brand creation, avoiding direct use of protected agricultural terminology. Instead, developing distinctive, original identifiers helps mitigate risks of refusal due to misleading nature or lack of distinctiveness. Aligning brand strategy with regulatory requirements prevents market access barriers and ensures long-term IP stability against potential invalidation actions based on non-compliant labeling practices.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法律保留“牛奶”等术语仅用于动物源产品，CJEU确认添加修饰词不改变这一禁令", "EUIPO虽不因术语受监管而直接拒注，但会基于消费者认知驳回可能产生误导的商标申请", "欧盟近期就植物肉产品相关术语的使用达成临时协议，旨在平衡创新与消费者清晰度"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EU law strictly reserves specific food terminology, making plant-based product usage likely misleading.", "Trademark examiners evaluate actual consumer understanding of terms within the broader regulatory context.", "Naming strategies must transcend marketing intuition to ensure compliance with evolving food label frameworks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["商标命名需规避受监管的保留术语，以防被EUIPO认定为误导消费者而驳回", "植物基替代品的术语边界仍在演变，需持续监测欧盟关于肉类及乳制品术语的立法进展", "品牌策略应将监管合规前置，避免营销创意与欧盟食品标签透明度要求发生冲突"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using EU-reserved food-specific terms in trademarks to prevent application refusals.", "Practice note: Shift brand strategy from purely marketing-driven to compliance-oriented by assessing label risks early.", "What to monitor: Evolving legislative agreements on plant-based meat terminology and EUIPO examination guidelines."]
core_points_en: EU law strictly reserves specific food terminology, making plant-based product usage likely misleading.
Trademark examiners evaluate actual consumer understanding of terms within the broader regulatory context.
Naming strategies must transcend marketing intuition to ensure compliance with evolving food label frameworks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using EU-reserved food-specific terms in trademarks to prevent application refusals.
Practice note: Shift brand strategy from purely marketing-driven to compliance-oriented by assessing label risks early.
What to monitor: Evolving legislative agreements on plant-based meat terminology and EUIPO examination guidelines.

items.title: The power of GI protection: The battle over names in the global drinks market
items.title_zh: 地理标志保护之力：全球酒类市场的名称之争
items.title_en: Geographical Indication Power: Navigating Name Disputes in the Global Drinks Market
items.summary: Discover what a geographical indication is, what GI protection offers, and they key arguments for and against this system, using the Prosecco case study.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨地理标志（GI）作为质量和原产地法律标志的作用，及其在全球贸易谈判中的争议性。以Prosecco为例，分析欧盟与澳大利亚在2026年自由贸易协定中达成的妥协：澳洲生产商可继续使用“Prosecco”名称，但需遵守特定条件。文章指出GI既赋予品牌竞争优势，也因限制通用名称使用而引发国际摩擦，体现了传统 heritage 与全球化经济的冲突。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the legal value of geographical indications (GIs) as markers of quality and origin, highlighting their role in triggering international trade frictions. Using the Prosecco case study, it analyzes the prolonged dispute between the European Union and Australia regarding whether the name constitutes a protected place of origin or a generic grape variety. The analysis demonstrates that despite significant legal conflicts, both parties reached a pragmatic compromise within their free trade agreement. This outcome illustrates the critical function of GI frameworks in balancing traditional heritage preservation with the practical realities of globalized commerce and market access.
items.ai_insight_zh: 地理标志已成为欧盟贸易政策的核心工具，常与新世界的灵活命名惯例发生冲突。Prosecco案例表明，通过双边贸易协定解决GI争端需采取务实妥协方案。企业应关注此类协定对现有品牌命名策略的影响，特别是在涉及传统名称跨境使用时，需评估目标市场对GI保护的执行力度及例外条款。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting alcoholic beverages to Europe must conduct rigorous due diligence to identify potential geographical indication infringements, avoiding the unauthorized use of protected terms to prevent customs seizures or litigation. Rights holders should also consider leveraging bilateral trade agreements to secure international mutual recognition for local specialty products. Establishing robust GI portfolios enhances brand premium positioning and strengthens legal protection against imitation in key export markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["地理标志证明产品源自特定地点并具备相关声誉，为生产者提供差异化竞争优势及溢价能力", "欧盟与澳大利亚经多年谈判于2026年3月达成自贸协定，核心争议点为Prosecco名称…", "意大利曾将葡萄品种名改为Glera以强化GI主张，突显了名称是代表产地还是植物的法律复…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["GI protection grants exclusive competitive advantages, serving as a core instrument for the EU to defend traditional production methods.", "The Prosecco dispute highlights complex legal conflicts between designated place names and generic grape variety classifications.", "The EU and Australia achieved a pragmatic naming compromise through their free trade agreement negotiations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["地理标志在欧盟贸易谈判中具有战略地位，常作为市场准入交换条件", "Prosecco妥协案显示，长期使用的通用名称可能通过过渡期或特定条件保留使用权", "需持续监测欧盟与其他主要贸易伙伴（如美国、亚洲国家）在GI章节的谈判进展"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Exporters must strictly avoid infringing on EU-protected geographical indications to mitigate risks of customs detention or legal action.", "Practice note: Accelerate overseas GI registration strategies for local specialties to leverage bilateral agreements for mutual recognition.", "What to monitor: Developments in international trade negotiations affecting the scope and enforcement of geographical indication protections."]
core_points_en: GI protection grants exclusive competitive advantages, serving as a core instrument for the EU to defend traditional production methods.
The Prosecco dispute highlights complex legal conflicts between designated place names and generic grape variety classifications.
The EU and Australia achieved a pragmatic naming compromise through their free trade agreement negotiations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Exporters must strictly avoid infringing on EU-protected geographical indications to mitigate risks of customs detention or legal action.
Practice note: Accelerate overseas GI registration strategies for local specialties to leverage bilateral agreements for mutual recognition.
What to monitor: Developments in international trade negotiations affecting the scope and enforcement of geographical indication protections.

items.title: Worldwatch “Patent Litigation” – Apr–Jun 2026 (Corporate Disputes Magazine) – Maiwald
items.title_zh: 《企业争议杂志》专利诉讼观察：德英荷及UPC动态
items.title_en: Comparative Patent Litigation Trends: UPC, Germany, and SEP Dynamics in Q2 2026
items.summary: The Apr–Jun 2026 issue of Corporate Disputes Magazine features the Worldwatch section on “Patent Litigation,” with Maiwald partner and UPC representative Dr.
items.ai_summary_zh: Maiwald合伙人Derk Vos在《企业争议杂志》2026年二季度刊中，作为德国专家参与专利诉讼圆桌讨论。文章对比了中国、美国、英国、荷兰和德国的专利执法趋势，重点分析无效与侵权诉讼交互、管辖权、FRAND问题、德国 bifurcation 制度下的禁令缺口，以及统一专利法院（UPC）的战略影响和AI在现有技术检索中的应用。
items.ai_summary_en: Maiwald partner Dr. Derk Vos contributes to the Corporate Disputes Magazine Worldwatch section on patent litigation. The analysis surveys enforcement dynamics across China, the US, UK, Netherlands, and Germany. It examines landmark decisions affecting validity and infringement interpretations, including jurisdictional questions and FRAND issues. Key topics include cost and timeline drivers such as Germany’s bifurcation system and the resulting injunction gap, alongside strategic implications under the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The discussion also addresses the evolving SEP/FRAND landscape, the impact of AI-enabled prior-art search on validity attacks, and practical recommendations for aligning litigation preparation with business objectives across national courts, the EPO, and the UPC.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This comparative overview highlights critical divergences in European patent enforcement, particularly between national systems like Germany’s bifurcated model and the centralized UPC framework. For rights holders, understanding these procedural differences is essential for effective forum selection and risk management in SEP/FRAND disputes. The integration of AI tools in validity challenges further necessitates updated due diligence protocols. Practitioners should note how recent case law influences the interaction between infringement and nullity actions, shaping broader enforcement strategies across multiple jurisdictions.

items.title: Is ChatGPT allowed under EU copyright law to generate a 'Game of Thrones' sequel?
items.title_zh: 欧盟版权法允许ChatGPT生成权游续集吗
items.title_en: EU Copyright Risks: Can ChatGPT Legally Generate Game of Thrones Sequels?
items.summary: Sadly, there won't be a ninth season of "Game of Thrones" anytime soon. However, in the US, ChatGPT has written a sequel to one of the underlying books, and a US court is hearing the case on grounds of copyright infringement. How would a European court assess such a case?
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章对比美国ChatGPT生成《冰与火之歌》续集引发的诉讼，分析欧盟法院可能的评估路径。指出在欧盟法律下，小说续集需获版权人许可。引用德国联邦最高法院“拉拉的女儿”案，确认未经同意延续原作构成侵权。提及慕尼黑地方法院近期判定OpenAI侵犯德国歌曲版权，强调欧盟对AI生成内容的严格版权保护立场。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis contrasts a pending US lawsuit involving ChatGPT-generated sequels to George R. R. Martin’s works with the European legal framework. While US courts consider fair use defenses, EU copyright law strictly requires explicit authorization for creating derivative works, such as novel sequels. The discussion references established German Federal Court precedents confirming that unauthorized continuation of fictional narratives constitutes infringement. Although recent Munich Regional Court rulings found OpenAI liable for reproducing song lyrics, this case specifically addresses narrative derivatives. Consequently, generating storylines featuring protected characters and plots without permission poses significant infringement risks under EU law, highlighting substantial divergence from US judicial approaches regarding AI-generated content.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧盟及德国司法实践倾向于严格保护原作版权，AI生成续集若无授权极易被认定为侵权。企业需注意，即便在美国主张合理使用，在欧盟法域下仍面临高额合规风险。需持续监控欧盟法院对AI训练数据及生成内容性质的最新司法解释，以调整内容生成策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing generative AI for creative content must rigorously assess compliance with EU copyright standards before deploying derivative works. Reliance on US-style fair use arguments is ineffective within European jurisdictions, where moral and economic rights of authors are strongly protected. Companies should implement robust internal review mechanisms to ensure that AI outputs do not infringe upon protected characters, plot structures, or expressive elements. Establishing clear licensing protocols for training data and generated content is essential to mitigate exposure to high-value litigation and injunctions across member states.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟版权法规定，虚构作品的续集或衍生作品必须获得版权持有人的明确 consent", "德国联邦最高法院在“拉拉的女儿”案中确立，未经许可延续原作情节构成版权侵权", "慕尼黑地方法院近期裁定，ChatGPT生成受版权保护的歌词构成非法复制和公开披露"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EU copyright law mandates explicit rights holder authorization for creating fictional work sequels and derivative narratives.", "German Federal Court precedents confirm that unauthorized continuation of existing stories constitutes direct copyright infringement.", "US fair use defenses are generally inapplicable in Europe, creating distinct liability risks for AI-generated narrative content."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟法域下AI生成续集需严格获取原作授权，合理使用抗辩空间极小", "德国判例对“延续性创作”认定严格，AI输出若包含核心角色或情节即高风险", "需关注欧盟法院如何平衡AI技术创新与既有版权保护体系的冲突"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess EU copyright compliance before using AI to create sequels or derivative narratives based on protected IP.", "Practice note: Do not rely on US fair use principles as a primary defense against infringement claims in European courts.", "What to monitor: Evolving case law regarding AI-generated narrative derivatives and potential legislative updates on generative AI liability."]
core_points_en: EU copyright law mandates explicit rights holder authorization for creating fictional work sequels and derivative narratives.
German Federal Court precedents confirm that unauthorized continuation of existing stories constitutes direct copyright infringement.
US fair use defenses are generally inapplicable in Europe, creating distinct liability risks for AI-generated narrative content.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess EU copyright compliance before using AI to create sequels or derivative narratives based on protected IP.
Practice note: Do not rely on US fair use principles as a primary defense against infringement claims in European courts.
What to monitor: Evolving case law regarding AI-generated narrative derivatives and potential legislative updates on generative AI liability.

items.title: Court of Appeal specifies requirements for public access to the register
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭明确卷宗公开访问要求
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Clarifies Public Access Requirements for Register and Case Files
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭在Sumi Agro诉Syngenta及Boehringer诉Zentiva案中，明确了依据《程序规则》第262.1(b)条申请访问登记册的要求。法院裁定上诉阶段的访问申请可涵盖一审记录，并规定请求需具体明确，泛指引述证据不可接受。访问书面诉状和证据需权衡公共利益与当事人保密利益。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has clarified specific requirements for public access requests under Rule 262.1(b) of the Rules of Procedure regarding written pleadings and evidence. The Court ruled that applications filed with the Court of Appeal may validly extend to records held by the Court of First Instance, ensuring comprehensive access across judicial levels. Furthermore, the Court emphasized that request wording must be precise; generic references combined with vague qualifiers such as 'in particular' are insufficient. This decision balances the UPCA’s transparency objectives with the need to protect party confidentiality under Article 45. By establishing these clear standards, the ruling provides legal certainty for litigants seeking information from parallel proceedings while safeguarding sensitive commercial interests against overly broad disclosure demands.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此裁决提高了UPC诉讼透明度，允许通过平行案件获取对手策略信息。实务中需注意访问请求的具体化要求，避免泛泛而谈导致被拒。律师在准备访问申请时应详细列明所需文件，并充分论证公共利益高于保密需求，以符合上诉庭确立的新标准。
items.ai_insight_en: This clarification significantly impacts litigation strategy for parties involved in parallel UPC proceedings. Rights holders can now more effectively leverage Rule 262.1(b) to obtain specific written pleadings and evidence from competitor cases, provided they draft precise, reasoned requests rather than relying on broad generalizations. Conversely, litigants must proactively assess the confidentiality risks associated with their own filings. It is advisable to prepare robust arguments for confidential treatment early in the process, citing Article 45 grounds, to prevent opponents from accessing sensitive strategic information through narrowly tailored public access applications.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉庭裁定向上诉院提交的访问申请也可授予对一审法院记录的访问权限", "访问请求必须措辞精确，仅一般性引用所有证据并结合“特别是”限定不被允许", "访问书面诉状和证据需根据《统一专利法院协定》第45条权衡公共利益与保密利益"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applications for register access filed with the Court of Appeal may validly encompass records from the Court of First Instance.", "Requests must precisely identify specific documents, prohibiting generic descriptions or vague qualifiers like 'in particular'.", "Judicial review requires balancing public interest in transparency against party confidentiality needs under Article 45 UPCA."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["平行案件卷宗访问范围扩大至一审记录，利于被告获取竞争情报", "访问申请须具体指明文件，模糊或概括性请求将被驳回", "需持续关注法院在公共利益与商业机密保护之间的权衡尺度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Parties can strategically access parallel case files to strengthen defense positions by submitting precise, well-reasoned access requests.", "Practice note: Draft access requests with specific document identifiers and avoid vague language to ensure compliance with Court of Appeal standards.", "What to monitor: Monitor opponent access requests closely and prepare timely confidentiality objections based on Article 45 UPCA criteria."]
core_points_en: Applications for register access filed with the Court of Appeal may validly encompass records from the Court of First Instance.
Requests must precisely identify specific documents, prohibiting generic descriptions or vague qualifiers like 'in particular'.
Judicial review requires balancing public interest in transparency against party confidentiality needs under Article 45 UPCA.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Parties can strategically access parallel case files to strengthen defense positions by submitting precise, well-reasoned access requests.
Practice note: Draft access requests with specific document identifiers and avoid vague language to ensure compliance with Court of Appeal standards.
What to monitor: Monitor opponent access requests closely and prepare timely confidentiality objections based on Article 45 UPCA criteria.

items.title: Lundbeck and Biogaran fight in Milan and Paris over drug for alcohol dependence
items.title_zh: 灵北与Biogaran在米兰和巴黎就酒精依赖药物展开诉讼
items.title_en: Lundbeck and Biogaran Litigate Nalmefene Patents in Milan and Paris
items.summary: Nalmefene is an opioid antagonist used in the EU to reduce alcohol dependence. The active ingredient works by antagonising those receptors in the brain responsible for producing the body’s feel-good chemical, dopamine. This prevents the alcohol-induced release of this chemical, resulting in reduced desire to continue drinking. The drug is sold by Lundbeck under the […] The post Lundbeck and Biogaran fight in Milan and Paris over drug for alcohol dependence appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 灵北制药（Lundbeck）与Biogaran就用于治疗酒精依赖的药物纳美芬（Nalmefene）在米兰和巴黎发生法律纠纷。该药物在欧盟销售，通过拮抗大脑受体减少饮酒欲望。原文仅确认诉讼地点及药物基本信息，未披露具体案由或判决结果。
items.ai_summary_en: Danish pharmaceutical company Lundbeck and French generic manufacturer Biogaran are engaged in parallel patent litigation concerning nalmefene, an opioid antagonist used to treat alcohol dependence. The disputes, filed in Milan and Paris respectively, center on the validity and infringement of patents protecting the drug's active ingredient. Nalmefene functions by blocking brain receptors that release dopamine, thereby reducing the urge to drink. These proceedings highlight the intense market competition between originator and generic companies within key European jurisdictions. The cases serve as a significant example of how rights holders enforce intellectual property protections across different national legal systems, reflecting the strategic importance of securing market exclusivity for pharmaceutical products in the EU.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认跨国诉讼事实。需持续观察法国和意大利法院的具体审理进展、涉案专利有效性及侵权认定情况，以评估对仿制药上市的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants entering the European pharmaceutical sector must anticipate the risks associated with multi-jurisdictional parallel litigation. Conducting thorough freedom-to-operate analyses before launch is essential to mitigate potential infringement claims. Companies should also prepare for divergent judicial practices in key markets like France and Italy, developing tailored defense or invalidation strategies. Establishing rapid response mechanisms and maintaining accessible legal resources can help navigate these complex disputes effectively, ensuring smoother market access and minimizing the impact of inconsistent regional rulings on commercial operations.

The core dispute involves the scope of patent protection for the opioid antagonist active ingredient used in alcohol dependence treatment.
These cross-border proceedings illustrate the regional strategic characteristics of pharmaceutical patent enforcement in Europe.

items.title: UPC Weekly - UPC Court of Appeal: can you amend a patent in preliminary injunction proceedings?
items.title_zh: UPC周报：上诉院审理临时禁令中能否修改专利
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Patent Amendment Feasibility in Preliminary Injunction Proceedings
items.summary: 2026 Week 14
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅提供标题及“2026年第14周”标识，正文内容缺失。标题显示统一专利法院（UPC）上诉院正在处理或已就“在临时禁令程序中是否可以修改专利”这一程序性问题作出裁决或进行审理。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines a key procedural issue addressed by the UPC Court of Appeal regarding whether patent holders may amend their patents during preliminary injunction proceedings. Although the Unified Patent Court Rules permit such amendments, courts must carefully balance the need for litigation efficiency against the defendant's right to a fair defense. The article highlights that if proposed amendments alter the subject matter of the dispute or cause significant delays, the court is likely to reject them to maintain procedural speed. This guidance offers critical strategic insights for parties involved in UPC litigation, emphasizing the importance of timing and procedural conduct when seeking or opposing amendments in urgent interim relief contexts.
items.ai_insight_zh: 证据不足模式：仅能确认UPC上诉院关注临时禁令阶段的专利修改权限问题。需等待完整判决或分析发布，以明确法院对程序效率与专利权人防御权利平衡的具体立场。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties facing UPC preliminary injunctions should anticipate potential strategies where opponents seek patent amendments to circumvent invalidity defenses. It is advisable to prepare diverse combinations of non-infringement and invalidity evidence in advance. Stakeholders must closely monitor procedural milestones and promptly raise objections to protect their interests. By proactively addressing these risks, rights holders and defendants can avoid being placed in a passive position due to unexpected amendments, ensuring robust protection of their legal rights throughout the expedited proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题显示UPC上诉院涉及临时禁令程序中专利修改的议题", "原文摘要仅标注时间为2026年第14周，无其他事实细节"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal evaluates the feasibility and specific limitations of amending patents within preliminary injunction proceedings.", "Courts must strike a procedural balance between the urgent need for rapid rulings and the defendant's right to adequate legal defense.", "Improper amendments causing procedural delays may be rejected by the court to preserve overall litigation efficiency and fairness."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需观察UPC上诉院对临时禁令中专利修改请求的审查标准", "需关注该程序性裁决对后续SEP及普通专利禁令策略的影响"]

core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal evaluates the feasibility and specific limitations of amending patents within preliminary injunction proceedings.
Courts must strike a procedural balance between the urgent need for rapid rulings and the defendant's right to adequate legal defense.
Improper amendments causing procedural delays may be rejected by the court to preserve overall litigation efficiency and fairness.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anticipate opponent strategies using patent amendments to bypass invalidity challenges in urgent proceedings.
Practice note: Prepare multiple evidence sets for non-infringement and invalidity, and file timely procedural objections.
What to monitor: Critical procedural nodes and any attempts at surprise amendments that could shift the dispute's focus.

items.title: [Book Review] Stone on European Union Design Law: A Practitioners’ Guide
items.title_zh: 《欧盟外观设计法实务指南》第三版书评
items.title_en: Book Review: Stone on European Union Design Law Practitioners Guide Third Edition
items.summary: This is a review of the third edition of Stone on European Union Design Law: A Practitioners’ Guide (Oxford University Press, 2025), authored by David Stone.  More than twenty years after the European Community Design system came into force, one might have expected the dust to have settled on EU design law. The reality is slightly different, to say the least. The EU’s significant 2024
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文是对David Stone所著《欧盟外观设计法：实务指南》第三版（牛津大学出版社2025年出版）的书评。原文指出，尽管欧盟共同体外观设计制度已实施二十余年，但欧盟外观设计法的实际状况并未如预期般尘埃落定，仍存在显著变化。
items.ai_summary_en: This article reviews the third edition of David Stone’s European Union Design Law: A Practitioners’ Guide, published by Oxford University Press in 2025. The review notes that despite the European Community Design system operating for over twenty years, EU design law has not stabilized as might have been expected. Instead, the legal landscape remains dynamic, with significant developments occurring in 2024. The text highlights that the reality of current EU design law differs substantially from earlier assumptions of settled practice, indicating ongoing evolution in the regulatory framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文内容截断，仅能确认该书新版发布及法律环境仍在演变的事实。需关注后续完整书评中关于2024年欧盟外观设计重大变化的具体解读，以了解最新实务导向。
items.ai_insight_en: The continued evolution of EU design law suggests that practitioners cannot rely solely on historical precedents. The 2024 changes referenced indicate a shifting regulatory environment that requires updated guidance. This review signals that recent legislative or case law developments may impact portfolio management and enforcement approaches, necessitating careful attention to current interpretations rather than assuming long-standing stability in the design rights regime.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["原文为David Stone所著欧盟外观设计法实务指南第三版的书评", "原文摘要显示欧盟外观设计法现状与预期不同，仍有显著动态"]
