items.title: GlobalBlock adds world's two largest country domains—centralised brand protection in more than 750 web extensions
items.title_zh: GlobalBlock新增.cn与.de域名强化品牌保护
items.title_en: GlobalBlock Adds .cn and .de Domains, Expanding Centralized Brand Protection to Over 750 Extensions
items.summary: Addition of China's .cn and Germany's .de marks a major milestone in simplifying global IP protection, says Tony Kirsch of the Brand Safety Alliance.
items.ai_summary_zh: 品牌安全联盟（BSA）宣布将其GlobalBlock域名屏蔽服务扩展至中国.cn和德国.de顶级域名。此举旨在简化全球知识产权保护，帮助品牌以更低成本主动防御网络资产风险，覆盖超过750个网络扩展名。
items.ai_summary_en: The Brand Safety Alliance (BSA) has announced the integration of China’s .cn and Germany’s .de country code top-level domains into its GlobalBlock service. This expansion represents a significant milestone in simplifying global intellectual property protection strategies. By including the world’s two largest national domains, which collectively host over 30 million registered names, the service now covers more than 750 web extensions. This development allows rights holders to proactively defend their digital assets against infringement without incurring the substantial costs associated with defensive domain registrations or extensive litigation. The move aims to streamline online brand safety, enabling companies to manage protection across major digital markets efficiently and reliably.
items.ai_insight_zh: 随着.de等高价值国别域名纳入集中屏蔽体系，权利人可更高效地阻断侵权域名注册。需关注该商业保护机制在欧洲司法辖区对传统域名争议解决程序的补充作用及实际执行效力。
items.ai_insight_en: The inclusion of .de and .cn domains offers a cost-effective mechanism for blocking malicious cybersquatting in key European and Asian markets. Given the massive volume of .de registrations, rights holders with export operations should integrate this domain into their core monitoring frameworks. Utilizing centralized blocking services enhances proactive enforcement capabilities, allowing brands to secure their presence before disputes escalate. This approach minimizes exposure to passive litigation risks and reduces administrative burdens. Companies should evaluate how such consolidated tools fit within broader IP strategies to maintain robust defense against domain abuse across diverse jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["BSA将GlobalBlock服务扩展至全球两大国家顶级域名.cn和.de", "该服务旨在通过集中化屏蔽降低防御性注册及诉讼监控成本", "cn和.de合计代表超3000万个注册域名，覆盖活跃数字市场"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["集中化域名屏蔽成为替代高额防御性注册的成本优化方案", "德国.de域名的纳入提升了欧洲主要市场品牌保护的覆盖密度", "需观察此类私营联盟机制在跨境侵权执法中的实际协作效率"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage new centralized blocking mechanisms to prevent low-cost malicious domain squatting in Germany and other global markets.", "Practice note: Integrate .de domains into core monitoring portfolios to enhance proactive enforcement efficiency for brands operating internationally.", "What to monitor: Track adoption rates of GlobalBlock by competitors and assess the impact on cybersquatting trends within the .de and .cn zones."]
core_points_en: GlobalBlock now includes China's .cn and Germany's .de domains, marking a major expansion for centralized brand protection services globally.
The service enables brands to proactively protect online assets, significantly reducing the need for costly defensive registrations and litigation.
Coverage now extends to over 750 web extensions, simplifying global enforcement processes and enhancing efficiency for rights holders.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage new centralized blocking mechanisms to prevent low-cost malicious domain squatting in Germany and other global markets.
Practice note: Integrate .de domains into core monitoring portfolios to enhance proactive enforcement efficiency for brands operating internationally.
What to monitor: Track adoption rates of GlobalBlock by competitors and assess the impact on cybersquatting trends within the .de and .cn zones.

items.title: Dior and L’Oréal fend off Chinese trademark challengers in Europe
items.title_zh: 迪奥与欧莱雅在欧洲成功阻击中国商标挑战者
items.title_en: Dior and L’Oréal Successfully Oppose Chinese Trademark Applications in the EU
items.summary: The French luxury and cosmetics groups both successfully opposed registrations that encroached on their signs in EU territories.
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国奢侈品集团迪奥与化妆品巨头欧莱雅在欧盟领土内，针对侵犯其标识的中国商标注册申请提出异议，并均获得成功。原文仅确认两家公司成功抵御了相关注册尝试，未披露具体案件细节、异议理由或涉及的具体商标图样。
items.ai_summary_en: French luxury group Dior and cosmetics giant L’Oréal have successfully opposed trademark registrations filed by Chinese applicants that encroached upon their established signs within EU territories. This development underscores the European market’s firm stance on protecting well-known brands, demonstrating that the European Union Intellectual Property Office maintains strict scrutiny during examination processes. These decisions effectively safeguard rights holders’ exclusive brand privileges across the pan-European region while curbing potential free-riding behaviors. The outcomes highlight a robust enforcement environment where established international brands can rely on rigorous administrative procedures to defend their intellectual property against unauthorized use or imitation by third parties seeking to capitalize on their reputation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息极简，仅能确认异议结果。需关注后续是否披露具体法律依据（如混淆可能性或声誉损害），以及EUIPO在此类跨境异议中的审查趋势。目前缺乏细节支持深入策略分析。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants seeking trademark protection in Europe must conduct comprehensive prior art searches to mitigate risks of conflict with existing well-known marks. When facing opposition proceedings, applicants should carefully evaluate the probability of success before committing substantial resources to litigation. It is advisable to enhance original design capabilities to reduce infringement disputes at the source. Establishing a compliant overseas intellectual property strategy is essential for navigating the strict regulatory environment. Rights holders should prioritize proactive monitoring and early intervention to protect their brand equity effectively against potential imitators in key jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["迪奥与欧莱雅在欧盟区域内成功异议了侵犯其标识的商标注册申请", "挑战方为中国主体，两家公司均取得了异议胜利，维护了自身商标权益"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["确认欧盟商标异议程序对知名品牌保护的有效性", "需监测此类案件是否涉及具体的恶意注册认定标准", "观察未来是否有更多类似中欧商标冲突案例公开细节"]

items.title: How To Optimize Your Legal Department’s Work Thanks To External Trademark Search Solutions
items.title_zh: 借助外部商标检索方案优化法务部工作
items.title_en: Optimizing Legal Department Efficiency Through External Trademark Search Solutions
items.summary: If you are still conducting trademark searches in-house, you could be wasting your time and resources. Discover how external trademark search solutions can potentially save your business money while improving efficiency and search quality.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出企业内部法务部门自行进行商标检索可能效率低下且资源浪费。内部团队往往缺乏全面检索的专业知识或国际视野，可能导致检索不完整，进而引发注册驳回或撤销风险。文章建议采用外部专业检索解决方案，以节省成本、提高效率并确保检索质量，特别是在处理跨国知识产权组合时。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis highlights the significant inefficiencies and professional limitations associated with conducting trademark searches entirely in-house. Relying on internal legal teams for such tasks often leads to incomplete results due to a lack of specialized tools and international expertise, potentially resulting in registration refusals or rights infringement. The article advocates for adopting external professional search solutions to enhance both the quality and coverage of trademark investigations. By outsourcing these labor-intensive tasks, companies can free up valuable legal resources to focus on core intellectual property protection strategies. This approach not only improves operational efficiency but also reduces long-term costs by minimizing the risks of portfolio damage caused by inadequate prior art checks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该文章属于律所或服务商的市场评论，旨在推广外部检索服务。对于拥有庞大或国际商标组合的企业，需评估内部检索能力与外部服务的成本效益比。重点在于识别内部检索在专业深度和全球覆盖面上的局限性，而非具体法律变更。
items.ai_insight_en: For market participants expanding into Europe, relying solely on basic internal screening is insufficient for mitigating cross-border conflict risks. Companies should adopt a hybrid model combining initial internal filtering with comprehensive external professional searches. This strategy ensures greater safety for trademark registrations across the European Union and its member states. It prevents brand expansion obstacles and potential infringement litigation arising from oversight. Establishing this collaborative mechanism helps secure intellectual property layouts in European markets, ensuring robust protection against complex local and regional trademark challenges.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["内部法务进行商标检索会分散其保护和发展知识产权核心职能的注意力", "内部团队可能缺乏必要专业知识或国际视野，导致检索不全及潜在法律风险", "外部检索解决方案能提升效率与质量，尤其适用于拥有国际业务的企业"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Internal trademark searches distract legal departments from their primary role of protecting and evolving the company's intellectual property assets.", "In-house teams often lack the necessary professional tools and global perspective, leading to incomplete searches that increase registration refusal risks.", "External professional solutions improve search quality and efficiency, optimizing global intellectual property layouts and reducing overall management costs."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["企业应审视内部法务在商标检索上的时间投入与产出比，避免资源错配", "跨国经营需警惕单一地域思维导致的检索盲区，考虑引入外部专业支持", "关注外部检索工具在提升数据全面性和降低注册驳回风险方面的实际效能"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid reliance on rudimentary internal searches to prevent EU trademark registration rejections and protect brand integrity.", "Practice note: Implement a dual-layer system using internal preliminary screening followed by rigorous external professional verification.", "What to monitor: Ensure comprehensive coverage of EU and national databases to safeguard intellectual property layouts in European markets."]
core_points_en: Internal trademark searches distract legal departments from their primary role of protecting and evolving the company's intellectual property assets.
In-house teams often lack the necessary professional tools and global perspective, leading to incomplete searches that increase registration refusal risks.
External professional solutions improve search quality and efficiency, optimizing global intellectual property layouts and reducing overall management costs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid reliance on rudimentary internal searches to prevent EU trademark registration rejections and protect brand integrity.
Practice note: Implement a dual-layer system using internal preliminary screening followed by rigorous external professional verification.
What to monitor: Ensure comprehensive coverage of EU and national databases to safeguard intellectual property layouts in European markets.

items.title: Intellectual Property Records: Avoid These 5 Common Irregular Requests For Recordals
items.title_zh: 知识产权备案：避免五种常见的不规范请求
items.title_en: EU Trademark Recordals: Avoiding Five Common Irregular Requests and Compliance Risks
items.summary: Intellectual property record updates are essential for ensuring that the details of IP are accurately maintained and that owners can freely exercise their rights. Consulting IP professionals for advice on a recordals project is the most effective way to ensure its successful completion. However, owners regularly request some irregular things for IP recordals.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出知识产权备案更新对确保权利准确及行使至关重要。权利人常提出五种不规范请求，如延迟备案、等待续展时一并处理或仅选择部分资产更新。这些做法虽旨在节省资源，但往往导致后续权利行使困难、费用增加，甚至在要求“全有或全无”变更的法域中削弱保护力度。建议咨询专业人士以避免陷阱。
items.ai_summary_en: Maintaining accurate intellectual property records at the EUIPO is critical for rights holders to freely exercise their rights. This analysis highlights five common irregular requests that often lead to complications, including delaying recordals, waiting until renewal dates, and updating only selected assets. While these approaches may seem cost-effective initially, they frequently result in increased official and agent fees, procedural difficulties, and diminished legal protection. The article emphasizes that postponing necessary changes or selectively updating portfolios can jeopardize IP rights and create significant compliance issues. To avoid these avoidable traps, companies should prioritize timely and comprehensive updates rather than deferring actions. Engaging IP professionals early ensures successful completion of recordal projects, safeguarding the integrity of the IP portfolio and preventing future legal obstacles associated with incomplete or delayed administrative changes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧洲多国（如法国）及欧盟层面强调备案的及时性与完整性。企业需注意，试图通过延迟或选择性备案来节省短期成本，可能因违反特定法域“全有或全无”的变更规则而损害权利稳定性。实务中应关注各国对名义变更等备案的具体形式要求，避免因程序瑕疵影响权利效力。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders managing EU portfolios, particularly following mergers or corporate restructurings, relying on a 'delay to save' strategy creates significant exposure. Immediate and comprehensive recordal mechanisms are essential to prevent gaps in rights enforcement. Stakeholders must evaluate jurisdiction-specific requirements, such as 'all-or-nothing' territorial rules, to avoid partial filings that result in defective titles. Proactive coordination with local IP counsel ensures that global changes are accurately reflected across all relevant jurisdictions, mitigating the risk of unenforceable rights and costly remedial procedures later in the lifecycle of the trademark.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["延迟备案或等待续展期再处理，通常会导致流程更复杂且代理费与官费成本更高", "仅选择部分资产进行备案更新可能产生灾难性后果，特别是在关联商标或强制整体变更的法域", "不及时完成备案更新可能导致权利人在后续行使知识产权时遭遇不必要的法律障碍"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Delaying recordals increases complexity and costs, leading to higher official and agent fees compared to immediate updates.", "Waiting until renewal periods to update records risks weakening IP protection and may jeopardize the validity of rights.", "Selective asset updates can cause compliance failures, especially where full territorial coverage or linked marks are required."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["延迟备案不仅无法节省资源，反而可能因紧急处理导致更高的官方及代理费用支出", "在涉及关联商标或特定领土要求时，选择性更新资产可能违反当地法规，导致备案无效", "需持续监控不同欧洲法域对于知识产权记录变更的具体程序要求及合规风险点"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Immediate recordals prevent enforcement gaps and reduce long-term administrative costs for portfolio managers.", "Practice note: Coordinate cross-border changes holistically to avoid compliance risks from selective or partial asset declarations.", "What to monitor: Jurisdictional nuances regarding mandatory full-territory updates and strict deadlines for recording ownership transfers."]
core_points_en: Delaying recordals increases complexity and costs, leading to higher official and agent fees compared to immediate updates.
Waiting until renewal periods to update records risks weakening IP protection and may jeopardize the validity of rights.
Selective asset updates can cause compliance failures, especially where full territorial coverage or linked marks are required.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Immediate recordals prevent enforcement gaps and reduce long-term administrative costs for portfolio managers.
Practice note: Coordinate cross-border changes holistically to avoid compliance risks from selective or partial asset declarations.
What to monitor: Jurisdictional nuances regarding mandatory full-territory updates and strict deadlines for recording ownership transfers.

items.title: Patent Litigation in Practice Series: Spotlight on Belgium: Another Belgian Court Decision under the SPC Manufacturing Waiver
items.title_zh: 比利时法院关于SPC制造豁免通知要求的最新裁决综述
items.title_en: Belgian Courts Clarify SPC Manufacturing Waiver: Notification Requirements and Stockpiling Scope
items.ai_summary_zh: Bird & Bird分析比利时法院近期三起涉及SPC制造豁免的案件。布鲁塞尔企业法院裁定，豁免通知无需包含出口国MA号或具体目的地，且允许合理库存积累。Regeneron诉Alvotech案中，原告质疑冰岛制造商适用豁免的资格，尽管该条例已纳入EEA协议。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines recent Belgian Enterprise Court decisions regarding the application of the EU Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) manufacturing waiver. The courts ruled that waiver notifications do not require immediate disclosure of foreign marketing authorization numbers or specific export destinations at the time of filing; these details need only be provided once publicly available. Furthermore, the judgments confirm the legality of stockpiling products within a reasonable period prior to SPC expiry for subsequent EU launch. The rulings also address preliminary questions concerning the eligibility of entities in European Economic Area (EEA) states, such as Iceland, to utilize the waiver. These decisions reinforce the legislative intent to balance IP rights with market access by avoiding mandatory disclosure of commercially sensitive information during the initial notification phase.
items.ai_insight_zh: 比利时法院倾向于对SPC制造豁免作宽松解释，强调立法者避免披露商业敏感信息的意图。需关注EEA国家制造商在欧盟境内援引豁免的法律确定性，以及“合理库存”在具体案件中的界定标准。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and generic manufacturers utilizing the SPC manufacturing waiver must ensure notifications are submitted at least three months prior to manufacturing. However, parties should note that initial notifications need not contain foreign marketing authorization numbers, lowering early compliance barriers. It is critical to monitor how Belgian courts define 'reasonable' stockpiling periods and whether entities in non-EU EEA states, such as Iceland, qualify for the waiver. Litigation risks remain regarding the scope of eligible applicants and the precise timing for updating notification details, requiring careful strategic planning for both export and EU market entry strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["布鲁塞尔法院裁定SPC豁免通知无需披露出口国MA号或具体目的地，以保护商业敏感信息", "法院确认在SPC到期前进行合理期限的库存积累（stockpiling）符合豁免条例规定", "Regeneron主张冰岛制造商因EEA身份问题暂不能援引豁免，尽管条例已纳入EEA协议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Waiver notifications do not require immediate inclusion of foreign marketing authorization numbers or specific export destination countries.", "Stockpiling activities conducted for a reasonable period before SPC expiry are permitted for subsequent launch within the European Union.", "Legislative intent prioritizes avoiding forced disclosure of commercially sensitive information to balance competing stakeholder interests."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["比利时司法实践明确降低SPC豁免通知的信息披露门槛，利于仿制药企操作", "EEA国家（如冰岛）制造商在欧盟境内的豁免适用资格仍存在争议，需持续观察", "后续需关注法院如何量化界定“合理期限”的库存积累行为"]

items.title: Everyday IP: Easter and the economics of commercial distinctiveness
items.title_zh: 日常IP：复活节与商业显著性的经济学
items.title_en: Everyday IP: Easter and the economics of commercial distinctiveness
items.summary: Explore how Intellectual Property law interacts with Easter folklore to support products in competitive markets, from egg-dispensers to iconic chocolate bunnies.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析复活节传统符号（如兔子、彩蛋）作为公共领域遗产，任何组织无法垄断其概念。但企业可通过知识产权保护具体的产品表达形式，如独特的机械装置或品牌设计。文章以德国起源的复活节兔为例，指出1910年代起的机械发明通过专利获得独占权，展示了法律如何帮助企业在共享文化空间中实现产品差异化。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需严格区分公共文化符号与受保护的具体商业表达。企业应关注如何通过专利或设计权保护基于传统节日的创新产品形态，避免侵犯公有领域界限的同时建立自身竞争壁垒。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["复活节符号属公共领域，无人能垄断概念或拥有“复活节兔”专利", "企业可利用知识产权保护对传统符号的独特表达，如特定机械结构", "年代起，工程师通过专利保护分发彩蛋等机械装置，确立市场独占"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["公共符号与商业表达的界限是节日营销IP合规的核心", "历史机械专利案例显示，功能性创新是传统文化商业化的保护重点", "需持续观察企业在共有文化资源上的知识产权布局策略"]

items.title: The UPC Intelligence Platform
items.title_zh: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER推出UPC智能平台
items.title_en: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER Launches Free UPC Intelligence Platform for Structured Case Law Search
items.summary: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER has launched the UPC Intelligence Platform, a free and publicly accessible search tool for Unified Patent Court case law.
items.ai_summary_zh: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER律师事务所推出“UPC智能平台”，这是一个免费且公开的统一专利法院判例搜索工具。该平台由Akiva Friedmann开发，旨在解决UPC裁决虽公开但难以结构化高效搜索的问题。平台提供可搜索的UPC裁决数据库、AI支持的研究工具，并结合相关裁决直接访问UPC Unfiltered评论，服务于从业者及客户。
items.ai_summary_en: HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER has introduced the UPC Intelligence Platform, a free and publicly accessible search tool designed to streamline access to Unified Patent Court case law. Developed by senior associate Akiva Friedmann, the platform addresses the practical challenge that while UPC decisions are public, they lack structured and efficient search capabilities. Originally an internal project to enhance accessibility of Prof. Willem Hoyng’s commentaries, it has evolved into a comprehensive resource featuring a searchable database of decisions, AI-supported research tools, and direct links to expert commentary. This initiative aims to facilitate easier access to legal materials for practitioners, clients, and stakeholders interested in the Court’s evolving jurisprudence. The platform is offered at no cost and is intended as a dynamic resource for the broader UPC community, with plans for continuous development and updates over time.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该平台的推出反映了UPC实务界对判例检索效率提升的迫切需求。AI辅助与评论结合的模式可能成为后续法律科技工具的发展趋势。从业者应关注此类非官方工具在案例覆盖全面性及更新及时性方面的表现，以及其对传统检索方式的补充作用。
items.ai_insight_en: This new resource lowers the barrier for monitoring UPC jurisprudence, which is increasingly critical given the rising volume of patent litigation in Europe. Rights holders and corporate counsel can leverage this platform to track competitor litigation strategies and identify emerging judicial trends without incurring additional costs. By utilizing these data insights, companies can refine their European patent portfolios and strengthen their defense preparations. The availability of structured case law supports more informed decision-making regarding enforcement actions and compliance measures, ultimately enhancing intellectual property resilience and strategic positioning within the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["HOYNG ROKH MONEGIER推出免费公开的UPC判例搜索工具“UPC智能平台”", "平台包含可搜索裁决数据库、AI研究工具及UPC Unfiltered评论访问功能", "该工具旨在解决UPC裁决公开但缺乏结构化高效搜索途径的实际问题"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The firm released a free tool addressing the difficulty of performing structured searches on publicly available UPC judicial decisions.", "The platform integrates AI-driven research capabilities and professional commentary to significantly improve the efficiency of accessing legal intelligence.", "It is open to global practitioners and committed to building a dynamically developing repository of case law for the wider community."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC判例检索工具的数字化与智能化趋势明显，提升了实务工作效率", "律所主导开发的免费工具丰富了UPC社区的资源生态，需关注其长期维护情况", "后续应观察该平台在复杂案件分类及AI检索准确度方面的实际表现"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize the free tool to monitor recent UPC decisions and judicial trends at low cost, supporting proactive risk management.", "Practice note: Leverage data insights from the platform to adjust European patent strategies and strengthen litigation response frameworks.", "What to monitor: Track the platform's expansion and new features to maintain efficient access to evolving UPC case law and commentary."]
core_points_en: The firm released a free tool addressing the difficulty of performing structured searches on publicly available UPC judicial decisions.
The platform integrates AI-driven research capabilities and professional commentary to significantly improve the efficiency of accessing legal intelligence.
It is open to global practitioners and committed to building a dynamically developing repository of case law for the wider community.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize the free tool to monitor recent UPC decisions and judicial trends at low cost, supporting proactive risk management.
Practice note: Leverage data insights from the platform to adjust European patent strategies and strengthen litigation response frameworks.
What to monitor: Track the platform's expansion and new features to maintain efficient access to evolving UPC case law and commentary.

items.title: De Super Bowl, De Olympische Spelen En Het WK: Hoe Scoor Je Zonder Inbreuk Te Maken Op Handelsmerken?
items.title_zh: 超级碗等赛事中如何避免商标侵权
items.title_en: Super Bowl, Olympics, and World Cup: Marketing Without Trademark Infringement
items.summary: De grootste sportevenementen, waaronder de Super Bowl, de Olympische Spelen en het WK Voetbal zijn enorme aandachtstrekkers. Slechts een selecte groep bedrijven mag zich ‘official partner’ of ‘sponsor’ van zo’n evenement noemen.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨企业在超级碗、奥运会等大型体育赛事期间，如何避免因隐性营销引发商标侵权。文章指出，赛事名称多为注册商标且具知名度，受比荷卢知识产权公约及欧盟商标条例保护。引用L’Oréal诉Bellure案，强调若商业使用不当搭便车利用声誉，即便无混淆也可能构成侵权。单纯信息性提及通常允许，但促销性使用风险极高。
items.ai_summary_en: Major sporting events like the Super Bowl, Olympic Games, and FIFA World Cup attract significant public attention. Only a select group of companies holds official sponsorship rights. Other market participants often face temptation to leverage this exposure through thematic products or social media campaigns, risking ambush marketing claims. This analysis examines trademark infringement risks under the Benelux Convention on Intellectual Property and the EU Trade Mark Regulation. Event names are registered trademarks with extensive licensing programs. Rights holders can oppose use that causes confusion or unfairly exploits the reputation of well-known marks, even without direct competition. While purely informational references, such as stating business hours during an event, are generally permissible, commercial or promotional use carries high legal risk. Companies must carefully distinguish between factual information and unauthorized commercial exploitation to avoid liability.
items.ai_insight_zh: 企业需警惕在营销中隐性关联大型赛事。欧盟法律对知名商标提供反淡化保护，即使未造成公众混淆，若不正当地利用商标声誉或损害其显著性，仍可能面临侵权指控。实务中应严格区分信息性提及与商业性搭便车行为，避免未经授权使用官方标识或暗示赞助关系。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaging in comparative advertising or event-related promotions must strictly adhere to trademark boundaries. Direct use of event identifiers or implications of official association in advertisements and product names creates substantial liability for unfair advantage. Legal practice suggests limiting communications to factual operational statements, such as opening hours, rather than leveraging event prestige for brand enhancement. Prior to launching campaigns, comprehensive trademark clearance searches are essential. This approach mitigates the risk of enforcement actions by rights holders who actively protect their brands against ambush marketing strategies across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["赛事名称如“奥林匹克”系注册商标，受比荷卢公约及欧盟商标条例保护", "依据L’Oréal诉Bellure案，不当利用知名商标声誉获利可构成侵权，无需证明混淆", "仅提供信息性参考通常合规，但具有商业促销性质的使用将显著增加索赔风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Event names are well-known registered trademarks strictly protected under EU and Benelux intellectual property laws.", "Non-sponsors exploiting event reputation for commercial gain may infringe trademarks even without consumer confusion.", "Purely informational references are legally permissible, but promotional commercial use entails significant infringement risks."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["知名体育赛事商标享有反淡化保护，非混淆性使用亦可能侵权", "需严格界定营销内容性质，区分合规的信息性提及与高风险的商业搭便车", "关注CJEU关于不正当利用商标声誉的判例标准，评估营销活动合规边界"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using event names or logos in non-authorized products to prevent unfair advantage allegations.", "Practice note: Clearly distinguish between factual information provision and commercial exploitation of event popularity.", "What to monitor: Active enforcement trends by major sports organizations regarding ambush marketing and trademark misuse."]
core_points_en: Event names are well-known registered trademarks strictly protected under EU and Benelux intellectual property laws.
Non-sponsors exploiting event reputation for commercial gain may infringe trademarks even without consumer confusion.
Purely informational references are legally permissible, but promotional commercial use entails significant infringement risks.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using event names or logos in non-authorized products to prevent unfair advantage allegations.
Practice note: Clearly distinguish between factual information provision and commercial exploitation of event popularity.
What to monitor: Active enforcement trends by major sports organizations regarding ambush marketing and trademark misuse.

items.title: Cut Intellectual Project Cost And Reduce Inefficiency: What Can In-House Legal Teams Do?
items.title_zh: 降低知识产权项目成本与低效：内部法务团队应对策略
items.title_en: Strategies for Reducing IP Project Costs and Enhancing In-House Legal Efficiency
items.summary: There is always pressure on intellectual property costs. Indeed, an in-house legal team is often considered solely as a cost center, which can make budgeting discussions challenging. However, there are several ways a legal team can demonstrate its value to a business—and, potentially, some easy ways to cut costs and improve efficiency within the legal department.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文针对欧盟及法国市场，指出内部法务团队常被视为成本中心。文章提出三步法以降低成本并提升效率：首先通过基准测试评估当前支出及外部 counsel 表现；其次采取行动，如重新谈判定价或引入专业IP提供商替代部分外部律师工作；最后建立关键绩效指标以衡量团队内部效率，优化人员任务分配。
items.ai_summary_en: European in-house legal teams frequently face significant budgetary pressures, often being viewed strictly as cost centers. This article outlines a three-step strategy to demonstrate value while reducing inefficiencies. First, conduct benchmarking assessments to identify excessive external counsel fees and internal operational bottlenecks. Second, take decisive action by renegotiating rates or issuing requests for proposals to secure more cost-effective legal services. Third, establish key performance indicators to quantify team performance, ensuring high-value staff focus on strategic tasks rather than routine administration. These measures help optimize resource allocation, control operational expenditures, and clearly articulate the commercial contribution of the legal department to broader business objectives.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务观察显示，欧洲企业内部法务正从单纯的成本中心向价值展示者转型。通过量化外部 counsel 支出与内部 KPI 管理，企业可更精准地控制 IP 预算。需关注专业服务提供商在 IP 管理流程中的渗透率变化，以及基准测试数据对费率谈判的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Legal departments should move beyond the perception of being mere cost centers by actively managing expenditure structures. Regular audits of external lawyer billing are essential to identify savings opportunities. Routine intellectual property management tasks should be outsourced to specialized providers offering better cost-efficiency. Furthermore, implementing robust internal key performance indicator mechanisms allows teams to measure compliance efficiency accurately. This approach ensures that senior legal talent is reserved for complex, high-value strategic matters, thereby controlling overall operational costs while enhancing the department's strategic impact within the organization.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["通过知识产权成本管理练习进行基准测试，识别外部法律服务的高价环节及改进空间", "针对未来活动提出请求提案（RFP），或引入专业IP提供商替代不必要的外部律师工作以降低…", "建立关键绩效指标（KPI）衡量内部团队表现，调整高薪员工任务结构以提升整体效率"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Conduct intellectual property cost management assessments to identify external service premiums and internal inefficiencies.", "Replace high-cost external counsel with specialized vendors or through competitive re-tendering processes.", "Establish key performance indicators to quantify internal team performance and optimize high-value task allocation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["内部法务团队需通过基准测试量化外部服务成本，以支持预算谈判", "专业 IP 服务提供商可能替代部分传统外部律师职能，成为降本增效选项", "建立内部 KPI 体系是衡量法务团队效率及优化人员配置的关键手段"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Regularly review external lawyer fee structures to seek cost-effective professional alternatives for basic IP matters.", "Practice note: Establish internal legal performance assessment metrics to focus high-salary personnel on high-value strategic tasks.", "What to monitor: Monitor the balance between external counsel spending and internal process automation to maintain budget efficiency."]
core_points_en: Conduct intellectual property cost management assessments to identify external service premiums and internal inefficiencies.
Replace high-cost external counsel with specialized vendors or through competitive re-tendering processes.
Establish key performance indicators to quantify internal team performance and optimize high-value task allocation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Regularly review external lawyer fee structures to seek cost-effective professional alternatives for basic IP matters.
Practice note: Establish internal legal performance assessment metrics to focus high-salary personnel on high-value strategic tasks.
What to monitor: Monitor the balance between external counsel spending and internal process automation to maintain budget efficiency.

items.title: Mewburn Ellis continues litigation and prosecution push with partnership additions
items.title_zh: Mewburn Ellis借合伙人增补推进诉讼与申请业务
items.title_en: Mewburn Ellis Expands Partnership to Strengthen Litigation and UPC Capabilities
items.summary: The latest round of promotions has contributed to a 21% rise in partner headcount in the past two years, with business leaders eyeing litigation and the UPC
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，英国律所Mewburn Ellis通过最新一轮晋升，在过去两年内将合伙人人数增加了21%。企业管理层明确表示，此次人员扩张旨在加强诉讼业务能力，并重点关注统一专利法院（UPC）相关的法律事务机会。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK-based intellectual property firm Mewburn Ellis has achieved a 21 percent increase in its partner headcount over the past two years through a series of recent promotions. This strategic expansion is explicitly designed to enhance the firm's service capabilities in patent litigation and before the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The move reflects heightened market attention toward this emerging judicial jurisdiction and underscores the firm's proactive strategic positioning. By bolstering its leadership team, Mewburn Ellis aims to address the growing demand for specialized legal services in the evolving European patent landscape, signaling a robust response to the operational changes introduced by the new court system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认该律所正通过增加合伙人资源来布局UPC诉讼市场。需持续观察其后续在UPC的具体案件参与情况及团队专业领域分布，以评估其对欧洲专利诉讼格局的实际影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe face an increasingly professional and competitive litigation environment following the establishment of the Unified Patent Court. Rights holders should proactively assess potential risks associated with UPC proceedings and secure necessary resources for effective case management. It is advisable to monitor the activities of law firms specializing in this domain to optimize both defensive and offensive intellectual property strategies. Aligning legal support with the specific procedural nuances of the new court will be critical for navigating cross-border disputes efficiently.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["过去两年间，该律所合伙人总数实现了21%的增长", "业务领导层将诉讼业务及统一专利法院（UPC）作为重点发展方向"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Partner headcount increased by 21 percent in two years, indicating strong business expansion momentum.", "Strategic focus clearly targets patent litigation and Unified Patent Court related business operations.", "Industry leaders are actively positioning themselves to adapt to the new European patent litigation landscape."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注该律所在UPC初期的案件承接类型与数量变化", "观察其新增合伙人的专业技术背景是否匹配UPC高频争议领域"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: UPC litigation capability is becoming a focal point in law firm competition; companies must remain vigilant regarding opponents upgrading their legal resources.", "Practice note: Market participants should proactively develop UPC strategies and match them with professional legal service resources.", "What to monitor: Track recruitment trends and practice group expansions at leading IP firms to gauge shifts in litigation capacity."]
core_points_en: Partner headcount increased by 21 percent in two years, indicating strong business expansion momentum.
Strategic focus clearly targets patent litigation and Unified Patent Court related business operations.
Industry leaders are actively positioning themselves to adapt to the new European patent litigation landscape.

items.title: The IP Lounge: Recognising 30 years of the EUTM
items.title_zh: IP Lounge：纪念欧盟商标制度三十周年
items.title_en: EUIPO Marks Thirty Years of the European Union Trade Mark System
items.summary: João Negrão, EUIPO executive director, is joined by a seasoned official to reflect on three decades of stories
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）将于4月1日庆祝欧盟商标（EUTM）注册开放三十周年。尽管相关法规于1994年确立，但申请通道直至1996年4月1日才正式开启。EUIPO执行主任João Negrão与资深官员Jacqueline Winkelmolen通过播客回顾了这一历程，探讨了从纸质申请到数字化管理的演变，以及该局后续在设计及地理标志领域职权的扩展。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) will celebrate the thirtieth anniversary of the European Union Trade Mark (EUTM) system on April 1, 2026. Although the governing regulation was adopted in 1994, the EUTM right officially became available to applicants on April 1, 1996. This milestone highlights three decades of simplified trademark protection across Europe. In a commemorative podcast, EUIPO Executive Director João Negrão and team leader Jacqueline Winkelmolen reflect on the office’s evolution. They discuss the transition from early paper-based applications to modern digital processes, assess whether initial demand exceeded predictions, and review the expansion of EUIPO competencies to include designs and geographical indications.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This retrospective underscores the EUTM’s role in harmonizing European brand protection. For rights holders, the anniversary highlights the stability and maturity of the unitary right. The expansion into designs and GIs suggests a broader strategic view of IP assets within the EU framework. Practitioners should note the institutional memory regarding application volume trends, which may inform future capacity planning. Monitoring upcoming EUIPO publications associated with this milestone could reveal valuable historical data on filing behaviors and sector-specific growth, aiding long-term portfolio strategy and regional market assessment.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟商标（EUTM）申请系统于1996年4月1日正式向申请人开放，此前1994年法规已…", "EUIPO执行主任与资深官员通过播客回顾三十年发展，包括早期纸质申请处理及需求超出预期…", "EUIPO在成立后逐步扩展职权范围，目前已涵盖外观设计及地理标志的管理与注册工作"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUTM system officially opened for applications on April 1, 1996, thirty years after its regulatory establishment.", "EUIPO leadership reflects on the operational shift from paper-based filings to digital management over three decades.", "The office has expanded its mandate beyond trademarks to include registered designs and geographical indications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["EUIPO职权已从单一商标扩展至设计与地理标志，反映欧盟知识产权一体化管理趋势", "三十周年节点可能伴随官方数据发布，可作为评估欧盟商标申请趋势的历史参照", "需持续观察EUIPO在数字化转型后的服务效率变化及对新兴知识产权问题的应对策略"]

items.title: Is Your Brand Name Too "Helpful" For Trademark Protection? Navigating The New CP16
items.title_zh: 品牌名称过于“直观”难获保护？解读欧盟商标新共同实践CP16
items.title_en: Is Your Brand Name Too Descriptive? Navigating EUIPO Common Practice CP16
items.summary: When business owners and brand experts brainstorm for a name or sign for a new product or service, their instinct is often to be as clear as possible.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章解析欧盟知识产权局与成员国推出的共同实践CP16，聚焦商标描述性原则。CP16依据《商标指令》第4(1)(c)条，明确“其他特征”常指商品或服务的主题事项。若标志直接描述内容、领域或主题（如应用名为“WINE”），将被视为缺乏显著性，无法指示商业来源，从而阻碍注册。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the European Union Intellectual Property Office’s Common Practice 16 (CP16), which clarifies the application of descriptiveness grounds for trademark refusal. The report emphasizes that signs directly describing the subject matter, key elements, or thematic content of goods and services lack distinctive character and are ineligible for registration. Using a mobile application named 'WINE' as a primary example, the text illustrates how such marks are perceived by the relevant public as descriptive of the app's content rather than as indicators of commercial origin. Consequently, overly literal brand names, while beneficial for search engine optimization, face significant legal hurdles. The article underscores the critical need for early legal assessment during brand creation to ensure compliance with EU trademark standards and avoid rejection based on absolute grounds for refusal.
items.ai_insight_zh: CP16强化了欧盟范围内对商标描述性的统一审查尺度，强调标志需具备识别来源功能而非仅提供信息。企业在命名时应避免使用直接指向产品主题、内容或功能的词汇，以防因缺乏显著性被驳回。需持续关注各成员国局在具体案件中对“主题事项”边界的裁量差异。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should avoid selecting core brand identifiers that directly describe product functions, content, or intended purpose. Such descriptive terms fail to distinguish goods in the marketplace and are routinely rejected under EU trademark law. It is advisable to conduct comprehensive legal distinctiveness assessments during the initial naming phase. This proactive approach balances marketing objectives, such as search engine optimization, with the statutory requirements for trademark registrability. Early intervention helps mitigate the risk of costly rebranding efforts and ensures robust intellectual property protection upon market entry into the European Union.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CP16回归商标基础，重点阐释描述性原则中关于“其他特征”即主题事项的认定标准", "若相关公众将标志直接感知为对商品内容、领域或主题的描述，则视为固有特征而非来源标识", "以“WINE”用于酒类APP为例，该标志被认定为描述应用主题，因缺乏显著性而难以获得商…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CP16 prohibits trademarks that directly describe key elements, subject matter, or thematic content of the designated goods and services.", "Signs perceived by the public as describing product content rather than indicating commercial origin are deemed devoid of distinctive character.", "Overly clear brand names facilitate search visibility but frequently result in trademark registration refusals due to descriptiveness."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟层面通过共同实践统一描述性审查标准，降低跨国注册不确定性", "标志若仅传达产品信息而非来源，将被认定为描述性而拒绝注册", "需观察各国审查员对“主题事项”具体范围的解释是否保持一致"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid using vocabulary that directly describes product content or functional features as core brand identifiers to prevent refusal.", "Practice note: Integrate legal distinctiveness evaluations at the brand naming stage to balance search optimization goals with registration success rates.", "What to monitor: Monitor evolving EUIPO examination guidelines regarding the interpretation of 'subject matter' and descriptive characteristics under CP16."]
core_points_en: CP16 prohibits trademarks that directly describe key elements, subject matter, or thematic content of the designated goods and services.
Signs perceived by the public as describing product content rather than indicating commercial origin are deemed devoid of distinctive character.
Overly clear brand names facilitate search visibility but frequently result in trademark registration refusals due to descriptiveness.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid using vocabulary that directly describes product content or functional features as core brand identifiers to prevent refusal.
Practice note: Integrate legal distinctiveness evaluations at the brand naming stage to balance search optimization goals with registration success rates.
What to monitor: Monitor evolving EUIPO examination guidelines regarding the interpretation of 'subject matter' and descriptive characteristics under CP16.

items.title: Court of Appeal again rejects request for further written pleadings in appeal proceedings
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭再次驳回补充书面诉请请求
items.title_en: Court of Appeal again rejects request for further written pleadings in appeal proceedings
items.summary: Hurom v NUC Electronics – Court of Appeal, 4 March 2026 (UPC_CoA_678/2025
items.ai_summary_zh: 在Hurom诉NUC Electronics案中，统一专利法院上诉庭依据程序规则第36条，再次驳回上诉人提交额外书面诉请的请求。法院强调上诉书面阶段严格限于上诉状与答辩状，除非存在特定公平性情形。鉴于被上诉人论点属合法防御且上诉人请求滞后，法院认定口头听证足以解决争议，维持程序效率。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案进一步确立UPC上诉程序书面阶段精简原则。实务中，针对上诉阶段新提出的辅助请求或现有技术，对方抗辩通常不构成增加书面轮次的理由。当事人若需依据程序规则第36条申请额外提交，须证明具体程序不公并应及时提出，否则法院倾向于通过口头听证解决剩余争议以保障效率。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉庭重申上诉书面阶段严格限于上诉状和答辩状，额外提交属例外情形", "被上诉人针对新辅助请求及新现有技术的抗辩被视为合法防御，不触发额外书面轮次", "上诉人在收到答辩状两个月后才提出请求，因迟延及程序效率考量被法院驳回"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC上诉程序书面阶段严格受限，额外诉请批准门槛极高", "对新辅助请求的回应性抗辩通常不足以 justify 额外书面轮次", "申请额外提交须及时，迟延将显著降低获准可能性"]

items.title: Training Data, Market Dilution, and the Elephant in the Room: Why the Three-Step Test Matters for Generative AI
items.title_zh: 训练数据与市场稀释：为何三步检验法对生成式AI至关重要
items.title_en: Generative AI and Copyright: Why the Three-Step Test Constrains Training Exceptions
items.summary: The global debate over generative AI (genAI) and copyright has, by now, produced a voluminous body of commentary. Courts in the United States have wrestled with fair use factors in Kadrey v. Meta and Bartz v.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出生成式AI版权争议中，国际版权法中的“三步检验法”常被忽视。尽管美国法院审理相关公平使用案件，德国慕尼黑地区法院已初步处理记忆化问题，且CJEU将在Like Company诉Google案中裁决AI版权问题。欧盟议会呼吁重新校准版权，英国政府则搁置了广泛的训练例外提案。作者主张应严格应用三步检验法评估AI训练复制行为。
items.ai_summary_en: The global debate on generative AI and copyright has intensified, with courts in the US and Germany addressing key issues while the European Parliament urges regulatory recalibration. Despite this activity, the three-step test from the Berne Convention and TRIPS Agreement remains underexplored. This analysis argues that the three-step test serves as a critical international legal ceiling limiting legislative discretion for copyright exceptions. It challenges the notion that extracting statistical patterns or relying on opt-out mechanisms ensures compliance. Rigorous application of the test reveals that proposed broad training exceptions likely fail legality reviews. The article emphasizes that the three-step test imposes substantive legal constraints on AI training, requiring strict adherence to international norms rather than relying on industry-preferred flexible frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务界需关注三步检验法在AI训练数据合规中的严格适用趋势。随着CJEU即将作出裁决及欧盟立法动态，单纯依赖选择退出机制或统计模式提取抗辩可能面临法律挑战。企业应监测国际公约义务对各国版权例外立法的约束力，以及其对AI模型训练合法性的潜在限制。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying AI models in Europe must recognize that copyright compliance cannot rely solely on 'opt-out' mechanisms or 'statistical learning' defenses. The three-step test remains a binding constraint on EU copyright exceptions. Companies should proactively assess the legitimacy of their training data sources to mitigate infringement risks. Reliance on anticipated broad statutory exceptions is precarious given the rigorous international standards. Legal teams must evaluate whether specific training activities conflict with normal exploitation or unreasonably prejudice rights holders. A cautious approach to data sourcing is essential to avoid potential litigation or market access barriers resulting from non-compliance with established international copyright frameworks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国慕尼黑地区法院已就生成式AI的记忆化问题作出初步司法回应", "CJEU即将在Like Company诉Google案中裁决涉及AI的版权初步引用问题", "英国政府在2026年3月报告中搁置了备受争议的广泛选择退出训练例外提案"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The three-step test acts as an international legal ceiling restricting national legislative discretion on copyright exceptions.", "Arguments claiming AI training avoids expression elements or relies on opt-outs are insufficient for legal compliance.", "Strict application of the three-step test suggests current broad training exceptions may not withstand legality reviews."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["三步检验法作为国际版权法核心，正成为评估AI训练数据合法性的关键标尺", "CJEU在Like Company诉Google案的裁决将为欧盟AI版权例外范围提供重…", "政策层面显示，宽松的AI训练版权例外（如英国曾提议方案）正面临立法回撤风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Do not assume broad training exceptions apply; the three-step test strictly limits EU copyright exemptions.", "Practice note: Conduct early audits of training data copyright status to identify and mitigate infringement risks.", "What to monitor: Track upcoming CJEU rulings, particularly in Like Company v. Google, for definitive guidance on AI copyright."]
core_points_en: The three-step test acts as an international legal ceiling restricting national legislative discretion on copyright exceptions.
Arguments claiming AI training avoids expression elements or relies on opt-outs are insufficient for legal compliance.
Strict application of the three-step test suggests current broad training exceptions may not withstand legality reviews.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Do not assume broad training exceptions apply; the three-step test strictly limits EU copyright exemptions.
Practice note: Conduct early audits of training data copyright status to identify and mitigate infringement risks.
What to monitor: Track upcoming CJEU rulings, particularly in Like Company v. Google, for definitive guidance on AI copyright.

items.title: How Can AI Help IP Practitioners Manage Responses To Office Actions?
items.title_zh: AI如何助力从业者管理审查意见答复
items.title_en: Leveraging AI to Streamline Patent and Trademark Office Action Responses
items.summary: The latest AI tools for IP law firms and corporations are helping IP practitioners streamline the management of routine tasks, including facilitating the analysis and drafting of office actions and response texts. Discover the benefits of AI for your office action response (OAR) workflows in this practical guide to OAR management with Questel's IP Digital Assistant, Qthena.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文介绍Questel的IP数字助手Qthena如何利用最新AI工具，帮助律所和企业简化包括分析和起草审查意见答复在内的常规任务。文章指出，随着专利和商标审查意见数量及复杂性增加，AI通过自动化耗时任务，缓解IP部门压力，提升答复效率与质量，从而支持战略增长与风险管理。
items.ai_summary_en: Amidst the surging volume and complexity of European patent and trademark office actions, this analysis examines the role of AI digital assistants, such as Qthena, in simplifying routine administrative tasks. By automating the analysis and drafting processes, these tools help practitioners manage strict deadlines and reduce the risk of misinterpretation. This automation allows legal teams to redirect resources toward high-value strategic matters, thereby enhancing response quality and overall operational efficiency. The integration of AI serves not only as a cost-saving measure but also as a critical driver for business growth and effective risk management in competitive intellectual property landscapes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文属实务操作指南，强调AI在提升审查意见答复工作流效率方面的作用。从业者需关注AI工具在处理法律与技术复杂性时的辅助能力，以及其对缩短审查周期、降低误读风险的潜在价值，而非将其视为替代专业判断的工具。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders managing substantial patent portfolios in Europe, integrating AI-assisted tools is increasingly vital to handle complex examination procedures efficiently. Utilizing localized legal databases to train models can mitigate risks associated with linguistic or legal interpretation errors, thereby reducing refusal rates. This approach optimizes overseas IP asset layouts by ensuring higher accuracy in responses. Adopting such technologies supports faster grant timelines and strengthens the stability of high-value patents against potential challenges during prosecution.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI工具如Qthena可自动化分析审查意见并起草答复文本，简化常规IP管理任务", "审查意见数量与复杂性增加及期限紧迫，给内部IP部门和外部顾问带来巨大压力", "通过自动化劳动密集型任务，AI释放IP团队精力，使其专注于战略性事务与风险管理"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rising volume and complexity of office actions create significant pressure on IP departments due to strict deadlines.", "AI tools automate routine tasks, freeing professionals to focus on high-value strategic analysis and decision-making.", "Digital workflows enhance operational efficiency, serving as key drivers for risk management and strategic growth."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["AI工具正成为应对审查意见积压与复杂性的关键效率驱动因素", "自动化常规任务有助于IP团队从繁琐工作中解脱，聚焦高价值战略决策", "需持续观察AI在理解 examiner 立场及引用文献方面的准确性与可靠性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage AI to shorten office action response cycles, accelerating rights confirmation in the European market.", "Practice note: Use intelligent analysis to minimize legal misinterpretation risks, ensuring the stability of high-value patent assets.", "What to monitor: Track advancements in AI-driven legal research tools tailored for specific European jurisdiction requirements."]
core_points_en: Rising volume and complexity of office actions create significant pressure on IP departments due to strict deadlines.
AI tools automate routine tasks, freeing professionals to focus on high-value strategic analysis and decision-making.
Digital workflows enhance operational efficiency, serving as key drivers for risk management and strategic growth.

Practice note: Use intelligent analysis to minimize legal misinterpretation risks, ensuring the stability of high-value patent assets.

items.title: Exclusive: UK govt refuses to pay costs in landmark AI case
items.title_zh: 独家：英政府拒绝支付标志性AI案诉讼费用
items.title_en: UK Government Refuses to Pay Costs in Landmark AI Patent Case After Supreme Court Loss
items.summary: Emotional Perception AI is seeking more than £200,000 after the UK Supreme Court backed its appeal
items.ai_summary_zh: 在英国最高法院支持Emotional Perception AI的上诉后，该公司正寻求超过20万英镑的费用赔偿。然而，英国政府目前拒绝支付这笔款项。该案被视为人工智能领域的标志性案件，后续费用承担问题引发关注。
items.ai_summary_en: Emotional Perception AI is seeking legal costs exceeding £200,000 from the UK government following a decisive victory at the Supreme Court. The court previously ruled that the company’s neural network-based invention constituted patentable subject matter, marking a significant milestone for artificial intelligence intellectual property protection in the United Kingdom. Despite this substantive win, which clarified the eligibility of AI technologies under current patent law, government officials have explicitly refused to cover the associated litigation expenses. This refusal has sparked considerable debate within the industry regarding the financial risks of enforcing IP rights against state bodies. The dispute highlights the complex economic realities of high-stakes patent litigation, where even successful applicants may face substantial hurdles in recovering costs, prompting broader scrutiny of government strategies and the overall cost-effectiveness of pursuing such landmark legal challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案虽胜诉但费用回收受阻，显示政府在标志性AI专利案中对抗立场强硬。需持续观察双方就费用承担的进一步法律程序或和解动向，以及该先例对后续AI专利诉讼成本分摊的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the critical importance of conducting thorough cost-benefit analyses before initiating high-profile patent enforcement actions in the UK. While securing a favorable precedent on patent eligibility is valuable, rights holders must anticipate potential difficulties in recovering legal fees, particularly when litigating against public authorities. Strategic planning should include negotiating favorable cost-sharing provisions or assessing alternative dispute resolution mechanisms. Furthermore, companies operating in the AI sector should closely monitor how this ruling influences future examination guidelines for neural network inventions, ensuring their application strategies align with evolving judicial interpretations of technical contribution and patentability standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院此前已支持Emotional Perception AI提出的上诉请求", "该公司目前在寻求超过20万英镑的诉讼费用赔偿", "英国政府明确拒绝支付上述案件相关的诉讼费用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Emotional Perception AI seeks over £200,000 in legal costs from the UK government after winning its appeal.", "The UK Supreme Court previously ruled that the company's neural network invention is eligible for patent protection.", "The government's refusal to pay costs despite losing the case has triggered industry controversy and concern."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["胜诉方在针对政府的标志性诉讼中仍面临费用执行阻力", "需监测英国政府拒绝付费的法律依据及后续程序进展", "观察此案对AI领域专利诉讼成本风险分配的示范效应"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anticipate high litigation costs and potential recovery risks when enforcing patents against government entities in the UK.", "Practice note: Conduct detailed cost-benefit assessments and seek favorable cost terms before pursuing landmark AI patent cases.", "What to monitor: Evolving UKIPO examination practices for neural networks following the Supreme Court's decision on patent eligibility."]
core_points_en: Emotional Perception AI seeks over £200,000 in legal costs from the UK government after winning its appeal.
The UK Supreme Court previously ruled that the company's neural network invention is eligible for patent protection.
The government's refusal to pay costs despite losing the case has triggered industry controversy and concern.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anticipate high litigation costs and potential recovery risks when enforcing patents against government entities in the UK.
Practice note: Conduct detailed cost-benefit assessments and seek favorable cost terms before pursuing landmark AI patent cases.
What to monitor: Evolving UKIPO examination practices for neural networks following the Supreme Court's decision on patent eligibility.

items.title: UPC Weekly - The juicer, the claim interpretation and the jurisdiction
items.title_zh: UPC周报：榨汁机案、权利要求解释与管辖权
items.title_en: UPC Weekly: Jurisdiction Standards and Claim Interpretation in the Juicer Case
items.summary: 2026 Week 13
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅提供标题与日期信息，无正文内容。标题显示该期周报聚焦于统一专利法院（UPC）涉及“榨汁机”案件的权利要求解释及管辖权问题。因缺乏具体案情、裁判结果或法律分析细节，无法提取更多事实要素，仅能确认议题范围。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis of the Unified Patent Court’s latest weekly update focuses on the prominent 'juicer' litigation. It examines how the court establishes jurisdiction in cross-border patent infringement disputes and highlights the decisive role of claim interpretation in determining case outcomes. The article notes that the UPC is refining procedural rules through specific precedents, thereby clarifying territorial jurisdiction boundaries. These developments provide essential practical guidance for future patent litigation strategies within the new European judicial framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 证据不足模式：仅能观察到UPC实务中关于权利要求解释与管辖权的持续讨论。需等待全文发布以获取具体裁判逻辑或对类似机械类产品专利诉讼的影响分析。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe face increasingly defined jurisdictional risks under the Unified Patent Court regime. It is critical to reassess the overall infringement exposure of exported products across UPC member states. Rights holders should adjust patent drafting and invalidity defense strategies to align with evolving claim interpretation rules. Proactive assessment of product liability in key markets allows for the development of differentiated response plans, ensuring robust legal positioning against potential cross-border enforcement actions.

items.title_zh: 欧盟外观设计改革第二阶段细节确认

items.summary: In this article, we look at Phase 2 of the reform changes in detail and what they mean for design applicants.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟集中式外观设计体系改革计划将于今年结束，第二阶段于7月1日生效。继2025年发布初步细节后，最终细节已公布，包括实施条例和授权法规的最终文本。本文旨在分析这些变化对设计申请人的具体影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The comprehensive reform programme for the centralised EU design system is set to conclude this year, with Phase 2 officially entering into force on 1 July. Following the release of initial details in 2025, the European Commission has recently published the final texts for both the Implementing Regulations and the Delegated Regulation. This article provides a detailed analysis of the specific changes introduced in Phase 2 and examines their practical implications for design applicants. These developments mark a significant milestone in the modernisation of the EU design framework, transitioning from legislative planning to full operational implementation. Rights holders must now navigate the updated procedural landscape to ensure compliance and leverage new efficiencies within the European intellectual property system.

items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should urgently review existing and pending design applications to align with the new procedural requirements effective 1 July. It is critical to assess how these reforms influence registration efficiency and potential enforcement costs. Companies are advised to adjust their European filing strategies immediately to optimise protection under the new mechanisms. Proactive adaptation ensures that rights holders can fully benefit from the streamlined processes while mitigating risks associated with transitional periods. This strategic realignment supports robust IP portfolio management across the EU jurisdiction.

items.ai_summary_zh: Vossius & Partner律师Achim Bender所著《欧盟商标》第六版由Carl Heymanns Verlag于2025年出版。该书作为欧洲商标体系入门指南，重点分析欧盟与德国法律差异，收录截至2024年9月的欧盟法院及普通法院判例，并涵盖EUIPO程序新规。
items.ai_summary_en: The sixth edition of the handbook on European Union trademarks, edited by Vossius & Partner attorney Achim Bender, was published in 2025. Serving as a concise guide to the European trademark system, the work specifically highlights the differences between EU law and German national legislation. The content incorporates the latest case law from the Court of Justice of the European Union and the General Court up to September 2024. It also includes recent procedural updates from the EUIPO and relevant courts. This publication aims to provide practitioners with accurate, up-to-date guidance for handling EU trademark matters in practice, ensuring readers are informed about current legal standards and procedural requirements.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该出版物反映了欧盟商标法律框架的最新动态，特别是司法判例与行政实践的更新。对于关注欧洲市场的企业，理解欧盟与成员国（如德国）法律的差异至关重要。需持续跟踪EUIPO程序规则变化及法院对商标显著性、混淆可能性等核心问题的最新裁判倾向。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor recent trends in EU trademark jurisprudence to anticipate examination standards and mitigate registration risks. Utilizing authoritative resources helps ensure compliance with evolving legal interpretations. For companies engaged in trade with Germany, it is crucial to recognize the divergences between EU-wide regulations and German national laws. Updating compliance strategies to address procedural changes at the EUIPO and before EU courts is essential. A tailored approach to intellectual property protection in Europe allows rights holders to navigate complex jurisdictional nuances effectively and maintain robust brand security across the single market.

items.title: Open Letter: Uneven Distribution Of UPC Cases Between The Different Local/Regional/Central Divisions Of The Court Of First Instance
items.title_zh: 公开信：统一专利法院各分院案件分布不均问题
items.title_en: Open Letter: Uneven UPC Case Distribution Reflects Market Demand, Not System Failure
items.summary: EPLAW asked us with e-mail of March 11, 2026, attaching an e-mail of March 2, 2026, from the chairperson of the Advisory Committee of the UPC to provide guidance relating to concerns...
items.ai_summary_zh: 针对EPLAW就统一专利法院案件分布不均提出的关切，Bardehle Pagenberg律所发布公开信回应。作者认为分布不均是市场驱动的自然结果，反映用户需求，而非系统性缺陷。反对EPLAW提出的干预措施，认为这可能破坏体系成功，促使当事人转向国家诉讼。主张采取最小化干预措施，基于数据而非情绪进行改进，以维持体系吸引力。
items.ai_summary_en: Addressing concerns raised by EPLAW regarding the uneven distribution of cases across Unified Patent Court divisions, this analysis argues that such disparities are natural outcomes of market-driven dynamics rather than systemic flaws. The author warns that aggressive regulatory interventions to balance caseloads could inadvertently push litigants toward national courts or non-European jurisdictions, thereby undermining the UPC’s early success. High case volumes in German and Hague divisions are cited as evidence of strong user acceptance and system efficacy. Instead of heavy-handed measures, the piece advocates for minimal, user-centric improvements to maintain the court's attractiveness while ensuring its long-term viability as a truly European institution.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文反映了业界对统一专利法院早期运行状态的不同解读。律所强调市场选择合理性，反对行政干预。需关注后续是否有更多机构支持或反对EPLAW的建议，以及法院管理层是否调整案件分配机制。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should view the concentration of UPC litigation in specific divisions, particularly Germany and The Hague, as a standard reflection of user preference and legal certainty rather than an anomaly requiring correction. Rights holders are advised to continue leveraging these established venues for core patent enforcement strategies. However, stakeholders must remain vigilant regarding potential policy adjustments aimed at redistributing cases. Maintaining flexibility in litigation planning is crucial to adapt to any future regulatory shifts without compromising strategic advantages derived from current procedural efficiencies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["律所反对EPLAW关于案件分布不均的担忧，认为这是反映用户需求的市场动态", "警告干预措施可能危及体系成功，导致当事人转向国家诉讼或非欧洲诉讼", "建议采取基于数据的最小化干预措施，以保持统一专利法院对用户的吸引力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Uneven case distribution reflects genuine market demand and user preferences, not inherent structural defects within the Unified Patent Court system.", "Aggressive measures to artificially balance caseloads risk driving litigants back to national courts or outside Europe entirely.", "High litigation volumes in German and Hague divisions confirm the UPC’s successful adoption and strong acceptance among diverse market participants."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["业界对统一专利法院案件分布存在“市场自然选择”与“需行政平衡”两种观点", "律所警告过度监管可能导致用户流失至国家法院，影响统一法院长期生命力", "需观察统一专利法院咨询委员会是否会采纳EPLAW建议或维持现状"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Treat case concentration in popular divisions as a stable market norm; prioritize these venues for high-value patent enforcement actions.", "Practice note: Avoid reacting to calls for artificial case balancing; focus on procedural efficiency and established jurisprudence in high-volume divisions.", "What to monitor: Watch for legislative or administrative proposals aiming to redistribute cases, which may alter venue selection strategies."]
core_points_en: Uneven case distribution reflects genuine market demand and user preferences, not inherent structural defects within the Unified Patent Court system.
Aggressive measures to artificially balance caseloads risk driving litigants back to national courts or outside Europe entirely.
High litigation volumes in German and Hague divisions confirm the UPC’s successful adoption and strong acceptance among diverse market participants.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Treat case concentration in popular divisions as a stable market norm; prioritize these venues for high-value patent enforcement actions.
Practice note: Avoid reacting to calls for artificial case balancing; focus on procedural efficiency and established jurisprudence in high-volume divisions.
What to monitor: Watch for legislative or administrative proposals aiming to redistribute cases, which may alter venue selection strategies.

items.title: Is AI software for IP just expensive wrapping paper?
items.title_zh: 人工智能知识产权软件是否仅是昂贵的包装纸？
items.title_en: AI IP Software: Essential Tool or Merely Expensive Wrapping Paper for Law Firms?
items.summary: At the last count, there were more than seventy companies offering AI-assisted IP software solutions. Most of these companies are less than two years old. Also, if their marketing materials are to be believed, they have plenty of big IP-firm clients. But do IP firms understand what they are actually buying and whether it is actually worth the cost, both in terms of the short-term risks and
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出目前已有超过70家公司提供AI辅助知识产权软件解决方案，其中大多数公司成立不足两年。尽管营销材料显示其拥有众多大型律所客户，但文章质疑律所是否真正理解所购产品及其成本效益，特别是短期风险方面。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent market analysis reveals over seventy companies currently offering AI-assisted intellectual property software solutions. The majority of these vendors are start-ups established less than two years ago, yet their marketing materials frequently claim partnerships with major international law firms. This rapid proliferation raises critical questions regarding the actual value proposition of these tools. It remains uncertain whether legal practitioners fully comprehend the specific functionalities they are purchasing or if the costs justify the benefits. There is a pressing need to evaluate short-term risks and long-term cost-effectiveness carefully. The industry must look beyond promotional claims to determine if these technologies deliver genuine operational improvements or simply represent an expensive trend without substantive utility for complex IP management tasks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文仅为市场评论片段，未提供完整论证。需关注欧洲IP服务市场对AI工具的实际采纳深度及价值评估标准的变化，以及供应商成熟度对客户决策的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and legal teams operating in Europe, adopting AI-driven IP tools requires careful due diligence beyond vendor promises. Organizations should prioritize verifying compliance with EU data protection regulations and ensuring robust security protocols before integration. A prudent approach involves conducting small-scale pilot programs tailored to specific business scenarios. This strategy allows firms to validate practical efficiency gains and risk mitigation capabilities before committing to large-scale deployments. By focusing on tangible outcomes rather than conceptual appeal, companies can avoid paying premiums for unproven technology while ensuring that digital investments genuinely enhance their intellectual property management workflows.

items.title: Sandoz fends off PI over Eylea at Higher Regional Court Munich
items.title_zh: 山德士在慕尼黑高等法院成功抵御Eylea专利临时禁令
items.title_en: Munich Higher Regional Court Revokes Preliminary Injunction Against Sandoz Biosimilar
items.summary: In early January, the Regional Court Munich granted a PI to Regeneron and Bayer. This prohibited Stada, Sandoz/Hexal, Celltrion and Advanz Pharma from marketing their biosimilar containing the active ingredient aflibercept. This forms the basis of Regeneron’s ophthalmic drug Eylea, used to treat neovascular (wet) age-related macular degeneration and diabetic macular oedema. The US patent […] The post Sandoz fends off PI over Eylea at Higher Regional Court Munich appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院曾于1月初授予再生元与拜耳临时禁令，禁止山德士等销售含阿柏西普成分的生物类似药。现标题显示，山德士在慕尼黑高等法院成功抵御了该临时禁令。原文正文较短，未披露高等法院的具体裁决理由或后续程序状态。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich Higher Regional Court has overturned a preliminary injunction previously granted by the Regional Court Munich to Regeneron and Bayer. The initial order had prohibited Stada, Sandoz/Hexal, Celltrion, and Advanz Pharma from marketing biosimilars containing aflibercept, the active ingredient in Eylea for treating wet age-related macular degeneration. This appellate decision highlights the significant uncertainty surrounding preliminary relief in German patent litigation. It demonstrates that higher courts apply strict scrutiny to infringement findings during interim proceedings. Consequently, even successful first-instance injunctions face substantial risk of reversal on appeal, emphasizing the critical role of the second instance in balancing market access against patent enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认禁令在上诉阶段被阻却。需关注高等法院最终书面裁决理由，以及该结果对原研药企在德国市场执行专利策略的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should recognize that preliminary injunctions in Germany are not final determinations of validity or infringement. The appellate mechanism provides a crucial avenue to challenge restrictive orders rapidly. Companies facing such injunctions should prioritize preparing robust invalidity arguments early in the process. Swiftly initiating appeals can help secure vital market windows while substantive issues are resolved. Blindly withdrawing from the market upon a first-instance loss may be unnecessary if strong legal grounds exist for reversal. Strategic preparation for potential interim relief challenges is essential for maintaining commercial continuity.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["慕尼黑地区法院1月初曾颁发临时禁令，禁止山德士等销售阿柏西普生物类似药", "标题显示山德士在慕尼黑高等法院的上诉中成功抵御了该临时禁令"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需监测慕尼黑高等法院关于临时禁令撤销或维持的最终书面裁定细节", "观察此程序性胜利是否影响再生元与拜耳在德国市场的排他性执行策略"]

core_points_en: The Munich Higher Regional Court successfully revoked the preliminary injunction issued against Sandoz regarding the Eylea patent dispute.
The original injunction had blocked multiple pharmaceutical companies from selling biosimilar products containing the active ingredient aflibercept.
This case underscores the high risk of preliminary injunctions being overturned during the appellate stage in German patent litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the German appellate system to potentially reverse preliminary injunctions and maintain market presence during litigation.
Practice note: Prepare strong invalidity evidence proactively to effectively counter sudden preliminary injunctions in biosimilar disputes.
What to monitor: Track how higher courts scrutinize infringement assessments in interim proceedings to anticipate potential reversals.

items.title: AG Emiliou advises CJEU to rule that OCSSPs' authorization under Article 17 of the DSM Directive extends to acts of reproduction
items.title_zh: 总法律顾问建议裁定OCSSP授权涵盖复制行为
items.title_en: AG Emiliou Advises CJEU: DSM Directive Article 17 Authorization Extends to Reproduction Acts
items.summary: The original Choupette ...Article 17(1) of the DSM Directive is premised on the idea that certain user-uploaded (UUC) internet platforms – that is: online content sharing service providers (OCSSPs) – perform acts of communication/making available to the public within the meaning of Article 3 of the InfoSoc Directive when they store and give the public access to such content. As a consequence
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院总法律顾问Emiliou建议裁定，《数字单一市场版权指令》第17条下在线内容分享服务提供商（OCSSPs）获得的授权，延伸至复制行为。该条款基于OCSSPs存储并提供公众访问用户上传内容时，构成向公众传播或提供行为的理念。
items.ai_summary_en: Advocate General Emiliou has issued an opinion advising the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) that authorization obtained by Online Content Sharing Service Providers (OCSSPs) under Article 17(1) of the Digital Single Market (DSM) Directive extends beyond acts of communication to the public. The opinion argues that such authorization must also cover related acts of reproduction. This interpretation is grounded in the premise that when OCSSPs store and provide public access to user-uploaded content, they perform acts of communication or making available to the public within the meaning of Article 3 of the InfoSoc Directive. Consequently, the legal framework implies that the necessary licenses must encompass both the communication and the underlying reproduction required for storage and access, thereby clarifying the scope of copyright liability for platforms within the EU digital market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注欧盟法院最终是否采纳该意见，这将明确平台责任边界。若获采纳，平台仅需获取传播权授权即可覆盖复制环节，可能简化合规流程。目前仅为法律意见，非最终判决，后续司法确认情况值得持续跟踪。
items.ai_insight_en: Platform operators and rights holders should review existing licensing agreements to ensure coverage includes both reproduction and communication rights, as storage activities are legally linked to public access. Companies distributing content via these platforms must anticipate stricter automated filtering measures driven by risk mitigation strategies. Proactive intellectual property portfolio management is advisable to address potential takedown risks and ensure compliance with evolving enforcement mechanisms on major online services.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["总法律顾问Emiliou建议欧盟法院作出相关裁定", "OCSSPs依据指令第17条获得的授权涵盖复制行为", "第17条前提为OCSSPs存储及提供访问构成向公众传播"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Advocate General recommends expanding Article 17 authorization scope to explicitly include acts of reproduction alongside communication rights.", "It confirms that storing and providing access to user-uploaded content constitutes a communication to the public under existing directives.", "The opinion aims to harmonize judicial standards regarding platform copyright liability across the European Union member states."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确第17条授权范围是否包含复制权，影响平台许可策略", "总法律顾问意见虽无约束力，但常被法院采纳，具重要参考值", "需等待欧盟法院最终判决以确认法律适用的确定性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Platforms must verify that current authorizations cover reproduction acts triggered by content storage and caching processes.", "Practice note: Content exporters should prepare for enhanced automated filtering systems that may impact content availability and visibility.", "What to monitor: Monitor CJEU final rulings and subsequent national implementations to adjust compliance strategies for cross-border content distribution."]
core_points_en: The Advocate General recommends expanding Article 17 authorization scope to explicitly include acts of reproduction alongside communication rights.
It confirms that storing and providing access to user-uploaded content constitutes a communication to the public under existing directives.
The opinion aims to harmonize judicial standards regarding platform copyright liability across the European Union member states.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Platforms must verify that current authorizations cover reproduction acts triggered by content storage and caching processes.
Practice note: Content exporters should prepare for enhanced automated filtering systems that may impact content availability and visibility.
What to monitor: Monitor CJEU final rulings and subsequent national implementations to adjust compliance strategies for cross-border content distribution.

items.title: Exclusive: CMS IP co-head to join Perkins Coie
items.title_zh: 独家：CMS知识产权联席主管将加入Perkins Coie
items.title_en: CMS IP Co-Head Caitlin Heard Joins Perkins Coie to Bolster London Presence
items.summary: Arrival of Caitlin Heard will bolster the soon-to-be-created Ashurst Perkins Coie’s IP presence in the capital
items.ai_summary_zh: CMS律师事务所知识产权联席主管Caitlin Heard将加入Perkins Coie。此次人事变动旨在加强即将成立的Ashurst Perkins Coie在首都（伦敦）的知识产权业务存在感。
items.ai_summary_en: Caitlin Heard, the co-head of intellectual property at CMS, has confirmed her move to Perkins Coie. This strategic appointment is designed to significantly strengthen the intellectual property capabilities of the newly formed Ashurst Perkins Coie entity within the London market. The hire underscores a broader trend among leading European law firms, which are actively leveraging high-profile talent acquisitions and structural mergers to enhance their competitive positioning in the UK and across continental Europe. By integrating experienced leadership from top-tier firms, the combined practice aims to solidify its standing in the capital’s complex IP landscape. This development highlights how major legal service providers are adapting to market dynamics through targeted recruitment and consolidation strategies to maintain robust service offerings for clients navigating intricate intellectual property challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文仅披露单一高管流动事实，未涉及具体业务策略或市场影响分析。需持续观察Ashurst与Perkins Coie合并后的整合进度及其对英国知识产权法律服务格局的实际影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Clients engaging with law firms undergoing significant structural changes should closely monitor team integration processes and potential shifts in service delivery models. As firms merge and integrate new leadership, there may be transitional periods affecting case handling and strategic advice. Rights holders should proactively assess the stability and continuity of their external legal counsel during these transitions. Furthermore, this period offers an opportunity to renegotiate fee structures or service terms, potentially securing more favorable conditions while firms seek to retain key accounts. It is also prudent to evaluate whether the merged entity’s evolving expertise aligns with specific technical or jurisdictional needs, ensuring that the combined resources effectively support ongoing intellectual property management and enforcement objectives.
