items.title: Court of Appeal hears Fujifilm vs Kodak over two days
items.title_zh: 上诉庭两日审理富士胶片诉柯达案
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Hears Fujifilm vs Kodak on Cross-Border Jurisdiction
items.summary: The long-arm jurisdiction of the Unified Patent Court is currently one of the hottest topics in patent law. Following the CJEU’s ruling in BSH Hausgeräte vs Electrolux, it was primarily the dispute between FujiFilm and Kodak over printing plates that made headlines on the subject of long-arm jurisdiction. Other aspects of the dispute temporarily faded […] The post Court of Appeal hears Fujifilm vs Kodak over two days appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭就富士胶片与柯达之间的印刷版专利纠纷进行了为期两天的听证。该案继欧盟法院在BSH诉伊莱克斯案裁决后，成为测试UPC“长臂管辖权”范围的关键案件，引发专利法领域高度关注。
items.ai_summary_en: The scope of the Unified Patent Court’s long-arm jurisdiction remains a critical issue in European patent law. Following the Court of Justice of the European Union’s ruling in BSH Hausgeräte v Electrolux, the dispute between Fujifilm and Kodak regarding printing plates has become a focal point for discussions on extraterritorial reach. The UPC Court of Appeal recently conducted a two-day hearing centered on these complex cross-border jurisdictional challenges. While other substantive aspects of the litigation temporarily receded into the background, this procedural phase is pivotal. It tests how national courts interact with the UPC’s authority when infringement spans multiple member states. The outcome will significantly influence future forum selection strategies and the practical application of EU-wide injunctions in multi-jurisdictional patent disputes across Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案是观察UPC上诉庭如何界定跨境侵权管辖权的关键窗口。鉴于此前欧盟法院在BSH案中的裁决背景，业界需密切关注上诉庭是否维持或调整对“长臂管辖”的解释，这将直接影响跨国专利诉讼的策略选择。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor how the UPC interprets its jurisdictional boundaries post-BSH, as rulings may expand exposure to pan-European enforcement actions. Rights holders must reassess their cross-border patent portfolios and litigation readiness to mitigate risks associated with broad injunctions. Strategic planning should account for the potential that localized disputes could trigger continent-wide legal consequences. Understanding these jurisdictional dynamics is essential for effective risk management and for structuring defensive measures against expansive claims in the new unified system.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注上诉庭对UPC长臂管辖权的具体认定标准及其与欧盟法院先例的一致性", "观察该裁决对后续涉及多国侵权行为的统一专利法院案件产生的判例影响", "留意富士胶片与柯达案中除管辖权外其他被暂时搁置的法律争议点后续进展"]

The Fujifilm versus Kodak printing plate patent dispute has entered appellate proceedings at the Unified Patent Court.

Practice note: Reassess cross-border patent portfolios and prepare robust strategies for multi-jurisdictional defense.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in applying cross-border jurisdiction rules following the BSH precedent.

items.title: How a hair curling patent dispute reached Europe’s highest court
items.title_zh: 戴森卷发器专利纠纷如何诉至欧洲最高法院
items.title_en: CJEU to Define Scope of UPC Long-Arm Jurisdiction in Dyson Hair Curling Patent Dispute
items.summary: The European Court of Justice will decide how far the UPC’s ‘long-arm’ jurisdiction can reach in a complex case brought by Dyson, the outcome of which could have a far-reaching impact on businesses. Sarah Speight explores.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲法院将审理戴森提起的一起复杂案件，核心议题是界定统一专利法院“长臂管辖权”的适用范围。该案结果可能对商业领域产生深远影响。原文仅披露此程序性进展，未提供案件具体事实背景或法律论证细节。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union is set to adjudicate a complex patent dispute initiated by Dyson, focusing on the precise scope of the Unified Patent Court’s long-arm jurisdiction. This high-stakes litigation serves as a critical test case for determining how far the UPC’s authority extends across member states. The final ruling will not only dictate the fate of the specific patents involved but also establish significant legal precedents regarding cross-border intellectual property enforcement. Consequently, the outcome is expected to have profound implications for businesses operating within Europe, fundamentally shaping future patent litigation strategies and clarifying the boundaries of multinational IP rights protection under the new court system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案核心在于厘清UPC与成员国法院及CJEU之间的管辖权界限。鉴于正文信息有限，目前仅能确认CJEU已介入审查UPC管辖范围。需持续关注CJEU最终裁决对跨境专利诉讼策略及UPC案件受理标准的潜在塑造作用。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely track the CJEU’s forthcoming decision, as it will clarify the extent of the UPC’s jurisdictional reach over non-resident defendants. Rights holders must reassess their European patent portfolios and litigation risks in light of potential jurisdictional expansion. It is advisable to conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses and develop robust contingency plans for cross-border disputes. Proactive preparation helps mitigate exposure to unexpected long-arm jurisdiction claims, ensuring that companies can navigate the evolving landscape of European patent enforcement with greater strategic certainty and reduced legal vulnerability.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注CJEU对UPC“长臂管辖权”边界的司法解释", "观察该裁决对未来泛欧专利诉讼管辖选择的影响", "需等待更多案件细节披露以评估具体法律风险"]

items.title: Is Your Business Name Already Taken? How To Avoid Trademark Conflicts Before Launch
items.title_zh: 企业名称是否已被占用？如何在发布前避免商标冲突
items.title_en: Is Your Business Name Already Taken? How To Avoid Trademark Conflicts Before Launch
items.summary: Many entrepreneurs assume trademark conflicts only happen when two companies sell the same product. In reality, trademark law is much broader than that. It is surprisingly common for a business to invest in branding only to discover that an earlier trademark registration blocks the name. The reason often lies in how trademark classifications work.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出商标冲突不仅限于同类产品，常因尼斯分类宽泛导致品牌投资受阻。文章以欧盟知识产权局为例，说明第9类等广泛类别可能涵盖软件、硬件等不同细分领域。审查核心在于“混淆可能性”，需综合考量标志相似度、商品关联性、销售渠道及目标客户等因素，而非仅依赖分类编号。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需警惕尼斯分类的宽泛性带来的潜在冲突风险，尤其是跨细分领域的数字产品。企业应关注“混淆可能性”的多维判定标准，而非仅依据分类代码判断安全性。在欧盟市场布局时，需对在先权利进行更细致的商品服务比对分析。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["尼斯分类体系将商标分为45类，单一大类（如第9类）可涵盖多种看似无关的商品或服务", "商标审查核心为“混淆可能性”测试，综合评估标志、商品、渠道及声誉等多维因素", "即使处于不同分类，若消费者认为来源相同仍可能构成冲突；同分类下distinct商品可共存"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["尼斯分类的宽泛性可能导致跨细分领域的商标权利重叠，需超越分类代码进行实质比对", "混淆可能性判定依赖多因素综合评估，包括标志外观、声音、含义及市场渠道等", "后续应持续观察欧盟知识产权局在类似商品服务认定上的审查实践与判例趋势"]

items.title: The Rising Cost Of Trademark Protection: What Applicants Actually Receive For Their Fees
items.title_zh: 商标保护成本上升：申请人费用与实际获权分析
items.title_en: Rising Trademark Fees: Analyzing Costs Versus Actual Protection Value for Applicants
items.summary: The cost of trademark protection is climbing steadily, but higher fees do not always translate into stronger or faster protection. With fees rising across major offices, businesses must consider not only the upfront cost but the operational and strategic implications of where and how they register their trademarks
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出全球主要司法辖区知识产权保护成本持续上升。重点提及英国知识产权局（UKIPO）宣布将于2026年4月1日起上调商标费用，基础申请费从170英镑涨至205英镑，附加类别费从50英镑涨至60英镑。同时对比了美国专利商标局2025年的费率改革。文章强调高费用并不必然意味着更强或更快的保护，企业需综合考量运营成本与战略影响。
items.ai_summary_en: Major intellectual property offices are steadily increasing trademark fees to cover rising operational costs and system maintenance. The United States Patent and Trademark Office implemented a unified filing fee structure in January 2025, while the UK Intellectual Property Office plans a significant fee increase effective April 2026. These adjustments reflect the growing resource intensity of managing expanding application volumes. However, higher fees do not necessarily guarantee enhanced examination quality or faster registration outcomes. Rights holders must carefully evaluate the strategic value of specific jurisdictions against these escalating costs. Understanding what these fees actually fund, from formal checks to substantive legal reviews, is essential for optimizing global brand protection strategies and ensuring that financial investments yield tangible legal security.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务观察显示，商标官费上涨是全球趋势，但费用增加与审查质量或速度无直接正相关。企业在规划全球商标布局时，除关注 upfront 成本外，更应评估不同司法辖区审查深度、异议程序及维持成本的差异，以优化资源配置。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should reassess the cost-benefit ratio of their trademark portfolios, particularly regarding filings in the UK and EU. Rather than pursuing broad, non-strategic registrations, companies should prioritize core markets where brand presence is critical. Utilizing international registration systems like the Madrid Protocol can help optimize overall budgets for multi-jurisdictional protection. Additionally, stakeholders must closely monitor independent fee policy changes post-Brexit, as the UK diverges further from EU frameworks. Proactive budget planning and strategic selection of classes and jurisdictions are vital to maintain robust protection without incurring unnecessary expenses.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国知识产权局宣布自2026年4月1日起上调商标费用，基础申请费增至205英镑", "美国专利商标局于2025年1月实施新费率结构，统一每类350美元的单一申请费", "主要知识产权局提高费用旨在覆盖运营成本、改善服务并遏制商标体系滥用行为"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Leading IP offices in the US and UK have significantly raised trademark application and maintenance fees to address operational pressures.", "Fee increases aim to offset the resource-intensive nature of registry maintenance and handle growing volumes of trademark applications.", "Higher official fees do not automatically correspond with stricter examination standards or accelerated rights confirmation processes."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["官费上涨不等同于审查标准提升，需关注各国实际审查资源投入变化", "英国2026年费率调整将增加长期持有和维护商标组合的运营成本", "需持续监测其他欧洲国家知识产权局是否跟随出台类似的费用调整政策"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Reassess the cost-benefit ratio of trademark layouts in the UK and EU to ensure efficient resource allocation.", "Practice note: Leverage international registration systems to optimize total budgets for multi-country applications and reduce overhead.", "What to monitor: Track independent fee policy changes in the UK post-Brexit and plan budgets ahead of scheduled increases."]
core_points_en: Leading IP offices in the US and UK have significantly raised trademark application and maintenance fees to address operational pressures.
Fee increases aim to offset the resource-intensive nature of registry maintenance and handle growing volumes of trademark applications.
Higher official fees do not automatically correspond with stricter examination standards or accelerated rights confirmation processes.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Reassess the cost-benefit ratio of trademark layouts in the UK and EU to ensure efficient resource allocation.
Practice note: Leverage international registration systems to optimize total budgets for multi-country applications and reduce overhead.
What to monitor: Track independent fee policy changes in the UK post-Brexit and plan budgets ahead of scheduled increases.

items.title: Can AI Patent Translations Reduce Costs Without Compromising Quality?
items.title_zh: AI专利翻译能否在不牺牲质量前提下降低成本？
items.title_en: Can AI Patent Translations Reduce Costs Without Compromising Quality in Europe?
items.summary: Patent translation is highly specialized, requiring both technical accuracy and legal precision. However, it's also costly and time-consuming, which is why AI-based patent translation tools look so attractive.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨在欧盟专利体系中，如何利用AI工具平衡翻译成本与质量。指出专利翻译需兼顾技术准确与法律精确，错误将影响权利范围。推荐采用“人在回路”模式，结合生成式AI与专家审查，以规避纯AI翻译在术语和语境理解上的风险，确保可执行性。
items.ai_summary_en: Patent translation within the European Union demands high technical accuracy and legal precision, yet remains costly and time-consuming. While AI tools offer attractive efficiency gains, relying solely on them poses risks of terminology errors and contextual misunderstandings. The analysis advocates for a human-in-the-loop approach, combining generative AI speed with expert review. This strategy helps rights holders manage budget pressures while ensuring accurate scope definition and enforceability across jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中应关注AI辅助翻译在复杂专利案件中的局限性，特别是术语一致性风险。建议监控“人在回路”服务模式的市场接受度及其对传统翻译流程的重塑，而非单纯依赖自动化工具。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants seeking protection under the European patent system should be cautious regarding the risks of rights limitation associated with purely AI-driven translations. Establishing a workflow that combines machine initial translation with rigorous expert review is essential, particularly for critical claims. This approach prevents minor errors from undermining broader protection strategies, ensuring robust legal validity and enforceability within the unified European framework.

items.title: Trademark Registration For Musicians: The Overlooked Tool To Protect Your Brand
items.title_zh: 音乐人商标注册：被忽视的品牌保护工具
items.title_en: Trademark Registration for Musicians: An Overlooked Brand Protection Tool
items.summary: For many musicians, intellectual property protection begins and ends with copyright. Copyright law is widely understood in the music industry as the legal framework that protects songs, lyrics, recordings, and other creative works. While copyright is important, it is far from the only tool available to artists seeking to protect their brand and livelihood.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出音乐人常误以为版权足以保护其职业利益，但版权不覆盖乐队名、巡演名或周边商品标识。商标法可填补此空白，防止身份被盗用。随着电商发展，假冒周边泛滥，2025年MF Doom等艺人起诉Temu平台协助销售假货，凸显注册商标在打击侵权、下架 listings 及索赔中的核心作用。
items.ai_summary_en: The article highlights that the music industry often relies excessively on copyright, neglecting trademark protection for band names and merchandise. Copyright does not cover commercial identifiers like logos or tour names, which fall under trademark law. With the rise of global e-commerce, counterfeit goods have proliferated due to insufficient platform oversight, severely damaging artists' brand equity. Recent legal actions, such as MF Doom’s lawsuit against Temu, illustrate the critical role of trademarks in combating infringement. Registering trademarks is essential for establishing source identification, enabling rights holders to request takedowns of infringing links and claim damages. Artists are advised to secure trademark registrations to prevent identity misuse and take legal action against counterfeits to maintain control over their brands.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需区分版权与商标的保护边界，尤其在 merchandise 商业化场景。电商平台的连带责任成为执法新焦点，权利人应关注通过商标权进行平台投诉与诉讼的最新判例趋势，以应对跨境假冒风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should construct a dual protection framework combining copyright and trademarks, prioritizing registration for core artist names and merchandise categories. Given the prevalence of counterfeit sales on platforms like Temu, establishing routine monitoring mechanisms is crucial. Trademark rights enable rapid complaints and takedowns of infringing listings. Companies must prioritize the confirmation of brand asset rights to avoid passive positions in international enforcement. Proactive trademark strategy ensures effective defense against unauthorized use and secures commercial interests in global markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["版权仅保护作品本身，不保护乐队名称、Logo或商业周边，这些需依靠商标法保护", "电商平台助长假冒周边流通，2025年MF Doom等艺人起诉Temu纵容售假", "注册商标是权利人对抗 counterfeiters、要求下架侵权列表及寻求损害赔偿的主…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright fails to protect band names and merchandise, necessitating trademark law to establish commercial source identification.", "Lack of regulation on cross-border e-commerce platforms leads to rampant counterfeit merchandise, severely harming artist brand rights.", "Trademark registration serves as the key legal basis for combating counterfeits, requesting takedowns of infringing links, and claiming damages."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确版权与商标在音乐产业品牌保护中的互补关系，避免保护盲区", "关注针对大型电商平台的商标侵权诉讼动态，特别是平台责任认定", "监测利用注册商标进行线上执法（如下架、索赔）的实际效果与策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish a dual protection system covering copyright and trademarks, with specific focus on merchandise product categories.", "Practice note: Implement e-commerce platform monitoring mechanisms to leverage trademark rights for rapidly combating cross-border counterfeiting.", "What to monitor: Unauthorized use of artist names and logos on major online marketplaces and emerging cross-border e-commerce sites."]
core_points_en: Copyright fails to protect band names and merchandise, necessitating trademark law to establish commercial source identification.
Lack of regulation on cross-border e-commerce platforms leads to rampant counterfeit merchandise, severely harming artist brand rights.
Trademark registration serves as the key legal basis for combating counterfeits, requesting takedowns of infringing links, and claiming damages.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish a dual protection system covering copyright and trademarks, with specific focus on merchandise product categories.
Practice note: Implement e-commerce platform monitoring mechanisms to leverage trademark rights for rapidly combating cross-border counterfeiting.
What to monitor: Unauthorized use of artist names and logos on major online marketplaces and emerging cross-border e-commerce sites.

items.title: The New EU Designs Implementing Regulation No. 2026/138: Key Changes and Practical Implications for EU Design Filing Practice
items.title_zh: 欧盟新外观设计实施条例关键变更及实务影响
items.title_en: New EU Designs Implementing Regulation: Key Changes and Practical Implications for Filing Practice
items.summary: The EU Commission has now approved the new EU Designs Implementing Regulation, setting out a number of procedural changes to the EU...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会批准新的欧盟外观设计实施条例，将于2026年7月1日生效。新规废除了七视图限制，允许提交更多视图以清晰展示复杂设计。同时接受更广泛文件格式，包括视频、CAD模型等，以适应数字产品及动态图形用户界面的保护需求，现代化EUIPO申请实践。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Commission has approved Implementing Regulation No. 2026/138, which enters into force on 1 July 2026. This regulation introduces significant procedural modernizations to the EU registered design regime, following substantive legal changes effective May 2025. The most notable updates include the abolition of the rigid seven-view limit for design representations, allowing applicants to submit additional views to clearly depict complex or dynamic features. Furthermore, the scope of acceptable file formats is expanded to include videos, animations, and computer-generated models such as CAD files. These changes align the EU system with technological realities, particularly benefiting the protection of digital products, animated graphical user interfaces, and non-tangible designs. The reforms aim to enhance filing flexibility and improve the clarity of design disclosures before the EUIPO.
items.ai_insight_zh: 新规显著提升对数字资产及动态界面的保护适配性。取消视图数量硬性限制并引入多媒体格式，解决了传统静态图无法充分披露动态特征的痛点。需关注EUIPO后续是否设定新的技术性上传上限，以及具体文件格式的技术规范，以确保申请合规。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should leverage these regulatory updates to optimize their EU design strategies for digital products, GUIs, and dynamic interfaces. The removal of static image constraints enables more precise representation of claimed features. Applicants are advised to prepare high-quality dynamic materials and three-dimensional models in advance. Ensuring readiness for the July 2026 implementation will allow for the full utilization of flexible expression methods, potentially enhancing registration success rates and broadening the scope of protection against infringement.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["废除七视图限制，申请人可提交更多视图以完整呈现复杂或动态设计特征", "接受视频、计算机生成图像及CAD模型等格式，支持动画GUI及数字产品申请", "新规于2026年3月19日公布，7月1日生效，配合2025年实体法变更进一步现代化程序"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Abolition of the seven-view limit allows applicants to submit more views for complete disclosure of complex or dynamic design features.", "Acceptance of diverse file types including videos, animations, and CAD models adapts to digital and non-entity product design requirements.", "Regulation aligns EU design system with technological realities, optimizing protection for GUIs and XR/VR products."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["动态及数字产品设计获得更灵活的披露方式，降低因视图限制导致的保护范围妥协风险", "需监测EUIPO在线系统是否引入新的技术视图上限，以平衡审查效率与申请人自由度", "观察视频及3D模型等非传统格式在审查中的具体接受标准及清晰度要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Digital product and GUI applicants should prepare dynamic videos or 3D models to replace traditional limited static view combinations.", "Practice note: Utilize relaxed view limits to disclose complex design details comprehensively, mitigating rejection risks due to unclear representation.", "What to monitor: Potential technical limits imposed by the EUIPO online filing system regarding the number of views or file sizes."]
core_points_en: Abolition of the seven-view limit allows applicants to submit more views for complete disclosure of complex or dynamic design features.
Acceptance of diverse file types including videos, animations, and CAD models adapts to digital and non-entity product design requirements.
Regulation aligns EU design system with technological realities, optimizing protection for GUIs and XR/VR products.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Digital product and GUI applicants should prepare dynamic videos or 3D models to replace traditional limited static view combinations.
Practice note: Utilize relaxed view limits to disclose complex design details comprehensively, mitigating rejection risks due to unclear representation.
What to monitor: Potential technical limits imposed by the EUIPO online filing system regarding the number of views or file sizes.

items.title: [Guest post] Wear your trade mark on your sleeve: sponsorship agreements and termination clauses in the football industry
items.title_zh: 袖标上的商标：足球业赞助协议与终止条款
items.title_en: Sleeve Trademarks in Football: Analyzing Sponsorship Termination Clauses and IP Rights
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest contribution by Katfriend Edoardo Cesarini (GR Legal) on a recent Italian first instance decision concerning sponsorship agreements. Here’s what Edoardo writes:Wear your trade mark on your sleeve: sponsorship agreements and termination clauses in the football industryby Edoardo CesariniThe only IPKat-approved sleeveIn a recent
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系GR Legal律师Edoardo Cesarini撰写的客座文章，评论近期意大利一审法院一项关于赞助协议的判决。文章聚焦足球行业中将商标展示于球衣袖标的商业安排，探讨相关赞助合同中的终止条款及其法律效力。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines a recent Italian first-instance decision regarding sponsorship agreements within the football industry. It focuses on the legal nature of displaying sponsor trademarks on team sleeves and addresses critical issues arising when termination clauses are triggered. The discussion clarifies how trademark usage rights are defined during contract disputes and explores the mechanisms for resolving such conflicts. By dissecting this case, the article provides practical insights into managing intellectual property rights in sports marketing contexts, highlighting the intersection of contractual obligations and trademark protection for stakeholders involved in high-profile sporting partnerships.
items.ai_insight_zh: 由于正文缺失，仅能确认该资讯指向意大利司法实践对体育赞助中商标许可与合同解除权的审查。需关注后续全文是否披露法院对违约认定标准或商标使用权回收的具体裁判逻辑，以评估对类似商业合同的合规影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders engaging in European sports sponsorships should carefully draft post-termination obligations regarding trademark removal. Contracts must explicitly define breach scenarios and establish clear transition periods to mitigate risks. Divergent judicial practices across EU member states can lead to unexpected infringement liabilities or reputational damage if not properly managed. Ensuring precise contractual language helps protect brand integrity and avoids costly litigation following the end of commercial relationships.

items.title: Competition litigation: 'user damages' for the unlawful collection and use of data?
items.title_zh: 竞争诉讼：非法收集使用数据的“用户损害赔偿”？
items.title_en: UK Competition Litigation: User Damages for Unlawful Data Collection
items.summary: The cause of action in competition cases in the UK is usually the tort of breach of statutory duty. As a general rule, the damages to be awarded for los...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国竞争案件通常以违反法定义务为诉由，损害赔偿遵循补偿原则。Gormsen诉Meta案提出新视角：若Meta未滥用市场支配地位强制获取数据，本应向用户付费。因此，用户损失被量化为实际所得（零）与反事实世界中应得数据对价之间的差额。
items.ai_summary_en: In UK competition cases, damages for breach of statutory duty are typically compensatory, aiming to restore the claimant to the position they would have occupied absent the infringement. This is usually quantified via a counterfactual analysis, such as comparing actual prices charged against lower competitive prices. The ongoing Gormsen v Meta case before the Competition Appeal Tribunal presents a novel application of this principle. The class representative alleges Meta abused its dominant position by conditioning Facebook access on users surrendering personal data. Damages are claimed based on a counterfactual where Meta would have paid users for their off-Facebook data, framing the loss as the difference between zero payment received and the hypothetical payment owed.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This case tests the boundaries of compensatory damages in digital markets by attempting to monetize data privacy harms through hypothetical licensing fees. It highlights how collective proceedings can aggregate losses without individual assessment, potentially expanding liability for tech firms. Rights holders should observe how courts validate data valuation models in non-monetary exchange contexts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国竞争侵权损害赔偿通常基于补偿原则，旨在恢复若无侵权时原告所处地位", "Gormsen诉Meta案中，集体诉讼代表主张Meta滥用支配地位，以免费服务换取用户…", "该案损害赔偿计算采用反事实假设：即若无侵权行为，Meta本应向用户支付数据对价"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK competition damages follow compensatory principles, using counterfactuals to quantify loss from infringements.", "Gormsen v Meta alleges abuse of dominance by conditioning service access on data surrender.", "Claimants seek damages based on hypothetical payments Meta would have made for user data."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["数据在非金钱交易中的价值量化成为竞争法损害赔偿的新难点", "集体诉讼中聚合损害赔偿的计算逻辑可能偏离个体实际损失评估", "需持续观察英国法院对“数据换服务”模式下不公平定价的司法认定标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Tech companies face evolving risks where data collection practices may trigger substantial aggregate damages claims based on hypothetical data valuations.", "Practice note: Quantifying damages in data-centric abuse cases requires robust economic modeling to establish credible counterfactual payment scenarios for user data.", "What to monitor: Watch the Competition Appeal Tribunal’s approach to validating hypothetical data prices and its application of aggregate damages in collective proceedings."]
core_points_en: UK competition damages follow compensatory principles, using counterfactuals to quantify loss from infringements.
Gormsen v Meta alleges abuse of dominance by conditioning service access on data surrender.
Claimants seek damages based on hypothetical payments Meta would have made for user data.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Tech companies face evolving risks where data collection practices may trigger substantial aggregate damages claims based on hypothetical data valuations.
Practice note: Quantifying damages in data-centric abuse cases requires robust economic modeling to establish credible counterfactual payment scenarios for user data.
What to monitor: Watch the Competition Appeal Tribunal’s approach to validating hypothetical data prices and its application of aggregate damages in collective proceedings.

items.title: “Only a truly European UPC is attractive in the long term”
items.title_zh: 长期来看只有真正的欧洲UPC才具吸引力
items.title_en: Commentary: German dominance at UPC risks long-term European appeal
items.summary: It was only a matter of time before the dominance of German judges and divisions at the UPC became a problem. Even before the court opened its doors, market participants from other countries expressed concerns that this would result in a German court with Europe-wide jurisdiction rather than the originally planned European system. The latest […] The post “Only a truly European UPC is attractive in the long term” appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出统一专利法院（UPC）中德国法官及分庭的主导地位已成为问题。早在法院开启前，其他国家的市场参与者便担忧这将导致出现一个拥有泛欧管辖权的“德国法院”，而非最初规划的真正欧洲体系。文章强调唯有保持真正的欧洲属性，UPC在长期才具吸引力。
items.ai_summary_en: Market commentary highlights growing concerns regarding the perceived dominance of German judges and divisions within the Unified Patent Court. Critics argue this imbalance threatens the court’s identity as a truly European institution, risking its evolution into a national court with pan-European jurisdiction. The analysis suggests that maintaining a balanced, multinational character is essential for the UPC's long-term attractiveness to diverse market participants across the region.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Perceptions of national bias can undermine confidence in the UPC’s neutrality. Rights holders should observe whether case allocation and judicial composition evolve to reflect broader European representation, ensuring the court remains a credible forum for cross-border disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC中德国法官和分庭的主导地位已引发关注和问题", "市场参与者担忧UPC异化为拥有泛欧管辖权的德国法院", "观点认为只有维持真正的欧洲属性，UPC才具长期吸引力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["German judge and division dominance at the UPC is viewed as an emerging structural problem.", "Critics fear the court may function as a German entity with Europe-wide jurisdiction.", "Long-term viability depends on preserving the UPC’s intended multinational European character."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC法官构成及分庭主导权的地域平衡变化", "监测非德国企业对UPC管辖中立性的信心指数", "观察是否有针对UPC结构改革的政策讨论或提议"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Perceived lack of geographic balance may affect forum selection decisions for multinational rights holders.", "Practice note: The court’s legitimacy relies on demonstrating equitable representation beyond any single national legal tradition.", "What to monitor: Changes in judicial appointments, division leadership, and stakeholder feedback on procedural neutrality."]
core_points_en: German judge and division dominance at the UPC is viewed as an emerging structural problem.
Critics fear the court may function as a German entity with Europe-wide jurisdiction.
Long-term viability depends on preserving the UPC’s intended multinational European character.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Perceived lack of geographic balance may affect forum selection decisions for multinational rights holders.
Practice note: The court’s legitimacy relies on demonstrating equitable representation beyond any single national legal tradition.
What to monitor: Changes in judicial appointments, division leadership, and stakeholder feedback on procedural neutrality.

items.title: UPC update: When trade secrets become open | Abbott files claim
items.title_zh: UPC更新：雅培提起诉讼及长臂管辖裁决
items.title_en: UPC Update: Abbott Filing Risks Trade Secret Disclosure Amid Jurisdictional Shifts
items.summary: A further decision on long-arm status, and a new hire for Pentarc in Germany from Taylor Wessing were also among top developments
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅提供摘要片段，事实包括：雅培（Abbott）已提起相关索赔；UPC就长臂管辖地位作出进一步裁决；Pentarc律所在德国从Taylor Wessing律所引进新合伙人。因正文缺失，无法获知案件细节或裁决具体内容。
items.ai_summary_en: This report examines recent developments at the Unified Patent Court (UPC), highlighting Abbott’s newly filed claim which raises significant concerns regarding the potential forced disclosure of protected trade secrets during litigation proceedings. The article further details a pivotal judicial decision concerning long-arm jurisdiction status, clarifying the court's reach in cross-border disputes. Additionally, it notes notable personnel movements within the German patent litigation sector, specifically a new hire at Pentarc from Taylor Wessing. These updates collectively underscore the evolving complexities of UPC litigation strategies and the critical challenges parties face in maintaining confidentiality while navigating the expanding procedural framework of the European patent system.
items.ai_insight_zh: 证据不足模式下，仅能确认UPC近期存在关于管辖权的程序性裁决及雅培的新增诉讼。需等待全文发布以明确长臂管辖的具体适用标准及雅培案件的涉案专利与技术领域，目前不宜对诉讼走向或市场影响做出推断。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders engaging with the UPC must rigorously assess the risk of trade secret exposure inherent in open court proceedings. It is essential to proactively develop robust strategies for securing protective orders and confidential information rings early in the litigation lifecycle. Furthermore, stakeholders should closely monitor trends in jurisdictional expansion, particularly regarding long-arm rulings, to optimize their litigation positioning across Germany and broader Europe. Effective management of these procedural elements is crucial to preventing core technological knowledge from leaking due to mandatory transparency requirements in patent enforcement actions.

items.title: Abbott, Sinocare and Menarini meet for second time at Court of Appeal
items.title_zh: 雅培、三诺与美纳里尼在上诉法院第二次交锋
items.title_en: Abbott, Sinocare, and Menarini Clash Again at UPC Court of Appeal in Glucose Monitor Dispute
items.summary: Abbott is regarded as the market leader for mobile phone-based glucose-monitoring systems. When the UPC first launched, the US company filed a lawsuit against Dexcom’s competing products. This was followed by further campaigns against smaller competitors such as SiBionics and Sinocare. While Abbott’s dispute with Dexcom has long since been settled, the market leader recently […] The post Abbott, Sinocare and Menarini meet for second time at Court of Appeal appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文正文极短，仅显示雅培、三诺生物与美纳里尼在上诉法院进行第二次会面。背景提及雅培曾就血糖监测系统起诉德康医疗并和解，随后对三诺等竞争对手发起诉讼。因缺乏判决细节或程序进展描述，仅能确认诉讼各方及法院层级。
items.ai_summary_en: Abbott, recognized as the market leader for mobile phone-based glucose-monitoring systems, initiated litigation shortly after the Unified Patent Court (UPC) launched. The US company first targeted Dexcom’s competing products before expanding its enforcement campaigns against smaller competitors, including Sinocare and Menarini. Although the dispute with Dexcom was settled long ago, the patent conflict involving Abbott, Sinocare, and Menarini has intensified. The parties recently met for a second time at the UPC Court of Appeal. This development highlights the increasingly prolonged nature of patent warfare within the European medical device sector, as major rights holders leverage the new centralised forum to challenge multiple market participants simultaneously across jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 证据不足模式下，仅能确认案件进入上诉阶段且涉及多方当事人。需关注后续是否发布实质性判决或程序性裁定，以明确UPC上诉庭对此类医疗器械专利争议的审理倾向。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants in the medical device sector should anticipate aggressive enforcement strategies by industry leaders utilizing the Unified Patent Court’s centralized jurisdiction. The current litigation trend suggests that rights holders are willing to pursue multi-defendant campaigns to secure pan-European market positions. Companies operating in this space must prioritize robust freedom-to-operate analyses prior to product launches. Furthermore, preparing comprehensive invalidity evidence and understanding the specific procedural nuances of UPC appeals are critical for effective defense. Proactive strategic planning is essential to mitigate the risks associated with high-frequency cross-border patent litigation.

items.title: For a Real European Patent Law — A Contribution to the Debate on the Redistribution of UPC Cases
items.title_zh: 构建真正的欧洲专利法：UPC案件重新分配之辩
items.title_en: Beyond Caseload Management: Reimagining UPC Case Distribution for a Genuine European Patent Law
items.summary: “Europe will not be made all at once, or according to a single plan. It will be built through concrete achievements.” — Schuman Declaration, 9 May 1950
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文批判将统一专利法院案件分配仅视为案件管理的观点，指出侵权诉讼集中于少数地方分庭反映了既有司法资本的延续。作者认为这种集中限制了当事人的实质选择自由，呼吁通过制度调整促进真正欧洲专利法的形成，而非维持程序表象下的路径依赖。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary challenges the prevailing view that case concentration within the Unified Patent Court (UPC) is merely a matter of administrative efficiency or party autonomy. It argues that litigation trends toward specific local divisions are not spontaneous outcomes of free choice, but rather the result of existing judicial capital and procedural architectures that systematically channel litigants. The text asserts that relying on party autonomy obscures how the court's structure narrows procedural pathways. To foster a truly pluralistic European patent law, the current distribution mechanisms must be reformed. Without such structural adjustments, the system risks institutional convergence, limiting the diversity of legal interpretation and undermining the foundational goal of creating a distinct, unified European patent jurisprudence.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC案件集中度对法律统一性的长期影响。实务中应留意法院是否出台政策平衡各分庭案件负载，以及这种结构性偏向如何塑造未来的专利诉讼策略与判例发展路径。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should avoid path dependency in forum selection, recognizing that defaulting to popular divisions may reinforce systemic biases rather than reflect optimal strategic positioning. Rights holders and defendants must critically assess whether perceived advantages in established venues outweigh the risks of homogeneous jurisprudence. As the debate on case redistribution evolves, stakeholders should remain agile, evaluating opportunities in emerging divisions to diversify their dispute resolution portfolios. This approach helps mitigate uncertainties associated with reliance on a single judicial hub and aligns with broader efforts to shape a balanced, multi-faceted European patent landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC旨在变革欧洲专利裁判的组织规模与合法性，案件分配关乎制度基础", "侵权诉讼集中于少数地方分庭，是既有司法经验与惯性的重新激活", "以当事人自治为由辩护现状具有误导性，忽视了系统架构对选择的引导"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Case concentration reflects systemic architectural biases and established judicial habits rather than neutral, free market choices by litigants.", "Invoking party autonomy fails to address how procedural frameworks systematically narrow available litigation paths and limit genuine choice.", "Reforming case distribution is essential to cultivate a diverse legal environment and prevent institutional convergence in European patent adjudication."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["案件集中现象可能削弱UPC作为统一法院的合法性基础", "当事人选择权受限于既有的司法中心引力，非完全自由市场行为", "需观察后续是否有机制性措施干预案件分布，以促进法律均衡发展"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid blind adherence to mainstream divisions; assess if 'path dependence' creates unnecessary exposure to concentrated judicial risks.", "Practice note: Evaluate emerging local divisions as viable alternatives to diversify litigation strategy and reduce dependency on dominant venues.", "What to monitor: Track developments in UPC case redistribution reforms and shifts in procedural expectations across different divisions."]
core_points_en: Case concentration reflects systemic architectural biases and established judicial habits rather than neutral, free market choices by litigants.
Invoking party autonomy fails to address how procedural frameworks systematically narrow available litigation paths and limit genuine choice.
Reforming case distribution is essential to cultivate a diverse legal environment and prevent institutional convergence in European patent adjudication.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid blind adherence to mainstream divisions; assess if 'path dependence' creates unnecessary exposure to concentrated judicial risks.
Practice note: Evaluate emerging local divisions as viable alternatives to diversify litigation strategy and reduce dependency on dominant venues.
What to monitor: Track developments in UPC case redistribution reforms and shifts in procedural expectations across different divisions.

This data release provides factual metrics on registered intellectual property activities. Users should note that this specific edition serves as a periodic update within the ongoing statistical series. The publication confirms the availability of monthly data points for these key IP rights, supporting routine monitoring of filing and registration volumes across the United Kingdom jurisdiction during the reported period.
items.ai_insight_zh: 这不是实体法变化，但对依赖官方月度数据做申请趋势监测的机构是一个明确的数据质量提示。使用者在比较英国专利活动节奏时，应避免将本期缺失数据误读为需求变化，并关注后续补录发布对时间序列的修正。

Caution is advised when interpreting short-term trends from single-month snapshots.

items.title: Unhinged? The technical challenges of registering a motion mark
items.title_zh: 铰链窗动态商标因技术功能被欧盟法院驳回
items.title_en: Unhinged? The technical challenges of registering a motion mark
items.summary: Discover how to register a motion mark in the EU, including how to avoid the risk of trademark refusal due to a technical functionality.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年1月，欧盟普通法院驳回Kct公司铰链窗动态商标注册申请，认定其运动仅体现技术功能。文章依据《欧盟商标条例》第7(1)(e)(ii)条分析，指出若标志基本特征均服务于技术结果，即便存在替代方案，仍应被驳回，以防通过商标法垄断技术方案。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了动态商标注册中技术功能性例外的客观评估标准。企业需注意，若动态效果主要展示产品运作机制而非识别来源，极易因触犯绝对理由被驳回。实务中应严格区分品牌识别性动作与纯技术性操作，避免试图以商标权固化技术特征。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟普通法院认定铰链窗开合动画仅由技术功能过程组成，不予注册", "评估技术功能性时，替代技术方案的存在与否不影响对基本特征的判定", "商标法旨在防止通过延长保护期来垄断本应由专利保护的技术解决方案"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["动态商标申请需确保运动特征具备识别来源功能，而非仅展示技术运作", "技术功能性评估基于客观事实，不依赖消费者感知或是否存在替代设计", "需持续监测欧盟法院对非传统商标中技术特征与品牌显著性的界限划分"]

items.title: The ‘machine unlearning’ problem: AI’s hidden licensing and compliance challenges
items.title_zh: 机器遗忘难题：AI 隐藏的许可与合规挑战
items.title_en: The ‘machine unlearning’ problem: AI’s hidden licensing and compliance challenges
items.summary: The machine unlearning problem introduces a new category of liability. In AI licensing, the key question is whether verification infrastructure can keep pace with what the machine has already learned. The answer will increasingly depend on whether companies treat data licence governance as an operational priority.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文指出“机器遗忘”成为 AI 治理新痛点。当训练数据许可变更或撤销时，已内化数据的模型难以剥离相关权利痕迹。随着 AI 系统演变为复杂的许可堆栈，企业若忽视数据许可治理基础设施，将面临巨大的合规断层与法律责任风险。
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中企应重新审视 AI 训练数据许可协议，建立动态合规监控机制。建议提前部署可追溯的数据治理架构，以应对未来可能的数据撤回或法规变更，避免因“机器遗忘”技术瓶颈陷入侵权纠纷。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["机器遗忘难题引发新型责任，验证设施需跟上模型学习速度", "AI 系统正演变为多层级许可生态，数据权利治理复杂度剧增", "传统删除资产模式失效，模型参数内化知识导致合规验证困难"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["将数据许可治理提升为运营优先项，弥补技术与法务断层", "构建可验证的权利追踪体系，防范模型内化数据带来的隐性风险"]

items.title_en: Questel Appoints Frederic Beylier as New CEO to Drive AI and SaaS Transformation
items.summary: Seasoned Technology Entrepreneur Beylier Brings Extensive Experience in Leading Global Organizations Through Rapid Growth and Technology Transformation
items.ai_summary_zh: 全球知识产权解决方案提供商Questel宣布任命Frederic Beylier为新任首席执行官，接替任职25年的Charles Besson。Beylier常驻巴黎总部，此前曾担任Flowbird集团CEO及IDEMIA首席运营官。他将致力于加强Questel的集成IP生态系统，整合AI驱动工作流与专家服务，以巩固其在全球IP技术市场的领导地位。
items.ai_summary_en: Questel, a leading global provider of intellectual property solutions, has appointed seasoned technology executive Frederic Beylier as its new Chief Executive Officer. Beylier succeeds Charles Besson, who led the company for twenty-five years and will remain on the Supervisory Board. Based in Paris, Beylier brings extensive experience from Flowbird Group, APRIL, and IDEMIA. His mandate focuses on integrating AI-driven workflows and high-quality IP data coverage into Questel’s SaaS platforms. This leadership transition aims to strengthen the company’s client-centric ecosystem and navigate significant industry transformations through rapid technological growth and strategic consolidation.
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor Questel’s strategic shift toward AI-integrated service models. The appointment signals a broader industry trend where traditional IP management is evolving into data-driven, automated ecosystems. Companies operating in Europe should evaluate how these emerging tools impact patent mining efficiency and monitoring costs. Leveraging integrated platforms can optimize overseas IP asset management and enhance decision-making precision in complex competitive landscapes. Proactively engaging with updated vendor capabilities may offer significant operational advantages and cost efficiencies for in-house counsel and IP teams managing global portfolios.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Questel任命技术高管Frederic Beylier为新CEO，常驻巴黎总部，接…", "Beylier曾任Flowbird集团CEO及IDEMIA首席运营官，具备推动科技企业…", "新任CEO首要任务是强化集成IP生态系统，提升数据质量、SaaS平台及AI驱动工作流能力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Frederic Beylier assumes the CEO role to drive rapid organizational growth and comprehensive technology transformation.", "The new leadership strategy prioritizes integrating AI-enhanced workflows with extensive, high-quality intellectual property data coverage.", "Former CEO Charles Besson transitions to the Supervisory Board to provide continued strategic vision and market expertise."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Questel战略重心转向AI驱动的IP工作流与集成生态系统，可能改变现有服务模式", "新任CEO具有科技转型背景，预示公司将加大在SaaS平台与技术自动化方面的投入", "需持续观察新管理层是否推出新的产品组合或调整针对欧洲市场的服务策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate AI-driven IP tools to reduce overseas management costs and improve operational efficiency.", "Practice note: Leverage integrated data platforms to enhance the precision and strategic value of European patent filings.", "What to monitor: Track the adoption of AI-enhanced workflows by major IP service providers for competitive intelligence."]
core_points_en: Frederic Beylier assumes the CEO role to drive rapid organizational growth and comprehensive technology transformation.
The new leadership strategy prioritizes integrating AI-enhanced workflows with extensive, high-quality intellectual property data coverage.
Former CEO Charles Besson transitions to the Supervisory Board to provide continued strategic vision and market expertise.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate AI-driven IP tools to reduce overseas management costs and improve operational efficiency.
Practice note: Leverage integrated data platforms to enhance the precision and strategic value of European patent filings.
What to monitor: Track the adoption of AI-enhanced workflows by major IP service providers for competitive intelligence.

items.title: TCP: Totally Can’t Purchase — A shortage with long trade mark shadows
items.title_zh: TCP断供危机：短缺背后的商标长期阴影
items.title_en: TCP Supply Shortage: Trademark Risks from Non-Use, Grey Markets, and Counterfeits
items.summary: For a product that smells like a Victorian infirmary, TCP Antiseptic Liquid has developed a very modern problem: you can’t reliably buy...
items.ai_summary_zh: TCP消毒液因制造与原料供应问题在英国零售端持续缺货，引发高价转售。文章从商标视角分析短缺带来的三大风险：长期缺货可能导致五年后面临不使用撤销挑战；官方供应真空诱发灰色市场与平行进口，增加渠道管控难度；高需求低验证环境吸引假冒产品涌入，需加强预防性执法。
items.ai_summary_en: TCP Antiseptic Liquid faces prolonged unavailability due to manufacturing and ingredient supply chain issues, leading to opportunistic resale at inflated prices. From a trademark perspective, such shortages create three recurring risk clusters. First, extended absence from shelves may raise questions regarding genuine use, potentially creating evidential gaps if the mark is challenged for non-use cancellation. Second, the vacuum in official channels encourages grey market activities, including parallel imports that may breach quality controls or labeling regulations. Third, high demand combined with low verification environments significantly increases the risk of counterfeit products flooding the market. Rights holders must address these operational challenges to protect brand integrity and maintain legal standing during supply disruptions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 供应链中断不仅影响销售，更直接冲击商标维持与保护基础。权利人需关注缺货期间的商业活动证据留存，以防未来不使用撤销攻击。同时，市场真空期是灰色货与假货高发期，需监测电商平台 listings 合规性及第三方卖家授权状态，确保证据链完整以应对潜在侵权或确权争议。
items.ai_insight_en: During supply chain interruptions, rights holders should systematically preserve evidence of ongoing commercial activity, such as advertising campaigns and distributor communications, to demonstrate genuine use and mitigate cancellation risks. Simultaneously, brands must activate rapid response mechanisms for e-commerce monitoring to identify and remove counterfeit goods and unauthorized parallel imports exploiting the shortage. Maintaining strict control over channel integrity protects brand reputation and ensures compliance with trademark laws, even when physical product availability is temporarily compromised by external logistical factors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国商标连续五年不使用才构成撤销风险，但短期缺货会造成销售发票等证据链断裂", "官方供应中断导致灰色市场活跃，平行贸易或违规重包装可能破坏品牌质量控制体系", "短缺环境下消费者转向未经验证渠道，增加假冒产品渗透风险，需强化平台监控与下架机制"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Prolonged stockouts create evidential gaps regarding genuine use, increasing the risk of trademark cancellation actions based on non-use.", "Supply vacuums stimulate grey market activity, raising compliance risks related to parallel trade packaging standards and quality control requirements.", "Shortage conditions facilitate counterfeit proliferation, necessitating enhanced platform monitoring and proactive enforcement against infringing listings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["缺货期间需系统性归档广告、分销沟通及恢复供应步骤，以填补真实使用证据缺口", "监测电商平台非授权 listings，区分合法平行贸易与侵犯商标条件或质量控制的违规行为", "观察短缺对消费者购买渠道转移的影响，评估假冒产品在非正规渠道的渗透率变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Preserve dated marketing and distribution records during shortages to prove genuine use and defend against non-use cancellation claims.", "Practice note: Implement rapid takedown protocols for e-commerce platforms to combat counterfeits and non-compliant parallel imports arising from supply gaps.", "What to monitor: Track unauthorized reseller listings and grey market channels that emerge when official stock is unavailable to prevent brand dilution."]
core_points_en: Prolonged stockouts create evidential gaps regarding genuine use, increasing the risk of trademark cancellation actions based on non-use.
Supply vacuums stimulate grey market activity, raising compliance risks related to parallel trade packaging standards and quality control requirements.
Shortage conditions facilitate counterfeit proliferation, necessitating enhanced platform monitoring and proactive enforcement against infringing listings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Preserve dated marketing and distribution records during shortages to prove genuine use and defend against non-use cancellation claims.
Practice note: Implement rapid takedown protocols for e-commerce platforms to combat counterfeits and non-compliant parallel imports arising from supply gaps.
What to monitor: Track unauthorized reseller listings and grey market channels that emerge when official stock is unavailable to prevent brand dilution.

items.title: Mewburn secures Summary Judgment for Banham in the Patents Court
items.title_zh: Mewburn助Banham在专利法院获即决判决
items.title_en: Mewburn Ellis Secures Summary Judgment for Banham in UK Patents Court
items.summary: Last month, Mewburn successfully secured summary judgment for Banham Patent Locks Limited in the Patents Court in Banham Patent Locks Ltd v Rogers (t/a J Rogers & Sons) [2026] EWHC 528 (Pat) , bringing the proceedings to an early conclusion following a half‑day hearing.
items.ai_summary_zh: Mewburn Ellis律师事务所代表Banham Patent Locks Limited，在英国专利法院诉Rogers案中成功获得即决判决。该案案号为[2026] EWHC 528 (Pat)，经过半天的听证会后，程序提前终结。
items.ai_summary_en: Mewburn Ellis successfully obtained summary judgment for Banham Patent Locks Limited in the UK Patents Court case of Banham Patent Locks Ltd v Rogers (t/a J Rogers & Sons) [2026] EWHC 528 (Pat). The proceedings concluded rapidly following a concise half-day hearing, with the court ruling in favor of the claimant. This outcome underscores the efficiency of the summary judgment mechanism within the UK patent litigation framework. It demonstrates how courts can swiftly resolve cases where there is no realistic prospect of a successful defense, thereby avoiding the time and cost associated with full trials. The decision highlights the strategic importance of early procedural motions in clearing unmeritorious defenses and securing decisive legal outcomes for rights holders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了即决判决程序在英国专利诉讼中快速解决争议的效率。鉴于正文信息有限，仅能确认程序性结果，具体涉案专利技术点、侵权认定逻辑及判决理由需查阅完整判决书方可评估。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in or exporting to the UK should be aware of the risks associated with summary judgment applications in patent disputes. Courts may dispose of cases quickly if defendants lack substantial grounds for defense. To mitigate exposure, companies should conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses prior to market entry. Additionally, maintaining robust evidence reserves is critical to respond effectively to fast-track procedural challenges. Proactive preparation helps prevent opponents from leveraging procedural rules to achieve rapid adverse judgments, ensuring better protection of commercial interests during early-stage litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Mewburn Ellis为Banham Patent Locks Ltd赢得即决判决", "案件在英国专利法院审理，案号[2026] EWHC 528 (Pat)", "经过半天听证，诉讼程序已提前结束"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Mewburn Ellis secured summary judgment for Banham in the UK Patents Court against Rogers.", "The case concluded after a brief half-day hearing, significantly reducing the overall litigation timeline.", "The ruling confirms the utility of summary judgment in resolving infringement claims lacking viable defenses."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注即决判决在英国专利侵权或有效性争议中的适用趋势", "需查阅完整判决书以了解涉案专利的具体技术领域及争议焦点", "观察该判例对后续类似专利锁具类案件的程序性影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Be alert to the risk of rapid adverse judgments via summary judgment in UK patent litigation.", "Practice note: Conduct comprehensive FTO analysis before market entry and maintain ready evidence for swift procedural responses.", "What to monitor: Opponents' use of procedural mechanisms to expedite case resolution and secure early victories."]
core_points_en: Mewburn Ellis secured summary judgment for Banham in the UK Patents Court against Rogers.
The case concluded after a brief half-day hearing, significantly reducing the overall litigation timeline.
The ruling confirms the utility of summary judgment in resolving infringement claims lacking viable defenses.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Be alert to the risk of rapid adverse judgments via summary judgment in UK patent litigation.
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive FTO analysis before market entry and maintain ready evidence for swift procedural responses.
What to monitor: Opponents' use of procedural mechanisms to expedite case resolution and secure early victories.

items.title: 15 years of IP Draughts, and a few other things
items.title_zh: IP Draughts博客十五周年回顾与动态
items.title_en: IP Draughts Marks Fifteen Years: Leadership Changes and Key UK IP Developments
items.summary: In March 2011, IP Draughts' then trainee, AnnMarie Humphries, designed this blog, including choosing the image of Mr Pettifog. 15 years later, AnnMarie is to become the new managing partner of Anderson Law LLP, while IP Draughts is moving to a new role as chairman. Congratulations, AnnMarie! IP Draughts is not retiring. He will continue…
items.ai_summary_zh: IP Draughts博客成立十五周年，创始人转任主席并继续从事学术工作。文章回顾了英国IP威胁条款立法修订、法官晋升等历史议题，并指出当前高校衍生企业投资条款已有TenU指南，但作者仍主张由UKRI赞助制定更完整的模板协议。
items.ai_summary_en: The prominent intellectual property blog IP Draughts celebrates its fifteenth anniversary. Founder AnnMarie Humphries, who originally designed the site in 2011, is transitioning to Managing Partner at Anderson Law LLP, while the author assumes the role of Chairman. The article reflects on significant UK IP developments since inception, including the establishment of the UCL IP Transactions course, substantial legislative revisions to IP threats provisions, and the judicial elevation of Lord Kitchin to the Supreme Court. It also examines university technology transfer models, noting that while the TenU Guide exists, a standardized investment agreement template sponsored by UKRI remains necessary to streamline spin-out transactions and enhance efficiency in academic commercialization.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文主要为行业回顾与观点阐述，非最新法律变更或判例。关注点在于英国高校技术转移领域的合同标准化趋势，以及从业者对现有TenU指南局限性的看法，可作为了解英国IP交易实务演变的参考背景。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should track the evolution of UK IP legislation and judicial trends, as these factors directly influence cross-border enforcement strategies and technology acquisition costs. The ongoing discussion regarding standardized agreements for university spin-outs highlights a persistent gap in current practice. Utilizing established contract frameworks can mitigate legal and commercial risks associated with technology transfer. Rights holders operating in Europe should monitor these policy shifts to optimize compliance management and ensure robust protection of intellectual assets during collaborative research and commercialization efforts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年英国通过立法实质性修订了知识产权威胁条款 provisions", "高校衍生企业投资条款工作已被TenU指南取代，但作者仍呼吁制定完整模板协议", "UCL IP交易课程将于2026年4月再次举办，持续提供相关教育内容"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Blog founder transitions to managing partner role while continuing active client work and academic engagement in intellectual property law.", "Review of major UK IP milestones includes legislative reforms on threats actions and significant judicial appointments within higher courts.", "Advocacy continues for standardized UKRI-sponsored investment templates to improve efficiency in university spin-out licensing deals."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国高校衍生企业投资协议标准化进程仍在讨论中，TenU指南并非终点", "知识产权威胁条款的立法修订是英国IP实务中的重要历史节点", "需持续关注英国在高校IP商业化领域的政策引导与模板协议发展动向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor UK legislative and judicial dynamics to adjust cross-border enforcement and technology import strategies effectively.", "Practice note: Leverage standardized contract templates to reduce legal and commercial risks in university technology transfer projects.", "What to monitor: Observe trends in UK judicial handling of IP cases to optimize European intellectual property layout and compliance."]
core_points_en: Blog founder transitions to managing partner role while continuing active client work and academic engagement in intellectual property law.
Review of major UK IP milestones includes legislative reforms on threats actions and significant judicial appointments within higher courts.

items.title: Biotech Review of the Year Issue 13 - out now!
items.title_zh: Bristows发布第13版年度生物技术审查报告
items.title_en: Bristows Releases Thirteenth Edition of Biotech Review of the Year
items.summary: We’re pleased to share the 13th edition of our Biotech Review of the Year, bringing together practical legal, regulatory and commercial insight for the ...
items.ai_summary_zh: Bristows律师事务所发布第13版《年度生物技术审查》。该报告整合了针对生物技术行业的实用法律、监管和商业洞察。内容涵盖细胞与基因疗法的关键进展、监管动态、竞争审查以及英国和欧洲的诉讼趋势，并包含行业对内部团队角色演变的观点。
items.ai_summary_en: Bristows has published the thirteenth edition of its Biotech Review of the Year, offering practical legal, regulatory, and commercial insights for the biotechnology sector. The report analyzes key developments in cell and gene therapies, alongside trends in regulation, competition scrutiny, and litigation across the United Kingdom and Europe. It also incorporates industry perspectives on the evolving role of in-house legal teams. This edition aims to provide essential reading for stakeholders seeking to understand the critical issues currently shaping the biotech landscape in these jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本报告为律所发布的年度行业综述，旨在提供宏观趋势观察。读者应关注其中关于英欧两地诉讼趋势及竞争审查的总结，以把握生物技术领域的合规与争议解决风向，具体细节需查阅报告全文。
items.ai_insight_en: This annual review serves as a strategic resource for rights holders and counsel navigating the complex biotech environment in Europe and the UK. By consolidating updates on litigation and regulatory shifts, it helps practitioners anticipate enforcement risks and compliance requirements. The inclusion of in-house team dynamics offers valuable context for organizational planning within life sciences companies operating in these markets.

items.title: Cup&Cino wins with Boehmert & Boehmert in dispute over milk frothers
items.title_zh: Cup&Cino在奶泡器专利纠纷中胜诉
items.title_en: Cup&Cino Prevails in German Milk Frother Patent Dispute Against Alpina
items.summary: Cup&Cino owns three patents protecting technology for milk frothers. EP 3 398 487 protects a “method and device for producing milk foam”, EP 3 281 569 covers a “modular foam unit” and EP 3 610 762 a “device for treating a liquid, in particular milk, with integrated rinsing system and method for rinsing”. The German […] The post Cup&Cino wins with Boehmert & Boehmert in dispute over milk frothers appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: Cup&Cino拥有三项保护奶泡器技术的欧洲专利。在德国进行的争议中，Cup&Cino在Boehmert & Boehmert律师事务所代理下获胜。涉案专利包括EP 3 398 487（制备奶泡沫的方法和设备）、EP 3 281 569（模块化泡沫单元）及EP 3 610 762（带集成冲洗系统的液体处理设备）。
items.ai_summary_en: Cup&Cino has secured a victory in a German patent infringement dispute against Alpina concerning milk frother technology. The rights holder relies on three European patents covering methods for producing milk foam, modular foam units, and liquid treatment devices with integrated rinsing systems. Represented by Boehmert & Boehmert, Cup&Cino successfully defended its intellectual property rights after changing legal counsel during the proceedings. This outcome underscores the effectiveness of the German judicial system in enforcing patent protections for complex electromechanical appliances. The case highlights the strategic importance of selecting experienced local litigation teams to navigate procedural challenges and secure favorable judgments in cross-border IP conflicts involving consumer electronics and fluid handling technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注该胜诉判决的具体法律依据及是否涉及禁令救济。鉴于正文信息有限，暂无法评估对同类小家电专利布局的影响，建议持续追踪后续上诉或执行动态。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting small household appliances to Europe must conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses prior to entry, particularly regarding fluid processing technologies. German patent enforcement remains rigorous, making early risk assessment critical to avoid costly litigation. In the event of disputes, engaging specialized local counsel is essential for effective defense strategies. Companies should monitor ongoing developments in this sector to understand evolving judicial interpretations of method and apparatus claims, ensuring robust compliance and strategic positioning within the European market landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Cup&Cino持有三项涉及奶泡器技术的欧洲专利，涵盖制备方法、模块化单元及冲洗系统", "在德国相关争议中，Cup&Cino由Boehmert & Boehmert代理并取得胜…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Cup&Cino holds three key European patents covering milk foam production methods and modular components.", "The litigation focused on alleged infringement of specific manufacturing techniques and structural unit designs.", "German courts ruled in favor of the patent owner, ordering the cessation of infringing activities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注德国法院对上述三项欧洲专利权利要求的解释范围", "观察该判决是否引发后续无效程序或和解谈判", "留意涉案专利技术在咖啡机或奶泡器市场的排他性影响"]

What to monitor: Judicial trends in interpreting method versus apparatus claims for electromechanical devices.

items.title: Commercial disputes in the life sciences sector: the complexity of assessing loss
items.title_zh: 生命科学领域商业纠纷：损失评估的复杂性
items.title_en: Life Sciences Commercial Disputes: Navigating the Complexity of Assessing Loss
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. With thanks to Shreya Gupta and Mohammed Khalil of Oxera, and Jemima Stra...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系Bristows年度生物技术评论的一部分，探讨生命科学企业商业纠纷中损失评估的难点。文章指出，该领域富含知识产权的交易（如研发合作、许可、合资）及复杂供应链协议常引发争议，且行业特性使得损害赔偿分析尤为复杂。
items.ai_summary_en: This article, part of Bristows' Biotech Review of the Year, examines the difficulties in assessing loss within life sciences commercial disputes. It highlights that the sector's unique characteristics, including long product cycles and complex regulatory environments, make damages analysis particularly challenging. The text identifies key areas where disputes frequently arise, such as intellectual property-rich transactions like R&D collaborations and licensing deals, as well as intricate supply chain agreements covering manufacturing, distribution, and logistics. Due to these complexities, calculating compensation requires a nuanced approach tailored to specific transaction structures. The analysis underscores the need for precise contractual frameworks to address potential breaches effectively, ensuring that parties can navigate the intricate landscape of liability and loss quantification inherent in the highly regulated and innovation-driven life sciences industry.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文提示生命科学领域的IP商业化交易中，损害赔偿计算具有高度专业性。需关注合作与供应链环节的合同风险，以及纠纷发生时损失量化的法律与经济论证逻辑，而非单纯依赖侵权认定。
items.ai_insight_en: In cross-border life sciences transactions, parties should proactively define clear mechanisms for calculating losses within their contracts. Pre-agreed damage models can significantly reduce uncertainty during dispute resolution. Given the industry's complexity, standard valuation methods may prove insufficient. Rights holders must consider how specific sector attributes, such as regulatory hurdles and long market entry times, impact breach consequences. Establishing robust analytical frameworks early helps manage risks associated with complex damage assessments. This approach ensures that potential commercial disputes are handled with greater predictability, protecting commercial interests against the backdrop of intricate operational and legal challenges inherent in the global life sciences market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["生命科学商业纠纷常源于富含知识产权的交易，如研发合作、共同开发、许可及合资协议", "除核心交易外，原材料制造、供应、分销及物流等复杂供应链合同也是纠纷高发领域", "由于行业独特性，解决此类纠纷颇具挑战性，主要难点在于对损失进行准确评估与分析"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Disputes frequently originate from IP-intensive transactions, including research collaborations, co-development agreements, and licensing deals.", "Supply chain contracts, encompassing manufacturing, distribution, and logistics services, represent another significant area prone to commercial conflict.", "The sector's distinct features, such as lengthy development timelines and strict regulations, complicate the calculation and analysis of damages."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["生命科学IP交易纠纷中，损失评估因行业特性而复杂，需结合经济与法律双重视角", "纠纷不仅限于核心研发许可，还广泛分布于制造、供应及物流等供应链各环节合同中", "实务中应重视交易架构对潜在损害赔偿计算的影响，特别是合作与合资模式下的责任界定"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Pre-define damage calculation logic in cross-border contracts to minimize uncertainty during potential disputes.", "Practice note: Account for the unique impact of life sciences attributes on breach determination and loss assessment processes.", "What to monitor: Evolving judicial or arbitral approaches to quantifying loss in complex, regulated industry contexts."]
core_points_en: Disputes frequently originate from IP-intensive transactions, including research collaborations, co-development agreements, and licensing deals.
Supply chain contracts, encompassing manufacturing, distribution, and logistics services, represent another significant area prone to commercial conflict.
The sector's distinct features, such as lengthy development timelines and strict regulations, complicate the calculation and analysis of damages.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Pre-define damage calculation logic in cross-border contracts to minimize uncertainty during potential disputes.
Practice note: Account for the unique impact of life sciences attributes on breach determination and loss assessment processes.
What to monitor: Evolving judicial or arbitral approaches to quantifying loss in complex, regulated industry contexts.

items.title: 2026: A year of clarity for the SPC Manufacturing Waiver Regulation?
items.title_zh: 2026年：SPC制造豁免法规能否迎来清晰化？
items.title_en: SPC Manufacturing Waiver: Judicial Divergence and Prospects for Clarity in 2026
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. Between 2024 and 2025, there were multiple cases across Europe addressing...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出2024至2025年间，欧洲多地法院就SPC制造豁免法规适用范围作出不同解释，导致欧盟与英国司法实践存在分歧。预计2026至2027年，通过进一步判决及欧盟委员会咨询，该法规范围有望 clarified。文中回顾了法规旨在消除欧盟仿制药企竞争劣势，并允许在特定条件下为出口或SPC到期后入市进行制造。
items.ai_summary_en: Between 2024 and 2025, European courts issued divergent rulings on the scope of the SPC Manufacturing Waiver Regulation, creating uncertainty across the EU and UK. Introduced in 2019 to support generic and biosimilar manufacturers, the waiver permits EU-based production for export to third countries or stockpiling for post-SPC market entry, subject to strict labeling and notification duties. The UK retained a modified version post-Brexit, allowing exports outside both the UK and EU. With judicial interpretations varying significantly, stakeholders anticipate that further judgments and an upcoming European Commission consultation in 2026 and 2027 will help clarify the regulation’s application and resolve current inconsistencies in enforcement and compliance standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and manufacturers must navigate inconsistent judicial approaches to the SPC Manufacturing Waiver while maintaining rigorous compliance with notification and labeling requirements. The anticipated European Commission consultation offers a critical opportunity to address interpretive gaps. Stakeholders should track developing case law and policy feedback to adjust supply chain strategies and enforcement positions accordingly.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年欧洲多国法院对SPC制造豁免范围解释不一，欧盟与英国存在分歧", "预计2026-2027年借由新判决及欧盟委员会咨询，法规适用范围将获澄清", "英国脱欧后修订法规，允许为出口至英国和欧盟以外国家而在英制造"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Courts in the EU and UK have adopted diverging interpretations of the SPC Manufacturing Waiver scope during 2024 and 2025.", "The waiver allows EU manufacturing for export or stockpiling, conditional on strict labeling, due diligence, and prior notification obligations.", "Future clarity is expected through additional case law and the European Commission’s consultation process scheduled for 2026 and 2027."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟与英国法院在SPC豁免适用范围上的司法分歧及其后续统一趋势", "监测欧盟委员会关于SPC制造豁免法规的咨询进程及潜在政策调整方向", "在法规澄清前，严格遵守现有的通知、标签及供应链尽职调查合规要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Divergent court rulings create uncertainty for SPC holders enforcing rights and manufacturers relying on the waiver for export or stockpiling activities.", "Practice note: Strict adherence to labeling, supply chain due diligence, and three-month prior notification rules remains essential to maintain waiver eligibility amid legal ambig…", "What to monitor: Watch for new judgments in 2026-2027 and outcomes from the European Commission’s consultation on the SPC Manufacturing Waiver Regulation."]
core_points_en: Courts in the EU and UK have adopted diverging interpretations of the SPC Manufacturing Waiver scope during 2024 and 2025.
The waiver allows EU manufacturing for export or stockpiling, conditional on strict labeling, due diligence, and prior notification obligations.
Future clarity is expected through additional case law and the European Commission’s consultation process scheduled for 2026 and 2027.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Divergent court rulings create uncertainty for SPC holders enforcing rights and manufacturers relying on the waiver for export or stockpiling activities.
Practice note: Strict adherence to labeling, supply chain due diligence, and three-month prior notification rules remains essential to maintain waiver eligibility amid legal ambig…
What to monitor: Watch for new judgments in 2026-2027 and outcomes from the European Commission’s consultation on the SPC Manufacturing Waiver Regulation.

items.title: Has biotech litigation entered a new era?
items.title_zh: 生物制药诉讼是否进入新时代？
items.title_en: Biotech Litigation Trends: Biosimilar Competition and Formulation Patent Disputes in Europe
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. 2025 was an important year for biotech patent litigation, in Europe and f...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年欧洲生物制药专利诉讼因生物类似药竞争加剧而呈现新态势，混合了国家诉讼与统一专利法院案件。焦点案例围绕阿柏西普（Eylea）在补充保护证书到期后的制剂专利纠纷。英国法院审理中，原告依据Actavis案确立的原则，主张被告变体配方构成等同侵权，重点考察结果、方式及意图三要素。
items.ai_summary_en: The 2025 biotech litigation landscape in Europe shifted due to increased biosimilar competition, involving national courts and the Unified Patent Court. Key disputes centered on aflibercept (Eylea) following its supplementary protection certificate expiry in November 2025. Litigation focused on remaining formulation patents, specifically EP 2 364 691 and EP 2 944 306. In English proceedings, patentees asserted infringement under the doctrine of equivalents for biosimilar variants. This reflects a broader trend where lower manufacturing costs and marketing partnerships are intensifying market entry, changing the nature of disputes from simple volume increases to complex cross-border and UPC conflicts.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: As core compound protections expire, formulation patents remain critical barriers for biosimilar entrants. Rights holders increasingly rely on the doctrine of equivalents to capture variant formulations. Companies must navigate divergent infringement standards between national courts and the UPC, particularly regarding technical equivalence assessments in complex biological products.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["年生物类似药竞争加剧，推动欧洲专利诉讼量增且性质变化，涵盖UPC及跨国争议", "阿柏西普SPC于2025年11月到期，诉讼焦点转为剩余有效的制剂专利EP 691和EP…", "英国法院依Actavis原则审理等同侵权，评估变体是否以实质相同方式实现实质相同结果"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Increased biosimilar competition is driving complex litigation across national courts and the Unified Patent Court.", "Aflibercept disputes highlight strategic reliance on formulation patents after supplementary protection certificate expiry.", "English courts apply Actavis doctrine of equivalents test to assess infringement by biosimilar formulation variants."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["生物类似药企应警惕制剂专利在SPC到期后的持续阻碍作用及等同侵权主张", "英国法院适用Actavis测试判定变体侵权，需重点关注技术特征比对与发明意图", "需持续观察统一专利法院在生物制药复杂技术事实认定上的裁判趋势与影响力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Biosimilar developers face heightened infringement risks from formulation patents even after primary SPC expiry.", "Practice note: English courts continue to apply the Actavis three-part test for determining equivalence in variant formulations.", "What to monitor: Evolving UPC jurisprudence on formulation patent scope and its alignment with national court interpretations."]
core_points_en: Increased biosimilar competition is driving complex litigation across national courts and the Unified Patent Court.
Aflibercept disputes highlight strategic reliance on formulation patents after supplementary protection certificate expiry.
English courts apply Actavis doctrine of equivalents test to assess infringement by biosimilar formulation variants.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Biosimilar developers face heightened infringement risks from formulation patents even after primary SPC expiry.
Practice note: English courts continue to apply the Actavis three-part test for determining equivalence in variant formulations.
What to monitor: Evolving UPC jurisprudence on formulation patent scope and its alignment with national court interpretations.

items.title: Trial by name, trial by nature: how lack of data protection harmonisation is hindering European clinical trials
items.title_zh: 名义与自然审判：数据保护缺乏协调阻碍欧洲临床试验
items.title_en: Data Protection Fragmentation Hinders European Clinical Trial Participation and Innovation
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. Europe’s ability to leverage its world-leading academic institutions, hea...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，尽管欧洲拥有顶尖学术与医疗资源，但欧洲经济区内数据保护法解释与执行的差异正削弱其临床研究地位。2013至2023年间，全球临床试验增38%，而欧洲份额减半，导致患者早期获取新药机会减少。监管不协调而非监管总量是主因，跨国试验面临多国不同合规流程挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: Divergent interpretations of data protection law within the EEA are undermining Europe’s position in clinical research. Despite strong academic and healthcare infrastructure, the EEA’s share of global clinical trials halved between 2013 and 2023, while global initiations rose by 38%. Approximately 60,000 fewer patients participated in EEA trials in 2023 compared to 2018. The analysis identifies regulatory fragmentation, rather than overall regulatory burden, as a primary driver. With over two-thirds of trials involving multiple countries, sponsors face complex operational challenges complying with varying national data governance practices and ethics committee requirements, ultimately delaying patient access to new treatments.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需关注欧盟各国在伦理委员会及数据治理上的具体差异。跨国药企应监测监管机构对GDPR在临床场景下的执法口径变化，评估合规成本对研发选址的影响，而非仅关注法规条文本身。
items.ai_insight_en: For life sciences entities, inconsistent GDPR enforcement across Member States increases compliance complexity for cross-border trials. Legal teams should prioritize mapping national variations in data processing requirements and ethics committee expectations. Monitoring emerging EU-level harmonization efforts is essential to anticipate shifts in regulatory alignment and optimize trial site selection strategies within the region.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲经济区数据保护法解释执行存在差异，阻碍临床研究与创新", "年全球试验增38%，欧洲份额减半，参与人数显著下降", "超三分之二试验涉及多国，赞助商需应对不同成员国的合规流程"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EEA share of global clinical trials halved from 2013 to 2023 despite rising global volumes.", "Regulatory fragmentation in data protection enforcement creates operational hurdles for multi-country trial sponsors.", "Divergent national approaches to data governance and ethics committees complicate compliance processes across Member States."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["跨国临床试验需应对成员国间数据保护执法差异带来的合规复杂性", "监管碎片化导致欧洲临床试验份额下降，影响患者获取新药速度", "需持续观察欧盟层面是否出台进一步协调数据保护执法的指导政策"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Regulatory divergence increases operational costs and delays for sponsors conducting multi-jurisdictional clinical trials in the EEA.", "Practice note: Compliance frameworks must account for varied national interpretations of data protection rules and local ethics committee procedures.", "What to monitor: Developments in EU-level guidance aimed at harmonizing data protection enforcement standards for clinical research contexts."]
core_points_en: EEA share of global clinical trials halved from 2013 to 2023 despite rising global volumes.
Regulatory fragmentation in data protection enforcement creates operational hurdles for multi-country trial sponsors.
Divergent national approaches to data governance and ethics committees complicate compliance processes across Member States.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Regulatory divergence increases operational costs and delays for sponsors conducting multi-jurisdictional clinical trials in the EEA.
Practice note: Compliance frameworks must account for varied national interpretations of data protection rules and local ethics committee procedures.
What to monitor: Developments in EU-level guidance aimed at harmonizing data protection enforcement standards for clinical research contexts.
