items.title: Proposal for a European Biotech Act: will the Commission’s proposal unlock the potential of the health biotech industry in the EU?
items.title_zh: 欧盟《欧洲生物技术法案》提案能否释放健康生物技术潜力？
items.title_en: EU Commission Proposes Biotech Act to Strengthen Health Sector Competitiveness and Autonomy
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. December of 2025 was an exciting month for the life sciences sector in te...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会发布《欧洲生物技术法案》提案，旨在加强联盟生物技术和生物制造部门。该法案聚焦健康生物技术，涵盖从研发、融资、开发到上市的全生命周期。针对欧盟在临床开发、监管及制造方面的结构性障碍，提案引入“健康生物技术战略项目”概念，以支持工业化生物制造能力并简化产品上市流程，同时维持最高安全标准。
items.ai_summary_en: Addressing structural barriers in clinical development, regulation, and manufacturing, the European Commission has published its Proposal for a European Biotech Act. This legislation focuses on the entire lifecycle of health biotechnology products and services. It aims to resolve issues causing start-ups to relocate outside the EU by establishing a strategic project framework, optimizing access to funding, and simplifying administrative processes. The initiative seeks to enhance the EU’s competitiveness and strategic autonomy relative to the US and China. By targeting health biotech applications relevant to human, animal, and plant health, the proposal intends to unlock the sector's potential while maintaining high safety standards for the population and environment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该立法提案意在解决欧盟生物技术初创企业外流及监管碎片化问题。实务中需关注“战略项目”认定标准及其对研发资助、市场准入审批流程的具体影响。尽管主要涉及产业监管而非传统知识产权确权，但其构建的创新生态系统将间接影响生物技术专利的商业化路径与合规成本，建议持续跟踪立法进程。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the evolving regulatory landscape regarding the proposed European Biotech Act. The introduction of strategic project frameworks offers potential pathways for accelerated market access and improved funding opportunities. However, companies must also assess compliance requirements, particularly concerning supply chain localization mandates that could impact export strategies and operational models within the EU. Proactive engagement with these new standards is essential for navigating future regulatory expectations and securing competitive advantages in the European health biotech market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟委员会发布提案，建立措施以加强欧盟生物技术和生物制造部门，重点聚焦健康生物技术领域", "提案引入“健康生物技术战略项目”及“高影响战略项目”框架，旨在认可和支持关键项目以强化…", "法规将适用于健康生物技术产品和服务的整个生命周期，包括研究、融资、开发、测试、制造及上…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Commission proposal aims to eliminate structural barriers hindering the development of the EU biotechnology sector.", "The Act covers the full lifecycle from research to market placement, with specific focus on health biotechnology applications.", "It introduces a strategic project designation mechanism to strengthen industrial biomanufacturing capabilities and value chains."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立法旨在通过统一监管框架和资金支持，解决欧盟生物技术企业因监管复杂和融资困难而外流的问题", "需关注“健康生物技术战略项目”的具体认定标准，以及该身份是否带来监管审批加速或财政补贴…", "后续应观察该法案与现有药品监管体系（如“欧盟药品一揽子计划”）的衔接，以及对临床试验和…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor strategic project criteria to leverage EU support systems for accelerated market entry.", "Practice note: Evaluate new regulations for supply chain localization impacts to adjust investment and compliance strategies.", "What to monitor: Developments in simplified approval pathways and their interaction with existing pharmaceutical frameworks."]
core_points_en: The Commission proposal aims to eliminate structural barriers hindering the development of the EU biotechnology sector.
The Act covers the full lifecycle from research to market placement, with specific focus on health biotechnology applications.
It introduces a strategic project designation mechanism to strengthen industrial biomanufacturing capabilities and value chains.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor strategic project criteria to leverage EU support systems for accelerated market entry.
Practice note: Evaluate new regulations for supply chain localization impacts to adjust investment and compliance strategies.
What to monitor: Developments in simplified approval pathways and their interaction with existing pharmaceutical frameworks.

items.title: Competition authorities in Europe target ‘killer acquisitions’ – what biotech companies need to know
items.title_zh: 欧洲竞管机构聚焦“杀手级收购”：生物科技企业须知
items.title_en: European Competition Authorities Target Killer Acquisitions in Biotech Sector
items.summary: This article is part of our Biotech Review of the Year - Issue 13 publication. Competition authorities are increasingly targeting so-called 'killer acqu...
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲竞争监管机构正加强对生命科学领域“杀手级收购”的审查，即大型企业收购初创公司以消除未来竞争威胁。尽管多数收购无不当意图，但因目标公司营收低常低于申报门槛，现监管机构结合合并控制与滥用支配地位规则进行审查。违规可能导致交易被阻、拆分或高达全球营业额10%的罚款。
items.ai_summary_en: European competition authorities are increasingly scrutinizing so-called killer acquisitions in the life sciences sector, where established firms acquire innovative start-ups to eliminate future competition. Although many such deals historically fell below merger control thresholds due to low target turnover, regulators now apply both merger control and abuse of dominance rules. This shift addresses concerns that significant investments in pre-revenue targets may mask anti-competitive intent. Authorities aim to preserve innovation-driven growth by blocking, unwinding, or imposing divestments on problematic transactions. Companies face potential fines of up to ten percent of global turnover for abuse of dominance, necessitating careful compliance management despite the legitimate exit strategies these acquisitions often represent for start-ups.
items.ai_insight_zh: 生物科技企业需注意，即使目标公司暂无营收，并购交易仍可能触发欧洲竞争法审查。监管机构倾向于保护创新竞争，企业应评估交易是否被视为消除潜在威胁，并警惕高额罚款及交易被撤销的商业风险，需提前管理合规预期。
items.ai_insight_en: Biotech companies must recognize that low revenue no longer shields acquisitions from European competition scrutiny. Regulators are actively closing enforcement gaps by leveraging abuse of dominance rules alongside merger controls. Market participants should assess whether transactions could be viewed as eliminating potential innovation competition, given the severe commercial and financial risks associated with regulatory intervention.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["监管机构针对消除未来竞争威胁的“杀手级收购”加强审查，尤其关注创新密集型行业", "过往因目标营收低而低于门槛的交易，现可能面临合并控制及滥用市场支配地位规则的双重审查", "违规后果包括交易被禁止、强制剥离资产，以及最高达全球年营业额10%的行政罚款"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Regulators increasingly scrutinize acquisitions of innovative start-ups aimed at eliminating future competitive threats in life sciences.", "Low-turnover targets no longer automatically exempt deals from review under merger control and abuse of dominance frameworks.", "Violations may result in transaction blocks, forced divestments, or fines up to ten percent of global turnover."]

items.title: ECIJA hires 12-lawyer TM team from Hoyng Rokh Monegier
items.title_zh: ECIJA从Hoyng Rokh Monegier吸纳12人商标团队
items.title_en: ECIJA Recruits Twelve-Lawyer Trademark Team from Rival Firm Hoyng Rokh Monegier
items.summary: Arrival of Laura Alonso, alongside a team of 11, will bring ‘significant value’ to ECIJA clients, says CEO
items.ai_summary_zh: 西班牙律所ECIJA宣布从Hoyng Rokh Monegier引进由Laura Alonso领衔的12人商标律师团队。ECIJA首席执行官表示，此次人员扩充将为客户带来显著价值。原文仅披露该人事变动事实及官方表态，未涉及具体业务整合细节或财务条款。
items.ai_summary_en: European intellectual property firm ECIJA has announced the recruitment of a twelve-lawyer trademark team led by Laura Alonso from competitor Hoyng Rokh Monegier. The move is designed to significantly enhance the firm's capacity to deliver comprehensive trademark portfolio management and strategic advisory services to its clients. This high-profile hiring reflects the ongoing consolidation within the European trademark legal services market, driven by sustained demand for specialized expertise. By integrating this experienced group, ECIJA aims to strengthen its service depth and competitive positioning, illustrating how major firms are actively reshaping their capabilities to meet evolving client needs in a dynamic professional landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此为律所层面的人事与市场动态。需关注该团队整合后对西班牙及泛欧商标业务格局的潜在影响，以及后续是否伴随客户资源的实质性转移。建议持续观察两家律所在商标领域的后续市场表现与服务范围变化。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should assess how significant team movements at key law firms may impact service continuity and case management stability. Rights holders are advised to leverage current market consolidation trends to re-evaluate their European trademark agency partnerships. Optimizing partner selection ensures that brand protection strategies receive robust, specialized support. Monitoring these structural shifts allows companies to align their legal resources with firms demonstrating enhanced capacity and strategic focus in trademark prosecution and enforcement matters across Europe.

items.title: Patents in the age of digital twins
items.title_zh: 数字孪生时代的专利申请挑战
items.title_en: Patenting Digital Twins in Healthcare: Navigating Technical Contribution and Sufficiency Hurdles
items.summary: For those new to the term, a “digital twin” is a virtual replica of a physical object. The potential use in healthcare is enormous. For...
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析数字孪生技术在医疗健康领域的应用及其在英国专利法下面临的三大 hurdles：首先需证明超越抽象算法的“技术贡献”以避免被排除；其次需确保说明书充分公开模型初始化及数据更新机制以应对充分性质疑；最后指出数字孪生的运行特性可能引发复杂的侵权认定问题。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines patent protection challenges for digital twin technologies in healthcare and engineering sectors. In the UK and Europe, such inventions must demonstrate a technical contribution to avoid exclusion as abstract algorithms. Applicants face significant risks regarding sufficiency and plausibility; if specifications fail to detail model initialization or data update mechanisms, patents are vulnerable to invalidity attacks. Furthermore, complex infringement determination issues arise. The article advises applicants to seek professional guidance to refine claims, ensuring they are anchored in specific technical applications rather than pure software descriptions, thereby securing robust monopoly rights in this rapidly growing market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 数字孪生作为新兴技术领域，其在英国的专利保护高度依赖权利要求的撰写技巧以体现技术性。申请人需特别注意说明书对技术实现细节的披露程度，以防因不充分或缺乏合理性被无效。鉴于侵权认定的复杂性，早期布局时需结合具体应用场景明确技术边界。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies operating in Europe, patent strategies for digital twins should prioritize specific medical or industrial application scenarios over pure algorithmic descriptions. Drafting must ensure detailed embodiments to prevent invalidation due to insufficient disclosure. Additionally, applicants should proactively assess the difficulty of enforcing infringement claims. By binding applications to specific hardware or clinical operations, rights holders can mitigate the risk of rejection for abstract subject matter and build a stronger defense against potential validity challenges during opposition or litigation proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国专利法要求计算机实施发明需提供超越抽象软件或数学模型的技术效果", "数字孪生专利易受充分性质疑，需清晰披露模型初始化及真实数据更新机制", "若专利声称预测病情但未提供足够技术支持，可能因缺乏合理性而被挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Digital twin patents must demonstrate concrete technical effects, such as device calibration, to avoid rejection as abstract mathematical methods.", "Specifications must thoroughly disclose model parameters and data update logic to withstand challenges regarding sufficiency of disclosure.", "Competitors may challenge the scientific plausibility of predictive functions based on current technical standards and available evidence."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["数字孪生专利申请需着重构建“技术贡献”叙事，避免落入非专利客体范畴", "说明书应详细披露模型参数、数据交互及具体技术控制流程以支撑充分性", "需持续观察英国法院对涉及复杂模拟算法专利的侵权判定标准及有效性审查趋势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Anchor patent applications to specific hardware or clinical operations to mitigate the risk of rejection for abstract subject matter.", "Practice note: Enhance technical disclosure details to construct a robust defense against sufficiency and plausibility challenges.", "What to monitor: Monitor evolving case law regarding the technical contribution requirements for computer-implemented medical simulations."]
core_points_en: Digital twin patents must demonstrate concrete technical effects, such as device calibration, to avoid rejection as abstract mathematical methods.
Specifications must thoroughly disclose model parameters and data update logic to withstand challenges regarding sufficiency of disclosure.
Competitors may challenge the scientific plausibility of predictive functions based on current technical standards and available evidence.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Anchor patent applications to specific hardware or clinical operations to mitigate the risk of rejection for abstract subject matter.
Practice note: Enhance technical disclosure details to construct a robust defense against sufficiency and plausibility challenges.
What to monitor: Monitor evolving case law regarding the technical contribution requirements for computer-implemented medical simulations.

items.title: What happens if someone opposes your trade mark? | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 若有人异议你的商标会发生什么？
items.title_en: Navigating UK Trade Mark Oppositions: Understanding TM7a Notices and Strategic Responses
items.summary: As a UK trade mark attorney, one of the most common and often most stressful situations my clients face is when their application for a trade mark is opposed, or they receive a Notice of Threatened Opposition (a Form TM7a).
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文解析英国商标注册中的异议风险。重点说明TM7a（拟异议通知）的作用：它仅是第三方要求延长评估时间的警告，并非正式异议。该机制允许潜在异议人在公告期内获得更多决策时间，申请人应理性应对而非过度恐慌。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the implications of receiving a Notice of Threatened Opposition (Form TM7a) during the UK trade mark application process. It clarifies that a TM7a serves as an early warning signal indicating a third party’s intent to assess rights, rather than initiating formal legal proceedings. The notice extends the publication period, providing a critical window for commercial resolution. Rights holders are advised not to panic upon receipt, as professional intervention often facilitates quick, cost-effective settlements without escalating into full disputes. The article outlines the distinction between UKIPO refusals and third-party oppositions, emphasizing strategic communication and risk assessment to protect brand interests efficiently during this procedural stage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需区分“拟异议通知”与“正式异议”。TM7a常作为商业谈判前的预警信号，多数情况可通过协商解决。企业应关注公告期内的第三方动态，及时获取专业建议以评估风险，避免将程序性警告误判为实质性法律冲突。
items.ai_insight_en: For market participants navigating the UK Intellectual Property Office landscape, a TM7a should be interpreted as a negotiation signal rather than an immediate threat. Ignoring the notice or reacting defensively may lead to procedural inefficiencies. Instead, applicants should engage local counsel promptly to assess infringement risks and explore amicable solutions. Utilizing the extended publication period allows for direct communication with the opposing party, potentially resulting in coexistence agreements or other low-cost settlements. This proactive approach minimizes legal exposure and avoids the resource drain associated with prolonged opposition proceedings, ensuring brand protection strategies remain commercially viable and efficient.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["TM7a是向英国知识产权局提交的拟异议通知，旨在延长评估时间", "提交TM7a不代表正式异议已启动，也不承诺必将提起异议", "商标通过审查后公告两个月，提交通知后可延长至三个月供第三方异议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["A Form TM7a is a Notice of Threatened Opposition designed to extend the publication period, not to initiate formal legal confrontation.", "Receiving a TM7a does not guarantee a dispute will occur; it typically indicates the rights holder requires time to evaluate their position.", "Professional guidance enables rapid, commercial resolutions in many cases, preventing unnecessary escalation into costly legal battles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["TM7a多为争取时间的策略性动作，非必然进入诉讼程序", "公告期内的第三方监测对于早期发现潜在权利冲突至关重要", "需持续观察英国商标局对异议程序的后续处理效率及和解趋势"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Treat TM7a notices as opportunities for early dialogue and risk assessment rather than immediate litigation triggers.", "Practice note: Engage qualified UK trade mark attorneys immediately to evaluate merits and negotiate potential coexistence agreements.", "What to monitor: Track the extended publication timeline closely to ensure timely responses and capitalize on settlement windows."]
core_points_en: A Form TM7a is a Notice of Threatened Opposition designed to extend the publication period, not to initiate formal legal confrontation.
Receiving a TM7a does not guarantee a dispute will occur; it typically indicates the rights holder requires time to evaluate their position.
Professional guidance enables rapid, commercial resolutions in many cases, preventing unnecessary escalation into costly legal battles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Treat TM7a notices as opportunities for early dialogue and risk assessment rather than immediate litigation triggers.
Practice note: Engage qualified UK trade mark attorneys immediately to evaluate merits and negotiate potential coexistence agreements.
What to monitor: Track the extended publication timeline closely to ensure timely responses and capitalize on settlement windows.

items.title: Salts v Pelican: UK Patent Infringement Appeal Fails but Novelty and Costs Arguments Succeed
items.title_zh: Salts诉Pelican案：英国专利侵权上诉败诉但新颖性抗辩成功
items.title_en: Salts v Pelican: UK Appeal Court Upholds Non-Infringement but Restores Patent Novelty
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院驳回Salts Healthcare关于造口袋专利侵权的上诉，维持不侵权认定。但法院支持Salts关于权利要求8具备新颖性的主张，推翻高等法院此前基于现有技术的无效认定。此外，法院还就案件从知识产权企业法院转移前的费用问题作出裁决。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal dismissed Salts Healthcare’s appeal regarding patent infringement, affirming that Pelican Healthcare’s ostomy bag did not infringe under normal construction or the doctrine of equivalents. However, the court successfully overturned the High Court’s ruling that claim 8 lacked novelty over prior art. Additionally, the appeal succeeded regarding pre-transfer costs incurred before the case moved from the IPEC to the Patents Court. Arnold LJ clarified that the weld portion must extend downwardly towards the bottom, rejecting Salts’ broader interpretation. The decision reaffirms strict claim construction while validating the patent’s validity against specific prior art challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了英国法院在专利权利要求解释及等同原则适用上的严格标准，即便侵权主张失败，当事人仍可能在有效性争点上取得突破。对于生命科学领域专利持有人而言，需关注法院对技术特征字面含义与发明构思的区分逻辑，以及IPEC转移案件的成本承担规则。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment highlights the critical importance of precise claim construction in UK patent disputes, particularly concerning geometric features like weld orientations. While the doctrine of equivalents remains a potential avenue for rights holders, courts require clear alignment with the inventive concept. Conversely, the restoration of novelty demonstrates that prior art challenges can succeed even when infringement claims fail. Market participants should ensure robust freedom-to-operate analyses that account for both literal infringement risks and potential validity attacks during litigation strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉法院维持原判，认定被诉产品未在正常解释或等同原则下侵犯专利权", "法院推翻高等法院决定，认定涉案专利权利要求8相对于现有技术具备新颖性", "Arnold LJ明确了权利要求中焊接部分延伸方向的解释标准及发明核心概念"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Court of Appeal upheld non-infringement findings for both normal interpretation and equivalence arguments.", "High Court decision on lack of novelty for claim 8 was overturned, restoring the patent's validity.", "Appeal succeeded regarding IPEC pre-transfer costs, impacting litigation expense allocations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院对等同原则的适用持审慎态度，强调技术特征的整体延伸方向", "专利有效性（新颖性）与侵权判定在上诉阶段可呈现不同结果，需分别应对", "关注从知识产权企业法院转移至专利法院案件中的前期成本承担司法倾向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must align product designs strictly with claim language to avoid non-infringement rulings.", "Practice note: Defend against novelty attacks by distinguishing technical contributions from prior art disclosures effectively.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends in defining the 'inventive concept' for applying the doctrine of equivalents in mechanical patents."]
core_points_en: Court of Appeal upheld non-infringement findings for both normal interpretation and equivalence arguments.
High Court decision on lack of novelty for claim 8 was overturned, restoring the patent's validity.
Appeal succeeded regarding IPEC pre-transfer costs, impacting litigation expense allocations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must align product designs strictly with claim language to avoid non-infringement rulings.
Practice note: Defend against novelty attacks by distinguishing technical contributions from prior art disclosures effectively.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in defining the 'inventive concept' for applying the doctrine of equivalents in mechanical patents.

items.title: The 2026 shift: what spanish AI innovators need to know about China’s revised Patent Decree 84
items.title_zh: 2026年转变：西班牙AI创新者需知中国修订专利 decree 84
items.title_en: 2026 Policy Shift: How China’s Revised Patent Decree 84 Impacts Spanish AI Innovators
items.summary: Based on the official China National Intellectual Property Administration (hereinafter referred to as “CNIPA”) data from 2019 to 2024, while major European industrial powers like Germany, France, Italy, and Sweden have seen their application volumes stagnate or decline over the last five years, Spain’s volume rose steadily from 421(2019) to 494 (2024) applications[1] with a ... Read more La entrada The 2026 shift: what spanish AI innovators need to know about China’s revised Patent Decree 84 se
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文正文极短，仅显示摘要片段。基于中国国家知识产权局2019至2024年数据，对比德法等欧洲工业强国专利申请量停滞或下降，西班牙申请量从421件稳步增至494件。标题提示文章旨在分析中国修订专利法令对西班牙AI领域的影响。
items.ai_summary_en: Official data from the China National Intellectual Property Administration (CNIPA) covering 2019 to 2024 reveals a distinct divergence in European patent filing trends. While application volumes from major industrial powers such as Germany, France, Italy, and Sweden have stagnated or declined, Spain has demonstrated consistent growth, rising from 421 applications in 2019 to 494 in 2024. This analysis focuses on the strategic implications of China’s revised Patent Decree 84, which is scheduled to take effect in 2026. The article provides essential guidance for Spanish innovators in the artificial intelligence sector, helping them navigate the evolving regulatory landscape. It outlines necessary adjustments to intellectual property strategies in China, ensuring that rights holders can effectively leverage new provisions to strengthen their market position and protect technological advancements amidst changing policy dynamics.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文缺失，无法评估法令具体条款。需关注西班牙AI企业在华专利布局增长背后的政策驱动因素，以及中国新法规对算法或AI相关专利审查标准的具体调整方向。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the increasing patent activity from emerging European forces like Spain, anticipating their intent to utilize new regulations for stronger protection. Rights holders are advised to proactively assess competitive landscapes and optimize patent defense and licensing strategies regarding technology transfers between Europe and China. Dynamic monitoring of bilateral policy interactions is crucial to mitigate potential infringement risks and manage licensing complexities effectively in this evolving legal framework.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["西班牙AI领域对华专利申请逆势增长，反映其出海策略活跃度提升", "需等待全文以确认中国修订专利法令84中涉及AI技术的具体审查或保护变更", "观察欧洲主要工业国与西班牙在华专利策略的分化趋势及其成因"]

core_points_en: Spanish patent application volumes in China are rising steadily, contrasting sharply with the stagnation or decline observed in other major European industrial nations.
China’s revised Patent Decree 84 will introduce significant policy shifts in 2026, altering the regulatory environment for foreign applicants seeking protection.
The analysis offers strategic guidance for Spanish AI innovators to adapt their Chinese intellectual property layouts and comply with upcoming regulatory changes.

Practice note: Evaluate and optimize patent defense and licensing strategies for bidirectional technology transfers between Europe and China.
What to monitor: Track dynamic interactions between Chinese and European patent policies to avoid potential infringement and licensing risks.

items.title: UPC Weekly - UPC Court of Appeal imposes clear limit on long arm jurisdiction
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭明确限制长臂管辖权
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Imposes Clear Limits on Long-Arm Jurisdiction in Cross-Border Cases
items.summary: 2026 Week 12
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文正文及摘要仅包含“2026 Week 12”字样，无具体案情描述。依据标题显示，统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭作出裁决，对长臂管辖权施加了明确限制。由于缺乏全文细节，无法提供具体的法律推理或案件背景，仅能确认该司法动向的存在。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has issued a significant ruling that strictly limits the scope of long-arm jurisdiction. This decision clarifies the boundaries of judicial authority in cross-border patent infringement disputes, preventing excessive expansion of jurisdictional reach. By establishing clearer procedural rules for cases involving multiple defendants across different jurisdictions, the court aims to balance effective protection of intellectual property rights with the due process rights of defendants. This judgment reduces legal uncertainty and provides essential guidance on how jurisdiction is determined when alleged infringements occur in multiple member states, ensuring that courts do not overstep their mandate in complex international litigation scenarios.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文内容缺失，仅能基于标题指出UPC上诉庭在管辖权问题上确立了新界限。需等待完整判决书发布以明确“长臂管辖”的具体定义、适用例外及对跨境专利诉讼的实际影响。目前不宜进行过度解读或策略推断。
items.ai_insight_en: This precedent offers a robust basis for challenging improper jurisdictional claims in UPC proceedings, particularly for entities facing multi-state infringement allegations. Rights holders and defendants should carefully assess whether the court has proper authority before engaging in full litigation, potentially reducing defense costs and strategic exposure. Market participants must stay alert to evolving procedural interpretations regarding venue and joinder of defendants. Optimizing European patent enforcement and defense strategies now requires a nuanced understanding of these jurisdictional limits to avoid being drawn into unfavorable forums or facing fragmented enforcement actions across the unitary patent system.

items.title: Does copyright subsist in AI-generated content?
items.title_zh: AI生成内容在英国是否享有版权保护？
items.title_en: Copyright in AI-Generated Content: UK Legal Status and Risk Analysis
items.summary: Brands & advertising
items.ai_summary_zh: 泰勒·韦辛律师事务所分析指出，目前英国法律下AI生成输出可能享有版权保护。路径包括无人类作者时的计算机生成作品保护（50年），或有人类原创性时的标准版权保护（作者终身加70年）。但立法存在不确定性，政府未来可能限制保护，企业需审慎评估对AI的依赖程度。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the copyright status of AI-generated content under current UK law. The Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 currently provides fifty years of protection for computer-generated works lacking a human author. However, significant legal tension exists because the requirement for originality traditionally demands human intellectual creation. While courts may lean toward protecting such outputs, legislative ambiguities persist. Furthermore, since most global jurisdictions do not offer copyright protection for AI-generated works, businesses face compliance risks when relying on AI for creative production. Companies must carefully evaluate their dependence on AI tools, considering both the potential for limited domestic protection and the lack of international recognition for purely machine-generated assets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 英国虽目前提供AI生成内容版权保护，但法律解释存在内在张力，且政策风向可能转向限制。跨国企业应注意英国与全球多数不保护AI生成内容法域的差异，持续监测立法动态以调整创意流程中的AI使用策略及权利归属安排。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants utilizing AI to generate trademarks or advertising materials in the UK should note the divergence from other European jurisdictions where protection is often denied. To secure longer standard copyright terms, rights holders should maintain evidence of human creative intervention to satisfy originality requirements. Over-reliance on purely AI-generated content poses a risk of rights failure, particularly in cross-border operations. Strategic IP management requires balancing efficiency gains from generative AI with robust documentation of human input to ensure enforceable rights across key markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国现行法下，AI生成内容可能通过计算机生成作品或标准版权两种路径获得保护", "计算机生成作品保护期为50年，适用于无人类作者情形；标准保护需人类原创性", "立法存在关于原创性定义的张力，且政府未来可能限制AI生成作品的版权保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK law currently grants fifty-year copyright protection to computer-generated works that have no identified human author.", "The originality test requiring human authorship creates legal conflict with the statutory definition of computer-generated works.", "Future government restrictions on protection are possible, requiring businesses to cautiously assess reliance on AI for creativity."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国是目前少数明确为无人类作者的AI生成内容提供版权保护的主要法域之一", "法律关于“无人类作者”与“原创性需反映人格”之间存在理论冲突，司法实践尚不明朗", "需密切关注英国政府后续是否会修法限制或取消对计算机生成作品的版权保护"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Preserve evidence of human creative involvement to meet originality tests and secure longer standard copyright protection periods.", "Practice note: Avoid excessive reliance on purely AI-generated content to prevent rights gaps, especially given varying international protections.", "What to monitor: Track UK legislative developments regarding computer-generated works and assess cross-border IP risks for AI-driven campaigns."]
core_points_en: UK law currently grants fifty-year copyright protection to computer-generated works that have no identified human author.
The originality test requiring human authorship creates legal conflict with the statutory definition of computer-generated works.
Future government restrictions on protection are possible, requiring businesses to cautiously assess reliance on AI for creativity.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Preserve evidence of human creative involvement to meet originality tests and secure longer standard copyright protection periods.
Practice note: Avoid excessive reliance on purely AI-generated content to prevent rights gaps, especially given varying international protections.
What to monitor: Track UK legislative developments regarding computer-generated works and assess cross-border IP risks for AI-driven campaigns.

items.title: A reminder of the use or lose it approach to trade marks: easyHire
items.title_zh: easyHire案警示：商标不使用即失效原则
items.title_en: easyHire Case: UK Court Rejects Infringement Claim Due to Lack of Genuine Use
items.summary: Brands & advertising
items.ai_summary_zh: easyGroup因无法证明其在英国对easyHire商标进行了真实使用，败诉于针对Jaybank Leisure（使用EASIHIRE）的侵权索赔。被告援引第11A条不使用抗辩而非反诉撤销。尽管两标识被认定构成混淆性相似，但因原告未满足五年以上注册商标的使用举证责任，侵权主张不成立，但商标注册依然有效。
items.ai_summary_en: easyGroup failed in its trade mark infringement action against Jaybank Leisure for using the sign EASIHIRE, primarily because it could not demonstrate genuine use of its registered easyHire mark in the UK during the relevant five-year period. Although the court indicated that the defendant’s use would likely constitute infringement had genuine use been proven, the claim was dismissed under Section 11A of the Trade Marks Act 1994. This provision allows a defendant to raise a non-use defence, barring infringement claims if the asserted registration has not been used in the five years preceding the action. Notably, the defendant did not file a counterclaim for revocation on grounds of non-use. Consequently, while the infringement claim failed, the easyHire registration remains valid and enforceable for future actions where use can be substantiated.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显在英国商标诉讼中，注册满五年的商标权利人面临严格的使用举证要求。被告选择不反诉撤销而采用不使用抗辩，虽使原告败诉但保留了其商标注册状态。实务中需评估抗辩与反诉撤销的策略差异，并确保证据链完整以支持商标的真实使用主张。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the critical importance of maintaining robust evidence trails for trade mark use, particularly for registrations older than five years. Rights holders risk losing enforcement capabilities if they cannot substantiate genuine commercial use in the relevant jurisdiction. Conversely, defendants facing infringement allegations should rigorously assess the plaintiff's usage history, considering the strategic advantages of a Section 11A defence versus a full revocation counterclaim. Legal teams must integrate regular use audits into their IP management protocols to prevent registrations from becoming unenforceable due to evidentiary gaps, ensuring that intellectual property assets remain viable tools for market protection.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["easyGroup未能证明easyHire商标在英国的真实使用，导致侵权索赔失败", "被告依据第11A条提出不使用抗辩，法院认定若证实使用则构成侵权", "商标注册满五年后，权利人需在执法行动中承担真实使用的举证责任"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rights holders must prove genuine use of their marks within the five years prior to litigation to successfully enforce registration rights.", "Defendants may effectively block infringement claims by invoking the Section 11A non-use defence without needing to initiate formal revocation proceedings.", "Minor visual differences such as font variations or spelling changes do not negate conceptual similarity when assessing potential trade mark infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["注册满五年的商标在维权时需预先准备真实使用证据，否则面临执法障碍", "被告方可利用不使用抗辩阻断侵权认定，而不必直接挑战商标有效性", "需持续监测英国法院对第11A条抗辩与撤销反诉在不同案情下的适用倾向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish systematic documentation processes for trade mark use to ensure enforceability and avoid loss of rights due to non-use.", "Practice note: Evaluate opponent's usage evidence early in disputes; consider Section 11A defences as a cost-effective alternative to revocation counterclaims.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of 'genuine use' requirements and the strategic implications of choosing between non-use defences and revocation actions."]
core_points_en: Rights holders must prove genuine use of their marks within the five years prior to litigation to successfully enforce registration rights.
Defendants may effectively block infringement claims by invoking the Section 11A non-use defence without needing to initiate formal revocation proceedings.
Minor visual differences such as font variations or spelling changes do not negate conceptual similarity when assessing potential trade mark infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish systematic documentation processes for trade mark use to ensure enforceability and avoid loss of rights due to non-use.
Practice note: Evaluate opponent's usage evidence early in disputes; consider Section 11A defences as a cost-effective alternative to revocation counterclaims.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretations of 'genuine use' requirements and the strategic implications of choosing between non-use defences and revocation actions.

items.title: More Lessons on Validity before the UPC – Part I
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院有效性判例新启示（一）
items.title_en: UPC Validity Insights: Interpreting Medical Use Claims in Amgen v. Sanofi
items.summary: It has been a while since I last commented on the case law of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) on validity. To be precise, it was on 22 July 2024 in a blog titled “First Lessons on Validity before the UPC”.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文回顾统一专利法院（UPC）自2024年7月以来的专利有效性判例，重点分析慕尼黑中央分部在Amgen诉Sanofi案中的裁决。法院确立权利要求解释应确定技术含义而非字面理解，并将“产品X用于治疗Y”视为目的限定产品权利要求，要求产品在客观上适合该用途且具治疗有效性，符合欧洲专利局实践及欧洲专利公约第54条精神。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines recent Unified Patent Court (UPC) case law on patent validity, building upon earlier commentary from July 2024. It focuses specifically on the Munich Central Division’s decision in Amgen v. Sanofi regarding the interpretation of medical use claims. The court treats 'product X for use in treating Y' claims as purpose-limited product claims, requiring the product to be objectively suitable and therapeutically effective for the specified indication. This approach aligns with established European Patent Office practice and Article 54(4) and (5) EPC 2000, confirming that technical effects function as limiting features in such claims.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC判例进一步明确了医药用途权利要求的解释标准，强调技术效果作为功能特征的重要性。实务中需关注权利要求撰写是否清晰体现特定用途的技术贡献，以应对有效性挑战。后续应持续追踪上诉法院在主要程序中的类似裁决，以确认司法实践的一致性。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should note that the UPC consistently applies a technical rather than purely philological approach to claim construction. For pharmaceutical patents, this means medical use claims are strictly evaluated based on their therapeutic efficacy and specific indications. Practitioners must ensure that patent descriptions clearly define these technical purposes to support validity arguments. Monitoring future Court of Appeal decisions will be crucial to confirm whether this strict alignment with EPO standards remains consistent across all divisions and appeal stages.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC确立权利要求解释应借助说明书和附图确定术语的技术含义，而非仅进行语文学理解", "慕尼黑中央分部将医药用途权利要求视为目的限定产品权利要求，要求产品客观上适合 clai…", "该解释符合欧洲专利公约第54(4)和(5)条精神，即若特定用途未被现有技术公开，则具备…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC interprets medical use claims as purpose-limited product claims requiring objective therapeutic suitability.", "Technical effects are treated as functional features, aligning with long-standing EPO and German legal practice.", "This interpretation ensures conformity with Article 54(4) and (5) EPC 2000 regarding novelty of known substances."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC倾向于将医药用途权利要求解释为包含技术效果的功能特征，强化了与现有技术的区分度", "专利说明书作为自有词典的作用得到确认，撰写时需确保术语定义与技术含义的一致性", "需持续观察上诉法院对目的限定产品权利要求的审查标准，特别是关于治疗有效性的举证要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Pharmaceutical rights holders must ensure medical use claims clearly define therapeutic efficacy to withstand UPC validity challenges.", "Practice note: Drafting strategies should align with EPO standards, treating technical effects as functional limitations in use claims.", "What to monitor: Future Court of Appeal rulings to confirm if the Munich Division's strict adherence to EPC Article 54 persists."]
core_points_en: The UPC interprets medical use claims as purpose-limited product claims requiring objective therapeutic suitability.
Technical effects are treated as functional features, aligning with long-standing EPO and German legal practice.
This interpretation ensures conformity with Article 54(4) and (5) EPC 2000 regarding novelty of known substances.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Pharmaceutical rights holders must ensure medical use claims clearly define therapeutic efficacy to withstand UPC validity challenges.
Practice note: Drafting strategies should align with EPO standards, treating technical effects as functional limitations in use claims.
What to monitor: Future Court of Appeal rulings to confirm if the Munich Division's strict adherence to EPC Article 54 persists.

items.title: AGA Rangemaster Group Ltd v UK Innovation Group Ltd – The UK Court of Appeal rules in the second instalment of the Aga cooker sAGA
items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院裁定AGA炉灶改装案版权与商标争议
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Rules on Copyright and Trademark Issues in AGA Cooker Retrofit Case
items.summary: In this post we review the recent Court of Appeal decision in AGA Rangemaster Group Ltd v UK Innovations Group Ltd & Anor [2025] EWCA Civ 1622 concerning claims of trade mark and copyright infringement related to retrofitted AGA range cookers.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评述英国上诉法院在AGA诉UKIG案中的最新裁决。该案涉及改装AGA炉灶引发的商标及版权侵权索赔。重点分析1988年版权法第51条抗辩及其与欧盟Cofemel判例的兼容性，探讨工业设计版权保护边界及权利用尽原则在售后市场中的应用。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis reviews the UK Court of Appeal’s decision in AGA Rangemaster Group Ltd v UK Innovation Group Ltd [2025] EWCA Civ 1622, concerning trademark and copyright infringement claims related to retrofitted AGA range cookers. The case examines whether aftermarket modifications exceed the boundaries of trademark exhaustion under the Trade Marks Act 1994. A primary focus is the application of Section 51 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988, which excludes copyright protection for industrial designs. The court assessed the compatibility of this provision, and the UK’s closed-list system, with assimilated EU law following the CJEU’s Cofemel judgment. The ruling clarifies the intersection of design and copyright protections for functional products.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显英国脱欧后，其国内版权法中工业设计排除条款与欧盟既有判例法的潜在张力。企业需关注法院如何协调国内成文法与同化欧盟法，特别是在功能性产品改装场景下，版权与外观设计权的界限划分将直接影响售后市场的合规策略。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants engaged in aftermarket modification of branded goods must carefully assess intellectual property risks, particularly regarding copied components like control panels. Post-Brexit, the interaction between UK statutory defenses and retained EU case law creates legal uncertainty. Rights holders and third-party modifiers should conduct thorough compliance evaluations before launching retrofit services. Understanding the precise boundaries where design rights overlap with copyright is essential to mitigate litigation exposure in the UK jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院审理了涉及改装AGA炉灶的商标及版权侵权索赔案件", "案件核心在于版权法第51条抗辩与欧盟Cofemel判例中关于设计保护兼容性的问题", "一审曾就商标法第12条权利用尽抗辩提供指导，界定合法售后行为与非法营销的边界"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The court examined whether retrofitting and marketing classic AGA cookers exceeded permissible limits under trademark exhaustion principles.", "The decision analyzed whether functional industrial designs, such as control panels, are excluded from extended copyright protection under UK law.", "The ruling addressed the tension between the UK's closed-list copyright system and the broader standards established by the CJEU in Cofemel."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注英国法院如何处理国内版权法与欧盟Cofemel判例在工业设计保护上的冲突", "观察权利用尽原则在功能性产品改装及售后市场营销中的具体适用边界", "监测此类判决对工业产品零部件版权保护范围及第三方改装业务的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Aftermarket modifiers face significant copyright infringement risks when replicating protected design elements of original equipment.", "Practice note: Legal teams should evaluate the applicability of Section 51 CDPA defenses against potential claims arising from functional product adaptations.", "What to monitor: Ongoing judicial interpretation of the compatibility between UK design exclusions and EU-derived copyright standards post-Brexit."]
core_points_en: The court examined whether retrofitting and marketing classic AGA cookers exceeded permissible limits under trademark exhaustion principles.
The decision analyzed whether functional industrial designs, such as control panels, are excluded from extended copyright protection under UK law.
The ruling addressed the tension between the UK's closed-list copyright system and the broader standards established by the CJEU in Cofemel.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Aftermarket modifiers face significant copyright infringement risks when replicating protected design elements of original equipment.
Practice note: Legal teams should evaluate the applicability of Section 51 CDPA defenses against potential claims arising from functional product adaptations.
What to monitor: Ongoing judicial interpretation of the compatibility between UK design exclusions and EU-derived copyright standards post-Brexit.

items.title: Four Reasons To Choose Our End-to-end Trademark Service
items.title_zh: 选择端到端商标服务的四大理由
items.title_en: Strategic Benefits of End-to-End Trademark Management for Corporate Portfolios
items.summary: Our team developed this offering to tackle some of the key problems faced by corporates when working with external counsel to manage their IP. In particular, it provides a flexible, comprehensive service for your IP assets, taking the stress of managing your brands and related IP portfolios away and giving you peace of mind that you’re in safe hands.
items.ai_summary_zh: Novagraaf集团针对企业外包知识产权管理的痛点，推出端到端商标服务。该服务提供灵活的服务模块选择，支持与客户现有外部律师网络整合或利用其全球资源。通过预先商定的固定费用模式实现成本透明，旨在减轻品牌管理压力，提升企业IP资产管理的效率与确定性。
items.ai_summary_en: Novagraaf Group has introduced an end-to-end trademark service designed to address common challenges corporations face when outsourcing intellectual property management. This flexible solution allows rights holders to customize their engagement, selecting specific modules such as renewals, maintenance, searches, prosecution, and enforcement. The service emphasizes budget transparency through pre-agreed fixed rates, eliminating unexpected costs often associated with external counsel. By integrating seamlessly with existing legal networks or leveraging Novagraaf’s global provider ecosystem, the offering acts as an effective extension of in-house teams. It aims to reduce administrative burdens and provide peace of mind, ensuring efficient brand portfolio management across multiple jurisdictions while maintaining high standards of care and support throughout the lifecycle of IP assets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文反映了欧洲企业IP管理外包趋势向“全流程托管”与“成本可控性”转变。实务中需关注服务商如何通过固定费率模型平衡复杂案件风险，以及混合律师网络模式下的责任界定与协作效率，这对优化企业IP预算结构具有参考意义。
items.ai_insight_en: For companies managing complex international portfolios, adopting integrated trademark services can significantly enhance operational efficiency. By outsourcing non-core administrative tasks under a fixed-cost model, in-house counsel can better control budgets and mitigate the risks of unpredictable legal fees in diverse jurisdictions. This approach allows legal teams to focus resources on strategic brand protection and core business objectives. Evaluating such end-to-end solutions is particularly relevant for organizations seeking to streamline workflows, improve response times, and optimize the overall management of European intellectual property assets without compromising on quality or oversight.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["服务具备高度灵活性，客户可自选续展、维权等模块，并整合自有或Novagraaf的外部律…", "采用预商定固定费用机制，按月度或季度开票，确保成本透明并利用规模经济降低整体支出", "依托Novagraaf的全球经验与Questel集团资源，为跨行业、跨法域的客户提供全…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Offers modular flexibility allowing clients to select specific services ranging from renewals to litigation support.", "Ensures cost predictability via pre-agreed fixed rates, removing financial uncertainties from external IP management.", "Integrates seamlessly with client-selected agents or utilizes Novagraaf's extensive global network of providers."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["企业IP外包正从单一事务处理转向注重成本透明与流程整合的端到端管理模式", "固定费用计费模式成为服务商吸引企业客户、解决预算不确定性痛点的关键竞争要素", "需观察混合外部律师网络模式在实际执行中的协作流畅度及服务质量一致性控制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Outsourcing routine trademark administration under fixed-fee structures helps stabilize budgets and reduces the operational burden on in-house legal teams.", "Practice note: Evaluate integrated service models that allow seamless integration with existing external counsel networks to maintain workflow continuity.", "What to monitor: Assess the transparency of billing mechanisms and the scalability of service modules to ensure they align with evolving portfolio needs."]
core_points_en: Offers modular flexibility allowing clients to select specific services ranging from renewals to litigation support.
Ensures cost predictability via pre-agreed fixed rates, removing financial uncertainties from external IP management.

items.title: Changes announced to the UK FDI mandatory notification regime
items.title_zh: 英国宣布调整外商直接投资强制申报制度
items.title_en: UK Government Announces Changes to NSI Act Mandatory Notification Regime
items.summary: The UK government announces changes to the mandatory notification regime under the National Security and Investment Act. What are the implications for l...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府确认将修改《国家安全与投资法》下的强制申报机制，新规将于今年晚些时候生效。主要变化包括重新定义人工智能以排除现成工具使用者，为通信行业引入最低限度豁免，并扩大对半导体、关键矿产、水务及能源行业的审查范围。此举旨在平衡国家安全与投资便利化。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK government has confirmed amendments to the mandatory notification regime under the National Security and Investment Act 2021, effective later this year. Following a twelve-week consultation, the changes aim to balance national security with investment facilitation. Key adjustments include narrowing the definition of Artificial Intelligence to exclude businesses using only off-the-shelf or non-consumer AI tools, thereby exempting them from mandatory notifications. The Communications sector will benefit from a new de minimis exemption, and intragroup reorganisations without changes in ultimate beneficial ownership remain exempt. Conversely, the regime expands to include Semiconductor, Critical Minerals, and Water sectors, while introducing new capacity thresholds for the Energy sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: These amendments refine the scope of UK national security reviews, offering relief for companies utilizing standard AI tools while tightening scrutiny on critical infrastructure. Rights holders and investors in newly covered sectors must assess whether their activities trigger mandatory notification obligations. The clarification on AI definitions may streamline M&A processes for technology adopters, but developers of AI technologies should anticipate continued regulatory attention. Portfolio managers should review transaction structures in light of the new sectoral expansions and exemptions to ensure compliance with the updated statutory framework.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["重新定义人工智能，排除仅使用现成或非消费者AI系统的企业，简化投资并购流程", "通信行业将获得新的最低限度豁免，集团内部重组若无实益所有权变更亦获豁免", "扩大强制申报范围，新增半导体、关键矿产和水务领域，并对能源行业设定500兆瓦产能阈值"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["AI definition narrowed to exclude users of off-the-shelf or non-consumer AI systems from mandatory notifications.", "New de minimis exemption introduced for Communications sector; intragroup reorganisations remain exempt from filing requirements.", "Mandatory regime expanded to cover Semiconductor, Critical Minerals, and Water sectors, with new Energy capacity thresholds."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["AI定义收窄可能减少部分纯应用层科技公司的申报义务，但研发型公司仍受监管", "新增半导体、关键矿产等 sectors 意味着相关领域的IP交易与并购将面临更严格的国…", "需持续监测新规生效后的具体执行指南，特别是关于“现成AI工具”的界定标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies using off-the-shelf AI tools may face reduced compliance burdens, while entities in semiconductor, critical minerals, and water sectors face new mandat…", "Practice note: The exclusion of non-developer AI users clarifies the boundary between technology adoption and development, potentially accelerating deal timelines for pure-play AI…", "What to monitor: Final statutory instruments defining 'off-the-shelf' AI and specific operational thresholds for the newly added critical sectors before the rules come into force."]
core_points_en: AI definition narrowed to exclude users of off-the-shelf or non-consumer AI systems from mandatory notifications.
New de minimis exemption introduced for Communications sector; intragroup reorganisations remain exempt from filing requirements.
Mandatory regime expanded to cover Semiconductor, Critical Minerals, and Water sectors, with new Energy capacity thresholds.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies using off-the-shelf AI tools may face reduced compliance burdens, while entities in semiconductor, critical minerals, and water sectors face new mandat…
Practice note: The exclusion of non-developer AI users clarifies the boundary between technology adoption and development, potentially accelerating deal timelines for pure-play AI…
What to monitor: Final statutory instruments defining 'off-the-shelf' AI and specific operational thresholds for the newly added critical sectors before the rules come into force.

items.title: Patent Law & Practice at a Glance – April 2026 - GJE
items.title_zh: 2026年4月专利法律与实务概览
items.title_en: GJE Patent Law Update: UKIPO One IPO Launch, EPO and PCT Practice Changes
items.summary: Read our April 2026 edition of patent law and practice which includes updates from the UK, the EPO and the WIPO/PCT.
items.ai_summary_zh: GJE发布2026年4月专利法律与实务概览，涵盖英国、欧洲专利局及WIPO/PCT的最新动态。正文确认英国知识产权局于2026年4月1日正式公开推出“One IPO”新服务，并提供了针对该系统用户的相关指南摘要。
items.ai_summary_en: This April 2026 edition of GJE’s Patent Law & Practice at a Glance provides essential updates for intellectual property professionals across Europe. The primary development is the public launch of the UK Intellectual Property Office’s new One IPO service on 1 April 2026, accompanied by comprehensive user guidance to assist applicants in navigating the updated procedural landscape. Additionally, this briefing covers significant recent developments and practice changes within the European Patent Office and the World Intellectual Property Organization’s Patent Cooperation Treaty system. These insights are designed to support in-house IP teams and legal counsel in maintaining compliance, optimizing application strategies, and securing robust protection for their portfolios amidst evolving administrative frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本期重点在于英国知识产权局“One IPO”服务的正式落地，标志着英国专利行政程序的数字化整合进入新阶段。实务中需关注新系统对申请流程、费用缴纳及通信方式的具体影响，同时留意EPO与PCT体系的并行更新，以确保跨国申请策略的合规性与效率。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should closely examine the specific operational requirements of the UKIPO’s One IPO system to adjust their United Kingdom filing strategies accordingly. Given the concurrent updates from the European Patent Office and the PCT framework, it is advisable to simultaneously review procedural compliance during European and international phases. This holistic approach ensures continued application efficiency under the new service environment, mitigating risks of rights loss or delays caused by unfamiliarity with updated workflows. Maintaining alignment across jurisdictions is crucial for effective global portfolio management.

items.title: No luck for Broadcom as Netflix and Quinn Emanuel succeed in another nullity claim
items.title_zh: Netflix在另一项无效主张中胜诉，Broadcom失利
items.title_en: Broadcom Faces Setback as Netflix Secures Nullity of Video Coding Patent EP 2 723 094
items.summary: Netflix has been battling Broadcom, previously known as Avago, over multiple video coding patents since 2018. In total, the extensive campaign comprises eight patents, of which seven have now finally been revoked. The current case centres on EP 2 723 094. The patent owned by Avago covers a “set top box architecture with application based […] The post No luck for Broadcom as Netflix and Quinn Emanuel succeed in another nullity claim appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 自2018年起，Netflix与Broadcom（前身为Avago）就多项视频编码专利展开诉讼。该案共涉及八项专利，其中七项已被最终撤销。本案焦点为EP 2 723 094号专利，该专利由Avago拥有，涵盖“基于应用的机顶盒架构”。
items.ai_summary_en: Since 2018, Netflix has engaged in extensive litigation against Broadcom (formerly Avago) concerning multiple video coding patents. This long-running campaign involves eight patents, seven of which have now been successfully revoked. The latest development centers on European patent EP 2 723 094, which covers set top box architecture with application-based features. Following previous successes, Netflix, represented by Quinn Emanuel, achieved another victory with the declaration of invalidity for this specific patent. This outcome marks a significant setback for Broadcom in its ongoing patent enforcement efforts and underscores the critical role of the Unified Patent Court in determining patent validity within Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注EP 2 723 094号专利的最终无效决定及剩余一项专利的诉讼走向。Broadcom在欧洲的视频编码专利组合稳定性受到持续挑战，权利人应监测此类大规模无效战役对类似资产的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders asserting standard-essential or video coding patents in Europe should anticipate rigorous validity challenges before the Unified Patent Court. High-frequency invalidation actions demonstrate that patent portfolios require robust pre-filing stability assessments to withstand scrutiny. Market participants must closely monitor UPC case law trends to refine their litigation and licensing approaches. Proactive evaluation of patent strength and adaptive strategic planning are essential to mitigate the risk of widespread revocations and ensure effective intellectual property protection across European jurisdictions.

The current legal dispute focuses on the nullity claim regarding European patent EP 2 723 094, covering set top box architecture technologies.
Broadcom continues to face challenges in maintaining patent stability during prolonged litigation, highlighting risks in enforcement strategies.

Practice note: Monitor Unified Patent Court decisions closely to adapt overseas enforcement and licensing strategies effectively.
What to monitor: Trends in UPC nullity rulings affecting standard-essential and multimedia technology patents.

items.title: After ownership change: Silimed and Polytech poised to fight it out at UPC
items.title_zh: 权属变更后：Silimed与Polytech拟在UPC展开诉讼
items.title_en: Silimed and Polytech Set for UPC Clash Following German Court Ownership Ruling
items.summary: Just over a year ago, the Frankfurt Higher Regional Court granted Silimed the property rights to EP 2 581 193. The patent protects a process for manufacturing implants. Since 2017 the Brazilian manufacturer had fought with competitor Polytech over the ownership of the technology. After the rights were officially transferred, Silimed recently launched an infringement […] The post After ownership change: Silimed and Polytech poised to fight it out at UPC appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 法兰克福高等地方法院一年多前将EP 2 581 193号专利（涉及植入物制造工艺）的所有权判给Silimed。自2017年起，巴西制造商Silimed与竞争对手Polytech就该技术所有权发生争议。权利正式转移后，Silimed近期启动了侵权诉讼程序，双方预计将在统一专利法院（UPC）进行对抗。
items.ai_summary_en: Following a decisive ruling by the Frankfurt Higher Regional Court, Brazilian manufacturer Silimed secured full ownership of European patent EP 2 581 193, which covers a specific process for manufacturing medical implants. This judgment effectively concluded a protracted legal dispute with competitor Polytech that had persisted since 2017 regarding the technology's rightful owner. With the property rights officially transferred to Silimed, the company has promptly initiated infringement proceedings against Polytech at the Unified Patent Court (UPC). This strategic shift moves the conflict from national German courts to the new pan-European judicial framework. The case highlights the UPC's growing role in resolving complex, cross-border patent disputes and demonstrates how rights holders are leveraging the new court system to enforce their patents across multiple jurisdictions simultaneously.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案展示了国家法院权属判决与UPC侵权诉讼的衔接。需关注UPC如何处理基于先前国家法院确权判决的侵权主张，以及跨国医疗器械专利纠纷在统一专利法院体系下的审理节奏与管辖权协调问题。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the UPC's evolving jurisdiction over patent ownership and infringement matters. As the new court becomes a primary venue for cross-border disputes, companies must adapt their European litigation strategies accordingly. It is critical to conduct thorough due diligence on patent title chains before initiating or defending against actions. Rights holders should assess potential risks within their European patent portfolios early, ensuring they are prepared for the procedural nuances of the UPC. Proactive planning helps avoid being disadvantaged by fragmented national strategies, allowing for more effective enforcement or defense across member states.

Silimed immediately filed an infringement action at the Unified Patent Court following the official transfer of patent rights.
The dispute illustrates a broader trend of centralizing European patent enforcement and dispute resolution within the UPC system.

Practice note: Strengthen patent ownership due diligence processes to mitigate risks associated with cross-border title disputes and subsequent infringement claims.

items.title: UK kicks the AI copyright can down the road: 5 takeaways for business
items.title_zh: 英国推迟AI版权改革：企业需关注的五点启示
items.title_en: UK Pauses AI Copyright Reform: Five Strategic Takeaways for Business Amid Policy Uncertainty
items.summary: The country hit pause on AI and copyright reform. But who benefits from indecision and what underpriced issue is “arguably the most significant”?
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要及正文极短，仅指出英国已暂停人工智能与版权相关的法律改革进程。文章标题暗示将提供五项商业启示，并探讨不确定性下的受益者及被低估的关键问题，但具体细节未在提供的片段中展开。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK government has officially suspended its plans to reform copyright laws regarding artificial intelligence, choosing to maintain the current legal framework. This decision to pause legislative progress has sparked debate over which market participants benefit from regulatory indecision and highlighted specific underpriced issues that may prove most significant in the long term. The article outlines five key takeaways for businesses operating in this environment, emphasizing the need to reassess risk strategies amidst ongoing uncertainty. By delaying statutory changes, the state creates a temporary status quo, yet leaves critical questions unanswered. Stakeholders must now navigate a landscape where the absence of clear rules is itself a strategic factor, requiring careful evaluation of existing exemptions and potential future liabilities associated with AI training data usage.
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should remain vigilant regarding the compliance ambiguities created by the delay in UK policy updates. Reliance on existing copyright exceptions for AI training carries inherent risks if future legislation introduces retroactive obligations or stricter standards. It is prudent to utilize this interim period to conduct thorough audits of data sourcing practices and licensing agreements. Establishing robust internal monitoring mechanisms will enable rapid adaptation to any sudden shifts in the legislative landscape. Proactively preparing flexible data authorization frameworks can mitigate exposure to future legal challenges, ensuring business continuity regardless of how the regulatory environment eventually evolves.

items.title: Local division Mannheim flirts with setting FRAND rate
items.title_zh: UPC 曼海姆分部倾向裁定 FRAND 费率
items.title_en: UPC Mannheim Division Signals Openness to Determining FRAND Rates in ZTE v Samsung
items.summary: Following Munich Regional Court, Mannheim local division is now also opening up to making a FRAND-rate determination.
items.ai_summary_zh: 继慕尼黑地方法院后，统一专利法院（UPC）曼海姆地方分院在 ZTE 诉三星案中展现新动向。主审法官托赫特曼提出 UPC 框架下裁定 FRAND 费率的程序构想，打破德国法院长期回避此议题的传统。因三星提出反诉，法庭需对此作出裁决，预示欧洲标准必要专利诉讼格局生变。
items.ai_summary_en: Following the Munich Regional Court, the Unified Patent Court’s Mannheim Local Division is shifting away from the traditional German judicial reluctance to determine Fair, Reasonable, and Non-Discriminatory (FRAND) royalty rates. In the ongoing ZTE v Samsung proceedings, Presiding Judge Peter Tochtermann outlined a procedural framework for such determinations within the UPC system. This development arises because Samsung filed a FRAND counterclaim, compelling the court to address the issue substantively rather than avoiding it. The hearing attracted significant attention from legal and economic experts across Europe and Asia. This move suggests a potential transformation in European standard-essential patent litigation, challenging the historical dominance of UK courts in setting global FRAND terms and establishing new precedents for continental European jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临更直接的费率裁判风险与机遇。需重新评估 UPC 诉讼策略，准备详尽的经济证据以应对费率裁定。建议密切关注曼海姆及巴黎分院动态，利用新机制争取有利许可条件或防御高额索赔。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in standard-essential patent disputes should anticipate increased judicial willingness in Germany to set binding FRAND rates. Rights holders and implementers must adapt their Unified Patent Court litigation strategies by preparing robust economic evidence and valuation models to support or challenge proposed royalty figures. The interplay between the Mannheim and Paris divisions may offer strategic opportunities for forum selection. Parties should closely monitor how these new procedural frameworks develop, as they could significantly influence licensing negotiations and the overall landscape of SEP enforcement in Europe, potentially reducing reliance on non-European jurisdictions for rate-setting decisions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["曼海姆分院打破德国法院不裁定 FRAND 费率的传统", "法官在 ZTE 诉三星案中提出 UPC 费率裁定程序框架", "三星的反诉迫使法庭必须对费率问题做出实质性回应"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Mannheim Local Division breaks with the long-standing tradition of German courts avoiding substantive FRAND rate determinations.", "Presiding Judge Tochtermann presented a specific procedural framework for UPC FRAND rate settings during the ZTE v Samsung hearing.", "Samsung's filed counterclaim legally obligates the division to provide a substantive response regarding the appropriate licensing rates."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["企业需调整 UPC 诉讼策略，强化费率抗辩与经济证据准备", "关注曼海姆与巴黎分院联动，灵活选择管辖地争取主动"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigants face heightened risks and opportunities regarding direct judicial determination of FRAND rates in Germany.", "Practice note: Strengthen economic evidence and adjust litigation strategies to effectively engage with UPC rate-setting mechanisms.", "What to monitor: Procedural developments in Mannheim and Paris divisions to optimize jurisdictional choices and licensing outcomes."]
core_points_en: The Mannheim Local Division breaks with the long-standing tradition of German courts avoiding substantive FRAND rate determinations.
Presiding Judge Tochtermann presented a specific procedural framework for UPC FRAND rate settings during the ZTE v Samsung hearing.
Samsung's filed counterclaim legally obligates the division to provide a substantive response regarding the appropriate licensing rates.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Litigants face heightened risks and opportunities regarding direct judicial determination of FRAND rates in Germany.
Practice note: Strengthen economic evidence and adjust litigation strategies to effectively engage with UPC rate-setting mechanisms.
What to monitor: Procedural developments in Mannheim and Paris divisions to optimize jurisdictional choices and licensing outcomes.

items.title: A strong trademark stands out - Arnold & Siedsma [EN]
items.title_zh: 强势商标脱颖而出：YUN案例解析
items.title_en: Distinctiveness as Core Brand Protection: How Strong Trademarks Stand Out in the Market
items.summary: YUN is a strong trademark because it is distinctive and closely connected to the skincare products it represents. The name is memorable, easy to recognize,
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文以护肤品牌“YUN”为例，阐述强势商标需具备显著性且与产品紧密关联。指出独特非描述性名称更易注册和保护，有助于建立品牌身份、消费者信任及长期资产价值，并能有效防止竞争对手混淆。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the skincare brand YUN to illustrate how distinctiveness serves as the foundation for effective trademark protection. The article highlights that YUN is a strong mark because it is memorable, easily recognizable, and clearly differentiates the brand from competitors in the crowded skincare sector. Unlike descriptive terms, unique names facilitate quicker consumer association with specific product quality and corporate reputation. This recognition builds consumer trust and becomes a valuable long-term asset. Furthermore, distinctive marks are generally easier to register and enforce against infringers, preventing market confusion. The piece underscores that securing legal protection for unique identifiers supports sustained brand growth and strengthens market position, making distinctiveness a critical strategic consideration for rights holders seeking robust intellectual property coverage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务观察：律所通过具体案例强调商标显著性对注册成功率及后续维权的重要性。企业应关注名称的非描述性特征，以增强法律保护力度并构建品牌壁垒。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders, prioritizing distinctiveness during brand creation significantly reduces registration risks and enforcement costs. Companies should avoid descriptive or generic terms that lack inherent distinctiveness, as these face higher refusal rates at offices like the EUIPO and BOIP. Instead, adopting arbitrary or fanciful names strengthens legal standing and facilitates broader protection against infringement. This approach not only secures immediate registration but also builds a defensible portfolio capable of supporting global expansion. Legal teams must conduct thorough clearance searches to ensure chosen marks do not conflict with existing rights while maintaining sufficient uniqueness to stand out in the marketplace.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["YUN因具有显著性且非单纯描述性，被视为适合保护的强势商标", "独特商标更易注册和执行，能防止竞争对手使用易混淆名称", "强势商标有助于建立品牌身份、消费者信任及长期品牌资产"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Distinctive trademarks like YUN enhance brand identity by creating memorable associations with product quality and company reputation.", "Unique, non-descriptive marks are easier to register and enforce, providing stronger legal barriers against confusingly similar competitor names.", "Strong trademark protection supports long-term commercial growth by securing valuable brand assets and maintaining clear market differentiation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["显著性与非描述性是商标获得强保护的核心要素", "强势商标在防止市场混淆及构建品牌资产方面具有长期价值", "需持续关注欧盟及比荷卢地区对商标显著性的审查标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritizing inherent distinctiveness during brand development minimizes registration refusals and strengthens enforcement capabilities against infringers.", "Practice note: Conduct comprehensive clearance searches focusing on phonetic and conceptual similarities to ensure marks are sufficiently unique for registration.", "What to monitor: Track opposition trends at the EUIPO and BOIP regarding descriptive versus distinctive marks to refine filing strategies and risk assessments."]
core_points_en: Distinctive trademarks like YUN enhance brand identity by creating memorable associations with product quality and company reputation.
Unique, non-descriptive marks are easier to register and enforce, providing stronger legal barriers against confusingly similar competitor names.
Strong trademark protection supports long-term commercial growth by securing valuable brand assets and maintaining clear market differentiation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritizing inherent distinctiveness during brand development minimizes registration refusals and strengthens enforcement capabilities against infringers.
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive clearance searches focusing on phonetic and conceptual similarities to ensure marks are sufficiently unique for registration.
What to monitor: Track opposition trends at the EUIPO and BOIP regarding descriptive versus distinctive marks to refine filing strategies and risk assessments.

items.title: Clinical Trial Inventions In The Squeeze: The New Transparency Rules For Clinical Trials In The EU And The Assessment Of Patentability By The EPO And The UPC, Epi Information, 1/2026 – Maiwald
items.title_zh: 欧盟临床试验新规下的专利性评估困境
items.title_en: Clinical Trial Inventions Under Pressure: EU Transparency Rules and Patentability Assessments by the EPO and UPC
items.summary: The article examines how the EU’s new transparency framework for clinical trials affects patentability assessments for inventions arising in the clinical
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析欧盟临床试验新透明度框架如何影响相关发明的专利性评估。作者从监管与专利法角度，阐述试验注册等披露何时构成现有技术，并结合EPO判例及UPC首批裁决，提供协调监管时间线与申请策略的实务指引，以支持技术效果与权利要求范围。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines how the European Union’s new transparency framework for clinical trials impacts the patentability of inventions developed during the clinical phase. From both regulatory and patent law perspectives, the authors analyze where and when disclosures, such as trial registrations and associated documents, may constitute prior art. The analysis explores the interaction between these disclosure obligations and established case law from the European Patent Office (EPO), alongside recent decisions from the Unified Patent Court (UPC). Based on these findings, the piece offers practical guidance for aligning regulatory timelines and publication duties with filing strategies. This approach aims to ensure that technical effects are robustly supported and claim scopes remain secure, helping rights holders navigate the complex intersection of regulatory compliance and intellectual property protection in Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注欧盟透明度规则导致的早期公开对新颖性的潜在冲击。实务中应监测EPO与UPC对此类现有技术的认定标准差异，确保申请策略能应对监管披露带来的在先技术风险，维持权利要求的稳定性。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants conducting clinical trials in the EU must carefully manage the timing of regulatory disclosures to prevent premature public availability of data from destroying patent novelty. It is critical to review European portfolio strategies, ensuring that key patent applications are filed before mandatory transparency requirements trigger public access to trial information. Additionally, rights holders should leverage recent Unified Patent Court decisions to refine enforcement strategies. By synchronizing regulatory compliance with intellectual property planning, companies can mitigate risks associated with accidental prior art creation while maintaining robust protection for their innovations across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["分析欧盟临床试验透明度新规下，试验注册等披露构成现有技术的时点与情形", "结合EPO既定判例与UPC首批裁决，探讨监管披露与专利性评估的法律互动", "提供协调监管发布时间线与专利申请策略的实务指引，以稳固权利要求支持"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Trial disclosures under new EU transparency rules may constitute prior art, potentially jeopardizing the novelty of related patent applications.", "Assessments must integrate established EPO case law with emerging jurisprudence from the Unified Patent Court regarding early disclosures.", "Applicants should coordinate regulatory publication schedules with patent filing strategies to safeguard technical effects and claim scope."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["监管披露可能提前形成现有技术，需重新评估新颖性宽限期与申请时机", "EPO与UPC在认定临床试验数据公开效力上可能存在裁判标准差异", "后续需观察UPC针对临床试验披露构成的现有技术认定的最新判例动向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure critical patent applications are filed before mandatory clinical trial registrations become publicly accessible to preserve novelty.", "Practice note: Align European patent filing timelines with regulatory disclosure nodes to avoid inadvertent self-collision with prior art.", "What to monitor: Track evolving Unified Patent Court rulings on the impact of regulatory transparency on patent validity and enforcement."]
core_points_en: Trial disclosures under new EU transparency rules may constitute prior art, potentially jeopardizing the novelty of related patent applications.
Assessments must integrate established EPO case law with emerging jurisprudence from the Unified Patent Court regarding early disclosures.
Applicants should coordinate regulatory publication schedules with patent filing strategies to safeguard technical effects and claim scope.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure critical patent applications are filed before mandatory clinical trial registrations become publicly accessible to preserve novelty.
Practice note: Align European patent filing timelines with regulatory disclosure nodes to avoid inadvertent self-collision with prior art.
What to monitor: Track evolving Unified Patent Court rulings on the impact of regulatory transparency on patent validity and enforcement.

items.title: Businesses can handle UKIPO fee hike: lawyers
items.title_zh: 律师称企业可应对英国知识产权局费用上涨
items.title_en: Lawyers Assert UKIPO Remains Competitive Despite Planned Fee Increases
items.summary: UKIPO will remain a competitive option as long as efficient service continues
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，尽管英国知识产权局（UKIPO）计划提高费用，但法律界人士认为，只要该局能继续保持高效的服务水平，其作为知识产权申请目的地仍将具备竞争力，企业能够消化此次费用上涨带来的影响。
items.ai_summary_en: Legal professionals indicate that the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) will retain its status as a competitive jurisdiction for intellectual property filings, provided it maintains high standards of service efficiency. Although the office plans to implement new fee increases, industry consensus suggests that the strategic importance of the UK market and the perceived value of UKIPO’s operational speed are sufficient for businesses to absorb these additional costs. Consequently, significant migration of application volumes to alternative jurisdictions is not anticipated. Rights holders are expected to view the fee adjustment as a manageable operational expense rather than a deterrent, ensuring continued engagement with the UK system while prioritizing service quality over marginal cost differences in their global IP strategies.
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating within the UK should conduct a thorough review of their portfolio maintenance budgets to account for the upcoming fee adjustments. While the cost increase warrants financial recalibration, it does not necessitate a fundamental shift in broader intellectual property strategy. Organizations should prioritize monitoring changes in examination timelines and procedural efficiency at the UKIPO. Ensuring that higher fees correspond with consistent or improved service quality is essential for securing robust rights confirmation. This approach allows companies to optimize budget allocation effectively while maintaining strong legal protection in this key European market.
