items.title: Proportionality considerations in proceedings before the UPC
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院诉讼中的比例原则考量
items.title_en: UPC Proportionality Analysis: Injunctions as Standard Remedies with Limited Discretion
items.summary: UPC
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析UPC在侵权诉讼中颁发禁令及纠正措施时的比例原则适用。依据《统一专利法院协议》第63和64条，法院拥有裁量权。在Belkin诉Philips案中，上诉法院指出纠正措施通常为侵权后果，例外情况下才因不成比例被拒。举证责任在于侵权人，需证明措施不成比例、其转化意愿及第三方利益。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines whether the Unified Patent Court applies proportionality principles when granting injunctions. Although the UPC Agreement states courts may grant injunctions, the Court of Appeal clarified in Belkin v. Philips that corrective measures are standard consequences of infringement rather than discretionary outcomes. Denial is exceptional, reserved for minor infringements or where infringers actively remedy violations. The burden of proof lies primarily with alleged infringers to demonstrate disproportionality and potential harm to third-party interests. This interpretation aligns with the Enforcement Directive, ensuring high intellectual property protection by making corrective measures the norm.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC司法实践倾向于将纠正措施视为侵权的自然结果，而非完全由法院自由裁量。侵权人若想避免禁令或召回，需主动承担高标准的举证责任，证明措施的不成比例性及自身整改意愿。权利人无需预先证明比例性，仅在侵权人完成举证后才需回应。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing patent litigation in Europe should not rely solely on proportionality defenses to avoid injunctions. Early assessment of product infringement risks is critical. Once involved in proceedings, parties must proactively provide evidence of their willingness and ability to modify products to non-infringing states. Given the high evidentiary threshold, companies must prepare robust documentation to counter claims of disproportionality. Passive strategies are likely to fail, as courts prioritize effective IP enforcement over defendant convenience unless significant third-party harms are proven.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC协议未明确禁止令前的比例测试，但规定法院“可以”颁发禁令及纠正措施", "Belkin诉Philips案中，法院认定纠正措施为常态，侵权人承担证明其不成比例的举…", "若侵权轻微或侵权人愿意且能将产品转化为非侵权状态，纠正措施可能被视为不成比例"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Corrective measures are typically mandatory consequences of patent infringement, limiting judicial discretion to deny them broadly.", "Proportionality defenses succeed only in specific scenarios involving minor infringements or active remediation efforts by defendants.", "Alleged infringers bear the heavy burden of proving disproportionate impact and demonstrating willingness to eliminate infringing features."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC对比例原则抗辩的接受度较低，纠正措施通常自动适用于确认侵权的案件", "侵权人需重点准备关于“转化非侵权状态意愿”及“第三方利益受损”的证据链", "后续需关注UPC在其他案件中是否延续此举证责任分配逻辑，尤其是对轻微侵权的界定"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders benefit from predictable enforcement norms, while defendants face higher hurdles to block corrective measures.", "Practice note: Establish rapid response mechanisms to assess modification feasibility and document remediation capabilities immediately upon notice.", "What to monitor: Track emerging case law defining 'minor infringement' thresholds and acceptable timelines for converting infringing embodiments."]
core_points_en: Corrective measures are typically mandatory consequences of patent infringement, limiting judicial discretion to deny them broadly.
Proportionality defenses succeed only in specific scenarios involving minor infringements or active remediation efforts by defendants.
Alleged infringers bear the heavy burden of proving disproportionate impact and demonstrating willingness to eliminate infringing features.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders benefit from predictable enforcement norms, while defendants face higher hurdles to block corrective measures.
Practice note: Establish rapid response mechanisms to assess modification feasibility and document remediation capabilities immediately upon notice.
What to monitor: Track emerging case law defining 'minor infringement' thresholds and acceptable timelines for converting infringing embodiments.

items.title: Copyright & AI in the UK: The Debate Rolls On
items.title_zh: 英国版权与人工智能：辩论持续
items.title_en: UK Copyright and AI Report: Legislative Action Deferred Pending Further Evidence
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国政府发布《版权与人工智能报告》，未提出具体立法提案，决定推迟关键决策以收集更多证据。报告回顾了2024年底启动的咨询过程，对比了维持现状、强制许可及引入文本数据挖掘例外等选项。目前英国法律暂无变更，各方需继续关注Getty Images诉Stability AI等案例及国际动态。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Government has published its long-awaited Report on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence, fulfilling obligations under the Data (Use and Access) Act. Following a high-engagement consultation period from late 2024 to early 2025, the report addresses intense lobbying from both creative industries and the AI sector. However, it offers no concrete legislative proposals or immediate changes to UK copyright law. Key decisions have been deferred as the government seeks further evidence before considering statutory interventions. While the document outlines a general direction regarding potential future frameworks, including references to international models like the EU’s approach, it provides no specific timelines for updates. Consequently, the current legal status quo remains intact, leaving significant uncertainty for stakeholders awaiting regulatory clarity on data usage and remuneration mechanisms.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本报告显示英国政府在平衡创意产业与AI发展上采取谨慎态度，避免立即立法。企业应密切关注后续证据收集进程及法院在Getty Images案中的裁决，这将直接影响未来数据使用的合规边界及许可模式。
items.ai_insight_en: In the absence of imminent statutory reform, market participants should maintain robust existing copyright compliance frameworks. Reliance on contractual licensing and data authorization mechanisms becomes critical to mitigate risks during this legislative window. Stakeholders are advised to track significant judicial outcomes, such as Getty Images v Stability AI, which will likely shape interim interpretations of fair use and text-and-data mining exceptions. Furthermore, given the report’s reference to international models, companies should assess how diverging approaches between the UK and EU regimes may impact cross-border data training strategies and operational compliance requirements.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国政府发布版权与AI报告，未提出具体立法提案，决定推迟关键决策", "政府将继续评估是否引入带有权利保留机制的商业性文本数据挖掘例外", "目前英国版权法无变更，权利人及开发者需追踪相关判例与国际进展"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国政府暂不修改版权法，采取观望态度以收集更多行业证据", "需持续监测Getty Images诉Stability AI等关键判例的司法走向", "关注政府后续关于文本数据挖掘例外及权利保留机制的政策更新"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Maintain current copyright compliance systems and rely on contractual data licenses to manage risk during the legislative gap.", "Practice note: Evaluate differences between UK and EU rules to optimize cross-border data training strategies and ensure regulatory alignment.", "What to monitor: Track key judicial precedents like Getty Images v Stability AI and ongoing international policy shifts affecting AI data usage."]
core_points_en: The UK Government released its Copyright and AI Report but declined to propose immediate legislative amendments to regulate artificial intelligence activities.
Critical policy questions were deferred, with authorities opting to gather additional evidence before pursuing any formal statutory changes.
Rights holders and AI developers must closely monitor evolving case law and international regulatory developments during this legislative pause.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Maintain current copyright compliance systems and rely on contractual data licenses to manage risk during the legislative gap.
Practice note: Evaluate differences between UK and EU rules to optimize cross-border data training strategies and ensure regulatory alignment.
What to monitor: Track key judicial precedents like Getty Images v Stability AI and ongoing international policy shifts affecting AI data usage.

items.title: Enforcement Era Of The Data Act Begins: What It Means For Dispute Resolution
items.title_zh: 欧盟《数据法案》执法时代开启：对争议解决的影响
items.title_en: Enforcement Era Of The Data Act Begins: What It Means For Dispute Resolution
items.summary: On 12 September 2025, the EU Data Act (Regulation (EU) 2023/2854) became applicable across the EU. Finland’s complementary enforcement legislation, the Act on the Supervision of Data Management and Data Sharing (laki datan hallinnan ja jakamisen valvonnasta 1148/2025), entered into force on 1 January 2026.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟《数据法案》于2025年9月12日生效，芬兰配套监管法于2026年1月1日施行。法案规定数据持有者需向用户免费开放数据，并允许在公平合理条件下向第三方共享。若涉及商业秘密且保密措施未落实，持有者可暂停共享。芬兰交通通信局为主要监管机构，已处理首起涉及瑞典数据持有者的跨境投诉，凸显跨境执法机制的实际运作。
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需关注数据共享与商业秘密保护的边界，特别是跨境场景下的监管协调。企业应评估合规义务，注意监管机构对“公平合理”条款及保密措施的具体执行标准，以及跨境投诉处理的程序要求。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟《数据法案》及芬兰配套监管法分别于2025年9月和2026年1月生效实施", "数据持有者须免费向用户提供机器可读数据，并在公平合理条件下向第三方共享", "芬兰交通通信局已受理首起涉及瑞典公司的跨境投诉，验证了跨境执法机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["数据持有者需建立符合法案格式要求的实时数据访问机制，并明确第三方共享的商业条款", "在涉及商业秘密时，需完善书面保密协议及通知程序，以合法行使暂停共享权利", "关注芬兰交通通信局等监管机构对跨境数据纠纷的裁量倾向及后续执法案例"]

items.title: EUIPO’s 2024 Examination Guidelines – two years on, NFTs, digital assets and geographical indications
items.title_zh: 欧盟商标局2024审查指南回顾：NFT、数字资产与地理标志
items.title_en: EUIPO 2024 Guidelines Review: Digital Evidence Standards, NFTs, and Geographical Indications
items.summary: The past year has seen fewer substantive updates in EU trade mark practice, with the 2025 revisions primarily focused on design law. For...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章回顾欧盟知识产权局2024年商标审查指南实施两年的影响。重点包括证据提交规则的结构化调整，明确维基百科等可编辑网站截图单独作为真实使用证据效力不足，而社交媒体证据需结合日期、URL及分析数据以增强可信度。此外，指南澄清了基于公共政策或道德理由驳回商标申请的语境化评估方法。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis reviews the practical impact of the EUIPO’s 2024 Examination Guidelines, which entered into force in March 2024. While recent revisions prioritize design law, the trademark guidelines remain crucial for procedural clarity and digital asset classification. A key focus is the stricter standards for submitting digital evidence. The guidelines explicitly state that printouts from editable platforms like Wikipedia are insufficient as standalone proof of genuine use. Instead, rights holders must provide robust corroboration, such as social media screenshots with verified publication dates, URLs, and analytics data. Recent Appeal Board decisions reinforce these requirements, rejecting unreliable web extracts. Additionally, the text clarifies how public policy refusals should be assessed contextually based on public perception at the time of filing, offering critical guidance for navigating modern trademark registration challenges.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中应重视数字证据的完整性与佐证链条，避免依赖单一网络来源证明商标使用。社交媒体营销材料需保留完整元数据以应对非使用撤销挑战。公共政策驳回的评估更趋具体化，需关注特定时期公众认知对商标注册性的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders operating in the EU must upgrade their evidence collection strategies to meet the EUIPO’s heightened standards for digital proof. Relying solely on simple web screenshots is no longer sufficient for defending against non-use cancellations or supporting applications. Practitioners should implement systematic protocols to capture comprehensive digital evidence chains, ensuring all social media submissions include verifiable timestamps, geographic indicators, and analytical data. Furthermore, applicants must closely monitor evolving case law regarding public policy grounds for refusal. Understanding the dynamic interpretation of moral and public order clauses is essential to anticipate compliance risks and prevent unexpected registration obstacles in the current legal landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["指南明确维基百科等可编辑网站截图不能单独作为商标真实使用的有效证据", "社交媒体证据被接受，但需提供截图、发布日期、URL及文件分析数据以佐证其可信度", "针对违反公共政策或道德的驳回，强调需结合相关公众在特定时间的感知进行语境化评估"]

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["数字证据需形成完整链条，单一网页截图证明力弱，需辅以其他客观数据", "社交媒体作为使用证据时，必须确保证据的可验证性与地域关联性", "公共政策驳回审查更注重动态语境，需监测申请时点的社会舆论环境"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must abandon reliance on single web screenshots and build complete digital evidence chains with metadata.", "Practice note: Systematically preserve social media data with timestamps and geographic indicators to ensure admissibility.", "What to monitor: Track evolving case law on public policy clauses to preemptively assess trademark registration compliance risks."]

Social media evidence requires corroborating metadata, including screenshots, publication dates, URLs, and file analytics.
Public policy refusals require a contextual assessment based on the relevant public's perception at the time of application.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must abandon reliance on single web screenshots and build complete digital evidence chains with metadata.
Practice note: Systematically preserve social media data with timestamps and geographic indicators to ensure admissibility.
What to monitor: Track evolving case law on public policy clauses to preemptively assess trademark registration compliance risks.

items.title: Reel and Hogan Lovells deflect damages suit in dispute with Fives ELC
items.title_zh: Reel与霍金路伟在Fives ELC纠纷中成功驳回损害赔偿诉请
items.title_en: Reel and Hogan Lovells Successfully Deflect Damages Claim in French Patent Dispute with Fives ELC
items.summary: French mechanical engineering company Fives ECL and competitor Reel are fighting over EP 17 40 740 B1. It protects a compact service module for installations for the electrolytic production of aluminium. The patent expired in March 2025. The parties, who operate in the market for specialised cranes used in aluminium smelters as part of the […] The post Reel and Hogan Lovells deflect damages suit in dispute with Fives ELC appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国机械工程公司Fives ECL与竞争对手Reel就欧洲专利EP 17 40 740 B1发生争议。该专利保护铝电解生产装置的紧凑型服务模块，已于2025年3月到期。双方主要在铝冶炼厂专用起重机市场运营。Reel及其代理律所霍金路伟成功驳回了针对其的损害赔偿诉讼。
items.ai_summary_en: French mechanical engineering firm Fives ELC and its competitor Reel are engaged in litigation concerning European patent EP 17 40 740 B1. This patent, which protects a compact service module for installations used in the electrolytic production of aluminium, expired in March 2025. Both companies compete in the specialized market for cranes utilized within aluminium smelters. Recent developments indicate that Reel, represented by Hogan Lovells, has successfully defended against a damages suit filed by Fives ELC. The defense effectively neutralized the claim for compensation, preventing an unfavorable judgment against Reel. This outcome highlights the strategic importance of robust legal representation in navigating post-expiry patent disputes and protecting market position in highly specialized industrial sectors.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注法院驳回损害赔偿的具体法律理由及程序阶段。鉴于专利已过期，后续是否仍有禁令或其他救济措施的争议值得观察。目前信息有限，仅能确认诉讼防御阶段的初步结果。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders often initiate damages claims even as patents approach expiration or shortly after lapse, creating significant litigation risks for competitors. Companies operating in heavy machinery or metallurgical equipment sectors must conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses before market entry. Proactive preparation is essential to mitigate exposure to high-value compensation claims. Effective defense strategies should leverage procedural rules and substantive arguments regarding patent validity and infringement scope. Market participants should anticipate aggressive enforcement tactics during the twilight period of patent protection and maintain readiness to contest unjustified allegations promptly.

Reel successfully defended against Fives ELC's damages lawsuit in France, avoiding liability for compensation.

items.title: Nissan, Volvo sued in Germany and Korea as battery patent wars intensify
items.title_zh: 日产沃尔沃在德韩被诉，电池专利战升级
items.title_en: Nissan and Volvo Sued in Germany and Korea as Battery Patent Wars Intensify
items.summary: Tulip Innovation, LG Energy Solution ramp up licensing campaign against Sunwoda
items.ai_summary_zh: 标题显示日产与沃尔沃在德国和韩国面临诉讼，电池专利战争加剧。摘要指出Tulip Innovation与LG能源解决方案正加强对欣旺达的许可活动。正文仅重复摘要内容，未提供具体案情、法院名称或诉讼细节，属于简短资讯片段。
items.ai_summary_en: Tulip Innovation and LG Energy Solution have significantly escalated their licensing campaign against Sunwoda, extending legal actions to include major automotive manufacturers Nissan and Volvo in both Germany and South Korea. This strategic expansion marks a critical intensification of global battery patent disputes, with Germany emerging as a primary judicial battleground due to its stringent enforcement mechanisms. The developments underscore the European market's heightened focus on intellectual property rights within the battery technology sector. Rights holders are increasingly leveraging cross-border litigation to secure favorable licensing terms, signaling a more aggressive enforcement landscape for all market participants involved in the electric vehicle supply chain.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息极度有限，仅能确认相关主体在多法域启动法律行动。需持续监测后续披露的起诉法院、涉案专利族及具体禁令请求，以评估对欧洲电动汽车供应链的实际法律风险敞口。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe must proactively assess patent infringement risks associated with battery technologies before commercial deployment. Conducting thorough Freedom to Operate analyses is essential to mitigate exposure to injunctions that could disrupt sales or manufacturing. Companies should establish robust rapid response protocols to address sudden cross-border licensing demands effectively. Strengthening core patent portfolios and engaging constructively in licensing negotiations can help avoid significant commercial losses. Given the rigorous enforcement environment in key jurisdictions like Germany, early preparation and strategic IP management are critical for sustaining market access and operational continuity.

items.title: The UPC - only three years old and already leading
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院：成立三年已居领先地位
items.title_en: UPC at Three: How Key Rulings Established Its Leadership in European Patent Litigation
items.summary: Finnegan’s Antje Brambrink and Moritz Meckel detail the most significant developments in the Unified Patent Court’s case law
items.ai_summary_zh: Finnegan律所专家Antje Brambrink和Moritz Meckel撰文，详细阐述统一专利法院（UPC）成立三年来在判例法方面的最重大进展。文章旨在梳理该法院早期的司法实践及其对欧洲专利体系的影响。
items.ai_summary_en: Finnegan partners Antje Brambrink and Moritz Meckel analyze the most significant developments in the Unified Patent Court’s case law since its inception. Despite being only three years old, the UPC has rapidly established a leading position in European patent litigation. The article highlights how the court has demonstrated maturity and influence through pivotal decisions that clarify the unified patent system. These rulings address complex issues regarding infringement and validity disputes, setting new benchmarks for judicial practice across Europe. The analysis underscores the UPC’s growing authority in shaping a cohesive legal framework, moving beyond fragmented national approaches to create a more predictable and efficient environment for resolving high-stakes patent conflicts within the participating member states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文缺失，无法评估具体判例细节。需关注UPC早期判例如何确立程序规则及实体解释标准，这将对后续欧洲专利诉讼策略产生基础性影响。建议持续追踪该律所发布的完整分析以获取具体案例指引。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should closely monitor UPC precedents to assess litigation risks and opportunities effectively. The court’s rapid development of consistent legal principles offers a more streamlined procedural landscape compared to traditional multi-jurisdictional strategies. Rights holders can leverage these unified procedures to optimize their intellectual property risk management frameworks. Adapting to this centralized adjudication model allows for more efficient enforcement and defense mechanisms. Companies must adjust their strategic planning to align with the UPC’s authoritative interpretations, ensuring robust protection and compliance within the new unified patent ecosystem.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["原文摘要显示，Finnegan律师详述了UPC判例法的重大进展", "标题指出UPC成立仅三年，但在相关领域已处于领先地位"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC has secured a leadership role in European patent litigation through decisive case law within just three years of operation.", "Legal experts examine emerging judicial trends in infringement and invalidity cases, revealing the court's evolving interpretative approach.", "UPC jurisprudence is actively constructing unified standards for patent enforcement, reducing fragmentation in European IP rights protection."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC早期判例对欧洲专利诉讼具有风向标意义", "需等待全文发布以确认具体的法律解释倾向", "观察UPC司法实践是否如标题所言已形成领先范式"]

Legal experts examine emerging judicial trends in infringement and invalidity cases, revealing the court's evolving interpretative approach.

items.title: Local division Mannheim holds single day hearing for ZTE vs Samsung
items.title_zh: UPC 曼海姆分部单日审理中兴诉三星侵权及 FRAND 抗辩
items.title_en: Mannheim UPC Holds Single-Day Hearing on ZTE Infringement and Samsung FRAND Defense
items.summary: The local division Mannheim will hear ZTE's infringement claim and Samsung's FRAND defence in the dispute over 4G and 5G.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院曼海姆地方分院将单日审理中兴对三星的 4G/5G 专利侵权案及三星的 FRAND 抗辩。因三星涉案专利 EP4096288 恐被欧专局判定无效，法院已中止相关程序，仅聚焦中兴主张的 EP3905730。此前三星在英国申请临时许可及在德国反垄断诉讼均告失败，局势对其不利。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in Mannheim has condensed its hearing schedule to a single day, focusing exclusively on ZTE’s infringement claim regarding patent EP 3 905 730 and the parties’ FRAND compliance. This procedural shift follows an unfavorable preliminary opinion from the European Patent Office’s Opposition Division concerning Samsung’s patent EP 4 096 288, leading the court to provisionally suspend related proceedings. The dispute centers on global 4G and 5G licensing terms. Samsung faces mounting legal setbacks across multiple jurisdictions, including a failed application for an interim license in the UK and a dismissed antitrust action in Frankfurt. Consequently, the current hearing prioritizes resolving the validity and infringement issues of the remaining active patent while assessing whether both litigants have adhered to fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory negotiation standards.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧 SEP 诉讼中需重视专利稳定性与 FRAND 合规举证。面对 UPC 集中管辖，应利用欧专局异议程序削弱对手专利基础，同时做好多法域联动应对，避免单一市场失利引发连锁反应。
items.ai_insight_en: In complex SEP litigation before the UPC, patent stability remains a critical strategic lever. Rights holders should actively leverage EPO opposition proceedings to challenge the validity of opposing patents, potentially securing stays or favorable settlements. Simultaneously, demonstrating strict FRAND compliance is essential, as courts closely scrutinize negotiation conduct across all jurisdictions. Companies must adopt a coordinated multi-forum strategy, ensuring that defensive positions in one region do not inadvertently weaken their stance in another. Proactive management of parallel validity challenges and consistent licensing offers can mitigate the risk of cascading losses across different national courts and the unified system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["曼海姆分院压缩庭审至单日，专注中兴侵权主张与双方 FRAND 合规性", "三星涉案专利获欧专局初步负面意见，导致部分诉讼程序被暂时中止", "三星在多法域接连受挫，包括英国临时许可被拒及德国反垄断诉讼败诉"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Mannheim Local Division reduced the hearing to one day, focusing on ZTE's infringement claim and FRAND compliance assessments.", "Samsung's patent EP 4 096 288 received a negative preliminary opinion from the EPO, causing provisional suspension of those specific proceedings.", "Samsung continues to face adverse rulings in other jurisdictions, including failed UK interim license applications and German antitrust dismissals."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 EPO 异议程序动摇对手专利根基，争取 UPC 诉讼主动权", "强化全球 FRAND 谈判证据链，防范多法域诉讼连环败诉风险"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage EPO opposition outcomes to challenge patent validity and influence UPC stay decisions effectively.", "Practice note: Maintain rigorous documentation of FRAND negotiations to demonstrate compliance across all concurrent legal forums.", "What to monitor: Track cross-jurisdictional rulings on interim measures and antitrust defenses to prevent compounding legal risks."]
core_points_en: Mannheim Local Division reduced the hearing to one day, focusing on ZTE's infringement claim and FRAND compliance assessments.
Samsung's patent EP 4 096 288 received a negative preliminary opinion from the EPO, causing provisional suspension of those specific proceedings.
Samsung continues to face adverse rulings in other jurisdictions, including failed UK interim license applications and German antitrust dismissals.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage EPO opposition outcomes to challenge patent validity and influence UPC stay decisions effectively.
Practice note: Maintain rigorous documentation of FRAND negotiations to demonstrate compliance across all concurrent legal forums.
What to monitor: Track cross-jurisdictional rulings on interim measures and antitrust defenses to prevent compounding legal risks.

items.title: Bristows’ SnippITs - Under construction: Court of Appeal infers termination rights in Zaha Hadid dispute
items.title_zh: 上诉法院在扎哈·哈迪德案中推断终止权
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Infers Mutual Termination Rights in Zaha Hadid Trademark Dispute
items.summary: This post is the latest in the Bristows’ SnippITs series, which pulls together the key practical takeaways from recent court decisions for the tech sect...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院推翻高等法院判决，裁定在Zaha Hadid Ltd诉Foundation案中，若商标许可协议未明确期限，应推断双方拥有经合理通知即可终止协议的相互权利。法院采用两阶段解释逻辑：首先判断协议是永久还是不定期；若为不定期，则必然隐含双方均有权终止。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal has reversed a High Court decision in the trademark licence dispute between Zaha Hadid Limited and The Zaha Hadid Foundation. The appellate court inferred a mutual right to terminate the agreement on reasonable notice, applying established construction principles from Winter Garden Theatre. This ruling clarifies that where parties intend an agreement to be indefinite rather than perpetual, the law necessarily implies a right for either party to end the relationship with reasonable notice. The judgment corrects the previous view that express unilateral termination clauses automatically exclude implied mutual rights. This decision significantly impacts how indefinite trademark and technology licences are interpreted under English law, emphasizing the distinction between perpetual and indefinite contractual terms.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了不定期商标许可协议的默认终止规则，强调合同解释中“合理性”的重要性。实务中需审查现有许可协议是否明确约定了期限或终止机制，以避免因被认定为“不定期”而面临不可控的解约风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders drafting trademark or technology licences should carefully distinguish between perpetual and indefinite terms to avoid unintended termination risks. Ambiguous 'indefinite' clauses may trigger implied mutual termination rights under English law, potentially undermining long-term commercial stability. Parties should explicitly define termination mechanisms, including specific notice periods and conditions, to override default legal presumptions. Reviewing existing indefinite licences is advisable to assess exposure to sudden termination claims. Clear contractual drafting ensures that business expectations regarding duration and exit strategies are legally protected against judicial inference.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["上诉法院推翻原判，推断商标许可中存在经合理通知的相互终止权", "法院采用两阶段逻辑：先定性协议期限，再据此推断是否隐含终止权", "若协议意在无限期而非永久，则法律上必然要求双方拥有终止能力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal overturned the High Court, establishing that indefinite agreements implicitly contain mutual termination rights on reasonable notice.", "A two-stage construction logic was adopted to distinguish between perpetual intentions, which lack implied termination, and indefinite ones.", "Indefinite trademark or technology licences remain terminable by both parties via reasonable notice even when express unilateral termination clauses exist."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["不定期许可协议可能被法院自动植入合理通知终止条款，影响商业稳定性", "起草许可协议时应明确约定固定期限或具体终止条件，排除默示推断", "需关注该案对长期品牌合作架构中控制权与退出机制的法律影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Indefinite licence agreements carry inherent risks of implied mutual termination, affecting long-term asset control and revenue stability.", "Practice note: Draft explicit termination clauses with defined notice periods to prevent courts from inferring reasonable notice rights in indefinite contracts.", "What to monitor: Monitor how UK courts apply this two-stage construction logic to other intellectual property licences involving indefinite durations."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal overturned the High Court, establishing that indefinite agreements implicitly contain mutual termination rights on reasonable notice.
A two-stage construction logic was adopted to distinguish between perpetual intentions, which lack implied termination, and indefinite ones.
Indefinite trademark or technology licences remain terminable by both parties via reasonable notice even when express unilateral termination clauses exist.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Indefinite licence agreements carry inherent risks of implied mutual termination, affecting long-term asset control and revenue stability.
Practice note: Draft explicit termination clauses with defined notice periods to prevent courts from inferring reasonable notice rights in indefinite contracts.
What to monitor: Monitor how UK courts apply this two-stage construction logic to other intellectual property licences involving indefinite durations.

items.title: [Guest Post] Music to my ears: Copyright protection of iconic guitars and other works of applied art
items.title_zh: 悦耳之音：标志性吉他及其他实用艺术作品的版权保护
items.title_en: Copyright Protection for Iconic Guitars and Applied Art Under EU Law
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文仅显示标题与来源信息，正文缺失。标题表明文章将探讨标志性吉他等实用艺术作品在版权法下的保护问题，属于欧盟知识产权领域的理论或实务分析。
items.ai_summary_en: This article examines the threshold for copyright protection of applied art, such as iconic electric guitars, within the European Union legal framework. It analyzes the application of the 'author's own intellectual creation' standard to industrial design. The analysis indicates that if a design reflects the creator's free and original expression, it remains eligible for copyright protection even when serving a functional purpose. This interpretation has significant implications for both the music industry and the broader design sector, clarifying how aesthetic elements in functional objects are treated under current EU jurisprudence.
items.ai_insight_zh: 由于缺乏正文，无法评估具体法律观点或案例引用。需关注后续全文是否涉及Cofemel案后欧盟对实用艺术品原创性标准的统一适用趋势。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting instruments or design-led products to Europe should assess whether product aesthetics meet the EU standard for original expression. Establishing robust copyright compliance review mechanisms is essential to mitigate infringement risks associated with design imitation. Rights holders are advised to leverage unified EU protection rules by documenting creative processes early. Proactive evaluation of design originality helps secure rights and defend against potential disputes, ensuring alignment with evolving judicial interpretations regarding applied art and industrial design protections.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题显示文章主题涉及标志性吉他等实用艺术作品的版权保护资格", "来源为The IPKat，通常聚焦欧洲及英国知识产权法律动态与分析"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applied art requires evidence of the author's personality and free creative choices to qualify for copyright protection.", "The coexistence of functional utility and artistic features does not automatically exclude works from copyright scope.", "CJEU case law establishes a high threshold for originality, requiring distinct intellectual creation beyond mere technical function."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需观察原文是否分析欧盟法院关于实用艺术品版权保护的判例法演变", "需关注文章是否探讨工业设计产品获得版权保护的具体门槛与证据要求"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Exporters must evaluate if product designs satisfy EU originality standards to secure copyright eligibility.", "Practice note: Implement internal compliance reviews to document creative decisions and prevent infringement liabilities.", "What to monitor: Evolving CJEU precedents on the intersection of functional utility and artistic expression in applied art."]
core_points_en: Applied art requires evidence of the author's personality and free creative choices to qualify for copyright protection.
The coexistence of functional utility and artistic features does not automatically exclude works from copyright scope.
CJEU case law establishes a high threshold for originality, requiring distinct intellectual creation beyond mere technical function.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Exporters must evaluate if product designs satisfy EU originality standards to secure copyright eligibility.
Practice note: Implement internal compliance reviews to document creative decisions and prevent infringement liabilities.
What to monitor: Evolving CJEU precedents on the intersection of functional utility and artistic expression in applied art.

items.title: HSF Kramer hires patent litigator as partner in Germany
items.title_zh: HSF Kramer在德国聘请专利诉讼律师为合伙人
items.title_en: HSF Kramer Appoints Patent Litigator as Partner in Germany
items.summary: New recruit, who has worked on more than 150 infringement cases, will join the firm’s disputes practice in Düsseldorf and strengthen its UPC offering.
items.ai_summary_zh: HSF Kramer律师事务所宣布新聘一名专利诉讼律师为合伙人。该律师曾处理超过150起侵权案件，将加入杜塞尔多夫争议解决团队，旨在加强事务所在统一专利法院（UPC）方面的服务能力。
items.ai_summary_en: HSF Kramer has appointed a new partner to its disputes practice in Düsseldorf, Germany. The recruit brings experience from more than 150 infringement cases. This addition aims to strengthen the firm’s capabilities before the Unified Patent Court. The move reflects ongoing adjustments by legal practices in key European jurisdictions to address patent litigation demands. The appointment underscores the firm's focus on expanding its dispute resolution team in a major German legal hub.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。

For rights holders, it signals continued capacity building in Düsseldorf. Observers should track case assignments to assess how new hires integrate into UPC proceedings. The move aligns with broader trends of enhancing local presence for pan-European patent enforcement.

items.title: [Blog] Plainte UDRP : Lancement d'une procédure accélérée et réduction des frais pour les retraits anticipés
items.title_zh: UDRP投诉：启动加速程序及提前撤诉减费
items.title_en: WIPO Updates UDRP Rules: New Expedited Process for Urgent Cases and Reduced Fees for Early Withdrawals
items.summary: L'OMPI vient d'annoncer deux évolutions importantes concernant la procédure UDRP : un traitement prioritaire des cas urgents et une réduction des frais en cas de clôture anticipée.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权组织（WIPO）宣布两项UDRP程序重要变更：一是针对紧急案件推出优先处理服务，承诺一个月内作出裁决；二是引入提前撤诉部分退费政策，若在正式通知前撤诉仅收100美元行政费。此举旨在平衡效率与公平，适应DNS现状。
items.ai_summary_en: The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) has announced two significant updates to the Uniform Domain-Name Dispute-Resolution Policy (UDRP) procedure. First, a new expedited service is available for urgent cases without complex factual issues, guaranteeing a decision within one month instead of the standard two-month average. This service costs 4,000 USD for one to five domain names held by the same registrant. Second, WIPO introduced a partial refund policy for complainants who withdraw their complaints before formal notification of the dispute. In such instances, administrative fees are reduced to 100 USD, compared to 500 USD if withdrawal occurs after notification. These measures aim to balance efficiency and fairness while adapting to current DNS realities. An updated complaint template and simplified payment options via credit card or WIPO account have also been introduced to streamline case preparation and processing.
items.ai_insight_zh: UDRP程序优化提升了商标维权的时效性与成本可控性。欧洲企业应关注加速程序对紧急侵权的响应价值，以及早期和解或撤诉的成本优势，需结合具体案情评估是否适用优先服务。
items.ai_insight_en: These procedural adjustments offer trademark owners more flexible tools for domain enforcement. The expedited track allows rights holders to address urgent cybersquatting incidents rapidly, minimizing potential business disruption. Conversely, the reduced fee structure for early withdrawals encourages parties to reassess claims promptly upon discovering legitimate defenses, such as existing licenses or personal name usage. This helps avoid unnecessary litigation costs when disputes can be resolved amicably or when the respondent has valid rights. Practitioners should evaluate the urgency and merit of each case carefully to leverage these new options effectively, ensuring cost-efficient resolution strategies aligned with specific enforcement goals.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["WIPO推出UDRP优先服务，无复杂案件确保一月内裁决，费用4000美元涵盖1至5个域名", "若投诉人在正式通知争议前撤回投诉，WIPO仅收取100美元行政费，此前标准为500美元", "新措施旨在适应DNS现实并满足当事人期望，同时更新了投诉书模板以简化准备流程"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["New expedited UDRP service guarantees decisions within one month for urgent, non-complex cases at a fixed fee of 4,000 USD.", "Partial refund policy reduces administrative fees to 100 USD if complainants withdraw before formal notification of the dispute.", "Updated complaint templates and simplified payment methods are introduced to enhance procedural efficiency and reduce delays."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["加速程序为时间敏感型商标争议提供新选项，但需评估案件复杂度是否符合条件", "提前撤诉降费机制鼓励当事人在程序早期解决误会或达成和解，降低维权试错成本", "需持续观察WIPO对新程序的实际执行效率及注册局配合程度对裁决周期的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize the expedited process for urgent infringement cases to secure faster decisions and mitigate business impact.", "Practice note: Assess case merits early; withdraw before formal notification if legitimate defenses exist to minimize administrative costs.", "What to monitor: Track WIPO implementation details and response times from registrars to optimize strategy under the new framework."]
core_points_en: New expedited UDRP service guarantees decisions within one month for urgent, non-complex cases at a fixed fee of 4,000 USD.
Partial refund policy reduces administrative fees to 100 USD if complainants withdraw before formal notification of the dispute.
Updated complaint templates and simplified payment methods are introduced to enhance procedural efficiency and reduce delays.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize the expedited process for urgent infringement cases to secure faster decisions and mitigate business impact.
Practice note: Assess case merits early; withdraw before formal notification if legitimate defenses exist to minimize administrative costs.
What to monitor: Track WIPO implementation details and response times from registrars to optimize strategy under the new framework.

items.title: Detecting Relative Movement Between A User's Face And An Augmented Reality Element: Non-technical
items.title_zh: 检测用户面部与AR元素相对运动：属非技术特征
items.title_en: EPO Board Rules AR Face-Tracking Interface Non-Technical Information Presentation
items.summary: The application underlying the discussed decision concerns a method for controlling an augmented reality (AR) user interface by detecting a user's face and its movement relative to AR elements displayed on a device screen.
items.ai_summary_zh: EPO技术上诉委员会在T 0971/24号决定中认定，检测用户面部与增强现实（AR）元素的相对运动并据此修改展示，仅涉及信息呈现，属于非技术特征。该决定重申控制GUI上AR对象展示不构成设备层面的“技术任务”，因此缺乏创造性步骤，维持审查部驳回申请的决定。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines EPO Technical Board of Appeal decision T 0971/24, which determined that a method for controlling an augmented reality interface by detecting relative movement between a user's face and AR elements constitutes the presentation of information as such. Consequently, the features lack technical character. Although the system employs hardware components like cameras for tracking, the core innovation resides in GUI interaction logic rather than technical processing. The Board concluded that these features do not contribute to an inventive step, leading to the refusal of the patent application due to lack of inventiveness under established case law regarding non-technical subject matter.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了AR交互中单纯基于视觉反馈的界面控制在EPO体系下难以获得专利保护。企业需注意将UI逻辑与底层硬件控制或具体技术问题解决相结合，以证明技术贡献，避免仅因信息呈现方式而被认定为非技术方案。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders developing augmented reality and human-computer interaction technologies, this decision highlights the risk of pure interaction logic being excluded from patentability. To enhance examination outcomes, applicants should emphasize underlying technical implementations, such as data processing algorithms, sensor fusion techniques, or specific hardware control mechanisms. Claims must demonstrate that the solution resolves a concrete technical problem, rather than merely providing aesthetic improvements or operational convenience through visual presentation rules.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["检测面部与AR元素相对运动及修改展示，被视为信息呈现本身，属非技术特征", "控制GUI上AR对象的展示不构成既定判例法意义上设备执行的“技术任务”", "技术上诉委员会T 0971/24号决定于2025年11月5日作出，维持驳回SNAP公司…"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Detecting relative facial movement and modifying AR element presentation is classified as non-technical information presentation.", "Controlling the display of AR objects within a graphical user interface does not constitute a technical task under EU case law.", "Even with hardware sensors, schemes focusing solely on human-computer interaction rules remain difficult to patent."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["纯GUI交互逻辑若无具体技术效果支撑，在EPO审查中极易被认定为非技术特征", "AR专利申请需着重论证界面控制如何解决具体技术问题，而非仅提升用户体验", "后续应关注EPO对涉及传感器数据处理的AR应用是否给予更宽泛的技术性认定"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: AR interface patents risk rejection if claims focus only on visual logic rather than technical implementation.", "Practice note: Draft specifications to highlight sensor fusion, data processing, and hardware control effects over GUI rules.", "What to monitor: EPO trends on distinguishing technical contributions from mere information presentation in UI inventions."]
core_points_en: Detecting relative facial movement and modifying AR element presentation is classified as non-technical information presentation.
Controlling the display of AR objects within a graphical user interface does not constitute a technical task under EU case law.
Even with hardware sensors, schemes focusing solely on human-computer interaction rules remain difficult to patent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: AR interface patents risk rejection if claims focus only on visual logic rather than technical implementation.
Practice note: Draft specifications to highlight sensor fusion, data processing, and hardware control effects over GUI rules.
What to monitor: EPO trends on distinguishing technical contributions from mere information presentation in UI inventions.

items.title: A German Court Rules That AI-generated Designs Do Not Benefit From Copyright Protection
items.title_zh: 德法院裁定AI生成设计因缺乏人类创作投入不享版权
items.title_en: German Court Rules AI-Generated Logos Lack Human Creativity for Copyright
items.summary: A Munich court has held that three AI‑generated logos lacked the required level of human creative input to qualify for copyright protection. Although prompts were used, the judges found that the AI's technical...
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院裁定，原告利用AI生成的三个标志因缺乏足够的人类创造性投入，不符合欧盟及英国版权法要求的原创性标准。尽管原告使用了提示词，但法院认为AI的技术贡献超过了原告的创意选择，导致作品无法体现作者个性，因此不构成受版权保护的作品。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich District Court recently ruled that three logos generated by artificial intelligence do not qualify for copyright protection due to insufficient human creative input. Although the claimant utilized prompts and iterative adjustments, the court determined that the technical contribution of the AI outweighed the human's creative choices. Consequently, the designs failed to reflect the author's personality, a core requirement for originality under EU and UK copyright law. This decision reaffirms that for a work to be protected, a human author must retain sufficient control over the creation process to ensure their personal intellectual imprint is expressed in the final output.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了在欧盟法域下，AI辅助创作获得版权保护的关键在于证明人类保留了充分的控制权并使个性得以表达。单纯提供提示词被视为技术贡献主导，难以满足原创性要求。企业需关注人机协作中人类创意介入的深度与可证明性，以评估知识产权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For entities utilizing AI-assisted design tools within European jurisdictions, it is critical to ensure substantial human creative intervention in the final output. Merely relying on prompt iteration may not satisfy the legal threshold for authorship. Rights holders should maintain detailed records of design modifications and human decision-making processes to establish a clear chain of evidence regarding creative control. This documentation is essential for mitigating risks associated with copyright ownership disputes and preventing assets from being classified as unprotected public domain works due to lack of identifiable human authorship.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["慕尼黑法院认定AI生成标志缺乏必要的人类创造性投入，不具版权保护资格", "法院判定AI的技术贡献超过原告创意选择，作品未体现作者个性故非原创", "仅使用提示词不足以确立版权，关键在于人类是否保留对创作的充分控制"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Munich Court finds AI-generated logos lack necessary human creative input for copyright eligibility.", "Using prompts alone does not sufficiently demonstrate human control or expression of personality in the work.", "EU copyright protection requires works to embody the specific creative choices and personality of a human author."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟版权保护仍以“人类作者”为核心，AI技术贡献过大将削弱版权主张", "提示词的详细程度并非决定性因素，关键在于人类对最终成果的控制力", "需持续观察后续案件对人类在AI生成过程中“创造性选择”认定的具体标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Entities using AI for design must ensure significant human creative control over final outputs to secure copyright.", "Practice note: Maintain comprehensive records of design iterations and human decisions to prove authorship and creative input.", "What to monitor: Monitor evolving case law regarding the threshold of human intervention required for AI-assisted works in Europe."]
core_points_en: Munich Court finds AI-generated logos lack necessary human creative input for copyright eligibility.
Using prompts alone does not sufficiently demonstrate human control or expression of personality in the work.
EU copyright protection requires works to embody the specific creative choices and personality of a human author.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Entities using AI for design must ensure significant human creative control over final outputs to secure copyright.
Practice note: Maintain comprehensive records of design iterations and human decisions to prove authorship and creative input.
What to monitor: Monitor evolving case law regarding the threshold of human intervention required for AI-assisted works in Europe.

items.title: Like Company v Google: CJEU Holds First-Ever Hearing on Generative AI and Copyright on 10 March 2026
items.title_zh: Like Company诉谷歌：欧盟法院举行生成式AI版权案首次听证
items.title_en: CJEU Holds First Hearing on Generative AI and Copyright in Like Company v Google
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月10日，欧盟法院在Like Company诉谷歌爱尔兰公司案中举行首次关于生成式AI与版权的口头听证。匈牙利媒体公司指控谷歌Gemini系统未经授权提取新闻内容并用于训练大语言模型。布达佩斯地区法院提请欧盟法院裁决四个初步问题，重点探讨欧盟版权法的域外适用范围及文本数据挖掘例外情形。
items.ai_summary_en: On 10 March 2026, the Court of Justice of the European Union held its first oral hearing addressing the intersection of generative artificial intelligence and EU copyright law in Like Company v Google Ireland Limited (C-250/25). The dispute centers on allegations that Google’s Gemini system extracted protected news content for training large language models without authorization. Key legal issues include the applicability of exceptions for press publishers’ rights and text and data mining under the Digital Single Market Directive. The hearing highlighted significant divergences among Member States and the European Commission regarding the territorial scope and extraterritorial application of EU copyright rules to AI providers operating within or targeting the EU market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案作为欧盟法院首例生成式AI版权听证，其裁决将界定AI训练数据使用的法律边界及欧盟版权域外效力。需密切关注法院对DSM指令例外条款的解释，这将直接影响AI提供商在欧运营的合规框架及责任认定标准。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing or deploying generative AI services must carefully assess compliance with EU copyright frameworks, particularly concerning the sourcing of training datasets. Rights holders should evaluate the legality of data extraction practices and consider implementing robust filtering mechanisms to mitigate infringement risks. Given the potential for broad territorial implications, stakeholders need to anticipate how the upcoming judgment may influence cross-border operations. Proactive adjustment of product compliance strategies and terms of service is advisable to align with evolving judicial interpretations of copyright exceptions in the context of artificial intelligence technologies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院审理Like Company诉谷歌案，聚焦生成式AI提取新闻内容及训练模型的版…", "听证会揭示成员国、欧盟委员会及当事方在欧盟版权法域外适用等核心问题上存在显著分歧", "争议焦点包括是否适用数字单一市场指令中新闻出版者权利例外及文本数据挖掘例外条款"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU addresses the copyright qualification of training data and output content generated by artificial intelligence systems for the first time.", "Disputes focus on whether text and data mining exceptions and press publishers’ rights apply to generative AI development scenarios.", "Member States and the European Commission show significant divergence on the extraterritorial application of EU copyright law."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟法院对生成式AI训练数据使用及版权例外的解释将确立行业合规基准", "成员国关于版权法域外适用的分歧可能影响跨境AI服务的法律责任范围", "需持续跟踪最终判决对数字单一市场指令第15条及第4条的具体适用指引"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: AI developers must review training datasets to avoid unauthorized use of protected EU news content and ensure regulatory compliance.", "Practice note: Establish strict copyright filtering mechanisms and reassess the legitimacy of data sources to mitigate litigation exposure.", "What to monitor: Anticipate the final judgment to adjust product compliance strategies and liability clauses for the EU market."]
core_points_en: The CJEU addresses the copyright qualification of training data and output content generated by artificial intelligence systems for the first time.
Disputes focus on whether text and data mining exceptions and press publishers’ rights apply to generative AI development scenarios.
Member States and the European Commission show significant divergence on the extraterritorial application of EU copyright law.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: AI developers must review training datasets to avoid unauthorized use of protected EU news content and ensure regulatory compliance.
Practice note: Establish strict copyright filtering mechanisms and reassess the legitimacy of data sources to mitigate litigation exposure.
What to monitor: Anticipate the final judgment to adjust product compliance strategies and liability clauses for the EU market.

items.title: CAYENNE / LITTLE CAYENNE: een rem op de reikwijdte van de reputatie van een merk
items.title_zh: CAYENNE/LITTLE CAYENNE：限制商标声誉保护范围
items.title_en: CAYENNE / LITTLE CAYENNE: een rem op de reikwijdte van de reputatie van een merk
items.summary: De bescherming van bekende merken kan zeer ver reiken. Merkhouders kunnen zich immers niet alleen verzetten tegen gebruik van identieke of overeenstemmende tekens voor identieke...
items.ai_summary_zh: 保时捷反对“LITTLE CAYENNE”在服装、食品及餐饮服务的商标注册，主张其“CAYENNE”商标声誉。法国法院裁定，尽管该商标在汽车领域知名，但未能证明公众会在非类似商品上建立具体联系，故驳回其跨类保护主张。此案强调声誉不自动延伸至所有类别，需证明具体关联。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了驰名商标跨类保护的举证门槛：仅证明知名度不足，须证实公众在非类似商品上产生具体联想。对于在多元领域运营的品牌，需关注法院对“联系”认定的严格标准，以及在比荷卢等适用相似规则法域的潜在影响。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法国法院裁定保时捷未能证明公众将“LITTLE CAYENNE”与其汽车商标建立具体联系", "商标声誉保护不自动延伸至所有类别，必须证明相关公众存在明确联想", "该判决原则同样适用于受欧盟商标条例及比荷卢知识产权公约管辖的地区"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["驰名商标持有人需注意，跨类保护并非自动获得，必须提供证据证明公众联想", "法院在评估非类似商品上的混淆或搭便车风险时，对关联性证据要求严格", "需持续观察此类判决在比荷卢及欧盟其他成员国法院的后续引用与适用情况"]

items.title: Auteursrecht op verpakkingen: wat Benelux-bedrijven kunnen leren van een champagnezaak
items.title_zh: 包装版权归属：比荷卢企业可从香槟案中学到什么
items.title_en: French Court Rules on Copyright for Champagne Packaging: No Implied Licenses or Accessory Use
items.summary: De zaak betrof een kunstwerk dat werd gebruikt op champagne-etiketten, op de verpakking van de flessen en in promotiemateriaal...
items.ai_summary_zh: 杜埃上诉法院在一项涉及香槟标签艺术作品的判决中强调，使用创意作品需有明确版权协议。法院驳回了“附属理论”抗辩，认定该作品是产品视觉识别的核心而非装饰性附属。同时，法院拒绝承认默示权利转让，指出版权利用权原则上必须通过合同或明确情境进行显式转让，仅凭使用事实不足以证明授权。
items.ai_summary_en: The Douai Court of Appeal recently ruled that a champagne producer infringed copyright by using an artwork on labels and packaging without explicit permission. The court rejected the defendant's 'accessory theory' defense, determining the work was central to the product's visual identity rather than mere decoration. It also emphasized that copyright transfers must be express, refusing to recognize implied licenses based on commercial context. This decision clarifies strict standards for rights acquisition in commercial use, highlighting that selling or displaying a work does not automatically grant exploitation rights. Rights holders and companies must ensure clear, written agreements cover all intended uses to avoid infringement claims and potential liability for unauthorized exploitation of creative works in marketing materials.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了商业包装中艺术元素的版权边界，否定了默示许可与附属使用的抗辩空间。企业在整合创意设计时，应关注权利获取的书面证据完整性，避免因权属不清引发侵权风险。对于依赖视觉营销的行业，需重新审视供应商合同中的权利转让条款，确保商业使用的合法性基础稳固。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants designing packaging for the European market should not assume that purchasing a physical artwork grants commercial usage rights. It is essential to sign written copyright assignment or licensing agreements explicitly covering all channels, including packaging and advertising. Failure to secure proper rights exposes companies to high compensation claims and product removal risks. Comprehensive review of the copyright chain for packaging designs is necessary to ensure coverage across all commercial application scenarios, preventing legal disputes over unauthorized use of protected creative elements in branding strategies.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院驳回附属理论，认定艺术作品构成产品视觉识别与营销的核心元素", "法院拒绝默示权利转让主张，强调版权利用权须基于明确合同或意图转让", "未经明确许可在包装及推广材料中使用艺术作品构成侵权，需承担法律责任"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Court rejected accessory theory, ruling packaging art is a core commercial element rather than minor decoration.", "Reaffirmed principle that copyright transfer requires explicit agreement, denying implied authorization from transaction context.", "Warns companies must obtain clear copyright license agreements beforehand for product packaging and marketing campaigns."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["商业包装中的艺术设计通常被视为核心营销元素，难以适用附属使用抗辩", "版权利用权的转让不能仅凭交易背景推断，必须保留明确的书面授权证据", "需持续监控法国及比荷卢地区关于默示许可认定的司法实践变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Do not rely on oral agreements or implied licenses; execute written copyright authorization documents.", "Practice note: Conduct comprehensive reviews of packaging design copyright chains to ensure full commercial coverage.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends regarding implied licenses and accessory use defenses in EU packaging copyright disputes."]
core_points_en: Court rejected accessory theory, ruling packaging art is a core commercial element rather than minor decoration.
Reaffirmed principle that copyright transfer requires explicit agreement, denying implied authorization from transaction context.
Warns companies must obtain clear copyright license agreements beforehand for product packaging and marketing campaigns.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Do not rely on oral agreements or implied licenses; execute written copyright authorization documents.
Practice note: Conduct comprehensive reviews of packaging design copyright chains to ensure full commercial coverage.
What to monitor: Judicial trends regarding implied licenses and accessory use defenses in EU packaging copyright disputes.

items.title: Are other UPC countries growing fed up with simply exporting cases to the UMC?
items.title_zh: 其他UPC成员国是否厌倦向慕尼黑出口案件？
items.title_en: Is the Unified Patent Court Becoming the Unified Munich Court Amid Case Concentration?
items.summary: Six months after the Unified Patent Court ("UPC") began its operations, we published an entry in this blog entitled The UPC is dead. Long live the UMC!; "UMC" standing for Unified Munich Court.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出统一专利法院运行半年后，慕尼黑及德国各地方法院仍吸引绝大多数案件，被戏称为“统一慕尼黑法院”。作者批评德国分院合议庭数量膨胀可能违反协议上限，并质疑在未获英国批准的情况下适用特权豁免议定书的合法性，建议欧盟法院介入解释相关法律文本含义。
items.ai_summary_en: Six months into its operations, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) faces criticism for disproportionate case concentration in Germany, particularly Munich, leading to the moniker 'Unified Munich Court.' The author highlights that German Local Divisions continue to attract the vast majority of litigation, expanding panel numbers beyond the Agreement’s four-division limit per member state. Furthermore, the commentary questions the legality of applying key protocols, such as those on provisional application and privileges, which technically require UK ratification. This situation raises concerns under the principle that no one should be a judge in their own cause, suggesting potential need for Court of Justice of the European Union intervention to clarify treaty interpretation and ensure procedural legitimacy across all contracting states.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC内部案件分配失衡引发的政治与法律张力，特别是非德国成员国对司法资源集中的不满。同时，关于特权豁免议定书适用合法性的争议若升级，可能触发欧盟法院的解释程序，影响法院运作基础。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should anticipate continued litigation concentration in Germany, necessitating robust local legal resources and strategic preparedness for proceedings before the Munich Local Division. Simultaneously, stakeholders must monitor ongoing debates regarding the procedural legitimacy of certain UPC operational protocols. Potential challenges or referrals to the Court of Justice of the European Union could impact jurisdictional stability and the enforceability of future decisions. Rights holders and defendants alike should remain agile, ready to adjust enforcement or defense strategies in response to any judicial clarifications concerning the foundational legal framework of the Unified Patent Court system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国各地方法院持续吸引绝大多数UPC案件，导致其他地区案件匮乏", "慕尼黑分院合议庭数量增至六个，涉嫌超出统一专利法院协议规定的上限", "作者质疑在缺乏英国批准情况下适用特权豁免议定书的法律效力及正当性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["German Local Divisions, especially Munich, dominate UPC caseloads, effectively centralizing litigation despite broader jurisdictional aims.", "Expansion of Munich panels allegedly violates Article 7(4) of the UPC Agreement, which caps local divisions at four per member state.", "Application of protocols requiring UK ratification is legally questionable, potentially necessitating CJEU clarification on treaty validity."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["观察非德国成员国是否采取法律或政治行动应对案件外流至慕尼黑", "监测关于特权豁免议定书适用合法性的争议是否会正式提交欧盟法院裁决", "关注UPC为应对案件激增而在程序规则或合议庭设置上的后续调整动态"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Litigation risk remains heavily skewed toward Germany; companies must strengthen local counsel networks and prepare specific defense strategies for the Munich ve…", "Practice note: Monitor developments regarding the legal validity of applied protocols, as procedural irregularities could affect case management and outcome predictability.", "What to monitor: Watch for potential referrals to the CJEU concerning treaty interpretation, which may reshape jurisdictional boundaries and ruling enforceability."]
core_points_en: German Local Divisions, especially Munich, dominate UPC caseloads, effectively centralizing litigation despite broader jurisdictional aims.
Expansion of Munich panels allegedly violates Article 7(4) of the UPC Agreement, which caps local divisions at four per member state.
Application of protocols requiring UK ratification is legally questionable, potentially necessitating CJEU clarification on treaty validity.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Litigation risk remains heavily skewed toward Germany; companies must strengthen local counsel networks and prepare specific defense strategies for the Munich ve…
Practice note: Monitor developments regarding the legal validity of applied protocols, as procedural irregularities could affect case management and outcome predictability.
What to monitor: Watch for potential referrals to the CJEU concerning treaty interpretation, which may reshape jurisdictional boundaries and ruling enforceability.

items.title: ‘A new approach’: how UPC opened the door to litigation insurance
items.title_zh: 新路径：UPC如何开启诉讼保险之门
items.title_en: UPC Appeal Ruling on Costs Opens Door for Litigation Insurance and Finance Growth
items.summary: Henrik Holzapfel and Chuck Larsen of McDermott Will & Schulte explain why a Court of Appeal ruling could promote access to justice and present a growth opportunity for litigation finance
items.ai_summary_zh: McDermott Will & Schulte律师分析指出，统一专利法院（UPC）的一项上诉裁决可能促进司法公正，并为诉讼融资领域带来增长机遇。该裁决被视为开启了诉讼保险在UPC体系下的应用大门，改变了以往对诉讼成本风险的评估逻辑。
items.ai_summary_en: Henrik Holzapfel and Chuck Larsen of McDermott Will & Schulte analyze a recent Court of Appeal ruling that significantly alters cost allocation rules within the Unified Patent Court system. By reducing the financial risk for plaintiffs who might otherwise bear full opponent legal fees upon losing, this decision enhances the viability of third-party litigation funding and insurance products. This judicial development not only promotes greater access to justice by balancing resource disparities but also creates substantial growth opportunities for the European patent litigation finance market. The analysis highlights how these procedural shifts facilitate broader participation in patent disputes, allowing rights holders to manage financial exposure more effectively while encouraging the expansion of specialized insurance solutions tailored to complex intellectual property conflicts.
items.ai_insight_zh: UPC裁决正逐步重塑欧洲专利诉讼的成本分担机制。诉讼保险与融资工具的引入可能改变原告与被告的风险博弈结构。实务中需持续观察保险公司对该类案件的承保标准及费率模型演变，以及其对和解策略的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants involved in European patent disputes should evaluate the integration of litigation finance tools to mitigate high cash flow pressures associated with cross-border enforcement. Rights holders are advised to reassess their litigation risk models, incorporating insurance mechanisms as a strategic component to hedge against substantial adverse cost orders. Monitoring the evolution of UPC case law regarding cost allocations is essential for optimizing overall enforcement and defense strategies. By leveraging these financial instruments, companies can better manage the economic uncertainties of patent litigation, ensuring sustainable access to justice regardless of their size or capital reserves.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院的一项裁决为诉讼保险进入专利诉讼领域提供了新契机", "该裁决被认为有助于促进司法公正，降低当事人参与诉讼的门槛", "此法律动态为诉讼融资市场带来了潜在的业务增长机会"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal optimized cost-sharing mechanisms, significantly lowering the financial risks faced by plaintiffs in patent disputes.", "This new precedent removes barriers for third-party litigation funding and insurance products entering the European patent sector.", "The ruling aims to balance litigation resources, thereby improving small and medium enterprises' access to judicial remedies."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC判例正在推动诉讼金融工具在欧洲专利争议中的制度化应用", "需监测诉讼保险承保范围是否覆盖UPC特有的程序风险及费用规则", "观察后续案件中，拥有诉讼保险支持的一方是否在程序推进上更具主动性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize emerging financing tools to alleviate the significant capital burden of patent litigation in Europe.", "Practice note: Reassess litigation risk frameworks by integrating insurance options into comprehensive intellectual property strategies.", "What to monitor: Track developments in UPC jurisprudence concerning cost rulings to refine financial hedging approaches."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal optimized cost-sharing mechanisms, significantly lowering the financial risks faced by plaintiffs in patent disputes.
This new precedent removes barriers for third-party litigation funding and insurance products entering the European patent sector.
The ruling aims to balance litigation resources, thereby improving small and medium enterprises' access to judicial remedies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize emerging financing tools to alleviate the significant capital burden of patent litigation in Europe.
Practice note: Reassess litigation risk frameworks by integrating insurance options into comprehensive intellectual property strategies.
What to monitor: Track developments in UPC jurisprudence concerning cost rulings to refine financial hedging approaches.

items.title: Another blow for Ona Patents as German Federal Patent Court revokes patent
items.title_zh: 德国联邦专利法院撤销专利，Ona Patents再受打击
items.title_en: German Federal Patent Court Revokes EP 1 354 491, Undermining Ona Patents' Damages Claim
items.summary: At the centre of the national dispute lies EP 1 354 491. Finnish wireless solutions provider Ekahau originally filed the patent. Although the patent has already expired, the parties are fighting over damages related to infringement at Munich Regional Court (case ID: 7 O 3152/24). In the parallel nullity proceedings, the Federal Patent Court has […] The post Another blow for Ona Patents as German Federal Patent Court revokes patent appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 争议核心为欧洲专利EP 1 354 491，原由芬兰Ekahau公司申请。尽管该专利已过期，双方仍在慕尼黑地区法院就侵权损害赔偿进行诉讼。在并行的无效程序中，德国联邦专利法院已撤销该专利，这对Ona Patents构成新的打击。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Federal Patent Court has revoked European patent EP 1 354 491 in parallel nullity proceedings. Originally filed by Finnish wireless solutions provider Ekahau, the patent has already expired. Despite its expiration, a dispute regarding past infringement damages remains active before the Munich Regional Court (case ID: 7 O 3152/24). The revocation decision significantly impacts Ona Patents, the current rights holder asserting claims. By invalidating the patent retroactively, the court's ruling threatens to dismantle the legal basis for the ongoing damages claim. This development highlights the critical interplay between validity challenges and infringement litigation in Germany, where successful nullity actions can effectively neutralize financial exposure even after a patent term has concluded.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案显示即使欧洲专利已过期，权利人仍可就既往侵权行为主张损害赔偿。平行无效程序的结果将直接影响侵权诉讼的基础，需关注德国法院在专利失效后对赔偿责任的认定逻辑。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates the strategic importance of initiating nullity proceedings in Germany, even against expired patents. Rights holders and defendants must recognize that a successful invalidity action can block retrospective damage claims, which often constitute the primary financial risk in post-expiry litigation. Practitioners should coordinate infringement defense strategies with parallel validity challenges to maximize leverage. Monitoring the outcome of such dual-track proceedings is essential for assessing litigation risks and potential liabilities in the European market, ensuring that expired IP rights do not unjustly sustain high-value compensation demands.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国联邦专利法院在无效程序中撤销了欧洲专利EP 1 354 491", "尽管专利已过期，当事人仍在慕尼黑地区法院就过往侵权损害赔偿进行诉讼", "该专利最初由芬兰无线解决方案提供商Ekahau申请，现涉及Ona Patents"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The German Federal Patent Court ruled to revoke European patent EP 1 354 491 during parallel nullity proceedings.", "Although the patent has expired, the Munich Regional Court continues to hear the case concerning past infringement damages.", "The invalidation ruling severely weakens Ona Patents' legal foundation for pursuing retrospective compensation claims."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["专利过期不必然免除既往侵权行为的损害赔偿责任，诉讼风险依然存续", "德国联邦专利法院的无效裁决将对慕尼黑地区法院的损害赔偿审理产生关键影响", "需持续观察Ona Patents在专利被撤销后的后续法律应对及赔偿诉求变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Successful nullity actions can eliminate liability for past infringement damages, even if the patent has expired.", "Practice note: Coordinate infringement defense with parallel validity challenges to effectively neutralize retrospective compensation claims.", "What to monitor: Outcomes of dual-track proceedings where expired patents are challenged to assess ongoing litigation risks."]
core_points_en: The German Federal Patent Court ruled to revoke European patent EP 1 354 491 during parallel nullity proceedings.
Although the patent has expired, the Munich Regional Court continues to hear the case concerning past infringement damages.
The invalidation ruling severely weakens Ona Patents' legal foundation for pursuing retrospective compensation claims.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Successful nullity actions can eliminate liability for past infringement damages, even if the patent has expired.
Practice note: Coordinate infringement defense with parallel validity challenges to effectively neutralize retrospective compensation claims.
What to monitor: Outcomes of dual-track proceedings where expired patents are challenged to assess ongoing litigation risks.

items.title: Carpmaels in Conversation: Ep. 40 - Sour grapes and spilled milk: when brands collide with protected terms - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 品牌与受保护术语冲突：香槟PDO与燕麦奶标语案解析
items.title_en: Brand Marketing vs Protected Terms: Analyzing Champagne PDO and Oatly Food Labeling Conflicts
items.summary: In the latest episode of Carpmaels in Conversation, Lara Elder and Louisa Blair discuss how brands navigate the legal boundaries around protected term. Reflecting on Della Vite’s “Cheat on Champagne” campaign, they explore why Champagne, a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), is so robustly protected, how PDOs differ from trade marks, and what these rules […]
items.ai_summary_zh: Carpmaels & Ransford律所播客探讨品牌如何规避受保护术语的法律边界。重点分析Della Vite“背叛香槟”营销活动为何触犯香槟原产地名称保护，以及英国最高法院裁定Oatly“后牛奶一代”标语违反食品标签保留术语规定的案例，阐释PDO与商标的区别及对营销的影响。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the legal boundaries between brand marketing strategies and protected geographical terms. Using Della Vite’s “Cheat on Champagne” campaign as a primary example, it elucidates the robust protection mechanisms afforded to Protected Designations of Origin (PDO) and highlights the fundamental differences between PDOs and standard trade marks. Furthermore, by referencing the UK Supreme Court ruling against Oatly’s “Post Milk Generation” slogan, the discussion reveals how food labeling regulations restrict creative advertising when they clash with reserved dairy terminology. The content underscores the compliance risks market participants face when promotional activities infringe upon these specific regulatory frameworks, emphasizing the need for careful navigation of intellectual property and food law intersections.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需关注欧盟PDO制度与英国食品标签法的交叉影响。品牌在跨境营销时，除商标检索外，必须筛查广告语是否触碰地理标志或法定保留术语红线，尤其是食品饮料行业。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants promoting agricultural or food products in Europe must rigorously screen marketing copy to avoid infringing on Protected Designations of Origin or Protected Geographical Indications. Establishing a proactive compliance review mechanism is essential to prevent infringement litigation or administrative penalties arising from creative slogans. Companies should ensure their advertising strategies align with EU geographical indication registries and food labeling laws to maintain safe market access and mitigate regulatory risks associated with protected term usage.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["香槟作为受保护原产地名称（PDO），其法律保护力度极强，品牌营销需谨慎避开此类术语", "英国最高法院裁定Oatly标语违反食品标签法中关于保留术语的规定，显示非酒类领域同样存…", "文章对比了PDO与商标的法律差异，指出创意营销需在受保护术语的法律边界内运作"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Protected Designations of Origin benefit from stricter legal protection mechanisms compared to ordinary registered trade marks.", "Creative marketing campaigns must not conflict with reserved terms defined under applicable food labeling and advertising regulations.", "Rights holders must clearly distinguish the legal boundaries between geographical indications and standard trade mark rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["品牌营销需建立受保护术语（如PDO/PGI）筛查机制，避免侵权风险", "关注英国最高法院对食品标签保留术语的解释趋势，评估对广告合规的影响", "持续监测欧盟及成员国对地理标志保护的执法动态，特别是针对暗示性使用的认定标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure marketing materials do not utilize terms reserved for EU geographical indications or protected food categories.", "Practice note: Implement a pre-clearance intellectual property compliance review process for all creative advertising campaigns.", "What to monitor: Updates to the EU register of protected geographical indications and relevant national food labeling enforcement actions."]
core_points_en: Protected Designations of Origin benefit from stricter legal protection mechanisms compared to ordinary registered trade marks.
Creative marketing campaigns must not conflict with reserved terms defined under applicable food labeling and advertising regulations.
Rights holders must clearly distinguish the legal boundaries between geographical indications and standard trade mark rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure marketing materials do not utilize terms reserved for EU geographical indications or protected food categories.
Practice note: Implement a pre-clearance intellectual property compliance review process for all creative advertising campaigns.
What to monitor: Updates to the EU register of protected geographical indications and relevant national food labeling enforcement actions.

items.title: The divergent approaches to harvested material in the UK Nadorcott decision and the UPOV Expert Study
items.title_zh: 英国Nadorcott案与UPOV研究对收获材料保护的差异
items.title_en: Divergent Approaches to Harvested Material: UK Nadorcott Decision Versus UPOV Expert Study
items.summary: The protection of harvested material has been a controversial feature of plant variety rights (PVR) since its introduction under the 1991 Act of the UPOV Convention. Historically, PVR protection only applied to propagating (or reproductive) material, but the extension to harvested material has meant that cut flowers, grain, fruit, and other important products might fall within the scope of
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出，自UPOV公约1991年法案引入以来，收获材料的保护一直是植物新品种权（PVR）的争议焦点。历史上PVR仅适用于繁殖材料，但扩展至收获材料意味着切花、谷物、水果等产品可能纳入保护范围。文章旨在对比英国Nadorcott判决与UPOV专家研究在此问题上的不同处理方式。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the significant divergence between the UK High Court’s ruling in the Nadorcott case and the international guidance provided by the UPOV Expert Study regarding the scope of Plant Variety Rights (PVR). Since the 1991 Act of the UPOV Convention extended protection from propagating material to harvested material, legal uncertainty has persisted concerning whether products such as cut flowers, grain, and fruit constitute infringement. The article contrasts the specific judicial interpretation adopted by UK courts with the broader international perspective offered by UPOV experts. This comparison highlights the resulting ambiguity in applying PVR laws, particularly when determining liability for final agricultural products versus traditional reproductive materials.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注英国法院在Nadorcott案中对收获材料保护的具体认定逻辑，以及其与UPOV国际标准的潜在冲突。这对涉及跨境农产品贸易及品种权合规的企业具有参考意义，建议持续追踪后续司法实践对保护边界的界定。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting agricultural produce, including cut flowers, grains, and fruits, must carefully navigate the differing standards for harvested material infringement between the UK and other jurisdictions. Legal risks arise when local judicial interpretations diverge from international guidelines like those from UPOV. Companies should conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses focused on plant variety rights in target markets before export. Aligning supply chain compliance strategies with both local case law and international rules is essential to mitigate potential disputes and ensure robust intellectual property protection across borders.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK courts have adopted a distinct judicial interpretation path for defining the scope of harvested material protection under plant variety rights.", "The UPOV Expert Study offers an international perspective and recommendations that differ significantly from established UK case law precedents.", "Legal tension persists in determining infringement, specifically regarding the distinction between propagating material and final harvested products."]

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Exporters of cut flowers, fruits, and grains must assess plant variety right infringement risks in destination countries to avoid unexpected claims.", "Practice note: Develop differentiated compliance strategies by integrating local judicial precedents with international UPOV guidelines for comprehensive risk management.", "What to monitor: Ongoing developments in national court decisions regarding harvested material definitions and their alignment or conflict with UPOV expert studies."]
core_points_en: UK courts have adopted a distinct judicial interpretation path for defining the scope of harvested material protection under plant variety rights.
The UPOV Expert Study offers an international perspective and recommendations that differ significantly from established UK case law precedents.
Legal tension persists in determining infringement, specifically regarding the distinction between propagating material and final harvested products.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Exporters of cut flowers, fruits, and grains must assess plant variety right infringement risks in destination countries to avoid unexpected claims.
Practice note: Develop differentiated compliance strategies by integrating local judicial precedents with international UPOV guidelines for comprehensive risk management.
What to monitor: Ongoing developments in national court decisions regarding harvested material definitions and their alignment or conflict with UPOV expert studies.

items.title: “An interim Venice judges’ conference is desperately needed”
items.title_zh: 评论：亟需召开 interim 威尼斯法官会议
items.title_en: “An interim Venice judges’ conference is desperately needed”
items.summary: Once a year in October, European patent judges meet in Venice for the European Judges’ Forum. The idea behind the so-called Venice conference is for judges to engage in a friendly exchange of ideas behind closed doors. Lawyers and other patent experts are only admitted in exceptional cases. Unlike other conferences, where publicity is high […] The post “An interim Venice judges’ conference is desperately needed” appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出欧洲专利法官每年十月在威尼斯举行“欧洲法官论坛”。该会议旨在让法官在闭门环境下进行友好的思想交流。除特殊情况外，律师及其他专利专家不得参加。这与高公开度的其他会议形成对比。标题显示有人呼吁亟需召开一次临时会议。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文仅为简短评论片段，未提供关于临时会议的具体背景、动因或议程。实务中应关注该非公开法官交流机制对欧洲专利裁判尺度统一性的潜在影响，以及后续是否有关于会议结论的间接披露。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧洲专利法官每年十月在威尼斯举行闭门交流的“欧洲法官论坛”", "该会议原则上排除律师及专利专家参与，仅例外情况允许", "标题观点认为目前亟需召开一次临时的威尼斯法官会议"]
