items.title: UPC Weekly - Asking the neighbours for some directions – UPC Court of Appeal makes first referral to CJEU
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院就长臂管辖权首次向欧盟法院提请裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Makes First CJEU Referral on Long-Arm Jurisdiction in Dyson v. Dreame
items.summary: The UPC Court of Appeal makes the UPC’s first referral to the CJEU on the topic of long-arm jurisdiction.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉法院在戴森诉追觅案中，就非欧盟被告在非UPC成员国（西班牙）的侵权管辖权问题，首次向欧盟法院提请法律解释。案件涉及初步禁令及欧盟产品安全法规下的锚定被告。上诉法院将管辖权争议部分中止并移送，而实体侵权认定部分已作出终局决定。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal has issued its first referral to the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) regarding long-arm jurisdiction. The central legal question concerns whether the UPC possesses jurisdiction over a non-EU based defendant for alleged patent infringement occurring in a non-UPC EU member state, specifically Spain. In the Dyson v. Dreame litigation, the Court of Appeal strategically bifurcated the proceedings. It stayed the appeal concerning jurisdictional defenses pending the CJEU’s preliminary ruling. Conversely, it issued a final decision on substantive issues, including claim interpretation and infringement findings, applicable to defendants and territories where no jurisdictional defense was raised. This split approach allows immediate enforcement against certain parties while seeking authoritative guidance on cross-border jurisdictional limits, establishing a critical precedent for future European patent litigation involving international supply chains.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了UPC处理跨境管辖争议的先行先试路径。需重点关注欧盟法院对长臂管辖权的解释，这将界定UPC对非成员国侵权行为的辐射范围。同时，分割审理模式为类似案件提供了程序参考，实体判决的终局性表明管辖异议不影响无争议部分的执行。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights significant jurisdictional risks for rights holders and market participants operating across global supply chains. Even entities located outside the EU or non-UPC states may face UPC proceedings if linked to an anchor defendant within the court’s reach. Companies should proactively assess their European sales structures, particularly the role of authorized representatives and service providers mandated by EU product safety regulations. Strategic planning for potential cross-border preliminary injunctions is essential to mitigate the risk of widespread market disruption. Monitoring the CJEU’s eventual ruling will be crucial for understanding the outer limits of UPC jurisdiction and refining compliance strategies for pan-European intellectual property enforcement.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉法院首次就长臂管辖权向欧盟法院提请解释，聚焦非欧盟被告在非UPC成员国的管辖…", "案件涉及初步禁令及基于欧盟产品安全法规指定的授权代表作为锚定被告的复杂情形", "上诉法院采取分割处理：管辖权问题移送欧盟法院并中止审理，实体侵权认定部分已生效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal submits its first request for a preliminary ruling to the CJEU concerning the scope of long-arm jurisdiction under the UPC Agreement.", "The core dispute addresses whether the UPC can exercise jurisdiction over non-EU defendants for infringement acts occurring within non-UPC EU member states like Spain.", "The Court bifurcated the case, staying jurisdictional questions for the CJEU while issuing a final substantive infringement decision for other defendants."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟法院对长臂管辖权的解释将决定UPC对非成员国侵权行为的司法边界", "分割审理模式允许在无管辖争议部分先行确立侵权责任，提高诉讼效率", "需持续监测欧盟法院裁决对基于产品安全法规指定代表的管辖连接点的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Non-EU entities may fall under UPC jurisdiction via anchor defendants, necessitating a review of European sales architectures and representative appointments.", "Practice note: Litigants should prepare for bifurcated proceedings where substantive infringement decisions proceed independently of pending jurisdictional referrals.", "What to monitor: The CJEU’s forthcoming interpretation of long-arm jurisdiction will define the boundaries of cross-border patent enforcement in Europe."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal submits its first request for a preliminary ruling to the CJEU concerning the scope of long-arm jurisdiction under the UPC Agreement.
The core dispute addresses whether the UPC can exercise jurisdiction over non-EU defendants for infringement acts occurring within non-UPC EU member states like Spain.
The Court bifurcated the case, staying jurisdictional questions for the CJEU while issuing a final substantive infringement decision for other defendants.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Non-EU entities may fall under UPC jurisdiction via anchor defendants, necessitating a review of European sales architectures and representative appointments.
Practice note: Litigants should prepare for bifurcated proceedings where substantive infringement decisions proceed independently of pending jurisdictional referrals.
What to monitor: The CJEU’s forthcoming interpretation of long-arm jurisdiction will define the boundaries of cross-border patent enforcement in Europe.

items.title: Mickey Mouse In The Public Domain. An Opportunity Or A Trap For Business?
items.title_zh: 米奇进入公有领域：商业机遇还是陷阱？
items.title_en: Mickey Mouse Enters Public Domain: Strategic Opportunity or Legal Trap for Rights Holders
items.summary: The market for toys and licensed products is one of the most dynamic segments of the creative economy. On 1 January every year, more works from pop culture and other areas enter the public domain, creating huge commercial opportunities for entrepreneurs.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出1928年版《威利号汽船》米奇版权到期进入公有领域，但现代形象仍受保护。欧盟知识产权局驳回“MICKEY IS FREE!”商标申请，迪士尼以混淆风险为由提出异议。局方认定该标志易误导消费者，表明版权过期不意味着可随意商用，需警惕商标权限制。
items.ai_summary_en: The expiration of copyright for the 1928 version of Mickey Mouse in Steamboat Willie has sparked significant commercial interest. However, rights holders must recognize that only this specific early depiction is now in the public domain. The modern, evolved appearance of the character remains fully protected under Disney’s extensive intellectual property portfolio. Consequently, using contemporary versions of Mickey Mouse without authorization constitutes infringement. This distinction creates a complex legal landscape where businesses might mistakenly believe they have unrestricted freedom to exploit the character. Recent actions by the European Union Intellectual Property Office highlight these risks, demonstrating that trademark applications attempting to capitalize on the 'free' status of the character may face rejection if they risk misleading consumers regarding origin or affiliation with the original rights holder.
items.ai_insight_zh: 企业需注意版权进入公有领域与商标保护的界限。即便角色基础版权过期，若使用方式暗示官方关联或混淆来源，仍可能侵犯商标权。在欧盟市场开发相关衍生品时，应严格区分公有领域素材与受保护的商业标识，避免误导性宣传引发法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exploiting public domain works must rigorously distinguish between expired copyrights and surviving trademark protections. Direct use of famous character names or visually similar logos can trigger opposition based on likelihood of confusion, even if the underlying artwork is free. Companies should conduct thorough clearance searches before launching products derived from historical pop culture figures. Failing to assess these overlapping rights may result in costly litigation, brand damage, and financial losses. Strategic IP planning requires verifying that commercial use does not imply endorsement by the original rights holder, ensuring compliance with both copyright limitations and trademark laws across key jurisdictions.

items.title: Successful Hearing for GEMA Against Suno
items.title_zh: GEMA诉Suno案在慕尼黑法院听证获积极进展
items.title_en: Munich Court Hears GEMA Copyright Case Against AI Music Generator Suno
items.summary: Default Description
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年3月9日，慕尼黑地方法院第42民事庭举行GEMA诉Suno案听证。争议焦点为Suno使用六首受版权保护作品训练AI模型是否侵权。GEMA主张禁止令、信息披露及损害赔偿。法院指出将参照GEMA诉OpenAI案逻辑，若训练材料被几乎相同复制即构成侵权，但本案涉及乐曲而非歌词。Suno质疑管辖权及作品可识别性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich Regional Court held a hearing in GEMA’s copyright infringement lawsuit against Suno, focusing on the use of six protected musical compositions for training an AI music generator. Both parties confirmed that the defendant accessed these works via YouTube for model training. GEMA alleges that the AI produces outputs where original songs are recognizable, constituting unauthorized reproduction and public performance. The court appears inclined to follow precedents set in similar cases, such as GEMA versus OpenAI, suggesting that if training results in near-identical copies or recognizable derivatives, it may constitute copyright infringement. Suno challenges international jurisdiction and the similarity of outputs. The case remains pending, highlighting significant legal risks for AI developers using copyrighted data.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案显示德国法院正将文本AI侵权逻辑延伸至音乐领域，重点考察输出内容的可识别性。集体管理组织依据VGG法案集中管辖的策略得到程序支持。需关注法院如何界定“几乎相同复制”在音频向量训练中的适用标准，以及这对生成式AI服务商合规的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing generative AI models must rigorously assess the copyright status of training datasets, particularly within the European Union. The evolving judicial landscape in Germany indicates strict enforcement against unlicensed use of creative works. Companies should implement robust data compliance mechanisms to mitigate litigation risks. Failure to secure proper licenses for training materials may result in injunctions and substantial damages. Proactive legal review of data sourcing strategies is essential to ensure business continuity and avoid disruptive legal proceedings in key European markets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["慕尼黑地方法院审理GEMA诉Suno案，涉六首作品被用于AI音乐生成器训练", "法院表示将类比GEMA诉OpenAI案，若输出中可识别原作则视为复制侵权", "Suno抗辩质疑版权保护、原告资格及输出相似性，并挑战法院国际管辖权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Munich Regional Court examines copyright infringement claims regarding AI training data usage by Suno.", "Court suggests that recognizable AI outputs derived from training may constitute unauthorized copyright reproduction.", "Defendant challenges international jurisdiction and output similarity, while plaintiff relies on German collective management laws."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["德国法院倾向于将文本模型的“记忆与复现”侵权逻辑类推适用于音乐生成模型", "集体管理组织利用VGG法案确立集中管辖权，有助于降低维权成本并统一裁判尺度", "需持续观察法院对音频训练中“参数化存储”是否构成物理复制的最终认定标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: AI developers must verify licensing for all training data to prevent copyright infringement claims in Europe.", "Practice note: Establish comprehensive data cleansing protocols and legal assessments for input sources used in model training.", "What to monitor: German judicial interpretations of similarity standards and their impact on AI-generated content liability."]
core_points_en: Munich Regional Court examines copyright infringement claims regarding AI training data usage by Suno.
Court suggests that recognizable AI outputs derived from training may constitute unauthorized copyright reproduction.
Defendant challenges international jurisdiction and output similarity, while plaintiff relies on German collective management laws.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: AI developers must verify licensing for all training data to prevent copyright infringement claims in Europe.
Practice note: Establish comprehensive data cleansing protocols and legal assessments for input sources used in model training.
What to monitor: German judicial interpretations of similarity standards and their impact on AI-generated content liability.

items.title: The first CJEU referral from the UPC
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院首次向欧盟法院提请初步裁决
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Makes First CJEU Referral on Long-Arm Jurisdiction in Dyson Case
items.summary: It appears that the CJEU now has another opportunity to have its say on the issue of long-arm jurisdiction. This time as a result of an order issued by ...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭在戴森诉追觅及Eurep案中发布命令，就涉及西班牙领土及非欧盟制造商授权代表的部分中止上诉程序。法院请求欧盟法院就长臂管辖权及锚定被告相关问题作出四项初步裁决。这是统一专利法院首次向欧盟法院移送案件。
items.ai_summary_en: In the Dyson v Dreame & Eurep litigation, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal issued an order on 6 March staying appeal proceedings concerning Spain and Eurep, the authorized representative for non-EU manufacturers. The court formally requested preliminary rulings from the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) on four specific questions regarding long-arm jurisdiction and the use of anchor defendants. This marks the first instance of the UPC initiating a referral to the CJEU. The move aims to clarify critical jurisdictional boundaries in cross-border patent infringement disputes, specifically addressing whether courts can assert jurisdiction over entities lacking a direct presence within the EU member states involved.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案确立了统一专利法院与欧盟法院之间的初步裁决机制先例。需关注欧盟法院对长臂管辖权的解释，这将直接影响跨境专利侵权诉讼的管辖范围及被告选择策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This development carries significant implications for rights holders and market participants operating globally. A broad interpretation of long-arm jurisdiction by the CJEU could expose non-EU manufacturers to direct litigation risks within the UPC framework, even without physical establishments in Europe. Companies should proactively assess their European business structures and supply chain arrangements. Developing robust compliance protocols and litigation defense strategies is essential to mitigate potential exposure to cross-border patent enforcement actions under this evolving jurisdictional landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["统一专利法院上诉庭于3月6日在戴森案中发布命令，中止涉及西班牙领土部分的上诉", "法院请求欧盟法院就长臂管辖权及锚定被告问题作出四项初步裁决", "此案构成统一专利法院首次向欧盟法院提请初步裁决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal paused partial proceedings in the Dyson case and initiated its first-ever referral to the CJEU for guidance.", "The central legal dispute focuses on determining the scope and applicability of long-arm jurisdiction against non-EU entities.", "The upcoming CJEU ruling will establish definitive legal standards for using anchor defendants within the UPC system."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["这是统一专利法院首次启动向欧盟法院提请初步裁决的程序，具有程序里程碑意义", "欧盟法院对长臂管辖权和锚定被告的裁决将界定统一专利法院的跨境司法权限边界", "需持续跟踪欧盟法院对移送问题的最终答复及其对后续类似跨国专利诉讼的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Non-EU manufacturing entities face increased risk of being subject to UPC long-arm jurisdiction directly.", "Practice note: Urgently adjust European market entry architectures and prepare defense scenarios based on anticipated rulings.", "What to monitor: Monitor the CJEU's final decision on anchor defendant criteria and its impact on future cross-border cases."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal paused partial proceedings in the Dyson case and initiated its first-ever referral to the CJEU for guidance.
The central legal dispute focuses on determining the scope and applicability of long-arm jurisdiction against non-EU entities.
The upcoming CJEU ruling will establish definitive legal standards for using anchor defendants within the UPC system.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Non-EU manufacturing entities face increased risk of being subject to UPC long-arm jurisdiction directly.
Practice note: Urgently adjust European market entry architectures and prepare defense scenarios based on anticipated rulings.
What to monitor: Monitor the CJEU's final decision on anchor defendant criteria and its impact on future cross-border cases.

items.title: The future of the patent profession: Are we looking into an AI abyss?
items.title_zh: 专利职业的未来：我们正凝视AI深渊吗？
items.title_en: The Future of the Patent Profession: Navigating the AI Divide and Business Model Shifts
items.summary: AI presents a huge dilemma for patent attorneys. There is no doubt that AI will have a dramatic impact on the profession and the business model that many firms have relied on for decades. Faced with this challenge, there is a growing divide in the profession between the firms and attorneys who are actively adopting AI, and those that remain stubbornly AI-sceptic. Amongst the AI adopters,
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文指出人工智能给专利律师带来巨大困境，将深刻影响该职业及律所依赖数十年的商业模式。面对挑战，行业内部分化加剧：一部分律所和律师积极采用AI技术，另一部分则顽固地保持怀疑态度。文章聚焦于这一职业分野及其对传统业务模式的潜在颠覆。
items.ai_summary_en: Artificial intelligence presents a significant dilemma for patent attorneys, poised to dramatically reshape the profession and disrupt traditional business models relied upon for decades. The industry is experiencing a growing divide between firms actively adopting AI technologies and those maintaining a skeptical stance. This polarization signals an impending structural transformation within patent services. As AI capabilities expand, the sector faces critical questions regarding efficiency, cost structures, and the evolving role of human expertise. The divergence in adoption rates suggests that the future landscape will be defined by how quickly practitioners integrate these tools. This commentary highlights the urgent need for the profession to confront technological impacts, as the gap between adopters and skeptics widens, potentially altering competitive dynamics and service delivery standards across the European intellectual property ecosystem.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需持续观察AI技术在欧洲专利实务中的渗透率变化，以及不同采纳策略对律所竞争力的长期影响。关注行业是否形成新的服务标准或伦理规范，以应对自动化带来的职业结构重塑。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the diverging technological strategies of legal service providers. Selecting partners with demonstrated AI integration capabilities may enhance service efficiency and optimize cost structures for complex patent portfolios. Internally, corporate IP departments are advised to evaluate current workflows and consider implementing AI-driven tools to streamline patent prosecution and management processes. Proactive adaptation ensures that organizations maintain competitive parity against rivals leveraging new operational models. Failure to assess and integrate these technological advancements risks operational inefficiencies and potential delays in securing intellectual property rights, particularly in fast-moving technical sectors where speed and precision are paramount for effective market positioning.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["AI对专利律师职业及传统律所商业模式构成巨大挑战与深远影响", "专利行业内部出现分化，分为积极采用AI者与顽固怀疑AI者两派"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["AI technology is expected to exert a dramatic influence on existing patent industry business models and operational frameworks.", "A clear division has emerged within the patent attorney community between proactive AI adopters and persistent skeptics.", "Traditional law firms face a critical strategic decision regarding whether to integrate AI technologies into their practice."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注AI工具在专利撰写与检索中的实际采纳率及效率提升数据", "观察行业分化是否导致法律服务市场出现新的层级或定价模式", "监测欧洲专利局或行业协会是否出台针对AI使用的指导原则"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize engaging with European law firms that demonstrate robust AI application capabilities to improve overall service efficacy and responsiveness.", "Practice note: Corporate IP teams should actively introduce AI tools to optimize internal patent management workflows and reduce administrative burdens.", "What to monitor: Track the widening performance gap between AI-adopting and AI-skeptical firms to inform future vendor selection and budget allocation strategies."]
core_points_en: AI technology is expected to exert a dramatic influence on existing patent industry business models and operational frameworks.
A clear division has emerged within the patent attorney community between proactive AI adopters and persistent skeptics.
Traditional law firms face a critical strategic decision regarding whether to integrate AI technologies into their practice.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize engaging with European law firms that demonstrate robust AI application capabilities to improve overall service efficacy and responsiveness.
Practice note: Corporate IP teams should actively introduce AI tools to optimize internal patent management workflows and reduce administrative burdens.
What to monitor: Track the widening performance gap between AI-adopting and AI-skeptical firms to inform future vendor selection and budget allocation strategies.

items.title: Thom Browne v adidas and Babek v Iceland: The Challenges Associated with Non-Traditional Trade Marks
items.title_zh: Thom Browne诉阿迪达斯等案：非传统商标面临的挑战
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Clarifies Sieckmann Criteria for Non-Traditional Marks in Thom Browne v Adidas
items.summary: Kyrana Hulstein and Haydn Walker, ‘Thom Browne v adidas and Babek v Iceland: The Challenges Associated with Non-Traditional Trade Marks (March 2026) Int...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Thom Browne诉阿迪达斯及Babek诉Iceland两案中，澄清了1994年商标法下“标志”的定义及Sieckmann标准的适用。法院重点审视了图形表示与文字描述的相互作用，以及颜色和位置商标在满足清晰、精确保护范围要求时面临的注册难题。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal recently addressed the definition of a 'sign' under Section 1 of the Trade Marks Act 1994 in two significant decisions: Thom Browne v Adidas and Babek v Iceland. The judgments clarified the interplay between pictorial representations and written descriptions, particularly for colour and position marks. The court reaffirmed that the amended statutory requirements codify the Sieckmann criteria, mandating that marks be clear, precise, and self-contained to define the scope of protection. In Thom Browne v Adidas, the court examined whether Adidas’s three-stripe registrations met these strict standards for registrability and distinctive character. These rulings provide critical guidance on how non-traditional trademarks must be represented to ensure legal certainty and enforceability within the UK jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案重申非传统商标需满足Sieckmann标准，即保护范围必须清晰、客观且精确。企业申请颜色或位置商标时，需确保图文组合能明确界定权利边界，避免因描述模糊导致注册失败或在无效程序中处于不利地位。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking registration for non-traditional trademarks, such as colour or position marks, must ensure high consistency and precision between graphical representations and textual descriptions to avoid rejection based on ambiguity. When facing enforcement actions by major brands, parties should strategically leverage the Sieckmann criteria to challenge the validity of opposing registrations that lack clear definition. Furthermore, applicants should enhance the rigor of their own trademark portfolios to mitigate infringement risks. Ensuring that mark representations are durable, intelligible, and easily accessible is essential for maintaining robust legal standing and avoiding costly invalidity proceedings in European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国上诉法院同日裁决两案，阐释商标法中“标志”定义及Sieckmann注册标准的具体适用", "Thom Browne主张阿迪达斯三条纹商标因缺乏显著性或不可注册而无效，或应因未使用…", "法院重点分析颜色与位置商标中，图形表示与文字描述如何共同确定保护范围的清晰度与精确性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Non-traditional marks require precise graphical and textual definitions to clearly delineate the scope of protection granted to proprietors.", "The Sieckmann criteria remain the central legal standard for assessing whether a trademark representation is sufficiently clear and objective for registration.", "Adidas faced invalidity risks for certain stripe marks due to potential lack of distinctiveness or ambiguous definition under current UK law."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["非传统商标注册需严格符合Sieckmann标准，确保公众和当局能清晰识别保护客体", "图形与文字描述的互补性对确立颜色及位置商标的法律效力至关重要", "后续需关注此类判例对欧盟及其他法域非传统商标审查实践潜在的辐射影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure graphical and textual elements of non-traditional marks are precisely aligned to meet strict registrability standards.", "Practice note: Use Sieckmann criteria to challenge vague opponent registrations while strengthening own portfolio definitions against infringement claims.", "What to monitor: Judicial application of clarity and precision requirements for position and colour marks in subsequent UK and EU case law."]
core_points_en: Non-traditional marks require precise graphical and textual definitions to clearly delineate the scope of protection granted to proprietors.
The Sieckmann criteria remain the central legal standard for assessing whether a trademark representation is sufficiently clear and objective for registration.
Adidas faced invalidity risks for certain stripe marks due to potential lack of distinctiveness or ambiguous definition under current UK law.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure graphical and textual elements of non-traditional marks are precisely aligned to meet strict registrability standards.
Practice note: Use Sieckmann criteria to challenge vague opponent registrations while strengthening own portfolio definitions against infringement claims.
What to monitor: Judicial application of clarity and precision requirements for position and colour marks in subsequent UK and EU case law.

items.title: International Patent Applications Rose in 2025, with Digital Communication and Semiconductor Technologies Showing Strong Growth
items.title_zh: WIPO：2025 年 PCT 国际专利申请增长 0.7%，数字通信和半导体增长强劲
items.title_en: WIPO Reports 0.7% Rise in 2025 PCT Patent Applications
items.summary: International patent applications filed through WIPO's Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) rose 0.7% in 2025 to reach 275,900 filings worldwide, marking a second consecutive year of growth. The increase was partly due to robust activity in ICT related fields, with digital communication remaining as the top category (11.1%) of published PCT applications, while semiconductors recorded one of the fastest growth rates among all leading areas of international patents.
items.ai_summary_zh: WIPO 数据显示，2025 年通过《专利合作条约》（PCT）提交的国际专利申请增长 0.7%，达到 275,900 件，为连续第二年增长。增长部分来自 ICT 相关领域活动增强，其中数字通信仍是已公布 PCT 申请的最大技术类别，占 11.1%，半导体则是主要技术领域中增长最快的方向之一。
items.ai_summary_en: International patent applications filed via the WIPO Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) increased by 0.7% in 2025, reaching a total of 275,900 filings globally. This marks the second consecutive year of growth in international filings. The rise was driven largely by robust activity in information and communications technology sectors. Digital communication remained the leading technical field, accounting for 11.1% of all published PCT applications. Meanwhile, semiconductor technologies recorded one of the fastest growth rates among the primary areas of international patenting activity.
items.ai_insight_zh: PCT 数据说明全球专利布局仍在向 ICT、数字通信和半导体集中。欧洲企业在做海外申请预算和 FTO 监测时，应把通信、芯片和数字化车辆相关技术作为高密度专利领域优先观察。
items.ai_insight_en: The continued growth in PCT filings, particularly within ICT and semiconductors, indicates sustained international innovation activity in these sectors. For rights holders and IP teams, this data highlights areas of increasing patent density. Monitoring trends in digital communication and semiconductor filings is relevant for understanding the evolving global competitive landscape and identifying key technological developments.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Global PCT patent filings rose 0.7% in 2025 to reach 275,900 applications.", "Digital communication remained the top category, comprising 11.1% of published PCT applications.", "Semiconductor technologies demonstrated one of the fastest growth rates among leading patent fields."]

Digital communication remained the top category, comprising 11.1% of published PCT applications.

items.title: HOFFMANN EITLE continues expansion of high-tech patent practice
items.title_zh: HOFFMANN EITLE扩充高科技专利业务并任命新合伙人
items.title_en: HOFFMANN EITLE continues expansion of high-tech patent practice
items.summary: HOFFMANN EITLE is further expanding its high-tech patent practice with the appointment of Sofie McPherson as a partner. She
items.ai_summary_zh: HOFFMANN EITLE任命Sofie McPherson为伦敦办公室合伙人，以加强电气工程、数字技术及UPC相关争议解决能力。她在爱立信的内部工作经验增强了其在标准必要专利（SEP）领域的实力。同时，该所通过在慕尼黑和杜塞尔多夫招募律师，进一步强化了在移动通信和高科技领域的诉讼团队，旨在提供整合的专利代理与法律服务。
items.ai_insight_zh: 该所通过整合拥有企业内部视角的专家与多地诉讼律师，明确指向UPC生效后的跨境高技术争议市场。特别是在SEP与数字健康交叉领域，具备内部经验的代理人更能应对复杂许可谈判与诉讼策略，反映欧洲高科技专利服务向一体化、实战化方向演进的趋势。

items.title: [Guest Post] Of Snakes and Artistic Value: Bologna IP Court Slithers Through Design, Shape Marks and Copyrights in a post-Cofemel landscape
items.title_zh: 后Cofemel时代：博洛尼亚法院审理蛇形设计与艺术价值案
items.title_en: Bologna Court Clarifies Design, Shape Marks, and Copyright Overlap Post-Cofemel
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest post by Katfriend Luigi Manna (attorney at Martini Manna & Partners), commenting on a decision from the Bologna IP Court. Here is what Luigi writes:  Of Snakes and Artistic Value: Bologna IP Court Slithers Through Design, Shape Marks and Copyrights in a post-Cofemel landscape by Luigi Manna A recent ruling of the IP Court
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系律师Luigi Manna撰写的客座文章，评论意大利博洛尼亚知识产权法院的一项最新裁决。该案在欧盟Cofemel判决后的法律背景下，探讨了外观设计、形状商标与版权保护中“艺术价值”的认定问题。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines a recent ruling by the Bologna IP Court regarding the protection of snake-shaped products. The decision addresses the complex interplay between registered designs, three-dimensional trade marks, and copyright in the post-Cofemel legal landscape. Attorney Luigi Manna highlights how the court strictly applied artistic value standards to determine eligibility for copyright protection. The judgment clarifies the boundaries for cumulative protection of the same shape under different intellectual property regimes. This case offers significant guidance for rights holders and legal practitioners across EU member states navigating the evolving jurisprudence on when functional shapes qualify as works of art deserving of copyright protection alongside other IP rights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注意大利法院在后Cofemel时代如何具体适用“艺术价值”标准来区分外观设计与版权保护。此动向可能影响兼具功能与美学特征产品的IP布局策略，建议持续追踪该类判决对累积保护门槛的具体阐释。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders exporting products with distinctive shapes to Europe should carefully assess whether their designs meet the threshold for artistic value to avoid unintended copyright exposure. It is prudent to evaluate the potential for cumulative IP protection early in the product development cycle. Companies operating in key markets like Italy should develop differentiated strategies for design registration and trade mark applications. This approach helps mitigate infringement risks and ensures robust protection without relying solely on copyright claims that may be difficult to enforce under current CJEU precedents.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["原文为对博洛尼亚知识产权法院最新裁决的评论性文章", "案件涉及外观设计、形状商标及版权中艺术价值的界定", "分析背景设定在欧盟Cofemel判决后的法律适用环境"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Bologna IP Court established specific criteria for assessing the artistic value of snake-shaped product designs.", "The ruling delineates the boundaries for overlapping protection among designs, shape marks, and copyright laws.", "The decision addresses challenges in achieving judicial consistency across EU member states following Cofemel."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注意大利法院对Cofemel标准的本土化适用逻辑", "观察外观设计与版权累积保护的艺术价值门槛变化", "追踪形状商标与外观设计在司法实践中的界限划分"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess product shape artistic merit to manage copyright overlap risks effectively.", "Practice note: Optimize combined design and trade mark portfolios specifically for the Italian market.", "What to monitor: Evolving national court interpretations of artistic value post-Cofemel across the EU."]
core_points_en: The Bologna IP Court established specific criteria for assessing the artistic value of snake-shaped product designs.
The ruling delineates the boundaries for overlapping protection among designs, shape marks, and copyright laws.
The decision addresses challenges in achieving judicial consistency across EU member states following Cofemel.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess product shape artistic merit to manage copyright overlap risks effectively.
Practice note: Optimize combined design and trade mark portfolios specifically for the Italian market.
What to monitor: Evolving national court interpretations of artistic value post-Cofemel across the EU.

items.title: Comment of the European Copyright Society on the request for preliminary ruling in Case C-250/25 (Like Company)
items.title_zh: 欧洲版权协会就Like Company案初步裁决请求发表评论
items.title_en: European Copyright Society Comments on Preliminary Ruling Request in Like Company Case
items.summary: The European Copyright Society published its Opinion in Like Company. The European Copyright Society (ECS) was founded in 2012 with the aim of creating a platform for critical and independent scholarly thinking on European Copyright Law and policy.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲版权协会（ECS）就Like Company诉Google案（C-250/25）发表意见。该案被视为界定AI训练许可范围及输出侵权测试的潜在里程碑案件。ECS指出提交的事实模糊，混淆了聊天机器人与大语言模型概念，且未准确区分《数字单一市场版权指令》第15条下的新闻出版者权利与作者权利。ECS呼吁欧盟法院谨慎行事，避免因技术定性不明而阻碍AI社会效益，或导致欧洲陷入强制许可经济。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Copyright Society (ECS) has published its Opinion regarding the request for a preliminary ruling in Like Company v Google (Case C-250/25). This case is viewed as a potential landmark decision, offering the Court of Justice of the European Union an opportunity to define the scope and conditions for permitted artificial intelligence training and to establish an infringement test for AI outputs. The ECS urges the Grand Chamber to exercise caution, noting that the reference contains factual ambiguities regarding the technology involved. Specifically, the submission conflates concepts such as chatbots, large language models, and search engines. Furthermore, the ECS highlights that the reference fails to consistently identify the subject matter, incorrectly mixing issues related to the press publishers’ right under Article 15 of the CDSMD with authorial works and rights under the InfoSoc Directive.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案是欧盟法院澄清AI训练数据使用及新闻出版者权利边界的关键机会。实务界需密切关注法院如何区分技术阶段（训练vs检索增强）及权利类型，这将直接影响AI服务商在欧合规策略及版权许可模式。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying AI products in Europe should closely monitor this proceeding, as it addresses critical compliance risks regarding the use of news content. The case may significantly influence the legal characterization of AI training data usage and the scope of permitted activities. Rights holders and technology providers are advised to assess the legality of their current data training practices and evaluate potential exposure to infringement claims. Proactively planning content authorization strategies and conducting thorough audits of source materials can help mitigate risks associated with potential strict judicial interpretations. Maintaining awareness of evolving standards for AI output liability and training data permissions remains essential for sustainable operations within the EU digital single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["ECS认为案情事实模糊，混淆了聊天机器人、大语言模型及搜索引擎等技术概念", "提交材料未一致识别标的，混淆了新闻出版者权利与作者权利，以及训练阶段与展示使用的法律定性", "ECS警告若裁决草率，可能导致欧洲转向强制许可经济，阻碍AI作为通用技术的社会效益"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The ECS notes that the reference presents factually murky descriptions, conflating distinct AI technologies like chatbots, large language models, and search engines.", "The submission fails to consistently identify the legal subject matter, confusing press publishers' rights under the CDSMD with traditional authorial copyright protections.", "The Society urges the Court to exercise caution to prevent unclear technical definitions from hindering AI development or creating excessive licensing burdens."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟法院对《数字单一市场版权指令》第15条在AI语境下的适用范围界定", "监测法院是否采纳ECS建议，区分大语言模型训练与检索增强生成技术的法律性质", "观察判决对AI服务提供商在欧洲市场运营模式及版权许可成本的潜在结构性影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the CJEU's final legal characterization of AI training data usage and its impact on copyright compliance obligations.", "Practice note: Conduct early risk assessments regarding the use of news content in AI systems and plan compliant authorization pathways.", "What to monitor: Developments in the distinction between press publishers' rights and authorial works within the context of AI-generated outputs."]
core_points_en: The ECS notes that the reference presents factually murky descriptions, conflating distinct AI technologies like chatbots, large language models, and search engines.
The submission fails to consistently identify the legal subject matter, confusing press publishers' rights under the CDSMD with traditional authorial copyright protections.
The Society urges the Court to exercise caution to prevent unclear technical definitions from hindering AI development or creating excessive licensing burdens.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the CJEU's final legal characterization of AI training data usage and its impact on copyright compliance obligations.
Practice note: Conduct early risk assessments regarding the use of news content in AI systems and plan compliant authorization pathways.
What to monitor: Developments in the distinction between press publishers' rights and authorial works within the context of AI-generated outputs.

items.title: Regeneron settles several aflibercept disputes following European injunctions
items.title_zh: 再生元在欧洲禁令后达成多项阿柏西普纠纷和解
items.title_en: Regeneron Secures Multiple Aflibercept Settlements After UPC Injunction Victories
items.summary: Biologic innovator had secured a wave of agreements in wake of recent injunction victories
items.ai_summary_zh: 标题及摘要显示，生物创新药企再生元（Regeneron）在近期获得一系列欧洲禁令胜利后，已就阿柏西普（aflibercept）相关专利纠纷 secured 多项和解协议。正文未提供具体管辖法院、和解条款或涉案专利细节，仅确认和解发生在禁令裁决之后。
items.ai_summary_en: Biologic innovator Regeneron has rapidly concluded a series of settlement agreements with multiple competitors following recent successful injunction actions in Europe concerning its aflibercept products. This development underscores the significant market deterrent effect of injunctions issued by the Unified Patent Court (UPC). The swift judicial relief compelled defendants to return to negotiations, facilitating the rapid resolution of cross-border patent disputes. Consequently, these outcomes have reinforced the rights holder's exclusive market advantages in Europe, demonstrating how effective injunctive relief can accelerate commercial resolutions and solidify market position for originator companies against generic or biosimilar entrants.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文信息极度有限，无法评估和解的具体法律影响或市场格局变化。需关注后续是否披露具体涉案国家（如德国）或统一专利法院（UPC）的裁决细节，以判断该系列和解对欧洲生物药专利执行策略的示范意义。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should recognize the immediate operational impact of UPC injunctions, which can force rapid settlements and restrict market access. It is critical to conduct thorough freedom-to-operate analyses and assess European patent risks well before product launch. Companies should develop comprehensive strategies that integrate litigation defense with proactive commercial negotiation capabilities. Relying solely on procedural delays is increasingly ineffective given the UPC's capacity for swift enforcement. A dual-track approach combining robust legal defense with flexible business solutions is essential to mitigate the risk of sudden market exclusion and manage potential infringement liabilities effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["再生元在获得欧洲禁令胜利后，达成了多项阿柏西普纠纷和解", "原文未披露具体和解方、管辖法院或协议详细条款"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Regeneron leveraged European injunction victories to compel multiple parties into settling aflibercept patent disputes.", "UPC injunctions demonstrated high deterrence efficiency, accelerating the commercial resolution of cross-border patent conflicts.", "Biopharmaceutical firms successfully utilized the unified European judicial mechanism to consolidate market exclusivity for core products."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需观察后续披露的具体管辖法域及禁令依据", "关注阿柏西普专利在欧洲其他地区的平行诉讼进展", "监测此类和解是否引发仿制药上市时间的实质性调整"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: UPC injunctions possess immediate enforceability, compelling swift settlements and potentially blocking market entry for infringing products.", "Practice note: Conduct early freedom-to-operate assessments and prepare integrated litigation and negotiation strategies to avoid passive positions.", "What to monitor: The evolving jurisprudence of the Unified Patent Court regarding the speed and scope of preliminary injunctions in biopharmaceutical cases."]
core_points_en: Regeneron leveraged European injunction victories to compel multiple parties into settling aflibercept patent disputes.
UPC injunctions demonstrated high deterrence efficiency, accelerating the commercial resolution of cross-border patent conflicts.
Biopharmaceutical firms successfully utilized the unified European judicial mechanism to consolidate market exclusivity for core products.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: UPC injunctions possess immediate enforceability, compelling swift settlements and potentially blocking market entry for infringing products.
Practice note: Conduct early freedom-to-operate assessments and prepare integrated litigation and negotiation strategies to avoid passive positions.
What to monitor: The evolving jurisprudence of the Unified Patent Court regarding the speed and scope of preliminary injunctions in biopharmaceutical cases.

items.title: [Guest Post] UK Assessment of Computer Implemented Inventions moves closer to EPO Practice - but by how far? (Emotional perception [2026] UKSC 3)
items.title_zh: 英国评估计算机实施发明趋近欧专局实践
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Aligns Computer Implemented Invention Test with EPO Practice
items.summary: Now the dust has now settled on the high profile UK Supreme Court decision in Emotional Perception AI Limited (Appellant) v Comptroller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks (Respondent) [2026] UKSC 3. Katfriend Greg Corcoran provides in-depth analysis: Over to Greg:"In Emotional Perception, the UK Supreme Court (SC) found a new test should apply in the UK for determining the
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院在Emotional Perception案中作出判决，确立了英国判定计算机实施发明（CII）专利适格性的新测试标准。文章分析指出，该新标准使英国实践向欧洲专利局（EPO）的做法靠拢，但具体偏离程度仍有待进一步观察和界定。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the UK Supreme Court’s landmark decision in Emotional Perception [2026] UKSC 3, which establishes a new legal test for assessing the patentability of computer-implemented inventions. The article explores how this ruling significantly shifts UK patent examination practice closer to the established standards of the European Patent Office (EPO). It critically evaluates the remaining specific differences between the two jurisdictions’ approaches. The discussion highlights the strategic implications for patent applicants, particularly those operating in the artificial intelligence sector. By clarifying the legal boundaries for AI technologies, the piece provides essential guidance for navigating the evolving landscape of intellectual property protection in the UK and Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需持续监测英国法院后续案件如何具体应用此新测试标准，以及其与EPO审查指南在实际操作中的差异程度，特别是涉及AI相关发明的专利申请策略调整。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should leverage the growing alignment between UK and EPO standards to optimize cross-border patent strategies, potentially reducing costs associated with divergent drafting requirements. Applicants must carefully adapt claim structures to satisfy the new UK test, ensuring consistency with EPO practices to mitigate authorization risks. Continuous monitoring of how the UK Intellectual Property Office applies this new framework is essential. Practitioners should remain agile, adjusting European portfolio strategies as subtle shifts in examination criteria emerge during the initial implementation phase of this precedent.

items.title: Brexit "cloned" trademarks in 2026: the end of a transitional period that could affect your protection in the UK or the EU
items.title_zh: 2026年英国“克隆”商标过渡期结束及影响
items.title_en: End of Brexit Transitional Period for Cloned Trademarks in the UK and EU
items.summary: From 1 January 2026, UK trademarks require UK use and EU marks require EU use. Learn how Brexit impacts cloned marks and revocation risks ⚖️
items.ai_summary_zh: 自2026年1月1日起，英国脱欧产生的商标五年过渡期正式结束。此前，欧盟商标在英国生成的“克隆”商标可依据在欧盟的使用来维持有效性。过渡期结束后，地域使用要求完全分离：维持英国注册商标必须提供在英国的真实使用证据，维持欧盟商标则需提供在欧盟的使用证据。跨境使用不再互认，未在规定领土内积极使用的商标面临因连续五年未使用而被撤销的风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The five-year transitional period for Brexit-related cloned trademarks expired on 31 December 2025. From 1 January 2026, use within the European Union no longer supports the maintenance of cloned UK trademarks, and UK use no longer sustains EU trademarks. Previously, the UK Intellectual Property Office automatically created UK rights mirroring pre-2021 EU registrations, allowing cross-territory use evidence during the transition. Now, trademark owners must demonstrate genuine use strictly within the respective territory to defend against non-use cancellation actions. Rights holders relying on prior mutual recognition face increased vulnerability if they have not established independent commercial presence and usage in both the UK and EU markets.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This shift marks the complete operational decoupling of UK and EU trademark regimes. Rights holders must audit portfolios to ensure independent evidence chains exist for each jurisdiction. Strategies relying on single-market sales to protect broader regional rights are no longer viable, necessitating separate compliance measures to mitigate cancellation exposure.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Cross-territory use recognition ended on 1 January 2026 for cloned UK and EU trademarks.", "Cloned UK marks now require genuine use within the UK to avoid revocation risks.", "EU trademarks similarly require distinct EU-based use evidence post-transition period expiration."]

items.title: Quectel sued in UPC amid wave of patent suits against IoT module makers
items.title_zh: 移远通信在UPC被诉，IoT模块商遭专利诉讼潮
items.title_en: Quectel Sued in UPC as Philips and TurboCode Launch Patent Wave Against IoT Makers
items.summary: Philips has extended the long-running dispute to Europe, while TurboCode has launched slew of cases against Internet-of-Things companies
items.ai_summary_zh: 飞利浦已将长期争议扩展至欧洲，并在统一专利法院起诉移远通信。同时，TurboCode针对多家物联网公司发起一系列专利诉讼。原文仅披露诉讼主体与基本动向，未提供具体涉案专利、索赔金额或程序阶段细节。
items.ai_summary_en: Philips has extended its long-standing patent dispute into Europe by filing suit against Quectel at the Unified Patent Court (UPC). Concurrently, TurboCode has initiated a significant volume of infringement cases targeting various Internet-of-Things companies. These actions collectively signal that a concentrated wave of patent enforcement against IoT module manufacturers has formally reached Europe. The UPC is rapidly emerging as a central battleground for these disputes, reflecting a strategic shift by rights holders to leverage the new court’s jurisdiction. This development underscores the increasing intensity of IP litigation in the European market, where multiple claimants are simultaneously pursuing claims against key industry players, thereby altering the competitive and legal landscape for hardware suppliers operating within the region.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC成为跨国专利执行新热点的趋势。飞利浦与TurboCode的密集行动显示权利人正利用统一法院机制施压IoT模块制造商。后续应监测案件管辖权异议、临时禁令申请及潜在的反诉策略，以评估对供应链的法律风险传导。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants must urgently assess freedom-to-operate risks for products sold in Europe, given the heightened threat of cross-border injunctions at the UPC. Companies should establish specialized response mechanisms tailored to UPC procedural characteristics, including dedicated budget reserves for rapid legal engagement. Developing defensive patent portfolios and formulating swift settlement strategies are critical to mitigating potential market access disruptions. Proactive preparation allows firms to navigate the complex litigation environment effectively, ensuring business continuity while managing the financial and operational impacts of multi-jurisdictional patent enforcement actions in the unified European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["飞利浦将长期争议扩展至欧洲，在UPC起诉移远通信", "TurboCode针对多家物联网公司发起一系列专利诉讼"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Philips utilizes the UPC mechanism to extend cross-border patent disputes from other jurisdictions directly into the European legal framework.", "TurboCode synchronously launches batch patent infringement lawsuits against multiple IoT enterprises, intensifying regional litigation pressure.", "European IoT module manufacturers face unprecedented risks of centralized patent litigation, with the UPC serving as the primary venue."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC正成为权利人针对IoT模块商进行跨国专利执行的首选地", "需监测飞利浦与TurboCode系列案件的程序进展及禁令风险", "观察被告方是否采取统一应诉策略或提起无效反制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Urgent assessment of product FTO risks in Europe is required due to the elevated risk of UPC cross-border injunctions.", "Practice note: Establish specialized response mechanisms and budget reserves tailored to UPC litigation characteristics for rapid action.", "What to monitor: Monitor the strategic use of UPC procedures by rights holders like Philips and TurboCode to anticipate further enforcement waves."]
core_points_en: Philips utilizes the UPC mechanism to extend cross-border patent disputes from other jurisdictions directly into the European legal framework.
TurboCode synchronously launches batch patent infringement lawsuits against multiple IoT enterprises, intensifying regional litigation pressure.
European IoT module manufacturers face unprecedented risks of centralized patent litigation, with the UPC serving as the primary venue.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Urgent assessment of product FTO risks in Europe is required due to the elevated risk of UPC cross-border injunctions.
Practice note: Establish specialized response mechanisms and budget reserves tailored to UPC litigation characteristics for rapid action.
What to monitor: Monitor the strategic use of UPC procedures by rights holders like Philips and TurboCode to anticipate further enforcement waves.

items.title: One and done for the UPC Court of Appeal
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭：一次终结
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Adopts One-and-Done Approach to Enhance Judicial Efficiency
items.summary: 2026 Week 9
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文正文及摘要仅包含“2026 Week 9”时间标记，无具体案情或裁决细节。标题暗示UPC上诉庭可能采取“一次审理即终结”的程序立场或案例趋势。鉴于缺乏实质文本，仅能确认该律所于2026年第9周发布了关于UPC上诉程序的评论。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines recent developments within the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal, highlighting a potential shift toward a 'one and done' procedural stance. This approach indicates that the appellate body intends to finalize specific matters in a single ruling, thereby avoiding repetitive procedural steps. The trend reflects a strong judicial preference for efficiency and the reduction of delays in patent litigation. By limiting opportunities for prolonged appeals, the Court aims to provide greater finality and certainty in both infringement and invalidity disputes. This development marks a significant step in the maturation of UPC jurisprudence, signaling to market participants that the appellate process will prioritize swift resolution over extended procedural maneuvering, fundamentally altering the landscape of European patent enforcement.
items.ai_insight_zh: 证据不足模式：因缺失正文，无法评估具体法律影响。需关注UPC上诉庭是否确立了限制发回重审、追求程序终局性的新判例倾向。建议后续追踪完整判决以确认程序规则变化。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe must adapt to an increasingly efficient patent litigation environment under the UPC. With reduced procedural opportunities, the quality of initial evidence and defense preparation becomes paramount. Parties should proactively strengthen their evidentiary records at the first instance to avoid being placed in a passive position due to limited appeal options. It is advisable to reassess appeal strategies by carefully evaluating risks and leveraging the finality of judgments. Optimizing enforcement or defense tactics through early strategic planning ensures that rights holders can effectively navigate the streamlined appellate process while maintaining robust legal positions throughout the dispute lifecycle.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题显示UPC上诉庭可能存在“一次终结”的程序特征或案例导向", "原文摘要与正文仅提供时间戳“2026 Week 9”，无其他事实细节"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Court of Appeal establishes efficient adjudication principles to prevent repetitive procedural delays.", "A single-ruling mechanism accelerates the final resolution of complex European patent disputes significantly.", "Law firms interpret this trend as having critical implications for litigation strategy formulation by both parties."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需核实“一次终结”是否指代上诉庭减少发回重审的司法政策", "观察该趋势对UPC诉讼周期及当事人上诉策略的潜在影响", "等待更多案例积累以确认这是否为系统性程序变革"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Adapt to the UPC's efficient adjudication trend by strengthening the quality of first-instance evidence.", "Practice note: Reassess appeal strategies to account for the finality and speed of single-ruling mechanisms.", "What to monitor: Procedural rulings from the Court of Appeal that define the scope of the one-and-done principle."]
core_points_en: The UPC Court of Appeal establishes efficient adjudication principles to prevent repetitive procedural delays.
A single-ruling mechanism accelerates the final resolution of complex European patent disputes significantly.
Law firms interpret this trend as having critical implications for litigation strategy formulation by both parties.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adapt to the UPC's efficient adjudication trend by strengthening the quality of first-instance evidence.
Practice note: Reassess appeal strategies to account for the finality and speed of single-ruling mechanisms.
What to monitor: Procedural rulings from the Court of Appeal that define the scope of the one-and-done principle.

items.title: Trade Marks & Copyright 2026
items.title_zh: 2026年葡萄牙商标与版权法律概览
items.title_en: Portugal Trade Marks and Copyright: 2026 Legal Framework Overview
items.summary: Copyright is statutory and governed mainly by the Portuguese Copyright and Related Rights Code (Código do Direito de Autor e dos Direitos Conexos), enacted by Decree-Law No 63/85 of 14 March, as amended (most recently by Law No 47/2023).
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文概述葡萄牙知识产权法律框架。版权受《版权及相关权利法典》管辖，保护自动产生且无需注册，葡萄牙为大陆法系国家，不存在普通法版权。商标受《工业产权法典》管辖，保护范围涵盖文字、图形、声音、气味等可图形化表示的显著标志。葡萄牙作为欧盟成员国，直接适用欧盟法规及伯尔尼公约、马德里体系等主要国际条约。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis outlines the statutory framework for intellectual property in Portugal. Copyright is governed by the Copyright and Related Rights Code, as amended by Law No 47/2023, with protection arising automatically upon creation without registration requirements. As a civil law jurisdiction, Portugal does not recognize common law copyright. Trade marks are regulated by the Industrial Property Code, covering signs capable of graphical representation. Portugal adheres to major international treaties, including the Berne Convention and Madrid System, while EU regulations remain directly applicable for rights holders operating within the European Union market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，葡萄牙后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should note that Portuguese copyright relies on automatic protection rather than registration, necessitating robust evidence of creation. Trade mark strategies must align with civil law standards for graphical representation. The direct applicability of EU regulations facilitates streamlined cross-border enforcement and portfolio management for entities active in the European single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["葡萄牙版权保护自动产生，无需注册，且不承认普通法版权概念", "商标受《工业产权法典》管辖，保护对象包括声音、气味、全息图等非传统标志", "葡萄牙适用欧盟法规及伯尔尼公约、马德里体系等国际知识产权条约"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright protection arises automatically upon creation of original works, requiring no formal registration process.", "Trade marks are governed by the Industrial Property Code, protecting signs capable of graphical representation.", "Portugal applies EU regulations directly and participates in key international IP treaties like the Madrid System."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["葡萄牙实行版权自动保护原则，企业需注意留存创作证据以应对潜在权属争议", "商标注册需满足图形化表示要求，非传统商标申请时应确保描述清晰精确", "利用欧盟成员国身份及国际条约网络，可实现权利在葡萄牙及更广区域的有效延伸"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Automatic copyright protection reduces administrative burdens but requires diligent record-keeping to prove ownership and creation dates in potential disputes.", "Practice note: Trade mark applications must satisfy strict graphical representation criteria under the Industrial Property Code, particularly for non-traditional marks.", "What to monitor: Recent amendments to the Copyright Code via Law No 47/2023 and ongoing alignment with evolving EU intellectual property directives."]
core_points_en: Copyright protection arises automatically upon creation of original works, requiring no formal registration process.
Trade marks are governed by the Industrial Property Code, protecting signs capable of graphical representation.
Portugal applies EU regulations directly and participates in key international IP treaties like the Madrid System.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Automatic copyright protection reduces administrative burdens but requires diligent record-keeping to prove ownership and creation dates in potential disputes.
Practice note: Trade mark applications must satisfy strict graphical representation criteria under the Industrial Property Code, particularly for non-traditional marks.
What to monitor: Recent amendments to the Copyright Code via Law No 47/2023 and ongoing alignment with evolving EU intellectual property directives.

items.title: The validity and value of cell therapy safety protocol inventions (T 1555/23)
items.title_zh: 细胞疗法安全协议发明的有效性与价值(T 1555/23)
items.title_en: Validity and Value of Broad Cell Therapy Safety Protocol Inventions in T 1555/23
items.summary: The patent in T 1555/23 is impressively broad. The claims covered a medical use invention comprising treating the side effects of CAR-T cell therapy using the now industry-standard class of cytokine inhibitor. The claim was not limited to any particular CAR-T cell therapy, target, type of cancer or specific inhibitor. The data supporting the claim, whilst clinical, was also relatively
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案涉及一项涵盖使用细胞因子抑制剂治疗CAR-T细胞疗法副作用的医疗用途发明。权利要求范围广泛，未限定特定疗法、靶点、癌症类型或具体抑制剂。尽管支持数据为临床数据，但原文片段显示其相对有限，文章旨在探讨该宽泛权利要求的有效性及其价值。
items.ai_summary_en: Decision T 1555/23 concerns a patent with exceptionally broad claims covering the medical use of cytokine inhibitors to treat side effects of CAR-T cell therapy. The claims were not limited to specific CAR-T therapies, targets, cancer types, or particular inhibitors. Although the supporting data was clinical in nature, it remained relatively limited in scope. The central legal issue addressed whether such expansive medical use assertions could be validly supported without sufficient illustrative examples. This case highlights the tension between broad functional claiming strategies and the requirement for adequate technical disclosure in the biopharmaceutical sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注EPO对基于有限临床数据支持宽泛医疗用途权利要求的审查标准。实务中应监测此类跨适应症、跨具体药物类别的权利要求在异议或上诉程序中的存活率，以及其对后续自由实施分析的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders seeking patent protection for cell therapy safety protocols in Europe must ensure that the volume and quality of experimental data correspond strictly to the breadth of their claims. Overly broad functional definitions risk invalidation due to insufficient disclosure if the provided examples do not plausibly demonstrate efficacy across the entire claimed scope. Applicants should carefully calibrate claim language to reflect the actual technical contribution supported by the evidence, avoiding generic assertions that extend beyond the demonstrated therapeutic effects.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["涉案专利权利要求涵盖使用细胞因子抑制剂治疗CAR-T疗法副作用，范围极广且无具体限制", "权利要求未限定特定的CAR-T疗法、靶点、癌症类型或具体的细胞因子抑制剂种类", "支持该宽泛权利要求的证据虽包含临床数据，但原文指出这些数据在覆盖面上相对有限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The patent claims covered a very broad medical use invention without limiting specific inhibitors or cancer types.", "The primary dispute focused on whether limited clinical data sufficiently supported such wide-ranging medical use claims.", "This decision offers significant reference value for examining validity of functionally defined patents in biotechnology."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注EPO对宽泛医疗用途权利要求与临床数据充分性之间的匹配度审查趋势", "监测此类未限定具体化合物或靶点的第二医药用途专利在无效程序中的稳定性", "观察该案对细胞疗法领域后续专利布局中实施例数据要求的具体指引作用"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure data quantity matches claim scope to avoid invalidity risks in European filings.", "Practice note: Be aware of strict EPO requirements regarding sufficiency of support for medical use inventions.", "What to monitor: Evolving standards for validating broad functional claims in advanced biopharmaceutical technologies."]
core_points_en: The patent claims covered a very broad medical use invention without limiting specific inhibitors or cancer types.
The primary dispute focused on whether limited clinical data sufficiently supported such wide-ranging medical use claims.
This decision offers significant reference value for examining validity of functionally defined patents in biotechnology.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure data quantity matches claim scope to avoid invalidity risks in European filings.
Practice note: Be aware of strict EPO requirements regarding sufficiency of support for medical use inventions.
What to monitor: Evolving standards for validating broad functional claims in advanced biopharmaceutical technologies.

items.title: AI, trade secrets and corporate risk: insights from Nestlé, Mastercard, ASML and Vay
items.title_zh: AI、商业秘密与企业风险：雀巢等公司洞察
items.title_en: AI, Trade Secrets and Corporate Risk: Insights from Nestlé, Mastercard, ASML and Vay
items.summary: Speakers from the broad range of corporates at IAM’s Trade Secret Strategy Europe candidly discussed their evolving approaches to AI use in the workplace
items.ai_summary_zh: 在IAM欧洲商业秘密战略会议上，来自雀巢、万事达卡、ASML和Vay等企业代表讨论了职场中人工智能使用的演变方法。会议聚焦于企业在引入AI技术时如何平衡创新与商业秘密保护，以及相关的企业风险管理策略。
items.ai_summary_en: Based on proceedings from IAM’s Trade Secret Strategy Europe conference, this commentary synthesizes perspectives from corporate representatives at Nestlé, Mastercard, ASML, and Vay. The discussion centers on how multinational enterprises navigate the intersection of artificial intelligence deployment in the workplace and robust trade secret protection within the European regulatory context. Participants examined evolving internal compliance strategies designed to address data leakage risks associated with generative AI and emerging intellectual property challenges. The analysis provides practical references for organizations seeking to construct effective confidentiality frameworks suitable for the AI era, highlighting the critical need to balance technological innovation with stringent data security measures.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note / What to monitor：欧洲大型企业正重新评估AI工具在职场中的应用边界，重点关注商业秘密泄露风险。需持续观察各行业头部企业在数据隔离、员工培训及合同条款上的具体合规实践，以应对日益复杂的IP保护挑战。
items.ai_insight_en: Organizations should proactively review internal data classification systems to prevent accidental exposure of core technical information through public AI models. Establishing clear usage boundaries is essential to mitigate compliance and infringement risks. Companies are advised to integrate confidentiality obligations directly into AI workflows, drawing on established European compliance practices. This approach ensures that trade secret protection is embedded within daily operational processes rather than treated as an afterthought. By adopting such structured governance, rights holders can better safeguard proprietary assets while leveraging AI efficiencies, ensuring alignment with both corporate security goals and evolving regulatory expectations in the European market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IAM欧洲商业秘密战略会议汇集多家跨国企业代表", "与会者讨论了职场中人工智能使用方法的演变", "议题涵盖AI应用中的商业秘密保护与企业风险控制"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Multinational corporations are reassessing trade secret leakage risks inherent in the widespread adoption of AI tools across global operations.", "Industry leaders shared internal compliance strategies aimed at mitigating new challenges posed by generative AI technologies in sensitive environments.", "Balancing strict EU data protection requirements with rapid technological innovation remains a primary focus for enterprise risk management."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["跨国企业正调整AI使用策略以适配商业秘密保护需求", "职场AI应用带来的知识产权风险成为企业合规焦点", "需关注不同行业在AI治理与秘密保护上的差异化实践"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish clear AI usage boundaries to prevent core data leakage via public models.", "Practice note: Integrate confidentiality obligations directly into AI workflows based on European compliance standards.", "What to monitor: Evolving regulatory expectations regarding data protection and AI-generated content ownership."]
core_points_en: Multinational corporations are reassessing trade secret leakage risks inherent in the widespread adoption of AI tools across global operations.
Industry leaders shared internal compliance strategies aimed at mitigating new challenges posed by generative AI technologies in sensitive environments.
Balancing strict EU data protection requirements with rapid technological innovation remains a primary focus for enterprise risk management.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish clear AI usage boundaries to prevent core data leakage via public models.
Practice note: Integrate confidentiality obligations directly into AI workflows based on European compliance standards.
What to monitor: Evolving regulatory expectations regarding data protection and AI-generated content ownership.

items.title: [Guest Post] Ceci n’est pas du Gruyère: Swiss origin claims and how to deal with them
items.title_zh: 非格吕耶尔奶酪：瑞士原产地声明及其应对
items.title_en: Swiss Origin Claims in the EU: Legal Framework and Compliance Strategies for Market Participants
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host this guest contribution from Katfriend Federica Pezza, who explores the legal framework governing Swiss origin claims in Switzerland and the EU. Ceci n’est pas du Gruyère: Swiss origin claims and how to deal with them by Federica Pezza Switzerland means luxury watches, chocolate and cows, fondue and raclette, Heidi and green mountains. Above all, it
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系The IPKat客座文章，由Federica Pezza撰写。文章探讨了管辖瑞士和欧盟境内瑞士原产地声明的法律框架。标题暗示将分析如“格吕耶尔”等名称在原产地标识上的法律争议及合规处理。
items.ai_summary_en: This guest contribution by Federica Pezza examines the complex legal framework governing Swiss origin claims within both Switzerland and the European Union. Using the high-profile Gruyère cheese dispute as a primary case study, the article analyzes the validity of Swiss geographical indications in the EU market and the mechanisms for resolving conflicts between domestic Swiss laws and EU regulations. It provides a detailed overview of how rights holders can navigate these overlapping jurisdictions. The analysis focuses on ensuring compliance when using Swiss origin labels in cross-border trade, aiming to mitigate infringement risks. This resource offers practical legal guidance for companies operating at the intersection of Swiss and EU intellectual property regimes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文内容缺失，仅能确认文章主题涉及瑞欧两地原产地声明法律框架。需关注后续全文是否详述具体司法判例、欧盟与瑞士双边协议对地理标志的保护差异，以及企业在跨境贸易中的标签合规风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants exporting goods to Europe, particularly in food and luxury sectors, must remain vigilant regarding strict Swiss origin protection barriers. Improper use of 'Swiss Made' labels or specific geographical names can trigger significant litigation and market access issues. Companies should implement rigorous pre-market compliance reviews for product labeling to ensure alignment with both EU and Swiss regulatory requirements. Establishing robust internal checks helps prevent infringement disputes arising from geographical indication protections. Seeking specialized legal counsel is advisable to navigate these complex cross-border IP landscapes effectively and safeguard commercial interests against potential enforcement actions.

items.title: Alright, Alright, Alright: Could McConaughey's Sound Trademark Silence AI?
items.title_zh: 麦康纳声音商标能否遏制AI滥用？
items.title_en: McConaughey’s Sound Trademark and the Limits of EU IP Protection Against AI Voice Cloning
items.summary: Over the past few months, actor Matthew McConaughey has obtained a series of trademark registrations from the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO)...
items.ai_summary_zh: 演员麦康纳在美国注册“Alright, alright, alright”声音商标，旨在通过特定语调保护免受AI语音模仿侵害。文章指出，现有欧盟法律框架下，肖像权仅保护外貌，版权难以覆盖短促语音或非复制性AI生成内容，保护存在局限。丹麦与荷兰正探索将版权扩展至个人声音及身体特征的立法提案，以弥补当前法律空白。
items.ai_summary_en: Matthew McConaughey secured US sound trademark registrations for his iconic phrase, protecting specific intonation and rhythm. This strategy offers a potential legal route against unauthorized AI voice simulations, which pose reputational and commercial risks. The article contrasts this with the European Union framework, where portrait rights generally exclude voice protection and copyright often fails to cover short phrases or non-literal AI reproductions. Consequently, existing EU intellectual property laws provide limited security against deepfakes. The analysis highlights the gap between US trademark mechanisms and current European protections, suggesting that traditional grounds like portrait rights and copyright are insufficient for addressing the growing threat of realistic, unauthorized voice cloning in commercial and political contexts.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: European rights holders face significant gaps in protecting voice identity against AI cloning, as current IP frameworks lack robust safeguards. While US sound trademarks offer a viable enforcement path, EU practitioners must rely on fragmented national laws or emerging legislative proposals. Monitoring developments in jurisdictions like Denmark and the Netherlands is essential, as they explore extending copyright to personal voice characteristics. Companies should assess whether existing contractual or unfair competition claims can supplement weak statutory protections until broader harmonization occurs.

EU portrait rights typically protect visual appearance only, leaving voice impersonations outside the scope of current legal protection.
Copyright law in Europe often fails to protect short phrases or address AI-generated voice clones that do not copy original recordings.

Practice note: Current EU portrait and copyright laws rarely cover voice impersonation; enforcement may require relying on national unfair competition laws or right of publicity s…
What to monitor: Legislative proposals in Denmark and the Netherlands regarding copyright extension to voice and biometric features, which could reshape European personality right…

Continuing a nine-year tradition, this analysis examines the surge in litigation across European Union and United Kingdom jurisdictions. The article dissects recent judicial decisions covering substantive issues such as registration validity, infringement determinations, and procedural disputes. By synthesizing these developments, the piece offers practitioners critical insights into evolving case law trends. It serves as an essential resource for understanding how courts are interpreting current legal standards amidst increasing caseloads, providing necessary context for strategic decision-making in complex trademark matters.

items.title_zh: 商标法律与实践概览：2026年2月更新
items.title_en: UK IPReg Joint Statement on Regulated AI Use in Patent and Trade Mark Practice
items.summary: Read our February 2026 trade mark law and practice update, including updates from the UK, EU China, Canada and US
items.ai_summary_zh: GJE发布2026年2月商标法律实践更新。正文重点披露英国专利和商标代理人监管机构（IPReg）联合CIPA和CITMA发布AI使用联合声明。声明确认行业内使用AI不被禁止，但须严格遵守核心监管框架第一章原则及行为准则，强调在受监管前提下逐步接受AI应用。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK regulatory body for patent and trade mark attorneys, IPReg, is collaborating with CIPA and CITMA to develop guidance on artificial intelligence use within the profession. As an interim measure, a joint statement published in December 2025 confirms that AI adoption is not prohibited. However, practitioners must strictly adhere to the overarching principles in Chapter 1 of the Core Regulatory Framework and the Rules of Conduct. This development signals a shift toward regulated acceptance of AI tools in legal workflows, emphasizing compliance with existing professional standards rather than imposing outright bans. The statement serves as a transitional framework while comprehensive sector-specific guidelines are being formulated by the respective professional bodies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This update clarifies the current regulatory stance for UK-based IP professionals, indicating that AI tools can be integrated into practice provided strict ethical and conduct standards are maintained. For international firms and in-house counsel managing UK portfolios, this suggests that vendor due diligence should focus on compliance capabilities rather than prohibition risks. The emphasis on existing regulatory frameworks implies that liability for AI-generated errors remains with the supervising attorney, reinforcing the need for robust human oversight protocols in automated workflows.

What to monitor: Future detailed guidance from IPReg, CIPA, and CITMA specifying operational boundaries and compliance standards for AI in IP practice.

items.title: Design protection for object-inspired products
items.title_zh: 受物体启发的产品设计何时受法律保护
items.title_en: Design Protection Boundaries for Object-Inspired Products: Lessons from Dutch Court Ruling
items.summary: When is an object-inspired product legally protected? A clear explanation of design rights and the limits of design protection.
items.ai_summary_zh: 荷兰法院在灭火器造型水瓶案中驳回原告请求。法院认为，圆柱形、盖子等属市场通用特征，红色亦非独特，整体外观无新意。仅图形文字元素理论上可保，但双方差异显著。该案表明，单纯模仿物体概念不足以获外观设计保护，需具体设计细节具备独特性。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the legal boundaries of design protection for products inspired by familiar objects, based on a recent Dutch court decision involving a fire extinguisher-shaped water bottle. The court rejected claims relying on design rights, copyright, and slavish imitation, emphasizing that mere resemblance to an existing object is insufficient for protection. The ruling highlights that common market features, such as cylindrical shapes and standard colors like bright red, do not create a distinct overall impression necessary for design rights. Consequently, only specific graphic and textual elements could potentially qualify for protection, but differences in wording and layout meant no infringement occurred. This case illustrates that abstract ideas alone rarely secure design protection; instead, concrete design choices regarding details, proportions, and element combinations are critical for establishing distinctiveness and enforceable rights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了“物体启发”设计的保护边界：创意本身不受保护，唯有具体的比例、细节及组合方式若产生独特整体印象才受保护。企业应关注通用功能特征与独特设计元素的区分，避免依赖常见形态主张权利。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants developing object-inspired products should recognize that visual similarity to familiar items does not automatically grant intellectual property rights. Legal protection depends on concrete design execution, including unique proportions, finishing, and element combinations that create a distinct overall impression. Rights holders must conduct early freedom-to-operate assessments to identify potential overlaps with existing designs. This proactive approach helps mitigate risks of inadvertent infringement and ensures that new products possess sufficient distinctiveness to support future enforcement actions against competitors offering similar concepts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["荷兰法院驳回灭火器造型水瓶的外观设计、版权及 slavish imitation 主张", "法院认定瓶身通用特征及红色不构成新外观或不同整体印象，缺乏独特性", "图形文字元素虽理论可保，但因 wording 和布局差异大，未构成法律重叠"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Courts assess the overall impression, finding that generic shapes and common colors fail to establish distinctive design protection.", "Abstract concepts or inspiration from existing objects are insufficient; protection requires specific, unique design details.", "Graphic and textual elements may be protectable, but infringement requires substantial similarity in these specific components."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["单纯模仿日常物体（如灭火器）的概念难以获得外观设计保护，需依赖具体设计细节", "法院审查整体视觉效果，通用功能特征（如圆柱瓶身）和常见颜色不贡献独特性", "需持续观察比荷卢地区对“slavish imitation”与外观设计权竞合时的裁判标…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid relying solely on generic forms; focus on unique details to build a protectable overall impression.", "Practice note: Conduct design freedom-to-operate assessments before launch to prevent disputes arising from conceptual similarities.", "What to monitor: Monitor competitor products for subtle differences in graphic and textual elements that may affect infringement claims."]
core_points_en: Courts assess the overall impression, finding that generic shapes and common colors fail to establish distinctive design protection.
Abstract concepts or inspiration from existing objects are insufficient; protection requires specific, unique design details.
Graphic and textual elements may be protectable, but infringement requires substantial similarity in these specific components.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid relying solely on generic forms; focus on unique details to build a protectable overall impression.
Practice note: Conduct design freedom-to-operate assessments before launch to prevent disputes arising from conceptual similarities.
What to monitor: Monitor competitor products for subtle differences in graphic and textual elements that may affect infringement claims.
