items.title: Colour trademarks: A rare but sought-after form of protection in the EU
items.title_zh: 欧盟颜色商标：罕见但备受追捧的保护形式
items.title_en: EU Colour Trademarks: High Bar for Registration and Distinctiveness Requirements
items.summary: Discover how to protect colour trademarks in the EU, including how to satisfy distinctive requirements.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章以德国Weischer公司颜色商标申请被拒为例，解析欧盟颜色商标注册难点。欧盟知识产权局认为该颜色缺乏显著性，相关公众难以仅凭颜色识别来源。文中对比红牛成功案例，指出颜色商标保护范围有限，需证明获得显著性，且法律上避免授予颜色垄断以维持公平竞争。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the stringent requirements for registering colour trademarks in the European Union, using the EUIPO’s rejection of Weischer GmbH’s application as a primary case study. The Office determined that the single colour lacked distinctiveness because it was customary in the trade and not easily remembered by consumers as an origin indicator. The article contrasts this with Red Bull’s successful enforcement, which relied on extensive use establishing acquired distinctiveness. It highlights that while colour is vital for marketing, legal protection is narrow. Applicants must prove the colour independently identifies commercial origin, often requiring substantial evidence of long-term use or combining colours with other elements to overcome inherent non-distinctiveness.
items.ai_insight_zh: 颜色商标在欧盟注册门槛极高，申请人需准备充分证据证明“获得显著性”。实务中应警惕单一颜色申请被驳风险，考虑结合图形或文字元素。需持续关注欧盟法院关于非传统商标显著性认定的最新判例动向。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders seeking to protect brand identity through colour, relying solely on a single, common shade carries a high risk of refusal due to lack of distinctiveness and potential monopolization concerns. To enhance registrability, applicants should prioritize accumulating robust evidence of long-term use to demonstrate acquired distinctiveness among the relevant public. Alternatively, adopting a combined strategy that integrates specific colours with figurative or verbal elements can lower legal risks and broaden protection scope. This approach balances marketing objectives with the strict legal standards required to secure and enforce colour-based intellectual property rights effectively within the EU market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟知识产权局驳回Weischer公司颜色商标申请，认定其缺乏显著性且为行业常用色", "颜色商标获准注册难度大，因公众难以仅凭颜色识别商品来源，保护范围较文字商标更窄", "欧盟判例法强调，为避免不当垄断，仅当颜色通过长期使用获得显著性时才可能获准注册"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The EUIPO rejected Weischer’s colour mark because the shade was customary in the metal products sector and lacked inherent distinctiveness.", "EU case law applies standard distinctiveness criteria to colour marks, yet proving origin function remains significantly harder than for verbal signs.", "Successful registration typically requires proof of acquired distinctiveness through extensive use or strategic combination with other trademark elements."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["颜色商标申请需重点举证“获得显著性”，单纯描述颜色特征极易被驳", "相比传统商标，颜色商标保护范围受限，维权难度较大，需综合布局", "关注欧盟知识产权局对颜色显著性审查的一致性标准及后续司法确认情况"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Single colour marks face high refusal rates; strong evidence of acquired distinctiveness is critical for success.", "Practice note: Consider combining colours with verbal or figurative elements to mitigate distinctiveness challenges and broaden protection.", "What to monitor: EUIPO decisions on non-traditional marks and CJEU rulings regarding acquired distinctiveness thresholds for colour combinations."]
core_points_en: The EUIPO rejected Weischer’s colour mark because the shade was customary in the metal products sector and lacked inherent distinctiveness.
EU case law applies standard distinctiveness criteria to colour marks, yet proving origin function remains significantly harder than for verbal signs.
Successful registration typically requires proof of acquired distinctiveness through extensive use or strategic combination with other trademark elements.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Single colour marks face high refusal rates; strong evidence of acquired distinctiveness is critical for success.
Practice note: Consider combining colours with verbal or figurative elements to mitigate distinctiveness challenges and broaden protection.
What to monitor: EUIPO decisions on non-traditional marks and CJEU rulings regarding acquired distinctiveness thresholds for colour combinations.

items.title: Victory for client Rematec before the UPC Court of Appeal | Grünecker
items.title_zh: Rematec在统一专利法院上诉庭胜诉
items.title_en: Victory for client Rematec before the UPC Court of Appeal | Grünecker
items.summary: Grünecker turned the tide and succeeded before the UPC Court of Appeal in proceedings for our client Rematec GmbH & Co KG,
items.ai_summary_zh: Grünecker律所代理客户Rematec在统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭取得胜诉。上诉庭撤销了一审法院完全撤销欧洲专利EP2548648B1的决定，转而驳回无效反诉并认定专利侵权成立。法院在解释权利要求时引用了EPO扩大上诉委员会G1/24号决定，结合说明书及引用的现有技术进行专利性评估。
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案凸显UPC上诉庭在专利权利要求解释中对EPO判例法（如G1/24）的遵循，强调说明书及引用现有技术的重要性。对于涉及复杂技术事实的欧洲专利诉讼，上诉阶段仍存在扭转一审无效认定的可能，需关注UPC对EPO审查标准的司法采纳趋势。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭撤销一审撤销专利决定，认定EP2548648B1侵权成立且有效", "法院援引EPO G1/24决定，结合说明书及引用文献解释权利要求以评估专利性", "Grünecker律所代理Rematec成功逆转一审不利局面，赢得上诉诉讼"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭在权利要求解释中明确参考EPO扩大上诉委员会决定，体现司法与审查标准趋同", "一审完全无效判决并非终局，上诉阶段基于证据重新评估可逆转结果", "需持续观察UPC在处理专利性与侵权关系时对现有技术引用的具体采信标准"]

items.title: Trademark revocation: How to enforce INPI and EUIPO cost decisions
items.title_zh: 商标撤销：如何执行欧盟及法国官方的费用裁决
items.title_en: Enforcing Cost Awards in EUIPO and INPI Trademark Revocation Proceedings
items.summary: Discover how to enforce cost provisions in EUIPO and INPI trademark revocation and invalidity decisions.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文解析在欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）和法国工业产权局（INPI）的商标撤销及无效程序中，胜诉方如何执行费用承担裁决。若败诉方拒绝支付，胜诉方需确保裁决已生效且明确金额，并获取无上诉证明。针对INPI裁决，需向该局申请执行令；而执行程序本身可能产生额外成本，需权衡执行效益。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis outlines procedures for enforcing cost provisions in trademark revocation and invalidity decisions issued by the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) and the French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI). While EUIPO rules on costs across opposition, invalidity, and revocation proceedings, INPI addresses them only in revocation and invalidity actions upon request. Successful enforcement requires a final decision specifying the cost amount and a certificate confirming no appeal was filed. If the losing party refuses voluntary payment, the winning party may seek an enforcement order from INPI or pursue other legal avenues, though practitioners must weigh these steps against potentially higher enforcement costs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需注意，获取有利裁决并不自动等同于收回成本。执行过程涉及额外法律与行政成本，有时甚至超过裁决金额。企业应评估败诉方偿付意愿及执行成本效益，特别是在跨国执行场景下，需严格遵循各机构特定的程序要求，如INPI的执行令申请机制。
items.ai_insight_en: Securing a favorable cost award does not guarantee reimbursement, as enforcement involves distinct procedural hurdles and additional expenses. Rights holders must verify that decisions are final and explicitly quantify costs before initiating recovery. Given that enforcement expenditures may surpass the awarded sums, a pragmatic assessment of the debtor’s willingness to pay is essential. Understanding the specific administrative mechanisms for each office, such as INPI’s enforcement order requirement, ensures efficient portfolio management and avoids futile expenditure on low-value recoveries.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO在异议、无效及撤销程序中均裁定费用，INPI仅在无效和撤销行动中依申请裁定", "执行费用裁决的前提是裁决包含具体金额、已生效，并提供证明未提起上诉的文件", "对于INPI作出的裁决，胜诉方在获得无上诉证明后，必须向INPI申请执行令以启动强制程序"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO awards costs in opposition, invalidity, and revocation cases, whereas INPI limits cost rulings to revocation and invalidity actions.", "Enforcement requires a final decision with a specified amount and a certificate attesting that no appeal has been lodged.", "Practitioners should assess whether enforcement costs exceed the awarded amount before pursuing compulsory recovery measures against non-compliant parties."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["执行费用裁决存在隐性成本，需预先评估执行投入与回收金额的性价比", "不同机构程序差异显著，EUIPO与INPI在费用裁定范围及执行启动方式上存在制度性区别", "确保证据链完整，特别是“无上诉证明”等程序性文件，是启动强制执行的关键前置条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate the economic viability of enforcing cost awards, as procedural expenses may outweigh the recovered amounts in smaller disputes.", "Practice note: Ensure decisions include explicit cost quantifications and obtain certificates of no appeal to satisfy prerequisites for enforcement orders.", "What to monitor: Track divergent enforcement mechanisms between EUIPO and national offices like INPI to streamline post-decision recovery strategies."]
core_points_en: EUIPO awards costs in opposition, invalidity, and revocation cases, whereas INPI limits cost rulings to revocation and invalidity actions.
Enforcement requires a final decision with a specified amount and a certificate attesting that no appeal has been lodged.
Practitioners should assess whether enforcement costs exceed the awarded amount before pursuing compulsory recovery measures against non-compliant parties.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate the economic viability of enforcing cost awards, as procedural expenses may outweigh the recovered amounts in smaller disputes.
Practice note: Ensure decisions include explicit cost quantifications and obtain certificates of no appeal to satisfy prerequisites for enforcement orders.
What to monitor: Track divergent enforcement mechanisms between EUIPO and national offices like INPI to streamline post-decision recovery strategies.

items.title: New dawn or damp squib? Mediation and arbitration at the UPC
items.title_zh: 新曙光还是雷声大雨点小？UPC调解与仲裁中心展望
items.title_en: New dawn or damp squib? Mediation and arbitration at the UPC
items.summary: Will the opening of the UPC's Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre lead to more alternative dispute resolution in European patent cases?
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）的专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）将于今年在斯洛文尼亚卢布尔雅那正式开幕。尽管UPC已运行两年，但PMAC直至2024-2025年才完成主任任命及规则草案咨询。最终规则预计2026年3月发布。PMAC设卢布尔雅那和里斯本两处席位，旨在为欧洲专利纠纷提供替代性争议解决平台，弥补当前专利领域ADR使用率低的现状。
items.ai_insight_zh: PMAC的启动为欧洲专利争议提供了新的非诉讼解决渠道。鉴于目前国际层面专利ADR占比极低，实务界需关注其规则落地后的实际采纳率。企业应监测首批调解员/仲裁员名单及后续案例流向，评估其在复杂技术争议中的效率与成本优势，而非仅依赖传统诉讼。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PMAC将于今年在卢布尔雅那举行开幕式，正式处理替代性争议解决案件", "仲裁与调解规则最终版预计2026年3月公布，首轮专家名单申请已于2025年10月截止", "相比WIPO等机构中专利案件占比仅3%，PMAC有望提升专利纠纷中ADR的使用率"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["PMAC作为UPC组成部分，其正式运作标志着欧洲专利争议解决机制的多元化完善", "需观察2026年最终规则发布后，大型企业是否愿意将高价值专利纠纷转向ADR", "对比WIPO数据，专利ADR市场潜力巨大但开发不足，PMAC的实际效能待案例验证"]

items.title: Trademark protection and marketing trends: The case of 'Quite Luxury’
items.title_zh: 商标保护与营销趋势：“Quite Luxury”案解析
items.title_en: EUIPO Rejects ‘Quite Luxury’ Mark: Distinctiveness Limits for Marketing Trends
items.summary: Commercial concepts catch the imagination, but can they be registered as a trademark? Discover how to approach marketing trends in brand-naming strategies.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局于2026年1月5日驳回Fashion Holding Düsseldorf GmbH的“Quite Luxury”欧盟商标申请。审查认为该标志仅被视为指代营销概念的促销用语，缺乏指示商品来源的显著性，依据《欧盟商标条例》第7(1)(b)条予以拒绝。
items.ai_summary_en: The EUIPO rejected Fashion Holding Düsseldorf GmbH’s EU trademark application for ‘Quite Luxury’ on 5 January 2026. The Office found the sign lacked distinctive character under Article 7(1)(b) of the EUTMR, ruling that the relevant public would perceive it as a promotional expression referencing a marketing concept rather than an indication of commercial origin. This decision highlights the legal challenges in registering terms associated with global trends like ‘quiet luxury’ or ‘clean beauty.’ While such concepts drive brand naming strategies in the fashion and luxury sectors, they often fail to function as trademarks because they describe sectoral codes instead of identifying specific goods sources.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling reinforces that commercially powerful marketing trends rarely satisfy trademark distinctiveness requirements. Rights holders should avoid relying on sectoral buzzwords for core brand protection, as examiners increasingly view them as descriptive or non-distinctive promotional messages. Strategic branding must differentiate between trend participation and proprietary source identification to secure enforceable rights in the EU market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO以缺乏显著性为由驳回“Quite Luxury”商标注册申请", "相关公众将该标志视为营销概念而非商品来源标识", "裁定依据为《欧盟商标条例》第7(1)(b)条绝对驳回理由"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO refused registration for lack of distinctive character under Article 7(1)(b) EUTMR.", "Public perceives ‘Quite Luxury’ as promotional marketing concept, not source identifier.", "Application covered fashion and luxury goods including clothing, shoes, and leather items."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["营销热词直接注册为商标在欧盟面临高驳回风险", "需区分商业概念吸引力与商标法要求的来源识别功能", "观察EUIPO对时尚行业通用术语显著性审查的最新尺度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brand names derived from current marketing trends face high refusal risks due to perceived lack of distinctiveness.", "Practice note: Examiners apply strict scrutiny to signs resembling sectoral codes, requiring evidence of acquired distinctiveness or strong stylization.", "What to monitor: EUIPO’s evolving stance on absolute grounds for refusal regarding emerging lifestyle and luxury industry terminology."]
core_points_en: EUIPO refused registration for lack of distinctive character under Article 7(1)(b) EUTMR.
Public perceives ‘Quite Luxury’ as promotional marketing concept, not source identifier.
Application covered fashion and luxury goods including clothing, shoes, and leather items.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brand names derived from current marketing trends face high refusal risks due to perceived lack of distinctiveness.
Practice note: Examiners apply strict scrutiny to signs resembling sectoral codes, requiring evidence of acquired distinctiveness or strong stylization.
What to monitor: EUIPO’s evolving stance on absolute grounds for refusal regarding emerging lifestyle and luxury industry terminology.

items.title: Adjusting the hurdles: UK Supreme Court backs European test for software inventions | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 英国最高法院支持软件发明欧洲测试标准
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Adopts EPO Hardware Test for Software Inventions, Replacing Aerotel
items.summary: On 11 February, the UK Supreme Court issued a landmark ruling in Emotional Perception AI Limited v Comptroller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks¹ that reshapes UK practice on the assessment of computer-implemented inventions, aligning it with the European Patent Office’s long-standing approach.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2月11日，英国最高法院在Emotional Perception AI案中作出里程碑裁决，重塑英国计算机实施发明的评估实践。法院摒弃长期使用的Aerotel测试，转而采纳欧洲专利局的“任何硬件”方法，使英国与欧洲实践保持一致。该裁决澄清了软件和AI发明的可专利性，简化了混合发明的技术特征评估，可能提升英国作为AI专利友好法域的声誉。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court issued a landmark ruling in Emotional Perception AI Limited v Comptroller General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks. This decision fundamentally reshapes the assessment of computer-implemented inventions by abandoning the long-standing Aerotel test. Instead, the Court adopted the European Patent Office’s established “any hardware” approach. This alignment harmonizes UK practice with European standards, specifically addressing the patentability of mixed inventions such as artificial neural networks. The ruling introduces a clearer intermediate step to determine which features genuinely contribute to technical character. By simplifying eligibility assessments, the decision enhances legal certainty and potentially strengthens the UK’s reputation as a favorable jurisdiction for software and artificial intelligence patents.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此裁决标志着英国在软件及AI专利适格性审查上与欧洲专利局实践的重大趋同。对于申请人而言，这意味着在英国主张此类发明专利权时，可更多参考EPO的审查逻辑，降低了因法域差异导致的策略复杂性。需关注后续UKIPO如何据此更新审查指南，以及该标准在具体AI算法案件中的适用边界。
items.ai_insight_en: This harmonization between UK and European patent practices offers significant strategic opportunities for rights holders managing cross-border portfolios. Applicants should reassess their technical disclosures to emphasize specific hardware interactions that drive technical effects, ensuring robust support under the new framework. Leveraging the convergence of standards allows for more streamlined prosecution strategies across both jurisdictions, potentially lowering compliance costs and reducing rejection risks associated with divergent legal tests. Focusing on concrete technical contributions rather than abstract algorithms will be critical for securing protection for AI-driven innovations in this evolving landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国最高法院采纳欧洲专利局“任何硬件”方法，取代原有的Aerotel测试标准", "裁决涉及人工神经网络专利，确立了评估混合发明技术特征的中间步骤", "此前英国知识产权局拒绝申请，经高等法院推翻后又被上诉法院恢复，最终由最高法院定谳"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Supreme Court replaces the traditional Aerotel test with the European Patent Office’s “any hardware” approach for assessing patent eligibility.", "The ruling clarifies the determination of technical contribution for mixed inventions, including those involving artificial neural networks and hardware combinations.", "This decision aligns UK software patent examination practices with European standards, reducing divergence and enhancing attractiveness for AI innovation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["英国软件专利审查标准与欧洲专利局实现实质性对齐，降低跨国申请策略复杂度", "引入评估混合发明技术贡献的中间步骤，为AI及算法类发明提供更清晰的适格性路径", "需持续观察英国知识产权局后续审查指南的更新及对具体AI案例的执行尺度"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage the convergence of UK and EPO standards to streamline patent prosecution strategies and reduce cross-jurisdictional compliance costs.", "Practice note: Revise technical disclosures to explicitly highlight hardware integration and specific technical contributions arising from AI implementations.", "What to monitor: Track subsequent UKIPO examination guidelines and case law developments applying the new “any hardware” test to diverse software categories."]
core_points_en: The Supreme Court replaces the traditional Aerotel test with the European Patent Office’s “any hardware” approach for assessing patent eligibility.
The ruling clarifies the determination of technical contribution for mixed inventions, including those involving artificial neural networks and hardware combinations.
This decision aligns UK software patent examination practices with European standards, reducing divergence and enhancing attractiveness for AI innovation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage the convergence of UK and EPO standards to streamline patent prosecution strategies and reduce cross-jurisdictional compliance costs.
Practice note: Revise technical disclosures to explicitly highlight hardware integration and specific technical contributions arising from AI implementations.
What to monitor: Track subsequent UKIPO examination guidelines and case law developments applying the new “any hardware” test to diverse software categories.

items.title: Patent Law & Practice at a Glance – February 2026 - GJE
items.title_zh: 专利法律与实务概览：2026年2月刊
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court AI Patentability Ruling and Global Practice Updates
items.summary: Read our February 2026 edition of patent law and practice which includes updates from the UK, the EPO and the WIPO/PCT.
items.ai_summary_zh: GJE发布2026年2月专利法律与实务更新，涵盖英国、欧洲专利局（EPO）及世界知识产权组织（WIPO/PCT）的最新动态。正文片段明确提及英国最高法院关于人工智能神经网络可专利性的判决，显示该期内容聚焦于AI技术在欧洲及英国的专利适格性最新司法立场。
items.ai_summary_en: This February 2026 update provides a comprehensive overview of recent developments in patent law and practice across key jurisdictions. The primary focus is the landmark judgment by the UK Supreme Court regarding the patentability of artificial neural networks in the Emotional Perception case. This decision establishes critical standards for assessing technical character in AI inventions within the United Kingdom. Additionally, the analysis covers significant updates to examination practices at the European Patent Office concerning computer-implemented inventions. It also details recent procedural changes and operational insights within the WIPO/PCT international application system. These combined insights offer essential guidance for multinational patent strategies, helping rights holders navigate evolving legal landscapes and optimize their global intellectual property portfolios amidst shifting regulatory frameworks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 鉴于正文篇幅有限，仅能确认英国最高法院对AI神经网络可专利性作出判决这一事实。需持续观察该判决对英国及欧洲其他地区AI相关专利申请审查标准的具体影响，以及EPO是否随之调整相关审查指南。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the evolving judicial interpretation of AI patentability in the UK following the new Supreme Court precedent. Rights holders are advised to align their European patent strategies with the distinct examination standards of both the EPO and the UKIPO. Optimizing claim drafting to address specific jurisdictional requirements regarding technical contribution can mitigate risks of rejection based on subject matter eligibility. Utilizing the PCT pathway allows for coordinated management of applications across China, Europe, and the US, ensuring consistent protection while adapting to local legal nuances and avoiding potential pitfalls associated with abstract algorithmic claims.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["本期更新涵盖英国、欧洲专利局及WIPO/PCT体系的专利法律与实务动态", "正文提及英国最高法院就人工智能神经网络的可专利性问题作出判决"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UK Supreme Court defines key criteria for determining the patent eligibility of artificial neural network technologies.", "The EPO has updated its examination guidelines for assessing inventive step in artificial intelligence related inventions.", "Recent rule amendments and procedural adjustments within the WIPO PCT international filing system are analyzed."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注英国最高法院判决对AI专利适格性认定的具体法律标准变化", "监测EPO及英国知识产权局后续是否发布针对该判决的审查指引或政策调整"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess the stability and grant prospects of existing AI patent portfolios against the new UK Supreme Court precedents.", "Practice note: Customize neural network technology claims to address differences between Chinese and European examination standards.", "What to monitor: Track further judicial clarifications on AI technical character and EPO examination practice updates."]
core_points_en: The UK Supreme Court defines key criteria for determining the patent eligibility of artificial neural network technologies.
The EPO has updated its examination guidelines for assessing inventive step in artificial intelligence related inventions.
Recent rule amendments and procedural adjustments within the WIPO PCT international filing system are analyzed.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess the stability and grant prospects of existing AI patent portfolios against the new UK Supreme Court precedents.
Practice note: Customize neural network technology claims to address differences between Chinese and European examination standards.

items.title: Working worse is no defence: a recent UPC order reinforces feature-based claim scope (UPC_CFI_630/2025)
items.title_zh: 功能实现不佳非抗辩理由：UPC裁定强化基于特征的权利要求范围
items.title_en: Inferior Functionality No Defense: UPC Reinforces Feature-Based Claim Scope in Preliminary Injunctions
items.summary: Court docket: LD Düsseldorf
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在Occultech诉Lepu Medical案中发布临时禁令裁定。法院明确，若被控产品具备权利要求的所有结构特征，即使未完全实现预期技术优势，仍落入保护范围。裁定重申说明书和附图用于理解技术语境，且临时禁令程序中被告承担专利无效举证责任，需详细论证现有技术译文准确性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Düsseldorf Local Division ruled in preliminary injunction proceedings that a device claim’s scope covers embodiments possessing all claimed structural features, even if they do not fully realize the technical advantages described in the patent specification. The Court emphasized that claim construction must be interpreted in light of the description and drawings, rather than isolating claim features. Furthermore, the decision clarifies procedural burdens: applicants must promptly respond to contested facts with sufficient certainty for provisional measures, as summary proceedings allow limited fact-finding. Defendants bear the burden of proving invalidity and must provide detailed reasoning for any disputed prior art translations; failure to do so may result in the Court adopting the applicant’s translation version.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁定明确了UPC临时禁令中“结构特征优先”的解释原则及被告的严格举证义务。实务中，被告仅主张技术效果差异难以规避侵权，必须针对无效证据提供详尽的译文比对与法律论证，否则法院将采信原告译文并可能颁发禁令。
items.ai_insight_en: In UPC preliminary injunction proceedings, relying solely on arguments regarding inferior product functionality is unlikely to succeed against a patentee asserting structural infringement. Rights holders should focus litigation strategies on demonstrating the presence of all claimed structural features, supported by the specification. Conversely, alleged infringers challenging validity must prepare high-quality translations of prior art documents accompanied by rigorous legal and technical arguments. Insufficient substantiation of translation disputes or invalidity grounds carries significant risk of unfavorable presumptions, given the limited scope for further evidence gathering in expedited procedures.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["装置权利要求范围涵盖具备所有结构特征的实施例，即便其未完全实现预期功能或技术优势", "临时禁令程序中，被告承担专利无效举证责任；若争议现有技术译文，被告须详述己方译文正确性", "权利要求解释需结合说明书和附图理解技术语境，该原则同时适用于侵权与有效性评估"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Device claims cover inferior embodiments with all structural features present, regardless of incomplete functional realization or technical advantage.", "Summary proceedings limit factual clarification; applicants must immediately substantiate responses to defendant contestations with required certainty.", "Defendants bear the burden of proving invalidity and must provide detailed justification for disputed prior art translations to avoid adverse findings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC临时禁令程序对被告举证要求极高，需预先准备详尽的现有技术译文对比分析", "权利要求解释回归结构特征本位，单纯强调被控产品技术劣势或功能缺失不再是有效抗辩", "需持续关注UPC上诉法院是否维持此类在简易程序中严格限制事实查明的裁判倾向"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Shift defense strategies from functional differences to challenging structural feature presence or raising robust prior art defenses.", "Practice note: Ensure invalidity challenges include precise prior art translations and comprehensive reasoning to meet the strict burden of proof.", "What to monitor: How the UPC applies these claim construction principles across different Local Divisions in future expedited infringement cases."]
core_points_en: Device claims cover inferior embodiments with all structural features present, regardless of incomplete functional realization or technical advantage.
Summary proceedings limit factual clarification; applicants must immediately substantiate responses to defendant contestations with required certainty.
Defendants bear the burden of proving invalidity and must provide detailed justification for disputed prior art translations to avoid adverse findings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Shift defense strategies from functional differences to challenging structural feature presence or raising robust prior art defenses.
Practice note: Ensure invalidity challenges include precise prior art translations and comprehensive reasoning to meet the strict burden of proof.
What to monitor: How the UPC applies these claim construction principles across different Local Divisions in future expedited infringement cases.

items.title: Key Decisions of the UPC Court of Appeal 2023-2025: Volume Two - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院上诉庭2023-2025关键裁决卷二
items.title_en: Key Decisions of the UPC Court of Appeal 2023-2025: Volume Two - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.summary: Since the UPC’s opening in June 2023, the Court has developed a growing body of case law across key areas. Our first volume examined substantive Court of Appeal decisions on infringement and revocation, applications for preliminary injunctions, and two notable appellate rulings on the scope of the UPC’s jurisdiction. Volume 1 is available via our […]
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文系律所对统一专利法院（UPC）上诉庭自2023年6月成立至2025年底发布的重大程序性裁决的综述。作为系列报告第二卷，重点梳理了证据保全、中止效力、保密性、诉讼费用、代理人资格、语言变更及诉讼中止等关键程序问题的判例，旨在澄清法院对《程序规则》的解释与适用，反映UPC司法实践的演变。
items.ai_insight_zh: 该分析揭示了UPC早期司法实践中程序规则的具体适用逻辑，特别是证据保全与诉讼中止等高频争议点。对于参与泛欧专利诉讼的主体而言，理解这些程序性先例有助于预判案件管理节奏与合规要求，需持续关注上诉庭对程序自由裁量权的界定趋势。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["第二卷综述UPC上诉庭2023至2025年间关于证据保全、保密性及诉讼费用等重大程序性…", "文章旨在通过判例分析，澄清UPC法院如何解释和适用《程序规则》中的关键条款", "律所提及代理了包括中央分部首个无效案及首个涉及补充保护证书（SPC）的案件"]

items.title: Différents angles de vue : JUB et Haute Cour de Justice anglaise
items.title_zh: 不同视角：统一专利法院与英国高等法院
items.title_en: Divergent Perspectives: UPC and English High Court Rulings in ACD v. Molecular Instruments
items.summary: Des approches et des conclusions différentes dans l’affaire Advanced Cell Diagnostics vs. Molecular Instruments  1.1 Advanced Cell Diagnostics (ACD) a assigné Molecular Instruments (MI) pour contrefaçon d’un de ses brevets européens aussi bien devant la Juridiction Unifiée du Brevet (JUB – Chambre Locale de La Haye) que devant la Haute Cour de Justice (suite) L’article Différents angles de vue : JUB et Haute Cour de Justice anglaise est apparu en premier sur Lavoix - Propriété intellectuelle .
items.ai_summary_zh: Advanced Cell Diagnostics就其欧洲专利侵权纠纷，同时在统一专利法院（海牙地方分庭）和英国高等法院起诉Molecular Instruments。文章指出两法院在该案中采取了不同的处理方式并得出了不同结论，展示了跨法域诉讼的复杂性。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the Advanced Cell Diagnostics (ACD) versus Molecular Instruments (MI) case concerning alleged infringement of a European patent. The plaintiff initiated parallel proceedings simultaneously before the Unified Patent Court’s Local Division in The Hague and the English High Court. Although both courts adjudicated the same patent and factual matrix, their approaches diverged significantly. The article highlights substantial differences in procedural handling, legal application, and final conclusions reached by each jurisdiction. This case underscores the inherent unpredictability and strategic complexity associated with conducting parallel litigation across Europe. It emphasizes the critical importance for rights holders and defendants to thoroughly understand the distinct procedural characteristics and judicial tendencies of each court when formulating cross-border enforcement or defense strategies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注UPC与英国法院在审理同一欧洲专利争议时的程序差异及裁判结果分歧。这对涉及英欧双法域的专利权利人而言，意味着需监控平行诉讼带来的不确定性及潜在的策略冲突。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants navigating European patent disputes must carefully assess procedural disparities between the UPC and national courts like the English High Court. Strategic planning should integrate evaluations of differing burdens of proof, compensation standards, and injunction tendencies across jurisdictions. Leveraging timing gaps and divergent rulings from parallel proceedings can create opportunities to secure more favorable settlement terms or preserve market position. Reliance on a single judicial system may limit strategic options; therefore, a coordinated multi-forum approach is often necessary to optimize litigation results and manage risk effectively in complex cross-border IP conflicts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["ACD就欧洲专利侵权同时在UPC海牙分庭和英国高等法院起诉MI", "原文摘要显示，两法院在该案中采取了不同的处理方法和结论"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["ACD filed parallel infringement actions against MI regarding the same European patent in both the UPC Hague Local Division and the English High Court.", "Despite identical facts and patents, the two courts adopted different procedural paths and reached materially different legal conclusions.", "The case illustrates how jurisdiction selection critically influences outcomes in European parallel litigation scenarios."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["监控UPC与英国法院对同一专利事实认定的潜在分歧", "观察平行诉讼策略在英欧分离后的实际执行效果与挑战"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assessing procedural differences between the UPC and national courts helps optimize overall European litigation strategy.", "Practice note: Utilizing divergent rulings in parallel proceedings can help secure advantageous settlements or protect market space.", "What to monitor: Track evolving judicial tendencies regarding evidence, damages, and injunctive relief across key European forums."]
core_points_en: ACD filed parallel infringement actions against MI regarding the same European patent in both the UPC Hague Local Division and the English High Court.
Despite identical facts and patents, the two courts adopted different procedural paths and reached materially different legal conclusions.
The case illustrates how jurisdiction selection critically influences outcomes in European parallel litigation scenarios.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assessing procedural differences between the UPC and national courts helps optimize overall European litigation strategy.
Practice note: Utilizing divergent rulings in parallel proceedings can help secure advantageous settlements or protect market space.
What to monitor: Track evolving judicial tendencies regarding evidence, damages, and injunctive relief across key European forums.

items.title: Oath taking of newly appointed legally qualified judges
items.title_zh: 统一专利法院五名新任法律法官将于多个分庭宣誓就职
items.title_en: UPC Announces Oath Taking for Five Newly Appointed Legally Qualified Judges
items.summary: Oath taking of 5 newly appointed legally qualified judges will take place at the Local division Munich (DE), Local division The Hague (NL), the Nordic-Baltic Regional division, Local division Düsseldorf (DE) and Central division Munich (DE)
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院公告称，根据行政委员会 2025 年 11 月 4 日的决定，五名新任法律法官已分别被任命至慕尼黑地方法庭、海牙地方法庭、北欧-波罗的海地区分庭、杜塞尔多夫地方法庭以及慕尼黑中央分庭，并将在公开法庭依《UPC 协定》章程第 6 条宣誓后正式履职。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has announced the oath-taking ceremonies for five newly appointed legally qualified judges. These appointments follow the Administrative Committee’s decision of 4 November 2025. The judges will assume duties at the Local Divisions in Munich, The Hague, and Düsseldorf, the Nordic-Baltic Regional Division, and the Central Division in Munich. In accordance with Article 6 of the UPC Statute, each judge must take an oath in open court before commencing their judicial functions. The specific assignments include Ms Ina Schnurr (Munich Local), Mr Robert van Peursem (The Hague Local), Ms Goda Ambrasaité-Balyniené (Nordic-Baltic Regional), Mr Ingo Rinken (Düsseldorf Local), and Mr Thomas Adocker (Munich Central).
items.ai_insight_zh: 这不是实体判例更新，但对观察 UPC 机构建设和案件承载能力仍有意义。随着各地方法庭和中央分庭补充法官资源，企业和诉讼团队应继续关注分庭构成、法官背景以及这些变化对程序节奏和司法一致性的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This administrative update signals continued operational scaling within the UPC judiciary. For practitioners, these appointments reinforce the court’s capacity to handle caseloads across key jurisdictions. While not altering substantive law, the distribution of new judges across major local divisions and the Central Division may influence procedural scheduling and panel compositions. Rights holders should note these additions as part of the court’s ongoing institutional development, ensuring consistent judicial availability in high-volume venues like Munich and Düsseldorf.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["五名新任法律法官将分别在 UPC 多个分庭宣誓就职", "相关任命依据是行政委员会 2025 年 11 月 4 日决定", "法官须依 UPC 章程第 6 条在公开法庭宣誓后履职"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Five new legally qualified judges are appointed to various UPC divisions following the November 2025 Administrative Committee decision.", "Judges must take an oath in open court per Article 6 of the UPC Statute before assuming duties.", "Appointments cover Local Divisions in Munich, The Hague, Düsseldorf, the Nordic-Baltic Regional Division, and Central Division Munich."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["法官任命与宣誓是 UPC 组织稳定运行的重要信号", "不同分庭的法官构成会影响当事人对程序环境的评估", "持续补充司法资源有助于支撑未来案件量增长"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Expanded judicial roster supports stable case processing times in key European patent litigation venues.", "Practice note: New judges will preside over future cases in their respective divisions, potentially affecting panel dynamics and hearing schedules.", "What to monitor: Observe any initial procedural orders or case allocations involving these newly sworn judges to assess early judicial trends."]
core_points_en: Five new legally qualified judges are appointed to various UPC divisions following the November 2025 Administrative Committee decision.
Judges must take an oath in open court per Article 6 of the UPC Statute before assuming duties.
Appointments cover Local Divisions in Munich, The Hague, Düsseldorf, the Nordic-Baltic Regional Division, and Central Division Munich.

Practice note: New judges will preside over future cases in their respective divisions, potentially affecting panel dynamics and hearing schedules.
What to monitor: Observe any initial procedural orders or case allocations involving these newly sworn judges to assess early judicial trends.

items.title: Client-attorney privilege in patent matters: Scope, limitations and best practices
items.title_zh: 专利事务中的客户-律师特权：范围、局限与最佳实践
items.title_en: Client-Attorney Privilege in Patent Matters: Scope, Limitations and Best Practices
items.summary: Understanding the legal and professional concept of client-attorney privilege is vital for navigating the discovery process during international patent disputes.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出国际专利纠纷增多，欧洲企业常卷入美国等普通法系国家的证据开示程序。文中辨析了保密性、职业秘密与法律特权的区别，强调仅特权可豁免文件披露。内容基于播客，旨在指导欧洲专业人士理解特权在跨境诉讼中的适用范围与限制条件。
items.ai_summary_en: As international patent disputes increase, rights holders frequently face discovery proceedings in common law jurisdictions like the United States. This analysis clarifies critical distinctions between confidentiality, professional secrecy, and legal privilege. While contractual confidentiality limits information dissemination, it cannot override court orders for document production. Similarly, professional secrecy imposes ethical duties on practitioners but does not automatically shield communications from disclosure in foreign litigation. Only established legal privilege offers procedural protection against mandatory discovery. The article examines the specific limitations and recognition conditions for privilege regarding communications between clients and European patent attorneys, providing essential guidance for navigating cross-border evidence disclosure risks effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本文属于实务观察笔记，重点在于厘清概念差异。需关注欧洲企业在面对普通法系证据开示时，如何依据不同法域对特权认定的差异来管理内部沟通记录，以及特权保护在跨境语境下的实际效力边界。
items.ai_insight_en: In cross-border patent disputes, particularly those involving US-style discovery, parties must proactively manage privilege risks rather than relying solely on general confidentiality measures. Communications should ideally be routed through qualified EU lawyers to maximize the likelihood of privilege protection under local laws. Companies should clearly distinguish between technical discussions and legal advice to ensure sensitive information remains protected. Engaging local counsel early is advisable to establish robust privilege frameworks. This strategic approach helps prevent inadvertent waiver of protections and minimizes exposure to mandatory disclosure of confidential internal materials during international enforcement actions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["保密性基于合同或组织承诺，不能阻止法官在诉讼中命令生产文件", "职业秘密是专业人士的法律或道德义务，不必然保证文件在境外法院免受披露", "特权是一种程序规则，允许在诉讼特别是证据开示中拒绝生产某些内部非公开文件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Contractual confidentiality commitments cannot successfully resist judicial orders compelling the production of internal documents during litigation.", "Professional secrecy constitutes an ethical obligation for practitioners but does not guarantee document protection in cross-border legal proceedings.", "Privilege recognition depends strictly on applicable jurisdictional rules, with significant differences existing between European and US approaches to patent attorney communicatio…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需区分保密承诺、职业伦理义务与诉讼特权在法律效力上的本质不同", "观察普通法系证据开示程序中，欧洲专利代理人通信受特权保护的具体条件", "关注跨境争议中，因资格管辖地不同导致的特权认定差异及其对文档披露的影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Distinguish clearly between confidentiality, professional secrecy, and legal privilege to avoid erroneous reliance on contractual clauses that fail in court.", "Practice note: Prioritize communications via EU-qualified lawyers in cross-border disputes to secure broader privilege coverage and protect sensitive internal documents.", "What to monitor: Evolving jurisdictional standards regarding patent attorney privilege, particularly how US courts treat communications involving non-lawyer European IP profession…"]
core_points_en: Contractual confidentiality commitments cannot successfully resist judicial orders compelling the production of internal documents during litigation.
Professional secrecy constitutes an ethical obligation for practitioners but does not guarantee document protection in cross-border legal proceedings.
Privilege recognition depends strictly on applicable jurisdictional rules, with significant differences existing between European and US approaches to patent attorney communicatio…
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Distinguish clearly between confidentiality, professional secrecy, and legal privilege to avoid erroneous reliance on contractual clauses that fail in court.
Practice note: Prioritize communications via EU-qualified lawyers in cross-border disputes to secure broader privilege coverage and protect sensitive internal documents.
What to monitor: Evolving jurisdictional standards regarding patent attorney privilege, particularly how US courts treat communications involving non-lawyer European IP profession…

items.title: Can I appeal an EPO opposition decision? | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 能否对欧洲专利局异议决定提起上诉？
items.title_en: Appealing EPO Opposition Decisions: Eligibility, Deadlines, and Board of Appeal Review
items.summary: A decision of the EPO Opposition Division (OD) can be appealed, but only by a party who took part in the opposition proceedings and is adversely affected by the decision.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文解析了针对欧洲专利局异议部决定的上诉机制。依据《欧洲专利公约》第107条，仅受决定不利影响的参与方可上诉，包括专利权人、异议人或有效介入人。上诉人须在决定通知后两个月内提交上诉状并缴费，四个月内提交理由书。上诉委员会将进行独立司法审查，结果可能为维持、撤销原判或发回重审。
items.ai_summary_en: Decisions issued by the European Patent Office Opposition Division may be appealed, but standing is strictly limited to parties who participated in the opposition proceedings and are adversely affected by the outcome. Eligible appellants include the patent proprietor, any opponent, or a valid intervenor, such as an assumed infringer acting under Article 105 EPC. To initiate a valid appeal, the affected party must file a notice of appeal and pay the required fee within two months of notification of the written decision. Furthermore, a statement setting out the grounds of appeal must be submitted within four months. The Board of Appeal then conducts an independent judicial review, which may result in upholding the original decision, setting it aside, or remitting the case to the Opposition Division for further examination.
items.ai_insight_zh: 作为律所实务指引，本文明确了EPO异议后上诉的资格限制与严格时限。从业者需重点关注“不利影响”的认定标准及两个关键期限，以确保程序权利不被丧失。上诉审查的独立性意味着案件结果存在变数，可能发回重审。
items.ai_insight_en: This analysis clarifies the procedural thresholds for challenging EPO Opposition Division outcomes. It emphasizes that mere participation is insufficient; appellants must demonstrate adverse effect. Strict adherence to the two-month filing and four-month reasoning deadlines is critical to preserve rights. The independent nature of the Board of Appeal review introduces variability in outcomes, ranging from final determination to remittal, requiring strategic preparation of grounds.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["仅受异议决定不利影响的参与方（如专利权人、异议人）有权提起上诉", "上诉人须在两个月内提交通知并缴费，四个月内提交上诉理由书", "上诉委员会进行独立审查，可维持、撤销决定或将案件发回异议部"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Appeal standing is restricted to participants adversely affected by the Opposition Division decision, including proprietors, opponents, or intervenors.", "Appellants must file a notice of appeal and pay the fee within two months of receiving the written decision notification.", "The Board of Appeal conducts independent review, potentially upholding, overturning, or remitting the case after grounds are submitted within four months."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["明确上诉主体资格限于受不利影响的程序参与方", "强调两个月缴费与四个月提交理由书的严格程序时限", "关注上诉委员会独立审查后的三种可能裁决结果"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Only parties directly impacted by the opposition outcome, such as proprietors or active opponents, possess the legal standing to appeal.", "Practice note: Strict compliance with the two-month notice and four-month grounds deadlines is essential to avoid inadmissibility of the appeal.", "What to monitor: The Board of Appeal’s independent review scope and its tendency to remit cases versus issuing final decisions on merit."]
core_points_en: Appeal standing is restricted to participants adversely affected by the Opposition Division decision, including proprietors, opponents, or intervenors.
Appellants must file a notice of appeal and pay the fee within two months of receiving the written decision notification.
The Board of Appeal conducts independent review, potentially upholding, overturning, or remitting the case after grounds are submitted within four months.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Only parties directly impacted by the opposition outcome, such as proprietors or active opponents, possess the legal standing to appeal.
Practice note: Strict compliance with the two-month notice and four-month grounds deadlines is essential to avoid inadmissibility of the appeal.
What to monitor: The Board of Appeal’s independent review scope and its tendency to remit cases versus issuing final decisions on merit.

items.title: EPO opposition vs appeal | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 欧洲专利局异议与上诉程序辨析
items.title_en: Distinguishing EPO Opposition and Appeal Procedures: Key Procedural Differences
items.summary: An EPO opposition and an EPO appeal are distinct procedures, each with its own character, timetable, evidential rules, and decision-making body.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文详述欧洲专利局异议与上诉程序的本质区别。异议为行政一审，由异议部全面重审事实，允许早期提交新证据；上诉为司法复审，由上诉委员会审查异议部是否犯错，严格限制新证据准入，侧重程序合规与法律适用，二者在举证规则与时限上存在显著差异。
items.ai_summary_en: European Patent Office opposition and appeal proceedings are distinct processes with different objectives, timelines, and evidentiary standards. Opposition is a first-instance administrative review where the Opposition Division conducts a fresh factual examination, allowing broader introduction of new evidence and claim amendments. In contrast, appeal functions as a judicial review by independent Boards of Appeal, focusing on errors in the initial decision rather than re-litigating facts. Appeals operate under stricter admissibility criteria, limiting new submissions after initial deadlines. Understanding these structural differences is essential for effective portfolio management, as strategies suitable for the flexible opposition phase may fail under the rigorous procedural constraints of the appellate stage.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should treat opposition as the primary window for comprehensive fact-finding and evidence submission. Given the Boards of Appeal’s restrictive approach to late-filed material, relying on appeals to introduce new arguments or data carries significant procedural risk. Effective case preparation requires exhausting all relevant prior art and technical arguments during the opposition phase to ensure a robust record for any subsequent judicial review.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["异议属行政一审程序，异议部进行全面事实调查，早期阶段允许提交新证据及修改权利要求", "上诉属司法复审程序，上诉委员会独立审查异议部决定，严格限制新事实与证据的准入", "上诉须在决定通知后两个月内提起，四个月内提交理由，后续仅例外情况接纳新材料"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Opposition is an administrative first-instance procedure involving a fresh factual assessment by the Opposition Division.", "Appeal constitutes a judicial review focused on legal errors, governed by strict admissibility rules for new evidence.", "Parties must file notice of appeal within two months and grounds within four months of the decision."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["异议阶段是事实构建的关键窗口，应尽早提交所有相关证据与论点", "上诉程序具有严格的证据封闭性，依赖异议阶段记录，新证据极难被采纳", "需关注上诉委员会对“初步相关性”及例外情形下新证据接纳标准的司法实践"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Opposition offers a broader opportunity to challenge or defend patent validity through extensive fact-finding compared to the limited scope of appeal.", "Practice note: Present all critical evidence and arguments during opposition, as Boards of Appeal apply stringent admissibility criteria to late-filed submissions.", "What to monitor: Evolving Board of Appeal case law regarding the admission of new facts and the definition of prima facie relevance in appeal proceedings."]
core_points_en: Opposition is an administrative first-instance procedure involving a fresh factual assessment by the Opposition Division.
Appeal constitutes a judicial review focused on legal errors, governed by strict admissibility rules for new evidence.
Parties must file notice of appeal within two months and grounds within four months of the decision.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Opposition offers a broader opportunity to challenge or defend patent validity through extensive fact-finding compared to the limited scope of appeal.
Practice note: Present all critical evidence and arguments during opposition, as Boards of Appeal apply stringent admissibility criteria to late-filed submissions.
What to monitor: Evolving Board of Appeal case law regarding the admission of new facts and the definition of prima facie relevance in appeal proceedings.

items.title: Supreme Court decision: a game-changer for software inventions in the UK - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 英最高法院裁决：英国软件发明专利格局巨变
items.title_en: UK Supreme Court Adopts EPO Hardware Approach for Software Patent Eligibility
items.summary: So it is finally here, the decision from the UK Supreme Court in the long-running saga starring Emotional Perception AI and the Comptroller-General of Patents. The stage was set to address the fundamentals of the UK’s approach for assessing statutory exclusions to patentability, including for software and AI. Has the decision rightly earned its place in the spotlight? With the last lines delivered, here are the must‑know consequences for innovators.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国最高法院在Emotional Perception AI案中裁定，废除沿用二十年的Aerotel测试，转而采用欧洲专利局G 1/19确立的“任何硬件”方法。只要权利要求包含至少一个硬件特征，即不被排除于专利适格性之外。法院要求遵循Duns原则的大部分内容，但在创造性判断上保留英国Pozzoli方法，并引入评估特征对创造性贡献的中间步骤。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Supreme Court has abandoned the twenty-year-old Aerotel test for assessing patent eligibility of software and AI inventions. In Emotional Perception AI, the Court adopted the European Patent Office’s “any hardware” approach from G 1/19. Claims including at least one hardware feature, such as a processor, are no longer excluded under Section 1(2) of the Patents Act 1977. The Court mandated adherence to most Duns principles regarding technical character assessment but retained the UK’s Pozzoli framework for inventive step. This ruling introduces an intermediate evaluation step between eligibility and inventive step, significantly lowering the initial barrier for patentability while maintaining distinct UK standards for obviousness.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision aligns UK patent eligibility practice more closely with the EPO, reducing uncertainty for software and AI applicants. By removing the strict Aerotel hurdle, rights holders may secure eligibility more readily. However, the introduction of an intermediate step for assessing technical contribution requires careful claim drafting. Practitioners must now navigate how isolated excluded features contribute to inventive step under the retained Pozzoli framework, necessitating updated prosecution strategies for UK filings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["最高法院废除Aerotel测试，改用EPO“任何硬件”标准，显著降低软件专利适格性门槛", "法院要求遵循Duns原则，但在创造性评估上拒绝采用EPO问题-解决方案法，维持Pozz…", "引入介于适格性与创造性之间的中间步骤，用于评估孤立看属排除对象但整体具技术性的特征"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Supreme Court replaces Aerotel test with EPO any hardware approach for software patent eligibility.", "Claims containing hardware features like processors are no longer excluded as computer programs as such.", "Court adopts Duns principles for technical character but retains UK Pozzoli test for inventive step."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["软件及AI企业需注意英国专利适格性门槛降低，含硬件特征的权利要求更易通过初审", "需观察英国知识产权局如何具体实施“中间步骤”，以明确技术性特征对创造性的贡献评估方式", "关注后续案例中法院如何将Duns原则与英国本土Pozzoli创造性测试相结合的具体司法…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Lowered eligibility hurdles facilitate patent protection for software and AI innovations in the UK.", "Practice note: Draft claims with explicit hardware features to satisfy the new any hardware eligibility standard.", "What to monitor: UKIPO guidelines and case law clarifying the intermediate step for technical contribution assessment."]
core_points_en: Supreme Court replaces Aerotel test with EPO any hardware approach for software patent eligibility.
Claims containing hardware features like processors are no longer excluded as computer programs as such.
Court adopts Duns principles for technical character but retains UK Pozzoli test for inventive step.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Lowered eligibility hurdles facilitate patent protection for software and AI innovations in the UK.
Practice note: Draft claims with explicit hardware features to satisfy the new any hardware eligibility standard.
What to monitor: UKIPO guidelines and case law clarifying the intermediate step for technical contribution assessment.

items.title: Latest developments in EU green trademarks (Part II)
items.title_zh: 欧盟绿色商标最新发展（第二部分）
items.title_en: EU Green Trademarks: Descriptiveness Risks and the Potential of Certification Marks
items.summary: In Part I (here) we shared thoughts about the phenomenon of green branding in the EU as well as the consolidation of the interpretation by the General Court (GC) of deceptiveness to combat green washing. In this second part, we look into other grounds for refusal or invalidity of green claims in trademarks, namely, descriptiveness and lack of distinctiveness, and at the big potential of EU certification marks.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文探讨欧盟绿色商标除欺骗性外的其他驳回或无效理由，重点分析描述性与缺乏显著性。普通法院判例强调公众将环保主张视为产品质量指示而非来源标识。EUIPO实践存在不一致，既驳回部分描述性词汇，又批准部分口号。此外，文章指出欧盟认证商标在促进可持续发展方面具有潜力，并获不公平竞争法支持。
items.ai_summary_en: This article continues the analysis of green branding in the EU, focusing on grounds for refusal or invalidity based on descriptiveness and lack of distinctiveness. The General Court has emphasized that consumers often perceive environmental claims as indications of product quality rather than source identifiers. Consequently, purely descriptive green signs cannot be protected as trademarks. While the EUIPO’s practice shows some inconsistency regarding green slogans, registered marks remain vulnerable to invalidation actions if deemed descriptive. The piece also highlights the significant potential of EU certification marks as a strategic tool for promoting sustainable economic practices and securing robust brand protection.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务中需警惕绿色标语因被视为质量指示而缺乏显著性的风险。EUIPO审查标准尚存波动，获准注册不意味绝对安全，后续仍面临基于描述性的无效挑战。欧盟认证商标作为替代方案值得关注，其法律框架正得到不公平竞争法规的明确支持，适合构建可信的绿色品牌体系。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should avoid using terms that directly describe environmental characteristics, as these are likely to be rejected for lacking distinctiveness. Instead, rights holders should consider leveraging the EU certification mark system to build credible green brands through verified compliance. This approach helps mitigate risks associated with subsequent invalidation proceedings and unfair competition litigation. By aligning with established certification standards, companies can create a more defensible intellectual property position while demonstrating genuine commitment to sustainability goals.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["普通法院判例确认“eco”等绿色标志因描述商品特征而缺乏显著性，需保留供公众自由使用", "EUIPO对绿色商标审查实践不一致，既驳回“upcycling”等描述词，又批准“Ma…", "已注册的欧盟绿色商标若经审视，可能因描述性或缺乏显著性而在后续程序中被宣告无效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The General Court confirms that purely descriptive green signs lack distinctive character and cannot be registered as EU trademarks.", "EUIPO examination practice regarding green slogans remains inconsistent, creating uncertainty for applicants seeking registration.", "EU certification marks are identified as a key future instrument for promoting environmental sustainability and compliance."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["绿色标语易被认定为缺乏显著性的质量指示，注册风险较高", "EUIPO审查实践存在不一致，已注册商标仍面临基于描述性的无效风险", "欧盟认证商标成为推广环保品牌的潜在有效工具，受政策鼓励"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid direct descriptive green vocabulary to prevent trademark rejection due to lack of distinctiveness.", "Practice note: Actively utilize EU certification marks to construct a compliant green brand strategy and enhance credibility.", "What to monitor: Monitor EUIPO case law trends regarding the distinction between descriptive claims and protectable green slogans."]
core_points_en: The General Court confirms that purely descriptive green signs lack distinctive character and cannot be registered as EU trademarks.
EUIPO examination practice regarding green slogans remains inconsistent, creating uncertainty for applicants seeking registration.
EU certification marks are identified as a key future instrument for promoting environmental sustainability and compliance.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid direct descriptive green vocabulary to prevent trademark rejection due to lack of distinctiveness.
Practice note: Actively utilize EU certification marks to construct a compliant green brand strategy and enhance credibility.
What to monitor: Monitor EUIPO case law trends regarding the distinction between descriptive claims and protectable green slogans.

items.title: Getty v Stability AI: Infringing Copies and gen-AI Training - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: Getty诉Stability AI：侵权复制与生成式AI训练
items.title_en: Getty v Stability AI: Analyzing Infringing Copies and Generative AI Training Copyright Issues
items.summary: The Getty Images v Stability AI [1] case has been highly anticipated for several years. In this article we examine the AI-driven copyright issues that arose during the case; analyse Mrs Justice Smith’s decision (including what, in our view, the court got right, and perhaps didn’t); and consider the future of copyright protection in a […]
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析备受关注的Getty Images诉Stability AI案中出现的AI驱动版权问题。作者审视了Smith法官的判决，评估法院认定的对错，并探讨在AI模型大规模使用训练数据背景下，版权保护的未来走向及原创性争议。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the high-profile Getty Images v Stability AI litigation, focusing on critical copyright disputes surrounding generative AI training. It provides a detailed evaluation of Mrs Justice Smith’s judgment, assessing the court’s reasoning regarding the classification of infringing copies and the legal nature of model weights. The article highlights both the strengths and potential weaknesses in the judicial approach to these novel technical issues. Furthermore, it explores the broader implications for future copyright protection frameworks as AI models increasingly rely on massive datasets for learning. The discussion addresses fundamental questions about originality and authorship in an era dominated by sophisticated algorithms, offering insights into how current legal standards may evolve to address the challenges posed by large-scale data consumption and automated content generation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案是厘清生成式AI训练数据版权边界的关键判例。需持续观察英国法院如何界定“侵权复制”及AI输出作品的原创性，这将直接影响AI模型开发者的合规义务与版权人的维权路径。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants deploying generative AI must navigate increasing regulatory scrutiny regarding training data compliance in the UK and EU. Rights holders should proactively audit data sources to ensure legality and implement robust data cleansing mechanisms to mitigate infringement risks. As Western jurisdictions shape global copyright norms through landmark cases like Getty v Stability AI, companies operating internationally must align their R&D and data strategies with evolving judicial precedents. Failure to establish clear provenance for training datasets may expose organizations to cross-border litigation and significant liability under emerging intellectual property frameworks.

items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Smith法官对AI训练数据使用的法律定性将确立重要先例", "需关注法院如何平衡技术创新需求与既有版权保护体系", "后续应监测该案对AI模型权重是否构成侵权衍生物的法律认定"]

Evaluates the judicial logic concerning infringing copy determinations and the specific legal attributes assigned to model weights.

Practice note: Monitor UK and EU judicial developments closely to adjust AI product research and data acquisition strategies.

items.title: Alright, alright, alright: Could McConaughey’s sound trademark silence AI?
items.title_zh: 麦康纳声音商标能否遏制AI滥用？
items.title_en: McConaughey’s Sound Trademark: A Potential Shield Against AI Voice Impersonation in the EU
items.summary: His sound trademark protecting his iconic phrase “Alright, alright, alright” offers Matthew McConaughey a new route to defend his trademark voice from AI.
items.ai_summary_zh: 马修·麦康纳在美国注册“Alright, alright, alright”声音商标以对抗AI语音滥用。文章指出，欧盟现行肖像权、版权及GDPR等法规在保护声音免受AI深度伪造方面存在局限。丹麦与荷兰正探索通过版权或邻接权扩展对声音的保护，凸显传统知识产权框架在应对新技术挑战时的不足。
items.ai_summary_en: Actor Matthew McConaughey secured US sound trademark registration for his iconic phrase “Alright, alright, alright,” protecting specific intonation and tone. This strategy aims to combat unauthorized AI voice synthesis and deepfakes. However, under current EU frameworks, portrait rights exclude voices, and copyright protection is often unattainable due to insufficient originality in short utterances. While the EU AI Act offers some regulatory safeguards, traditional intellectual property laws remain inadequate against sophisticated voice cloning. Consequently, Nordic countries like the Netherlands and Denmark are exploring legislative reforms to grant individuals quasi-copyright protections over their voices and physical characteristics, highlighting a significant gap in existing European legal defenses.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务观察：传统知识产权在应对AI语音克隆时存在保护缺口。需密切关注欧盟成员国是否通过立法扩张声音权益保护范围，以及声音商标在跨境AI侵权纠纷中的实际执行力与认定标准。
items.ai_insight_en: Reliance on traditional trademark registrations alone may prove insufficient for defending against AI-driven voice impersonation within the EU. Rights holders should adopt a multi-layered defense strategy that integrates compliance with the EU AI Act and explores data rights protections. It is critical to assess the feasibility of sound trademarks while simultaneously establishing barriers through unfair competition laws and data privacy regulations. Given the evolving legal landscape, particularly in Nordic jurisdictions, market participants must remain agile. Proactive monitoring of legislative developments regarding the intellectual property status of human biometric features will be essential for constructing robust, future-proof brand and personality protection frameworks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["麦康纳获美声音商标注册，保护其特定语调节奏，旨在防御AI未经授权使用其声音", "欧盟现行法律中，肖像权不涵盖声音，版权难保护短促语句，对AI语音合成缺乏有效救济", "丹麦提议将声音纳入版权保护，荷兰探讨基于邻接权的保护方案，以弥补现有法律空白"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["McConaughey registered a sound trademark covering specific vocal intonation to prevent unauthorized AI imitation of his distinctive voice.", "EU portrait rights do not cover voices, and copyright law generally fails to protect short spoken phrases lacking sufficient creative originality.", "Nordic nations are advancing legislation to provide personal voice and bodily feature protections similar to copyright against AI misuse."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["现有IP框架对AI语音合成的保护效力有限，声音商标成为新兴防御策略", "欧盟成员国正探索立法改革，可能将声音纳入版权或邻接权保护范畴", "需持续观察声音商标在对抗AI深度伪造时的司法实践及跨国适用性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate sound trademark viability alongside unfair competition and data compliance strategies to mitigate AI voice infringement risks.", "Practice note: Develop multi-dimensional defense systems combining IP rights with regulatory compliance under the EU AI Act framework.", "What to monitor: Legislative trends in Nordic countries regarding quasi-copyright protections for personal voice and bodily characteristics."]
core_points_en: McConaughey registered a sound trademark covering specific vocal intonation to prevent unauthorized AI imitation of his distinctive voice.
EU portrait rights do not cover voices, and copyright law generally fails to protect short spoken phrases lacking sufficient creative originality.
Nordic nations are advancing legislation to provide personal voice and bodily feature protections similar to copyright against AI misuse.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Evaluate sound trademark viability alongside unfair competition and data compliance strategies to mitigate AI voice infringement risks.
Practice note: Develop multi-dimensional defense systems combining IP rights with regulatory compliance under the EU AI Act framework.
What to monitor: Legislative trends in Nordic countries regarding quasi-copyright protections for personal voice and bodily characteristics.

items.title: Parallel imports and trademark exhaustion: How to protect your rights and limit your risks
items.title_zh: 平行进口与商标权用尽：权利保护与风险限制指南
items.title_en: Parallel Imports and Trademark Exhaustion: Protecting Rights in the EEA
items.summary: Discover the importance of vigilance around parallel imports, including the risks that parallel trade will lead to exhaustion of rights in trademark portfolios.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文阐述欧洲经济区内商标权用尽原则，即经同意首次销售后权利人原则上不得反对后续流通。但若商品状态改变、包装受损或声誉受损，权利人可反对平行进口。对于EEA外经许可销售的产品，权利未用尽，可通过海关备案阻止其进入EEA。
items.ai_summary_en: Parallel imports of genuine goods challenge brand owners' control over distribution and pricing within the European Economic Area. Under the principle of trademark exhaustion, rights are generally exhausted after the first marketed sale with consent, preventing opposition to further intra-EEA trade. However, this exhaustion is not absolute. Brand owners may oppose parallel imports if valid reasons exist, such as altered product conditions, damaged packaging, or reputational harm. Crucially, trademark rights are not exhausted for products first marketed outside the EEA. Consequently, rights holders can prevent unauthorized imports from third countries by registering trademarks with customs and submitting Applications for Action, thereby distinguishing authorized distributors from unauthorized parallel traders.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders operating in Europe, distinguishing between intra-EEA and extra-EEA sourcing is critical for enforcement strategy. While intra-EEA parallel trade is largely protected, customs registration remains a vital tool for blocking non-EEA imports. Maintaining accurate lists of authorized distributors ensures precise customs interventions, balancing brand protection with legal compliance regarding exhaustion principles.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["在EEA内经同意首次营销后，商标权用尽，权利人原则上不能反对区内进一步营销", "若产品状况改变、包装受损或展示损害商标声誉，权利人拥有反对平行进口的正当理由", "EEA外经许可销售的产品权利未用尽，权利人可通过海关行动申请阻止其进口至EEA"]

items.title: BSH before the UPC and the German courts – first applications, an emerging tendency and open guiding questions [Update on BSH case law]
items.title_zh: BSH案在UPC与德国法院：最新应用、趋势及待解问题
items.title_en: BSH Case Law: Early UPC and German Court Applications, Emerging Trends, and Open Questions
items.summary: Update on the article “BSH as a Gateway to Cross-Border Patent Enforcement: The Regeneron/Bayer vs Formycon and Onesta vs BMW Cases” from December 1, 2025 The CJEU’s decision in BSH v Electrolux (C-339/22) has by now been expressly taken up... Der Beitrag BSH before the UPC and the German courts – first applications, an emerging tendency and open guiding questions [Update on BSH case law] erschien zuerst auf BOEHMERT & BOEHMERT .
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文更新2025年12月文章，指出欧盟法院在BSH诉伊莱克斯案（C-339/22）中的裁决已被明确采纳。文章分析了该判例在统一专利法院及德国法院的首批应用情况、显现出的司法倾向以及尚存的指导性法律问题。
items.ai_summary_en: This update examines the practical impact of the CJEU’s BSH v Electrolux ruling (C-339/22) on subsequent patent litigation within the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and German national courts. The analysis details how these judicial bodies have expressly adopted the CJEU’s reasoning in their initial decisions. It identifies emerging tendencies in cross-border patent enforcement, highlighting specific early-stage applications that signal a shift in judicial approach. Furthermore, the article addresses several critical guiding questions that remain unresolved, creating legal uncertainty for rights holders and defendants alike. By mapping these developments, the piece provides essential context for understanding the current landscape of European patent enforcement, offering practitioners a clearer view of how precedent is being applied and where strategic gaps persist in the evolving jurisprudence.
items.ai_insight_zh: 需关注欧盟法院BSH判例在UPC与德国法院的具体适用差异及 emerging tendency。目前仅知裁决已被采纳，具体指导性问题尚未在摘要中详述，需查阅全文以评估对跨境执行策略的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and market participants should closely monitor how the UPC interprets and applies CJEU precedents to anticipate outcomes in cross-border disputes. Given the evolving judicial landscape, it is prudent to assess infringement risks for core products in Europe proactively. Companies should leverage observed trends to optimize venue selection for litigation strategies. Additionally, preparing contingency plans for potential cross-border injunctions is advisable, particularly concerning pending legal issues where guidance is still developing. This proactive approach helps mitigate risks associated with the increasingly unified yet complex European patent enforcement environment.

items.title: UK IPO: Combination authorisation blocks SPC for later single-ingredient product - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: 英知局：组合授权阻碍后续单方产品SPC申请
items.title_en: UK IPO: Prior Combination Authorisation Blocks SPC for Later Single-Ingredient Product
items.summary: Most therapeutics are first developed and approved as monotherapies, with their use in combination with other actives generally introduced only at a later stage, if at all. The SPC implications of this regulatory sequence have been the subject of extensive case law, most recently addressed by the CJEU in joined cases C‑119/22 and C‑149/22 Teva […]
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局在BL O/0705/25号决定中驳回了一项基于后续单方避孕药授权的SPC申请。尽管活性成分此前仅以固定剂量组合形式获批，英知局认定该组合授权构成法规意义上的“首次授权”。因此，后续单方授权不符合SPC条例第3(d)条要求。此举表明英知局继续遵循欧盟法院既有判例，即含该活性成分的最早授权即为首次授权。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office rejected a supplementary protection certificate application for drospirenone in decision BL O/0705/25. The applicant sought an SPC based on a later marketing authorisation for a single-ingredient contraceptive, despite earlier approvals of the same active ingredient in fixed-dose combinations with estrogen. The Hearing Officer determined that the earlier combination authorisations constituted the first authorisation under Article 3(d) of Regulation (EC) No 469/2009. Consequently, the later monotherapy approval did not satisfy the statutory requirement for being the first authorisation to place the product on the market. This ruling reinforces strict interpretation of first authorisation rules in contexts where regulatory approval sequences move from combination therapies to standalone products.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights significant risks for SPC strategies involving active ingredients initially approved in combinations. Rights holders should anticipate that UK IPO practice will treat any prior authorisation containing the active ingredient as the definitive first authorisation, even if the initial product was a fixed-dose combination. This approach limits the ability to secure term extension based on subsequent monotherapy approvals, requiring careful sequencing analysis during portfolio planning and regulatory interaction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英知局认定含活性成分的早期固定剂量组合授权构成SPC条例下的“首次授权”", "基于后续单方产品营销授权的SPC申请因不符合第3(d)条规定被正式驳回", "该决定确认英知局继续遵循Medeva等欧盟法院判例确立的审查标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["UK IPO rejected SPC for single-ingredient product due to earlier fixed-dose combination authorisations.", "Earlier combination approvals deemed the first authorisation under Article 3(d) of the SPC Regulation.", "Decision confirms strict application of first authorisation rules regardless of later monotherapy regulatory status."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["早期组合授权将阻断基于后续单方授权的SPC申请资格", "英国目前仍严格沿用脱欧前欧盟法院关于“首次授权”的判例逻辑", "需关注该审查标准对从组合疗法转向单方疗法产品的专利寿命影响"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Earlier combination approvals may permanently block SPC eligibility for later single-ingredient formulations of the same active.", "Practice note: UK IPO applies Article 3(d) strictly, treating any prior market authorisation containing the active as the first authorisation.", "What to monitor: Future appeals or guidance on whether this logic extends to step-down combinations or partial component overlaps."]
core_points_en: UK IPO rejected SPC for single-ingredient product due to earlier fixed-dose combination authorisations.
Earlier combination approvals deemed the first authorisation under Article 3(d) of the SPC Regulation.
Decision confirms strict application of first authorisation rules regardless of later monotherapy regulatory status.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Earlier combination approvals may permanently block SPC eligibility for later single-ingredient formulations of the same active.
Practice note: UK IPO applies Article 3(d) strictly, treating any prior market authorisation containing the active as the first authorisation.
What to monitor: Future appeals or guidance on whether this logic extends to step-down combinations or partial component overlaps.

items.title: The Dracula paradox: temporal limitations in EU trademark law
items.title_zh: 德古拉悖论：欧盟商标法中的时间限制
items.title_en: The Dracula Paradox: CJEU Limits National Tolerance Rules in EU Trademark Law
items.summary: The CJEU's recent judgment in Lunapark (C-452/24) has sent shockwaves through EU trademark law by holding that national courts cannot limit trademark rights for reasons of tolerance beyond the concept of acquiescence provided in Articles 18(1) and 9(1)-(2) of Directive 2015/2436.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院在Lunapark案中裁定，成员国法院不得以“容忍”为由，超出《商标指令》规定的“默许”概念限制商标权。该案涉及芬兰法院对长期未维权商标适用“合理时间”要求被挑战。法院认定第18(1)条完全 harmonise 了因权利人懈怠而限制权利的条件，仅适用于对后续注册商标的默许，排除了对未注册标志的容忍，确保欧盟法律适用的统一性。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union’s recent judgment in Lunapark (C-452/24) significantly impacts EU trademark law by ruling that national courts cannot restrict trademark rights based on tolerance beyond the specific acquiescence provisions in Directive 2015/2436. The case involved a dispute where Lunapark held the registered 'DRACULA' mark, while Hardeco’s predecessor had used the sign for years without exclusive rights. Despite Lunapark’s delay in initiating proceedings, the CJEU rejected the Finnish court’s application of a 'reasonable time' requirement. The Court emphasized that Article 18(1) fully harmonizes conditions for limiting rights due to inactivity, excluding unregistered signs from protection via general national principles. This decision ensures uniform application across the EU but raises concerns regarding legal certainty and the distinction between substantive limitations and procedural enforcement rules.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该判决强化了欧盟商标保护的统一性，但可能削弱第三方基于长期使用的合理预期。实务中需关注成员国如何区分程序性时效与实体权利限制，以及损害赔偿与禁令救济在时间限制上的不同处理，以平衡法律确定性与公平结果。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should recognize that long-standing non-enforcement by a rights holder does not automatically create a defense against future infringement claims. Reliance on implied consent or national limitation periods may be insufficient under the current harmonized framework. Companies must proactively secure registration for key brands rather than depending on prior use alone. For businesses utilizing unregistered signs, accelerating registration processes or negotiating coexistence agreements is critical to mitigate litigation risks. Furthermore, when facing delayed enforcement actions, defendants should prepare robust evidence challenging the substantive basis of infringement, as procedural time-bar defenses face heightened scrutiny following this ruling.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟法院裁定成员国不得以一般容忍原则超越指令规定的默许规则限制商标权", "商标指令第18(1)条完全协调了因权利人懈怠限制权利的条件，仅涵盖对后续注册商标的默许", "该判决排除成员国通过国内法一般原则规避协调后的默许规则，保障欧盟范围内法律适用统一"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Member States are prohibited from using general legal principles to exceed Directive limits on restricting trademark rights through tolerance.", "Long-term use of an unregistered sign does not constitute a statutory defense against the exclusive rights of a registered EU trademark.", "The judgment preserves systemic unity but is criticized for overlooking procedural autonomy and third parties' legitimate expectations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["欧盟法院强调商标指令对默许规则的完全协调，限制了成员国法院引入额外时间限制的权力", "判决未充分区分实体限制与程序性执行规则，可能影响成员国基于等效性和有效性原则的时效抗辩", "需观察后续案件中，长期使用但未注册的标志使用者在面对侵权诉讼时的法律地位及救济限制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Long-term non-enforcement by a rights holder does not guarantee immunity from future injunctions or damages under EU law.", "Practice note: Secure early trademark registration for core assets; do not rely on implied consent or national tolerance doctrines as primary defenses.", "What to monitor: How national courts apply equivalence and effectiveness principles to procedural time limits in light of full harmonization mandates."]
core_points_en: Member States are prohibited from using general legal principles to exceed Directive limits on restricting trademark rights through tolerance.
Long-term use of an unregistered sign does not constitute a statutory defense against the exclusive rights of a registered EU trademark.
The judgment preserves systemic unity but is criticized for overlooking procedural autonomy and third parties' legitimate expectations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Long-term non-enforcement by a rights holder does not guarantee immunity from future injunctions or damages under EU law.
Practice note: Secure early trademark registration for core assets; do not rely on implied consent or national tolerance doctrines as primary defenses.
What to monitor: How national courts apply equivalence and effectiveness principles to procedural time limits in light of full harmonization mandates.
