items.title: Neurim in the mirror - Carpmaels & Ransford - Law Firm
items.title_zh: Neurim案回响：德国法院就SPC“首次授权”再提请先决裁决
items.title_en: German Court Refers Neurim Question to CJEU on SPC First Authorisation Definition
items.summary: There has been a new reference made to the CJEU on the correct interpretation of Article 3(d) of the SPC Regulation (Regulation (EC) No 469/2009), this time from the Bundespatentgericht (BPatG), the German Federal Patent Court. The reference concerns what counts as a “first authorisation” to place a product on the market. The CJEU has […]
items.ai_summary_zh: 德国联邦专利法院就SPC条例第3(d)条中“首次上市授权”的定义向欧盟法院提起新的先决裁决请求。案件涉及勃林格殷格翰的兽药Aservo Equihaler，虽含活性成分ciclesonide的人药已获批，但公司主张兽药与人药应严格区分。此前Santen案已推翻Neurim案的宽松解释，回归严格标准，本案将延续这一法律争议。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Federal Patent Court has referred a new question to the CJEU regarding the interpretation of Article 3(d) of the SPC Regulation. The case concerns whether a prior human marketing authorisation precludes a Supplementary Protection Certificate for a veterinary product containing the same active ingredient. Boehringer Ingelheim sought an SPC for Aservo Equihaler, the first veterinary authorisation for ciclesonide. However, the German Patent Office rejected the application, citing the CJEU’s Santen decision. The DPMA argued that Santen overruled the earlier Neurim ruling, requiring assessment based on the active ingredient rather than therapeutic use. This reference seeks clarification on whether strict regulatory distinctions between human and veterinary sectors should apply when determining the ‘first authorisation’ for SPC eligibility.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案核心在于厘清Santen案后不同治疗领域（人用与兽用）是否构成独立的“首次授权”。若欧盟法院维持严格解释，将限制跨界活性成分的SPC获取；若采纳区分观点，可能为特定兽药开辟新路径。需密切关注欧盟法院最终裁决对既有案例法的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral highlights significant uncertainty in European SPC practice regarding cross-sector authorisations. Rights holders developing veterinary or human medicines with known active ingredients face potential barriers to obtaining patent term extensions if prior authorisations in other therapeutic fields are deemed relevant. The outcome will determine whether the Santen strict standard applies universally or if exceptions remain for distinct regulatory pathways. Stakeholders should assess their portfolios for similar conflicts where prior human or veterinary approvals might invalidate subsequent SPC applications, potentially impacting market exclusivity strategies across different medical sectors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国联邦专利法院就SPC条例第3(d)条“首次授权”定义向欧盟法院提起新先决裁决", "勃林格殷格翰主张兽药与人药授权应严格区分，尽管其活性成分人药已获批准", "继Santen案推翻Neurim案后，欧盟法院在SPC适用范围上再次面临法律解释挑战"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["German court asks CJEU to clarify if prior human drug authorisation blocks SPCs for veterinary products with identical active ingredients.", "Boehringer argues for strict regulatory separation between veterinary and human medicinal product authorisations to satisfy SPC requirements.", "CJEU must resolve tension between Santen's strict active ingredient approach and Neurim's flexible therapeutic use interpretation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟法院是否会在Santen案基础上进一步细化“首次授权”在不同监管框架下的认定标准", "观察该裁决对拥有跨人用/兽用活性成分组合的制药企业SPC布局策略的潜在影响", "留意捷克、法国和荷兰等国在类似案件中的司法实践差异是否会通过此案得到统一"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prior human or veterinary authorisations may block SPCs for new therapeutic uses of known active ingredients under current strict interpretations.", "Practice note: Evaluate regulatory history carefully to identify conflicting prior authorisations that could jeopardize SPC eligibility in Europe.", "What to monitor: Await CJEU ruling to determine if distinct human and veterinary regulatory frameworks preserve SPC rights despite shared active ingredients."]
core_points_en: German court asks CJEU to clarify if prior human drug authorisation blocks SPCs for veterinary products with identical active ingredients.
Boehringer argues for strict regulatory separation between veterinary and human medicinal product authorisations to satisfy SPC requirements.
CJEU must resolve tension between Santen's strict active ingredient approach and Neurim's flexible therapeutic use interpretation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prior human or veterinary authorisations may block SPCs for new therapeutic uses of known active ingredients under current strict interpretations.
Practice note: Evaluate regulatory history carefully to identify conflicting prior authorisations that could jeopardize SPC eligibility in Europe.
What to monitor: Await CJEU ruling to determine if distinct human and veterinary regulatory frameworks preserve SPC rights despite shared active ingredients.

items.title: The UPC's LD Hamburg remains firm on late-filed submissions (UPC_CFI_461/2024; UPC_CFI_718/2024)
items.title_zh: UPC汉堡分院坚持驳回逾期提交的抗辩材料
items.title_en: UPC Hamburg Rejects Late-Filed Invalidity Attacks and Evidence in Dolle v. FAKRO
items.summary: Court docket: LD
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院汉堡地方法院在Dolle诉FAKRO案中，针对EP2476814专利侵权及无效反诉作出裁决。法院强调UPC程序的“前端加载”特性，严格限制逾期提交的证据和无效理由。被告在提出无效反诉后补充的新事实及庭审中新提出的创造性抗辩均被认定为不可受理，即便部分证据已在案卷中。
items.ai_summary_en: In the case of Dolle A/S v. FAKRO Dachflächenfenster GmbH, the UPC Local Division in Hamburg ruled that new facts, evidence, and invalidity grounds submitted by the defendant after filing the counterclaim for revocation were inadmissible. The Court emphasized the front-loaded nature of UPC proceedings, requiring all validity challenges to be introduced simultaneously with the revocation counterclaim. Consequently, late-filed attacks presented during oral hearings were rejected to ensure the opposing party’s right to prepare an adequate defense and to maintain procedural efficiency. This decision underscores strict adherence to procedural timelines, distinguishing UPC practice from more flexible approaches seen elsewhere.
items.ai_insight_zh: 该裁决凸显UPC对程序效率的重视，其逾期证据接纳标准比欧洲专利局异议部更为严格。当事人需在诉讼早期全面准备无效攻击理由，避免依赖后续补充。实务中应密切关注UPC各分院对“充分理由”的认定尺度，以确保程序合规。
items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in UPC litigation must adopt a front-loaded strategy, ensuring all prior art and invalidity arguments are comprehensively presented at the initial stage. Relying on later supplementation, a habit common in EPO opposition proceedings, risks exclusion of critical evidence. Rights holders and defendants should conduct thorough early searches and legal assessments to avoid losing substantive rights due to procedural non-compliance. This approach enhances precision in cross-border enforcement and defense, aligning with the UPC’s emphasis on swift, efficient resolution without compromising fair trial standards.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院强调UPC程序的前端加载性质，要求无效抗辩须与反诉同时提出", "被告在反诉后逾期提交的新事实、证据及新抗辩理由均未被法院接纳", "即便基于已有证据，在口头听证阶段首次提出的创造性抗辩亦因程序原因被驳回"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Invalidity attacks must be filed concurrently with the counterclaim for revocation, with strict limits on subsequent submissions.", "New invalidity grounds or documents are generally prohibited during oral hearings to protect defense rights and accelerate proceedings.", "This strict stance exceeds EPO Opposition Division flexibility, prioritizing procedural efficiency and fairness in UPC litigation."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC程序对逾期材料采取比EPO异议程序更严格的排除态度", "无效抗辩必须在提起反诉时完整提出，后续补充空间极小", "需持续观察其他分院在类似情形下对逾期证据可采性的裁量差异"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure all invalidity evidence is submitted with the initial counterclaim to avoid inadmissibility under strict UPC rules.", "Practice note: Adapt litigation strategies to the UPC's front-loaded system, avoiding reliance on post-filing amendments common in EPO practice.", "What to monitor: Observe future Local Division decisions regarding admissibility thresholds for late-filed evidence and procedural sanctions."]
core_points_en: Invalidity attacks must be filed concurrently with the counterclaim for revocation, with strict limits on subsequent submissions.
New invalidity grounds or documents are generally prohibited during oral hearings to protect defense rights and accelerate proceedings.
This strict stance exceeds EPO Opposition Division flexibility, prioritizing procedural efficiency and fairness in UPC litigation.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure all invalidity evidence is submitted with the initial counterclaim to avoid inadmissibility under strict UPC rules.
Practice note: Adapt litigation strategies to the UPC's front-loaded system, avoiding reliance on post-filing amendments common in EPO practice.
What to monitor: Observe future Local Division decisions regarding admissibility thresholds for late-filed evidence and procedural sanctions.

items.title: EU steps up fight against IP crime to protect innovation and competitiveness
items.title_zh: EUIPO 联合 Eurojust 推进打击知识产权犯罪协作
items.title_en: EUIPO and Eurojust Convene 10th High-Level Meeting on IP Crime
items.summary: EU steps up fight against IP crime to protect innovation and competitiveness Reinforcing the EU´s commitment to stronger collaboration, the 10th High-Level Meeting on Intellectual Property (IP) Crime, organised by EUIPO and hosted by Eurojust in The Hague, brought together representatives from other
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO 发布消息称，第十届知识产权犯罪高级别会议在海牙举行，由 EUIPO 主办并由 Eurojust 承办，会议聚焦通过更强的跨机构协作打击知识产权犯罪，以保护创新与竞争力。这一动态体现出欧盟仍在把知识产权执法与更广泛的竞争力和跨境刑事协作框架结合推进。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) and Eurojust convened the 10th High-Level Meeting on Intellectual Property Crime in The Hague. This gathering reinforces the EU commitment to stronger collaboration among enforcement bodies to combat IP crime. By bringing together representatives from relevant institutions, the meeting aims to protect innovation and competitiveness through coordinated action. The event highlights the ongoing institutional focus on cross-border cooperation and the alignment of judicial and administrative efforts to address intellectual property infringements within the European framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 这不是实体法修订，但对品牌保护、海关协作和线上线下反假货执法都有现实意义。权利人应持续关注 EUIPO、执法机构和司法协作平台之间的数据共享、联合行动和证据协同机制，因为这类机制往往决定跨境执法效率。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the EU's continued prioritization of multi-agency cooperation in IP enforcement. For rights holders, it signals sustained institutional attention to cross-border criminal IP violations. Monitoring future outputs from this forum may reveal enhanced mechanisms for data sharing and joint operational coordination between judicial and administrative authorities.

The meeting focused on reinforcing EU commitment to stronger collaboration against IP crime.
